Act 1 Success__line_112: Sieger := Good to keep an eye on the sky! Not much you can do about this one though!
Act 1 Success__line_113: Sieger takes a good look at the island chunk, sizing it up.
Act 1 Success__line_114: Sieger := I wouldn’t worry though, it’ll probably crumble off of your farm before too long, haha!
Act 1 Success__line_119: Sieger := Ahh, well I might have a clue as to why.
Act 1 Success__line_120: Sieger’s demeanor changes, becoming more serious than he’s been so far.
Act 1 Success__line_121: Sieger := I’m pretty sure that these Catfolk came from the nearby Dungeon Island.
Act 1 Success__line_122: Sieger := There’s been some rumors of Poachers in this region of the Frontier, they might have tracked the Dungeon Island here.
Act 1 Success__line_132: Sieger := Oh, you didn’t know? They’re despicable people.
Act 1 Success__line_133: Sieger := They trawl through the Frontier and harvest the essence of monsters through grisly means.
Act 1 Success__line_134: <PLAYERNAME> := How do they do that, Sieger?
Act 1 Success__line_136: A tight, clammy feeling gathers in your stomach at the word.
Act 1 Success__line_138: Sieger := Terrible, but profitable among the morally bankrupt.
Act 1 Success__line_139: Fio := Worse ways to get it than bloodletting.
Act 1 Success__line_144: Sieger := Indeed, this no doubt confirms that they’re here.
Act 1 Success__line_148: <PLAYERNAME> := So that’s probably why they attacked me, right? They can’t take the chance.
Act 1 Success__line_149: Sieger := Yes. We’ve had a few reports of increased aggression from monster groups along this region of the Frontier, no doubt because of those poachers.
Act 1 Success__line_150: Sieger takes a deep breath, and his confident demeanor returns.
Act 1 Success__line_151: Sieger := No need to fret though. It’s the Union’s duty to bring law to the Frontier, and I, Sieger, will ensure that justice is served in the end!
Act 1 Success__line_152: Fio := Well I hope you're as competent as you are confident.
Act 1 Success__line_153: Sieger := I assure you ma'am, I am quite capable!
Act 1 Success__line_154: <PLAYERNAME> := Well, I don’t envy the poachers if you find them, but I don’t think I would pity them either.
Act 1 Success__line_155: Sieger := Hahaha, they’ll have some time to rethink their ways, thirty to forty years behind bars to be precise!
Act 1 Success__line_156: Sieger := So worry not, <PLAYERNAME>, all will be well in time!
Act 1 Success__line_160: <PLAYERNAME> := Is there anything I can do to help?
Act 1 Success__line_161: Sieger := You can! I’m glad to hear that your heart also beats to a rhythm of justice!
Act 1 Success__line_162: <PLAYERNAME> := I feel awful about what’s happening to them!
Act 1 Success__line_163: Sieger := As any decent person should. But unlike any citizen, you can do something about it!
Act 1 Success__line_164: Sieger := You’re a Frontiersman of course! You can venture out into the Frontier, and even to Dungeon Islands.
Act 1 Success__line_166: Sieger := And you put up enough of a fight just now, that I think you could make a difference if you do.
Act 1 Success__line_167: Sieger := That said, no one should face a cabal of greed such as theirs alone!
Act 1 Success__line_169: Fio := All for money and money for all!
Act 1 Success__line_170: Sieger := Exactly! I’m sure you’ve met more than a few with the merits to venture out, you should talk to them, yes!
Act 1 Success__line_171: <PLAYERNAME> := Of course, I bet I can convince one or two people to come with me, but how would I get there?
Act 1 Success__line_172: Sieger := Well for that, you’d want to talk to Jubelle! She’s great. She can set up all of the logistics for transportation out into the wilds. I’d be sunk without someone like her.
Act 1 Success__line_173: <PLAYERNAME> := Why’s that? Aren’t you the Commander?
Act 1 Success__line_174: Sieger := Of course, I have an excellent sense of justice!
Act 1 Success__line_175: Sieger := Not so great a sense of direction. Hahaha!
Act 1 Success__line_176: <PLAYERNAME> := Hah... I see. Well I’ll definitely talk to her if I can!
Act 1 Success__line_177: Sieger := Excellent! Though of course, you have your other duties as well. I can’t lay any blame on you if you find yourself unable to do so, your intent shows me your strength of character!
Act 1 Success__line_178: Sieger := Now, is there anything else you have need of?
Act 1 Success__line_182: <PLAYERNAME> := I have to get going for now, the day’s still early. Thank you again for the help though, Sieger!
Act 1 Success__line_183: Sieger := Of course, any time a citizen, or Unioneer, is in need of help, I’ll be there!
Act 1 Success__line_184: Sieger := All right men, if they’re restrained let’s get them down to the outpost.
Act 1 Success__line_192: Sieger := And after that, let’s head down to the pub. I could use a second breakfast! Haha!
Act 1 Success__line_193: Fio := I could use a second breakfast.
Act 1 Success__line_194: Sieger turns to leave, but then turns back for one last moment.
Act 1 Success__line_196: Sieger := Now, one of you better buy the first round, I bought it last time!
Day7Start__line_4: You find Jubelle waiting for you.
Day7Start__line_6: Jubelle := We have one last thing to do, and then I consider you officially up to speed on how things work here in Cloverton.
Day7Start__line_8: You open your mouth to speak, but are interrupted by a growling noise. Has some monster escaped? Is there a threat nearby?
Day7Start__line_12: Jubelle := <PLAYERNAME>, have you been eating three meals every day?
Day7Start__line_14: Jubelle := Too excited to cook and eat? You have been feeding your monsters at least?
Day7Start__line_17: Jubelle := <PLAYERNAME>, if you don’t eat how can you expect to have the energy to run your farm! For shame!
Day7Start__line_19: // cue stamina card for tutorial explaining how the stamina system works.
Day7Start__line_21: Jubelle := <PLAYERNAME>! I’d like to take you down to the market.
Day7Start__line_36: Jubelle := Because your farm is in a shambles, and we need to buy things to fix it!
Day7Start__line_40: Jubelle := Oh no you don’t! You’re coming with me.
Farm Scene Field__line_4: <PLAYERNAME> := Hmm. This is good soil, but picky. If I'm not careful with what I plant I could make things harder on myself.
Farm Scene Field__line_5: <PLAYERNAME> := I'll have to water it carefully.
Farm Scene Field__line_6: <PLAYERNAME> := The seeds here are all the same as home. Nothing crazy. No flesh-eating popnionterras. Only stuff I'm used to.
Farm Scene Field__line_7: <PLAYERNAME> := But seasons remain a factor, if I don't account for that when planting I could ruin my harvest.
Farm Scene Field__line_8: <PLAYERNAME> := I'll have to pay special attention to what gets planted when if I want to take full advantage of this place.
Farm Scene Field__line_9: <PLAYERNAME> := Hngh! This place is wild, but so bountiful! If I make it right, this really could be an amazing farm!
Farm Scene Field__line_10: <PLAYERNAME> := All right! Lets get to it!
Day2Uproot__line_4: Kreyton := Alright, good, ya know how ta till soil an' plant seeds, great. Ya know about seasonal plantin', right?
Day2Uproot__line_6: Kreyton := Tell me what they taught ya in that fancy school.
Day2Uproot__line_8: <PLAYERNAME> := That each type of crop has it's prefered seasons and growth times, you have to carefully tend them, see to their needs to not just keep them from dying, but to try and improve the seeds for the next crop. It's possible to grow them out of season, but it can be really difficult, and the more sensitive plants can die outright from the wrong sort of weather.
Day2Uproot__line_10: Kreyton := An' ya mentioned sumthin’ about fertilizer?
Day2Uproot__line_12: <PLAYERNAME> := Monster essence can be collected and used directly on the soil to help the plants grow. Depending on the nature of both plant and essence, effectiveness will vary. While proper use of fertilizer can make plants healthier, helping them grow faster and survive better out of their natural season, but you have to use the right kind of monster essence for the right kind of plant.
Day2Uproot__line_14: Kreyton := Sounds like ya got good grades.
Day2Uproot__line_16: <PLAYERNAME> := Only in the subjects I liked.
Day2Uproot__line_18: Kreyton := Alright, ya know how ta plant an' till, go harvest some fruit from those mavericks out in yer field an' rip out the dead plants.
Day2Uproot__line_20: ///update quest with harvest a plant and uproot a plant
Kaleida Barks___nodes[3]._stepChain[0]._: Rainy days like this are a relief to me. Delving into matters of the occult becomes much easier with the right atmosphere.
Even if the rain is going to continue coming down, I can still perform rituals within the safety of my caravan!
Fresh rainwater has MANY occult uses. For one, if I boil it in my cauldron, it helps remove many of the impurities inside before reseasoning it!
Later tonight, if the rain is still falling, I fully intend to curl up with my grimoire and let its pages, and the rain, lull me to sleep.
Kaleida Barks___nodes[4]._stepChain[0]._: It’s really blustering today. It makes it hard to do readings… Not for any arcane reason, but because the wind is liable to carry my cards off if I don’t pin them down!
With the way the wind is blowing today, I have to give it my all to keep my hair out of my face!
Back home, we’d usually be flying so low that the wind never really had an effect on us. Though Fi-Star would get covered from snoot-to-tail in leaves.
On days like this, Jam would be working on new dance moves. She uses the wind as inspiration for making her movements more fluid. At least, that’s what she’s told me.
Kaleida Barks___nodes[5]._stepChain[0]._: Ooo, what delightfully stormy weather! I’ll be setting out jars later to see if I can catch lightning in a bottle!

You know, back on Fi-Star, when it started to storm like this, we’d put out lightning rods on certain parts of their body. Ocan said that the lightning strikes are strong enough to give Fi-Star a much-needed massage while traveling.
With the storm here, I can take a little time and hunt down some frogs!... Not for experiments, but because I like how they feel when I pick them up and pet them!
The passed usually don’t have much to say, but with storms like this, they become much more talkative!
Kaleida Barks___nodes[6]._stepChain[0]._: Whenever Fi-Star wants a quick nap, they’ll ascend to the High Climb, so I’m very used to snowy days!
I’ll be setting out snow collectors later! Freshly-fallen snow makes for good ritual ingredients, AND delicious snow cones!
With it snowing like this, I really feel like making some snow Brownies…. No that’s not a food, it’s like a snow Human, but Brownie-sized!
Ehehe! You can’t hear it {1} but when the snow hits the ground, the sound makes my ears tingle!
Kaleida Barks___nodes[7]._stepChain[0]._: This sun is intolerable.. I’d much rather be inside, curled up with one of my tomes.
By the fates! I wish I could find my wide-brimmed hat, I lost it during the move and it’s the only thing that protects me against the sun.
Shade is at a premium for me, oh how I wish my Cloudskipper could be at my side at all times of the day, to keep this horrendous sun at bay.
I should visit Orchid, her books on meteormancy would do wonders to put some clouds in this sky.
Kaleida Barks___nodes[9]._stepChain[0]._: The summer heat feels really good at this clime! The heat is much more dry, Fi-Star loves to travel through more humid areas, leaving most of the caravan a sweaty mess.
You would be surprised to know how BUSY someone like me is during the summer! All the festivals and celebrations going on coincide with SO many moon-phases and spiritual confluxes!
I’ve been helping Bella with her gardening. I know a few spells to encourage growth, but more than that I’ve gathered some cicada shells for her compost!
The Solstice is normally a time for performing larger harvest rituals. With Josiah and Beth here, I’ve elected to pick their brains about more esoteric Solstice rituals they’ve seen.
I’ve been press-ganged into assisting Camellia during Autumn. Not in the Monarch per se... but I have a well trained palate for libations, thanks to Liz. So, she’s asked me to taste which of her seasonal cocktails and brews to serve.
With the cooler weather, I’ve been considering putting up more heavy curtains to keep the cold out, but they’re just sooooo unattractive… Maybe I can live with the chill for a little longer.
I love to study the Hallowed Haunt whenever it comes around, but technically I’m only allowed to do that if I have a license for it… Not that anyone from the Kingdom has asked~
On Fi-Star, we’d start making a lot of our spiced drinks around this time of year. Liz is probably working overtime on her still, so she can keep up with the demand for her ‘nogs.
Every year I make plans for the solstice. I always make sure to grab the blood of Lavapple, and the juice of a virgin… Or was it the other way around…
Winter is the best season for communion with the dead. You may not think it, but the cold creates the perfect conductor for restless spirits to speak with the living!
I always get too many requests for fortunes for next year. So I have been working on a handy dispenser that will give someone their fortune for next year for the price of five Krone!

Being as cold as it is, normally I would be busy helping cover Fi-Star in quilts with everyone else. They occasionally go up to higher climates to cool down, but it’s tradition to pull out its winter clothes so it stays warm!
Joy King Day… It’s one of my most hated holidays. By the winds and fates I wish that Joy King would stop their insistence of using Slapstick comedy everywhere they go.….
I think it’s about time that I switched my curtains out, don’t you agree? Maybe next time the Caravan comes back, I’ll see about getting a frillier design.
I get many patrons asking for love fortunes around Matchmaker Day. Truthfully, I cannot see how love will develop, but my predictions help bring confidence to uncertain relationships!
-Yawn- Ooof, sorry {1}. I was doing some spring cleaning and uncovered my mirror. I spent HOURS working on my hair, can you tell~?
I’ll be taking my time looking through Valpongis’s wares today! When he appears for Market Day he always pulls out some of his rare stock just for me!
With the Market here, I COULD replace my current curtains. The colors I have currently are NICE, but maybe I should add a splash of color… Oh don’t mind me {1}! I’m just thinking aloud.
Market Day is giving me the chance to do a lot of the shopping I’ve been neglecting. I need mirror polish, some more of my favorite teas, and possibly to pick up a small gardening kit!
It’s so nice seeing everyone again. I’ve been catching up with Jam and Ocan about what’s recently happening on Fi-Star.
You should check in with Liz if you haven’t already. She makes the best starshine in the Kingdom. Not that she has much competition, but even if she did it’d be top shelf!
If you haven’t already experienced it yet, Jam’s dances really are the best. Sure, she can be a bit arrogant about her skills, but she’s really good, and people seem really into that.
Bireli has some really interesting, esoteric stuff. A lot of it is really interesting, but I’m not sure I always agree with the conclusions he draws about them…
Athletes are very superstitious by nature, and I’ve seen quite a few today because of the Combat Contest. A blessing here, a curse there, and suddenly all those trainers and monsters are raring to compete.
I’ve been on stand-by at the healer’s tent for the Combat Contest today. Even if I can’t do much, I can still remove some of the ailments the competitors received during the fight!
I plan on taking some time to watch the Combat Contest today. I’m normally not allowed to gamble, but Camellia has offered a very tempting drink-bet based off of who wins.
I can’t wait to see what the chefs cook up today! I’d love to see some try their hand at brownie recipes… Not the desert though, I mean- oh you know!
There are a LOT of delicious smells coming from today’s Cooking Contest! I can barely handle how much my stomach growls.
I heard that the cooking champion, Agatha, is releasing a book soon. I hear it’s an autobiography-cookbook combo!
We have a holiday much like Matchmaker Day on the Fi-Star, but it comes during the summers. Usually, caravan elders would be the ones to play matchmaker, and pair people off to strengthen relations between caravans.
Fates and winds, I hope that Yuuga doesn’t try to pair me with anyone. We brownies should have- diplomatic immunity for these kinds of things!
There is a substantial reward for all that complete Josiah’s trial. If you require my mystical aid during the trial’s climax, please don’t hesitate to call on me!
Beth and Josiah make a cute couple don’t you think? I think it’s kind of romantic for them to travel together across the world… A caravan of two.
The Haunt has come, and now my wards will pay off. I’ve sold almost everyone on protection for their houses from The Mirror. Hopefully no one will have forgotten to use them.
Sebastian is here for the Haunt. I’ve been able to pry a few stories from him with some bottles of Starshine! What he’s told me has really changed how I view The Mirror World.
I’ve foreseen that Brume Roberts will be up to some mischief this year, but I think you’ll be able to teach him a lesson on how not to be so naughty~
Clara will need help this year with the provisions. While I can’t see for certain, I imagine you would no doubt be helpful!
Ugh, that little nuisance is back in town, Jerrikan. He’d hitch a ride on the Caravan every so often and he was just as abrasive then as he is now. Needless to say, he doesn’t get any rides from us anymore.
Don’t buy into Jerrikan’s ‘oh I’m a poor spirit who is bound here and was someone’s servant’ act. He’s a very strong fiend-spirit, and would gladly wreak great destruction given the chance.
I LOVE when Valpongis is around! He always has some historical tomes on hand he’s willing to let me buy!
Valpongis is a good acquaintance of mine. He’s delightfully bombastic, and can upsell his merchandise better than most Brownies!
Digger has surfaced to sell today. I might take this chance to see if I can get ahold of some ores, my mirror is starting to look like it’ll need a new back-plating.
Digger’s people have done a lot of trading with mine. They have a lot of ore they need to offload, and our craftworkers always enjoy buying in bulk!
I smell a savory aroma coming from the town, a rotisseur has probably arrived! Everything they cook up makes my mouth water.
The rotisseurs’ cooking reminds me of home. We’d always eat a big meal after a successful bazaar, and Liz would even break out her good starshines and wines!
The Greenthumbs are always a welcome sight for gardeners! I’ve been going with Bella lately when they’re in town. So we might go later to pick out some seeds for her garden!
We have moss farms on the back of Fi-Star. Sometimes we’d buy seeds from Greenthumbs and use the moss beds to start them!
I spoke to Vodan this morning. She’s such a sweet woman, reminds me of Ocan. I can assure you that the eggs you get from her are high-quality, and well-loved.
Last time I spoke to Vodan, I asked her if I could have some monster egg shells for a ritual. I didn’t expect her to get so angry. I should apologize, since she’s in town today.
I don’t mind Skandr so much. He’s well-meaning, despite his dark sense of humor. It helps to know he doesn’t mean any harm!
I love when Skandr comes around! He has a good sense of humor, though I wish he wouldn’t tease Vodan quite so much when they share a stall.
I love when Skandr comes around! He has a good sense of humor, though I wish he wouldn’t tease Vodan quite so much when they share a stall.
I don’t mind Skandr so much. He’s well-meaning, despite his dark sense of humor. It helps to know he doesn’t mean any harm!
I try not to throw out anything that breaks. Instead, I wait till days like today, when Rekk is in town. Sometimes, I’ve even bought back some of what I’ve donated to him.

Rekk being in town reminds me of when he’d hitch a ride on Fi-Star. He always managed to fix up quite a few things to buy his passage, but he also had a habit of using things people left out to do it.
It’s a good thing that Cat creature was taken care of. At least now those miners will be safer, going out into that savannah archipelago.
I can’t imagine how those Union scouts must have felt, nor do I wish to foretell their ends, even if it could give us more information…
Fates folded…. Will Wizards EVER learn not to tamper with things they don’t understand? I don’t want to speak ill of the dead, but whatever that wizard was trying to revive in that laboratory probably should have stayed dead. -Sigh- and now they both are…
I attempted to glimpse the final moments of Tro, that wizard you found. There was… A lot of regret. I think he genuinely was trying to do good but… Well, you saw what his reward for it was.
Ending the Kelpie’s rampage sent many small ripples through fate. Had you not interceded, I foresaw many more deaths before the Union finally prevailed.

Strangely… The Kelpie you encountered seems similar to the cat creature from before. Something was done to them to change their nature. For what purpose? I can not say, but I don’t think the poachers are behind it…
With Sascha defeated, perhaps now her fate will be changed. It is difficult to see how you may have changed it, but I think she will grow as a person now, and change for the better.
I think Gehman’s defeat will give him the time he needs to come to terms with his gifts. Perhaps now he will put them to better use than- whatever it was he was making before.
I wish we’d been able to capture Rook. Intentions or not, he is turning his ‘pets’ to a life of crime with his antics.
Once I learned of Rook entering the farm, I took the time to foretell of any other visitors, so that no one could break and enter again… If only such alarms worked with Fio…
My rancor for Boethilde is focused solely on Vaskin. It takes a special sort of scum to bind a Mirror World entity like that.
Boethilde seemed much more… sad than she did ambivalent. Like she had been going through the motions with no care for the outcome.
Fates, I feel like Vaskin wasn’t punished enough for what he’s done. He callously used people in order to attain… what, Power? Influence? Just… Augh! Sorry, I do not want to speak of him right now.

I keep turning things over in my head and wonder… just how did Vaskin start his enterprise? The sort of money and pull needed for that isn’t something you just come across… Hmm.. Maybe I should consult my crystal ball…
I did a reading for a man once, I have never seen such a future, save for yours. They were to be successful beyond comprehension, a singular genius… But then they would spurn their lover. In her rage, she cursed them to forever watch over the fields and successes of others. Only to be released when someone could match them.

I once told the fortune of a man… The cards showed me that he would become obsessed with selling junk and trying to pass it off as treasure. His fortunes would be poured into accruing more junk, until it would become all he talked about.

I read the fortune of a noble whose daughter was sickly and dying.. seeking a solution. I told him I could only glimpse that his obsession would be her ruin. But, I could not see how, or if he would stop before too late. When he left, I could see resolve burning in his heart. All I can hope is that he failed, and his daughter is at peace.

I have read many fortunes, and seen many pasts. The strangest have come from a further distance than I could ever imagine - like leaves carried across a vast ocean to a new land. Some even came from great cataclysms and ruined worlds. How they came to be here is something I still don’t know, and few ever spoke to me after their reading.
Life outside of Fi-Star has been different. Sure, when I’m in my caravan, it’s like I never left home, but I step out and my feet are on solid ground

It’s been taking some time to get used to Cloverton. I’ve spent days learning everyone’s names, and a few things about them. Perhaps, I could have just done a reading for those things, but I thought it best to get to know them more socially.

I’ve not had a lot of difficulty adjusting to life since I started staying here! The biggest difficulty I’ve had is waiting for merchants to come in to get the specialist stuff I need. Back on Fi-Star, I could trade or buy anything I needed on any afternoon. It seems difficult to not live in a mobile city.

It’s taken some effort to sleep soundly at night. I’m still not used to waking up to… Well, nothing. Back home, I’d wake up to the deep rumbling of Fi-Star, as if they were waking me up with a ‘good morning’.
I didn’t have much experience with farming back on Fi-Star. Most of our agrarian efforts went into cultivating mosses and herbs.
I’ve been impressed with your farming setup so far {1}! Sure, I don’t know much about it, or how to manage crops, but the results speak for themselves!
It must be difficult to keep up with so many different monster species and to keep them all in-line. I’d love to pick your brain sometime on how you manage all this~
Hey {1}! If you need any help running things, you can always rely on me. Otherwise I’ll just take in the lovely view~
KatarinaUpgradedIntro___nodes[0]._stepCh: Catarella approaches you, smiling brightly as you take in the scenery.
Well, what do you think? Pretty as a plemon, isn’t she?
KatarinaUpgradedIntro___nodes[1]._option: I think I got off at the wrong station.
I think I should repair dilapidated stations more often if I get to see that smile (Flirt)
KatarinaUpgradedIntro___nodes[2]._stepCh: I think I got off at the wrong station. There’s no way this is Katarina!
What an amusing jest. It has not lost its impact after hearing captains tell it to me all day~
KatarinaUpgradedIntro___nodes[3]._stepCh: I think I should repair dilapidated stations more often if I get to see that smile.
Really? If that is the case, then I can submit plans to repair other stations along The Frontier.
I dare say, I will be a Regional Station Manager in nine to twelve months~
P-please don’t. This was already a monumental task…
Catarella holds back a warm and delicate laugh.
KatarinaUpgradedIntro___nodes[4]._stepCh: Much of the repairs are complete. We’re able to offer better services to merchant and Union vessels, and we’ve reduced the number of night shifts. Though, Quartermaster Keely insists we should continue doing evening inventory meetings.
Also, I’m certain by now that our new suppliers are settled in to working here. Bang and… Gerlicht was it?
My mistake. Goodness sake, for once I don’t need to be on top of everything. What a relief.
I’m glad it’s smooth sailing now!
Speaking of: Installation of the new engines finished up just this morning. Our operational range has expanded enough to make regular runs to the nearby forest.
You should check in with Joaqim. I’m certain he is as eager to set sail, as you are to explore another archipelago.
You can say that. I definitely want to get my boots on the ground as soon as possible.
He’ll be at the new helm, through the lodge. I’ll leave you to it. Feel free to check in anytime, I’ll have a little more free time now~
HelpfulPreBossMan___nodes[0]._stepChain[: Agh! Oh it’s you. I hope I was able to help you out there like you helped me. If not, well… oh well.
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[2]._stepC: You approach the older, rotund man. His vocation is unmistakable, given his spatula prosthetic and large, stained apron which still seems too small around his ample ale belly.
He silently grumbles to himself, clearly vexed about something. You’re pretty sure this is the mess sergeant Joaqim was talking about, given his equipment.
Mngh… Damn Cats- They… how am I supposed to cook with-
Yeah, this is definitely the mess sergeant.
Uh, excuse me. You’re the mess sergeant, right? I was hoping I could-
The man turns around, a frown on his face.
Sorry, but I’m a bit in the middle of a cookware cat’tastrophe, as it were. You’ll need to settle for some rations from the general store… if they’re even open.
Actually, I think I’m here about that. The name’s {0}, I’m the frontiersman from Cloverton! Joaqim mentioned that the mess sergeant was having a bit of trouble.
At the mention of your title, the man’s expression softens a bit.
Well, good to meet you, {0}. But, a bit of trouble is putting it lightly. It feels like fate itself has been putting a thumb on the scale, against me.
But if Joaqim sent you, then perhaps that thumb might finally be easing up.
The name’s Basile. Basile Heartwode. Basile will do just fine though, and no need for titles.
Nice to meet you, Basile. So tell me about these catfolk that… uh- purr’loined your cookware?
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[4]._stepC: If I hear another one like that out of you, I’m going back home.
More like they……… Nope, I thought I had something but nope.
He can joke if he likes, but I don’t think *we* should be making jokes about his predicament.
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[5]._stepC: … Yeah, so a few days ago I was busy, seasoning some of the cookware. It was real nice pieces too, just a bit rusty.

It was a lot like finding a barnacle-encrusted clam, and opening it up to find a couple pearls. So I took to cleaning, washing, seasoning, and all the works. Then I left them outside to cool while we got some baking done.
Well, soon as we were done with the meager lunch, that I could manage with just a cauldron and a small pan, I come out and see that all of my cookware was stolen!
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[7]._optio: ... How do you know it was catfolk?
What will you do without the pots and pans?
Would these be the pans you’re looking for?
So I need to find a catfolk with pots and pans! (Continue)
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[8]._stepC: So, if it was during the lunch rush? How do you know that some catfolk took them?
Well, they left a bunch of paw prints all over the rack I left them out on.
And I don’t see much reason for anyone here to go stealing them, not unless they don’t like to eat, and only a *fool* would miss out on three square meals.
So far, everyone’s seemed a bit put off by the galley’s lacking menu, so I’m going to assume some cat burglar took them.
Basile tips his hat back with his spatula, very assured of his position.
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[9]._stepC: What will you do without the pots and pans? In case I can’t find the one who took them?
I’ll do what I can, but it’s a lot like trying to paint a fence with a hairbrush, you know?
All I have right now is a small cauldron and some rusted through skillets. I won’t have any of the personnel on this station go hungry, but we don’t have the time to prepare meals for anyone else.
Sounds like a lot of work just to feed the staff.
Yep, everyone else is going to have to scrounge for rations and salt turnog. At least till I can get my cookware back and my galley on track.
Them’s the orders from Mistress Catarella, and I can’t say I disagree with them.
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[10]._step: You reach into your rucksack, pulling out the set of cookware.
Would these be the pans you’re looking for?
Woah there, you’re as quick as a hummingbird harpy, that or I’m just lucky.
This is just like having a kind stranger walk up and return your money purse you didn’t know you dropped… I hope it wasn’t much trouble?
Well, it was a bit of a hassle, but I managed to get them back from the hairball that took them.
Basile shakes his head, but he seems far too happy about getting his cookware back.
I thought it was one of them.
Hmm, as long as they don’t try and sneak off with them again, then I guess everything’s square.
Hopefully not…? I *think* she went off to do something else, so hopefully she won’t be troubling you again.
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[12]._step: I bet that’s the last we’ve seen of her!
I think you convinced her! You do have a way with words, afterall.
I’m certain we’ve convinced her to renounce her pilfering ways… Or she found a new muse for her mischievousness…
Totally! Unrelated, but have you ever wanted to buy a bridge?
The manner of one like Bang is beyond my ken…
I admire your optimism, {0}. Like a plankton in a coal mine…
You saw the glint in her eyes, right?
At the very least, we can probably find her when she runs off with them again…
You might as well order a backup pair when you can…
Yeah, nah, I don’t like those odds.
Aye, probably. Now, how's about that scran?
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[13]._step: I suppose I’ll keep an eye on these then, just in case.

For now, I’ll get these back in the galley so that we can start cooking up proper meals for the station. Course, they’ll need to be reseasoned, but these are sturdy ol’ girls, they’ll be cooking up big platefulls of food!
That means we should be able to cook en surplus, and I can start offering food to everyone docking or visiting… For a reasonable cost, of course.
Glad to hear it, I’m sure Catarella and Joaqim will be happy to hear it.
Well they better, and if you’re going into the savannah again, you be sure to stock up on some meals. Got it?
With that, Basile hands the pans off to one of his staff.
Now where to begin… Perhaps a Bofroot Pilaf…
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[14]._step: So, I need to find a catfolk with pots and pans! I think I can do that while I take a look around.
I’d be properly grateful if you did. Course, you might be looking for a needle in a grassleaf stack, you know?
If you do manage to wrestle them away from whatever barnacle brained cat that took them, I’ll finally be able to get the galley back up to speed.
Then I can feed any crew coming in on ships, and  you can stock up on some meals before you head out into that cat and centaur dungeon. It’s a win win.
Sounds good to me! I’ll find your pots and pans, Basile.
Basile gives you a nod, before getting lost in thought. Likely, about how to make a meal plan with what he has.
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[15]._step: Basile gives you a nod as you approach, tipping his hat with his spatula.
Good to see you again, {0}. I don’t suppose you might have found my cookware out there somewhere?
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[17]._step: You reach into your rucksack, pulling out the set of cookware.
Are these the pans you were looking for?
Well I’ll be…! You actually managed to find them.
Yep, it was a bit of a hassle, but I managed to get them back from the hairball that took them.
Basile shakes his head, but he seems far too happy about getting his cookware back.
As long as they don’t try and sneak off with them again, then I guess everything’s square.
I… can’t promise that! But, I’m *fairly* sure she won’t run off with them again. She seems to have- other interests for now.
MarinerBasileBangQuest___nodes[18]._step: Sorry, afraid I haven’t found them just yet.
Well, that’s a shame, those were some damn good pieces.
I’ll keep looking though! Don’t worry, they have to be somewhere out there.
Like I said, the sooner we get them back, the sooner we can start cooking up a storm, and providing food to more than just the staff.

GelrichtStatues___nodes[2]._stepChain[0]: A fearsome statue of a ruthless Homunculus, which had cruelly infested a Catfolk warrior.
You shiver as you recall the creature’s cunning ambush in the mines, and the chaotic battle above…
GelrichtStatues___nodes[3]._stepChain[0]: An intimidating statue of a powerful Homunculus, which had taken over the body of a Stonebreaker.
The formidable strength of the Homunculus is so well-rendered in stone, it makes you flinch to recall its thunderous strikes!
GelrichtStatues___nodes[4]._stepChain[0]: A curious statue of Sting, ex-poacher and your formidable former foe.
When you look closely at this statue, you realize just how tensely Sting carries herself. Is she less confident than she lets on?
GelrichtStatues___nodes[5]._stepChain[0]: A terrifying statue of the leering leech of luxury himself, Vaskin Robbins.
Vaskin’s grinning mask makes you question if his web has truly been undone, or if the worst is yet to come…
GelrichtStatues___nodes[6]._stepChain[0]: An impressive statue depicting a proud Unicorn warrior with a long and lonely gaze…
GelrichtStatues___nodes[6]._stepChain[2]: Unicorns are a very rare breed of Centaur, having a horn like a Stonebreaker, but no hind legs like the rest of our kind.
As a result, they are often admired for their uniqueness, but looked upon with suspicion for their differences.
GelrichtStatues___nodes[6]._stepChain[4]: Such is often the case, with all outcasts…!
GelrichtStatues___nodes[7]._stepChain[0]: A statue of a particularly beautiful Centaur Mason. He looks to the horizon with an alert gaze, as though hearing a distant call…
GelrichtStatues___nodes[7]._stepChain[2]: This is the legendary stonemason known only as Oz. The story goes that one day, Oz heard a strange voice that no one else could hear.
Believing himself to be hearing the call of divine inspiration, Oz obeyed the voice and abandoned his tribe, never to be seen again.
GelrichtStatues___nodes[7]._stepChain[4]: It’s said you can still hear his lonely cries emanating from some forsaken vale, deep within the Savannah…!
GelrichtStatues___nodes[8]._stepChain[0]: A somber bust of a venerable Centaur has been lovingly rendered in stone, with much respect to detail.
GelrichtStatues___nodes[8]._stepChain[2]: This is the great Stonebreaker known as Kaga. He is a Keeper—a sort of historian among my people. A thankless task, it goes without saying.
Perhaps you’ve met? His home lies somewhere in the nearby Savannah.
GelrichtStatues___nodes[9]._stepChain[3]: You didn’t think you could get away without the honor of one last statue, could you?
GelrichtStatues___nodes[9]._stepChain[5]: I wanted to ensure your heroic poise was memorialized for generations to come!
GelrichtStatues___nodes[9]._stepChain[7]: (I’m not sure if this “heroic poise” is entirely accurate, but I ain’t exactly complaining!)

JoaqimCatarellaIntroToForest2___nodes[1]: Well well, here’s {0}. Right on t-
Ahhh, {0|Elle|El|La} Gran {0|Jefesite|Jefesito|Jefesita}! Welcome, welcome!
We were just discussing the latest reports, when it became apparent that sharing my intelligence would greatly benefit your investigation.
As if there was enough of your intelligence to share with anyone, Captain.
… Well, perhaps there would be more to share if your contribution consisted of more than a packing slip.
It’s nice to see you two are getting on, but can we get to the sitrep?
Of course, sugar. Rest assured that despite appearances to the contrary, our information networks are as reliable as ever…
Indeed, {0|Jefesite|Jefesito|Jefesita}. We have recently received reports from our scouts, who have returned from their foray into the Forest depths.
There is a Holstaur village present in this region, which was known to some of our more adventurous merchant captains.
However, they seem to have adopted a more defensive posture as of late.
I suspect the reason will come as a surprise to no one…
Pirates, I assume!
But of course, my dear Frontiersman. Interestingly, these Pirates don’t appear to have any entrenched base of operations in the area, unlike in the Savannah.
In fact, one of the only other settlements in the region appears to be some sort of <color=blue>ice creamery.</color> This creamery has appeared in the dockets of some merchant contacts of mine.

Curiously, the distributor - one ‘V. Robbins’ - rarely applies for repeat business. An abnormality when you are in the business of consistent product quality. It could be a sign of having something to hide.
Or simply a sign of a business struggling in a remote location, perhaps?
One day you will regret having ever doubted the power of a thorough spreadsheet, Captain…
For now, the impetus rests on {0} to conduct their investigation in the capacity of a Frontiersman.
Indeed. In order to keep a low profile, we’ve jointly decided to resist mounting a full expedition.
Of course, our passage has also been impeded by a number of <color=red>unusually stout gates</color>... We’ll have to figure out a way to pass them by.
IntroConvo

And currently I only vouch for three parolees, okay? Three!
Anyway, the letter was obviously from <color=red>Rook.</color> He told me he wants to “pay me back,” and that he’d help us with those gates blocking our passage.
Rook! That dastardly criminal is going to help us?
You’ve proven yourself able, {0}, but must that awful man become your fourth parolee in Cloverton?

Cat! I have some good news!
Good news… Wait, Cat?
Cat. You know, short for Catarella!
… I see… Now, you say you have good news?
I believe that I found the last member of the caravan.
You would be right. The last one came through just a short while ago.
I suppose that means congratulations are in order.
Your actions have brought back several personnel. An action such as that can not go unrewarded.
Oh! Very much appreciated!
Now then, if there is no more business, I simply must get back to work. This entire debacle has left my time tables cattywampus. However, it would have been a lot worse without your help.
I’ll let you get back to it Cat, see ya around!
Take care, sugar.

{0}, a pleasure to see you again. How are you faring in the Savannah?
I’ve been doing alright, stopping by to give you a progress report. I have been looking for the people who were with that caravan.
But I haven’t found all of them yet.
Hmm… I suppose I will not fault you for trying. Be sure to stay safe out there.
It would be even more exhausting for me if I needed to arrange for *another* search party.
Thanks for the concern Catarella, I’ll try my best!
That is all I ask.

Catarella is scrutinizing her clipboard, tapping a pencil against the wood.
Station Master… Mistress?... Catarella, you look… Consternated.
Catarella quirks an eyebrow, slightly lowering her clipboard.
Do you even know what that word means {0}?
It, uhm… Means…. Anxious?
…Huh, you *are* full of surprises {0}. Plenty of rank-and-file use big words to sound smart.
But ask them what it means and they get *very* embarrassed.
Do- do you ask everyone that when they use polysyllabic words?
A bit of a test for my sanity, and a little teasing if they’re pleasant, like you.
Well the question stands, you looked fairly concerned.
Concerned, tired, pick one really…
I have just received a report that demonstrates perfectly why one should wait for a marine escort.
Some fool with more Korona than sense set out with several station porters to assess his mine.
Nevermind that the Union presence is sparse, or that he hired absolutely no mercenaries. Apparently, the mine can’t wait.
Sounds like you’re about to say everything went Jelbabu shaped.
Considering they haven’t reported back when they should have? It’s gone all Cattywampus!
Catarella lets out a sigh.
In response, I have not been able to untangle the dense web of duty rosters to assemble the marines needed to go on a rescue mission.
I do declare, I would not care an iota if only Mr. Medvedov was out there. But, all the workers he hired…
Yeah, I’d feel pretty bad if something happened.
Katarina Station can ill afford these setbacks, sugar. Let alone the reputation that would come from our workers being used as scratching posts…
This is just another headache for me to deal with, nothing you should worry yourself about.
Well, what if I *want* to worry myself with it? I’m already going there to look for poachers myself!
Catarella seems relieved to hear it, her shoulders looking a little less tense.
Well, if you’re fixin’ to head out there, and think you can manage it without backup… You would not only be helping me, but all of those workers.
Then I’ll keep an eye out for them Catarella, you can count on me!
Then thank you kindly.
There are twelve people total, not including that puddin’ brained caravan leader. Direct any of them back here, and I will personally see that you are rewarded. Sound good?
Deal!
A deal’s a deal then. I hope that I’ll hear back from you soon, {0}.

JoaqimBarksAndHub___nodes[2]._stepChain[: Clear skies, and a warm sun! If you see our dear Stationmaster, {0}, make sure to give her my condolences~
It would bring me great pleasure to lead a patrol, on such a beautiful day… I admit that I’m a bit envious of your freedom, Frontiersman.
Ahhh, such a crisp, clear day! Reminds me of many a good journey, and many a bloody battle, too…
I wonder where will you go on such a beautiful day, {0|Jefesite|Jefesito|Jefesita}? Spare a thought for a grounded sailor, wherever you may travel.
JoaqimBarksAndHub___nodes[3]._stepChain[: In my homeland, the wisepeople say that rain is like a passionate lover: For every flourishing field, a home is swept away in a torrent of misery…
Tsk! Visibility is poor today… Although we rarely have to move, there’s ships out there counting on us for guidance.
You’d think a rainy day is always bad for una gatita, but La Sombra is no doubt enjoying a stroll right about now…
A rainy day keeps the Catfolk away. Unfortunately, it doesn’t make our work any easier, either.
JoaqimBarksAndHub___nodes[4]._stepChain[: There’s a stiff breeze, today! Good weather for a swift journey… If only I wasn’t chained to this rock.
The smell of grass on the wind almost reminds me of home… If it weren’t for the reek of stagnant water. And the pollen. And the Catfolk hair.
Pirate aircraft are designed to be as swift as possible. They’ll be running circles around us, in this weather…
JoaqimBarksAndHub___nodes[5]._stepChain[: They say Misfortune has her finger on the Scales on a stormy day. You would be wise to take care in your travels, {0|Jefesite|Jefesito|Jefesita}!
Such evil weather, and still so much to do…
Have you seen our dear Stationmaster? I believe La Sombra is being spoiled by this weather.
Does your Frontiersman’s guidebook really advise you to work in a storm, {0|Jefesite|Jefesito|Jefesita}?
JoaqimBarksAndHub___nodes[6]._stepChain[: This snow will discourage many a shipmaster from leaving port. Hopefully, our pirate “friends” will stay in their holes...
Inclement weather keeps all manner of pests away. Although it’s cold, we will take this opportunity to shore up our defenses.
Excuse me if my color is off, Frontiersman… I never quite got used to the snow. My homeland is quite warm and dry.
This snow would be calming, if there wasn’t so much work to do. Perhaps you could make yourself useful today, {0|Jefesite|Jefesito|Jefesita}?
JoaqimBarksAndHub___nodes[7]._stepChain[: The best way to beat the heat? Find yourself a sturdy chair on an open porch, and an ice-cold bottle of bitterbrew!
Taking it easy today, {0|Jefesite|Jefesito|Jefesita}? No doubt your elite training advises you not to work too hard when it’s warm.
I’m sure you’re not used to this heat, Frontiersman. Why not find some shade and relax, leave all the work to us? It’s only a matter of survival, after all!
Ho-ho~, you look a little burnt, {0|Jefesite|Jefesito|Jefesita}! Here, take a swig and cool off. It’ll numb the pain!
JoaqimBarksAndHub___nodes[9]._stepChain[: La Sombra detests the sun and heat, but I find it very relaxing! Alas, our situation compels us both to work irregardless of our feelings…
Ah, how I miss strolling down the promenade of my home city in the summer sunshine, a bottle of wine in one hand, my arm around a stalwart companion!~
I miss my days serving on a ship. Sweating under the summer sky as you commit mind, body and soul to your station… I was really alive, then!
Don’t hesitate to take a break, {0|Jefesite|Jefesito|Jefesita}. I’m sure you’re used to taking a nice, long break during the Summer recess…
JoaqimBarksAndHub___nodes[10]._stepChain: We’ll no doubt see a bit more energy from the Stationmaster, now that fall has arrived. The gloomier the season, the happier the girl.
In my homeland, Fall brings sweet relief from the long, dry heat of summer. I’ll always miss those warm, sunny days, however.
If there’s one thing I don’t miss about sailing, it’s hurricane season… But even this behemoth could take serious damage, if we’re caught unprepared!
Looking for some leaves to jump in, {0|Jefesite|Jefesito|Jefesita}? Just try not to get in anyone’s way.
JoaqimBarksAndHub___nodes[11]._stepChain: If you’re wondering if the Stationmaster ever smiles, Winter is prime season to catch her in the act. Especially if she’s got a cup of hot tea in her claws…
Ugh! I’ll never get used to these Frontier winters. The windchill penetrates right to the bone…

Another Provision’s Day, another big feast. We’re still cooking up food for people getting their 4th and 5th servings!
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[21]._st: Careful out there, {0}. You would do well to stay inside instead of going out and about. Better to relax and eat something sweet.
The Hallowed Haunt always unnerves me. Doesn’t feel right to have all these unliving about, it just isn’t natural. Something sweet ought to settle the nerves.
This holiday’s got the scouts as jumpy as a catboy in a room full of rocking chairs. Me too, for that matter.
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[33]._st: Serious business down there in the lower parts of the isles. I heard you went up against some kind of infected cat? Looks like you got back in one piece, more or less.
Now that the nearby islands are safer, I’ve had to scold a few marines coming back with armfulls of speedwheels. Filling up on empty carbs is no way to run a squad…
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[34]._st: {0}. Glad you’re back in one piece. I was worried when you didn’t come back through Katarina Station the other day.
I heard rumors about some strange door out in the savannah. Some of the marines who’ve been on patrol say it’s buried now. Shame to let a question go unanswered.
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[35]._st: A lot of the marines are singing your praises after you clobbered that kelpie. I even opened a few extra casks the other night, to celebrate.
Now that we’ve repelled another wave of homunculi down on the islands, hopefully we’ve cleared out most of the beasts.
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[36]._st: Well shoot, guess I should apologize to that little hairball. After hearing about that poacher camp you handled, I can see why all those catfolk were so riled up.
Looks like you did good, {0}. Those poachers seemed like the lynchpin to all of this savannah’s problems. Now we can get back to business as usual.
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[37]._st: {0}. Heard you tangled with some kind of underhanded seeker. Glad to see you’re in one piece.
Sure glad the forest is a bit safer. It makes getting three square meals out to the marines much easier. They’ll be singing your praises now that they’re not masticating bofroot jerky, {0}.
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[38]._st: {0}! Glad to hear that raid you undertook went well. The marines mentioned you put a stop to a serious operation.
Good on you, {0}. Sounds like that factory was more packed with monsterkin than a baker’s dozen of speedwheels in a half dozen box.
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[39]._st: Who would have thought Vaskin Robbins was the mastermind behind all of the problems out here. And to think I thought his overly sweet desserts were the extent of his villainy.
Looks like you finally put a stop to all these problems we’ve been having. I’ve had to put in an order for extra casks of beer and bottles of spirits for the occasion!
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[42]._st: {0}, ahoy! I trust you’re behaving yourself out there? We’ve all got a job to do, after all. You make the place safer, and I make sure everyone’s nourished and ready to go!
I haven’t got the patience for monotaskers in my kitchen. Gempas juicers? Automatic mado shuckers? Bah! Some people will fill their kitchen up to the roof with useless gadgets!
Ahh! Nothing like a strong brew of tea to keep you going on a busy day. Doctor says I should drink two cups a day, but one more couldn’t hurt.
-Yawn!- I stayed up late last night marinating turnogs for dinner service.
Mistress Catarella will be wanting her meal soon. She doesn’t ask for much for her meals, but she has rather refined tastes.
I’ll need to tell my assistants to begin preparing Captain Joaqim’s meal. Nothing too complicated, but he has tastes that lean towards the luxurious.
Mngh… marines keep asking for milkshakes and gempas jelly… That’s just not nutritional… maybe make- Oh, {0}! Sorry, been planning next week’s menu…
{0}! I trust you’re eating well. You need to set a good example for the marines that witness your successes.
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[43]._st: Enough about that though, you’re probably hungry, or going to be soon, right?
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[44]._ra: What can Basile cook up today?
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[44]._op: What have you cooked up today? (Shop)
Basile, can I ask you something?
I think I’ve got everything for now! (Leave)
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[45]._st: So, what have you cooked up today?
Take a look, we’ve been cooking up a storm!
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[46]._st: Basile, can I ask you something?
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[47]._ra: What would you like to ask Basile?
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[47]._op: What made you want to be a mess sergeant?
I want to talk about something else (Back)
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[48]._st: What made you want to be a mess sergeant?
The position was offered to me, and I accepted.
That was a bit of a simpler answer than you expected.
I had already done a bit of management before, when I served as a galley cook on The Tranquille. My superior recommended me when the opening came up for Katarina Station.
The thought of getting to work with fresh ingredients from The Frontier sounded like a good proposition! Especially if I was using them to keep an entire station fed.
So far, it’s been one of my favorite postings. And, as long as Mistress Catarella and Captain Joaqim ensure we’re well supplied, it’ll stay that way.
You nod. It’s good to know that Katarina Station’s food supply is in good hands.
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[49]._st: I don’t eat many full meals these days. I eat plenty here and there while taste testing and doing food prep.
But if you had to pick?
a nice, simple, bofroot stew. Just like my pa used to make.
It sure was. It sure was.
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[50]._st: I think I want to talk about something else.
MarinerBasileBarksAndHub___nodes[51]._st: I think I’ve got everything for now!
Safe travels, {0}. I’ll be here should you need a good meal for the road.
