BangBarksNHub___nodes[49]._rawContextHea: How do you play with Bang?
You hold out your hand to let Bang smell you. She leans forward delicately and takes in all the latest smells on your skin with interest. Satisfied, she rubs her face on your hand, signaling for you to begin scratching.
Your dextrous human fingers rub ably around her cheeks, chin and forehead. You scratch the sensitive spots in and around her ears, making her twist her head with pleasure.
To top it off, Bang flops onto her back so you can give her some bellyrubs. You can feel her deep purrs resonating through your hand like a motor!
BangBarksNHub___nodes[50]._stepChain[10]: Without warning, Bang suddenly twists around and gives you a playful bite.
BangBarksNHub___nodes[50]._stepChain[14]: Okay, no more! Keep up the good work, nekky, hehe~
BangBarksNHub___nodes[50]._stepChain[16]: … The pleasure is all mine, I assure you.
You pull a loose thread off your shirt, which quickly gets Bang’s attention.
You dangle the thread over her head and start flicking it around. Her enormous eyes follow the thread with the unsettling accuracy of a natural predator.
Bang leaps for the thread with a grunt and flops clumsily onto the ground. She continues to twist and roll at your feet, batting and swiping incompetently at the string dangling in front of her face.
After a minute or so, Bang lays panting on the ground with her round belly heaving up and down, seemingly defeated…
BangBarksNHub___nodes[53]._stepChain[10]: … But then she suddenly gets to her feet, looking for all the world as though she didn’t spend the last minute embarrassing herself.
BangBarksNHub___nodes[53]._stepChain[13]: You really showed that string who’s boss, Bang.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_3: Only moments after entering Katarina, two marines approach you with urgency.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_10: Marine := Frontiersman <PLAYERNAME>! We’ve been waiting for you to arrive.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_13: Marine := Captain Joaqim wished to speak with you! Would you like us to inform him of your arrival?
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_15: menu: Do you want to speak to Joaqim?
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_18: "Any chance we can have a raincheck for that?":
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_27: <PLAYERNAME> := Any chance we can have a raincheck for that?
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_28: Marine := Well, it’s not exactly like he can order you. But, he did tell us we can’t leave our post till he’s had a chance to speak to you…
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_30: <PLAYERNAME> := He knows my only weakness, inconveniencing others…
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_31: <PLAYERNAME> := Alright, let him know I’m here.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_32: Marine := Thank you, {sir/miss/pal}. I owe you one.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_42: Joaqim := Well well, {Jefesito/Jefesita/Jefesite}. It’s good to see you’re in one piece.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_44: <PLAYERNAME> := Thanks, I ended up in quite a scrap while I was out there. I ran into an individual whom I’m confident will give us a lead in our investigations.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_45: Joaqim := Not to worry, I’ve already sent a squad in the direction of that commotion.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_48: A smile slowly crosses Joaqim’s face, as he gestures to you.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_49: Joaqim := When our prestigious Frontiersman marches into the Frontier, with no clear reason why, it pays to send a few scouts to check in. If not for my curiosity, then at least your safety.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_51: Joaqim := And when said Frontiersman seems to march directly into the territory of an alleged sniper… Well, that curiosity begins to become questions. Ones which Commander Sieger may inquire about in a report.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_58: menu: How do you explain this?
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_59: "I guess I’m just lucky, or unlucky?":
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_61: "I heard there was a sniper and went after them?":
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_68: <PLAYERNAME> := I guess I’m just lucky, or unlucky?
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_69: Joaqim := Hmm, perhaps. Strange occurrences do seem to follow you like plankton to an eggtrap…
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_71: Joaqim := However, one generally doesn’t waltz through No Man’s Boughs while a sniper is active. Even one as 'lucky' as you is bound to have a religious experience.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_73: Joaqim puts a finger to the middle of his forehead.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_74: Joaqim := As they say in the navy… You’ll get hole-y.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_81: <PLAYERNAME> := I heard there was a sniper, and came to the forest to go after them?
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_83: Joaqim := Noble of you, but no report has reached Cloverton yet about a sniper. I should know, I haven't sent any yet…
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_91: <PLAYERNAME> := Well, the thing is… I might have known he was there.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_93: Joaqim := You knew of their existence, and didn’t think to warn anyone?!
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_96: Joaqim := Aha! So, the perfect Frontiersman has a reckless, ulterior motive? Perhaps I’m rubbing off on you, {Jefesito/Jefesita/Jefesite}...
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_97: <PLAYERNAME> := Rook, the sniper, left me a letter by my bedside, threatening to hurt people if I didn’t accept his invitation to a duel. I was worried what he’d do if I didn’t accept.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_99: Joaqim := … I can see why those details didn’t come up, in casual conversation…
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_100: <PLAYERNAME> := And, if I won, I could keep everyone safe, and expedite our investigations into Robbins in one move… But, Rook got away…
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_102: Joaqim := So, you’re back to square one?
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_104: <PLAYERNAME> := Not quite, but I don’t think he’ll try that again, and now I’m sure that I’m on the right track, even without new evidence.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_105: Joaqim seems to think this over for a moment, before shrugging.
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_107: Joaqim := Perhaps not a total loss, then! You’ve preserved your honor, and I commend your efforts to protect *your Captain.*
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_108: Joaqim := ... And everyone else, of course!
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_110: Joaqim := Now, I believe I’ll go and share a stiff drink with our scouts. I’m sure once their report crosses my desk it will not invite any unnecessary questions. Hasta la vista, {Jefesito/Jefesita/Jefesite}!
Act2Part1_Post-Rook Denouement__line_112: <PLAYERNAME> := (... I’m just going to leave out that the Captain wasn’t mentioned in Rook’s letter…)
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[2]._stepCh: Another beautiful clear day, eh {0}? Hm-hm, I’ll make a note in the logbook…
I hope those blinds I ordered will arrive soon. The sun is creating sub-optimal lighting conditions in the armory!
Clear skies mean one of two things: Easy passage for supply trains, or easy pickings for pirates. We’ll find out which holds true by the end of the week…
-sigh!- I’m sure the Stationmaster is suffering in this blinding sunlight… Can’t forget to order that new parasol she requested…
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[3]._stepCh: Let me get your opinion, {0}: How would you describe the rate of precipitation? Light? Moderate? Heavy? Hm, I’ll make a note in the log…
Hmph… I hope the rainwater doesn’t seep into our stores again. Rainfall on the frontier can be shockingly torrential!
Hm, this rain won’t be good for our supply ships… We’ll have to prepare for another week of late shipments.
Stationmaster Catarella always looks much calmer when it rains, but I just can’t stand getting wet! My notes might get ruined…
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[4]._stepCh: Hm, that’s a strong breeze alright… I’ll have to check my instruments soon and take note of any changes in local pressure systems.
I *could* open the windows and let the breeze air the place out, but I personally prefer that “stuffy armory” smell.
Feel that breeze? If a pressure system is creating a high-altitude jetstream, we might see our supplies come early for once.
I wonder what the Stationmaster is up to on such a breezy day… No doubt longing for the rain to fall… -sigh-!~
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[5]._stepCh: My instruments are giving some impressive readouts on this storm! According to my logs, we’re already set to break some records this year.
There were barely enough sandbags to protect the Armory from this storm… Luckily, the Station has a robust flood-control system!
Hmph, I’m sure our supply flotillas will be delayed by this weather. Better they hunker down somewhere safe than risk a total loss.
Oh!~ I just spotted the Stationmaster watching the storm from her office window. D-do you think she noticed me?~
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[6]._stepCh: Give me your professional opinion, {0}: How would you describe the crystal structure morphology of this snowflake?
I’ve been so focused on my work that I didn’t notice the temperature drop! Hm, I think I’ve got a sweater buried somewhere around here…
I hope this snowfall doesn’t get worse… If we miss another supply run, that’s one too many this past month.
D-do you think the Stationmaster likes snow? I mean, technically it’s not rain, but it has a certain “coziness,” don’t you think?
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[7]._stepCh: Hm, this heat is tremendous… Based on my logs, these heat waves only seem to be getting worse.
I keep telling the Captain to be careful with our essence usage during these heat waves, or we’ll lose refrigeration!
This heat will slow everything down… Hopefully our supply runners have enough water to keep themselves going.
Ohhh, curse this heat! My dear Stationmaster is no doubt suffering terribly today…
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[9]._stepCh: Hm-hm, another hot one… Based on my logs, the average Summer temperature is trending higher every year. I wonder what the cause could be…?
It’s times like this I wish I was small enough to squeeze into a cool, dry storage unit… Hm, that sounds rather nice…
Tropical storms aside, Summer is rather easy on our supply lines. Speaking of which, I’ll have to report my predictions to HQ as soon as possible!
Ah, my dear Stationmaster is surely suffering!~ Summer is the worst time of year for a lover of all things dark, cool and wet…
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[10]._stepC: Hmm, a hurricane was recorded off the eastern face of High Climb last year. Are we in for another wet, windy Fall this year…?
Fall keeps the Armory nice and cool, day and night. All that’s missing is a nice cup of nectar to keep my eyes open.
I’m hoping our supply lines will keep clear of any windstorms on route. Fall can be a turbulent time of year, especially on the Frontier.
Our dear Stationmaster is surely enjoying this cooler weather!~ Will she grace the Armory with her organizational prowess once again?~
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[11]._stepC: Hm, winters have been getting wetter every year. Surely there’s some cause to these changes… I’ll have to compare notes with stations by The Forbidden Terrace.
Winter weather can be harsh on our stores, if I’m not careful! I’ll have to check the caulking on our storage units again, soon…
Our supply trains will be struggling through this Winter weather. I’ll prepare as warm a welcome as I can with our limited supplies… Perhaps Basile can assist with that…
In spite of our struggles, Stationmaster Catarella thrives in this dark, frozen season. As cold as she is, she warms my heart with her ~inextinguishable~ work ethic!
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[12]._stepC: Based on my logs, certain Spring flowers typically arrive around this time. Let me know if you see any good quality specimens!
Spring always brings the best interior conditions… But I guess it’s important to take a break for fresh air, sometimes.
With any luck, this Spring will be mild enough not to disrupt our supply lines. A surprise thunderstorm is the last thing we need out here…
The Stationmaster busies herself inside as much as she can, in Spring… Her office hours are *legendary* this time of year!
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[17]._stepC: I’ve heard Matchmaker Day is kicking off in Cloverton. A chance to go on a date with anyone, regardless of rank!? Ridiculous… Unless…?
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[20]._stepC: With Provision Day kicking off in nearby Cloverton, our supplies are looking close to normal for once! Still, it’s far too distant to make a long term difference…
Ah, Provisions Day! So much to account for and inventory! Perhaps near a crackling fire, with a Stationmaster who has a panache for the bureaucratic…? That would be a gift…
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[33]._stepC: With the Acid Mines safe, some of our supply issues ought to clear up… Perhaps we could divert some of our routes through Sunnyfur territory… Hmmm…
Few Unioneers have direct experience dealing with homunculi… I wonder if this posting will get reinforcements, due to the scope of the infestation?
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[34]._stepC: A Wizard’s laboratory was uncovered in the Savannah? Hmmm… Depending on its surviving contents, this could either improve our lot or worsen it significantly.
Seems the Captain has ordered additional patrols around the old Wizard’s lab, but we haven’t been told what was inside. Frankly, I’m not sure I’d be comfortable scavenging from it, even if we could.
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[35]._stepC: The entrenched Unioneers have finally been relieved! And of course it was *you* who rushed to their aid. Hardly surprising, considering how we met.
Another large homunculus was defeated… But is the infestation getting worse? I’ll check the data I’ve been collecting from scouts to see if there’s a trend.
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[36]._stepC: Eh? The Poachers!? Great Sky, I was so busy with other problems on the Station that I forgot they were out there!
Our supply lines are still being targeted by Pirates, in spite of the Poachers’ capture… So, there must be a wider organization at play…
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[37]._stepC: So, one of the Poachers’ lieutenants has been defeated? Good riddance to that bloodthirsty blackguard!
Now that the sniper in the woods has been defeated, our foragers can finally resume their much-needed duties. Another feather in your cap, {0}!
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[38]._stepC: I hear one of the Poachers’ lieutenants was a Doulahan! Such spirits are exceedingly rare… I’ll have to report this sighting to HQ as soon as possible.
I heard that Doulahan was managing an ice cream factory? I wonder how efficient their supply chain management was…
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[39]._stepC: So, the boss of the Poachers has finally been caught! I suppose his ice cream business will be confiscated… Repurposing his supply chain would certainly be an interesting project.

I’ve been looking over the court documents, and I have to admit I’m impressed by Vaskin’s work ethic. He’s a terrible person, yes, but managing such a large enterprise, completely undercover? It’s quite impressive.
It’s still hard to believe that Vaskin Robins was the villain behind all of these problems. His web of intrigue was truly labyrinthine. Impressive, but terrible…
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[42]._stepC: Hmmm… One pallet of dry goods, one pallet of canned goods, one pallet of… Joy King Brand “Human in a Can”? How’d this get in here?!

Another refrigerated crate of Robbin’s Ice Cream…? Hmmm, we don’t have enough Gale Cells to keep these cool… Maybe we can offer the melted ice cream to the Catfolk, keep 'em off our backs for a while…
More Holy Avenger replicas arrived in our weapons shipment. Are those blockheads at Logistics playing some kind of prank…?
I found a hairball skulking around our supplies yesterday looking through a dozen crates. -tsk tsk-! I made sure she understood our inventory system perfectly, to prevent such inefficiencies.
Ah, the Stationmaster’s handwriting is so small, so neat and legible, so to-the-point!~
The Stationmaster dropped by for another visit last night!~ I mean, it was for our regularly scheduled review, b-but it’s still important to take these things seriously…
I saw the cover of the Stationmaster’s book recently. It had a most curious title: “Arctic Dungeon Barbarians”! Do you think it’s some kind of astronomical thesis…?
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[45]._stepC: Frontiersman {0}! Glad to see you made it back from the battlefield in one piece.
Likewise, Keely! What’ve you been up to?
I’m finishing my second round of inventory. There wasn’t much left to tempt any cat-burglars, but you never know…
Now that the area is pacified, we can increase our supply lines and re-open the Armory stores. And it’s all thanks to you!
Now, feel free to peruse our stock if you find yourself in need of any new equipment.
As a Frontiersman, you also have access to our Union-exclusive training space, where you can hone your skills!
So like the one in Cloverton? Sounds good! See you on the flip side, Keely.
I… Yes. I, too, will see you on “the flip side.”
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[46]._stepC: Oh, are you here to check our stores, Frontiersman? Just took a fresh inventory!
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[47]._optio: Got a minute to chat, Keely?
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[50]._stepC: Got a minute to chat, Keely? I’m sure you have some good insights!
Well, you might say I have a *pretty* good grasp of how things work on a Union base…
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[51]._rawCo: What would you like to talk about?
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[52]._stepC: How could you tell I was a Frontiersman on sight? Most people don’t realize who I am until I explain it.
It was obvious! I just finished re-reading the latest revision of the Central Kingdom Uniform Regulation Pamphlet 670-1 not too long ago.
Besides, I’d recognize those Ivory Tower digs anywhere. They’re almost intentionally drab.
Hey, I like them! They’re practical!
… Anyway, couldn’t you immediately tell I was a Quartermaster from *my* uniform?
{0}! As a Frontiersman, you’re as obligated as any Union soldier to keep up on uniform regulations!
Look, I usually just rely on context clues, okay? It’s kinda my whole *thing*!
Since you saved my life, I’ll “fail” to mention this in my daily log…
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[53]._stepC: Are you close to the Captain? He kinda seems like he doesn't like me being around.
Seems a bit resentful, does he? Let me put it this way…
The Captain’s a Navy man. Clawed his way to the top by sheer force of will, nearly lost his life in the process. Suddenly, at the prime of his career, he’s stationed out here in the crack of nowhere.
How do you think it makes him feel when a newfish suddenly shows up and runs circles around him?
… This isn’t the first time I’ve been called a newfish, though.
Don’t get me wrong, {0}! Everyone knows a well-educated Frontiersman can be a real magic bullet in a tight spot…
Especially those of us who didn’t qualify for the program. Understand?
Oh… I never thought of it that way. To be honest, I was basically born into the program.
“In order to put yourself in someone else’s shoes, sometimes you have to ask their shoe size first.”
That was certainly something you said, just now!
Pulled it straight out of Union Aphorism Regulation Pamphlet 259-95! Did it work?
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[54]._stepC: Do you have a read on the Stationmaster? She seems a bit-
Um, do you need a minute?
The Quartermaster clears his throat bashfully.
To be totally honest, {0}, I might harbor a bit of a one-sided crush on the Stationmaster!
-sigh-!~ Ever since we shared a strong pot of nectar to get through a long, hot Savannah night…
She’s a bit of an insomniac, you see. And what better time to do inventory than the dead of night?
Since you said it, it must be true!
Sadly, we’ve since had to ration our supply of nectar concentrate…
Oh, what I wouldn’t give to spend a few more nights basking in the meticulous machinations of her Obsessive Compulsive DOMINANCE!~
Sounds like a job for your local Frontiersman!
No, no, I couldn’t accept anything but pre-approved military-issue nectar. The paperwork alone would require at least three pots just to get through it!
KeelyArmorerBarksNHub___nodes[55]._stepC: The Quartermaster clears his throat and flips through some pages on his clipboard.
Very good, Frontiersman. Just fill out this free release of liability, and we can get started.
CatarellaFirstTimeIntroduction___nodes[0: You disembark onto the dock, taking in the sights of the station. While it’s certainly seen better days, it’s nice to have a staging area before heading into this archipelago of savannah islands.
Before you can get to exploring anything, a dark haired woman in an elegant white dress approaches you. Her tired looking eyes are rather lively as they scan over her clipboard and your face.
Well hello there. Welcome to Katarina Station. May I have your name, sugar?
Her voice is wispy, but polite, with an endearing twang in her accent.
The woman pulls out a clipboard, her eyes flicking over it before focusing on something written down, and nodding.
The woman crooks a worn out smile at you and puts her clipboard away, twirling her umbrella in her hands.
Commander Sieger sent word ahead that you would be arriving soon. Welcome to Katarina station, my name is Catarella, the… Station Master.
Now that you’re here, things may begin to run smoothly. You should meet Captain Joaqim as well, post-haste… However…
I believe I have enough time to orientate you, should you need it…
CatarellaFirstTimeIntroduction___nodes[1: Depressing?
Embarrassing.
In disrepair…
Disheveled?
Run down.
Old Timey!
I was going to say adventure-y!
PartyChimeKatarinaCondition
CatarellaFirstTimeIntroduction___nodes[2: I am getting a strange sensation of… Mischief? amusement? It’s hard to tell.
Nyehehehe~ Yeah, ask her about her name. It’ll be funny!
More importantly… Why isn’t everyone a cat?
I dunno if I woulda’ asked that, {0|Caraid|Laddie|Lassie}...
Woah! You’re totally right! That does sound-
CatarellaFirstTimeIntroduction___nodes[3: Catarella and Katarina… Those names seem kind of…
Why does this place look so…
I think I got my bearings in order! (Cont.)
CatarellaFirstTimeIntroduction___nodes[4: Catarella and Katarina… Those names seem kind of…
CatarellaFirstTimeIntroduction___nodes[6: Catarella’s eyes are trained on yours, her expression is inscrutable, save for a quirked eyebrow.
Well, uhm, w-what I mean is that they’re kind of… Similar?
Similar you say? Why I have never heard this before! Care to elaborate on your meaning?
I.. Uhm.. Well, I mean… It… -
A sly smirk crosses her stoic face.
Sorry, I couldn't help myself, sugar. That question has come up a lot ever since I was assigned here.
Truthfully, there is no relation between me and the station name. As far as my knowledge extends, Katarina was once the name given to an expedition that explored this section of the frontier.
The station acted as their outpost for a while, and was abandoned when they left; some number of decades ago.
Joaqim and I are now responsible for its restoration.
CatarellaFirstTimeIntroduction___nodes[7: Why does this place look so…
CatarellaFirstTimeIntroduction___nodes[9: Oh! Well… That is in no small part thanks to this station being abandoned for… decades, at the very least.
Any particular reason why it was abandoned?

From what the dossier I received said, it was decommissioned shortly after The Crystal Wars. Once its purpose was served, The Central Kingdom little cared what happened to it. It was stripped of everything useful, and left to drift.

A rather common story in the frontier, it seems. It takes a lot of resources, time, and manpower to maintain a station for the sake of operations. It is often more feasible to just move on when a location has served its purpose.
That’s a shame. Well, looks like it could use a refurbishment.
Don’t I know it, sugar. Which is why Joaqim and I are present.
Some repairs here, and refinements there. Perhaps even a few more modernization projects, and I believe we’ll be able to turn this ugly plankton into the most beautiful moon jelly you have ever seen!
CatarellaFirstTimeIntroduction___nodes[1: So, what do you do here? You said you were a Station Master? Station Mistress?
Station Master is fine, Sugar. As for my duties - you might be surprised, but Katarina Station is… a station…
You nod, that seems reasonable enough.
Thus, in addition to its vital support role for The Union, it’s a convenient port for many ships transporting goods from all around this here corner of the Frontier.
As Station Master, it is my solemn duty to manage all the aspects of Katarina Station, to ensure it runs safely and efficiently.
Needless to say, it’s been quite a lot of hard work getting everything up and running, Sugar. Most of my days are filled with arrivals, transfers, supply shipments, and meetings upon meetings…
I do, however, take pride in my work, so please maintain an orderly, respectful atmosphere.
Can do!
Introductions
I think I have my bearings in order!
I’m glad I could bring you up to speed, sugar.
Catarella takes a pocket watch from her voluminous skirts, checking the time.
And, just in time. We have used all of our allotted time for introductions. You should go and acquaint yourself with Joaqim, post haste.
O-oh?
I’d say not to really worry about it, sugar.
Joaqim doesn’t adhere to schedules and planning like I do. So he’s not liable to notice.
I, however, *do* adhere to schedules. So please, follow me.
Catarella gestures for you to follow after her, her heeled boots rapping against the wooden dock. You follow after her to meet with this ‘Joaqim’.
Oh, and be sure to come see me when you are finished speaking with Captain Joaqim. I may be able to point you in the direction of the reprobates you are after.
Continue

JoaqimUnlockForestDialogue___nodes[5]._s: Happy to hear it, Captain. So, what’s the Station capable of now?
No need to indulge me, my dear Frontiersman! I could go on for hours, and I’m sure la Sombrissima has already upstaged me in that regard.
The most important thing for *you* to know, is that you may now disembark for the Forest—From the same platform you used to reach the Savannah.
So, the Savannah is still within range? I might need to do some more exploring in that area.
As much as I want that *fly-infested tornado of allergens* off my charts, I am loath to defy the needs of my savior…
So, yes, the Savannah will remain within range—As will any archipelago we discover on our patrols, speaking optimistically.
Personally, I’m happy to hear you speaking optimistically for once!
There’s a reason we say “optimism fills the lungs of fools,” in my country… The *less foolish* part of me believes this operation will remain incomplete…
Still, I have every faith that you will make the most of what is available to you, as you have until this point.
Just leave it to me, Captain. I’m pretty much physically incapable of letting anyone down!
Just remember to drink some water, from time to time~
JoaqimUnlockForestDialogue___nodes[6]._s: ‘Old Rock,’ huh? Are we talking about you, or the Station?

JoaqimUnlockForestDialogue___nodes[7]._s: I should get going! There’s still lots to do, and I’m eager to check out the Forest!

Adios, and good luck! My support for your mission is as undying as my gratitude.

Everyone holds their tongue as the Stationmaster takes the big pile of evidence, and flips through the pages with a bored frown.
Well, what is it Catarella? Fraud? Forced labor? Monster abuse?! Kidnapping?!
<speed=slow>……</speed><speed=slowest>...</speed> Tax evasion.
FADE_OUT_MUSIC
What!? That’s it!?
<shake><color=red>FELONY tax evasion!!!</shake></color>
Enough! I’ve heard and seen so much FlimFlan over the course of this operation, it’ll be pouring out of my ears for the next year at least!
PLAY_SIEGER_THEME
Between the testimony of the Holstaurs of Hoovren, {0}’s actions undertaken as a Union Frontiersman and Commander Goldra’s investigation-
And the tax evasion...
-AND the tax evasion…
I believe we have the evidence to draw up a warrant for the arrest of <color=red>Vaskin Robbins</color>. He’s our strongest lead at the moment, and it’s about time something was done!
PB Return Continue
Ready to roll out?
I’ve been ready!
I’ll follow your lead.
PB Return Menu 02
Flamin’ FlimFlan, I’ve been ready! Just say the word!
I understand your feelings, but there’s still much that needs doing before we can take action.
PB Return Let’s Go
I’ll follow your lead, Sieger. What’s the next step?
PB Return Lead
While I begin the search for Vaskin’s current whereabouts, I want you to go home, get some rest, and prepare yourself for some real action.
Once you’ve gotten some rest and prepared your team, return to Katarina and seek out Joaqim. I will instruct him to make sure you can find us, wherever we may end up.
Go home, rest, then come back and speak with Joaqim. Easy!
And rest well, {0}. No matter what happens over the course of my search, I’ll need you in top form!
MOVE_OUT_OF_LODGEHOUSE
You breathe a long sigh of relief as you step out the door of the Lodge. Things were looking pretty sticky there, for a minute!
PB Return End

Whatever those creepy old drawings mean, {0}, it can’t be good…
Perhaps we should have been more careful in our investigating… But, I’m confident we did the right thing.
Those ancient diagrams… They were quite troubling… What could Vaskin possibly want with Homonculi…?
We should move quickly, Wavemaker… I believe Vaskin is about to undertake a dangerous ritual.
I’m not entirely confident I understand wots goin’ on, but I’m ver confident we can fix it by blowin’ it up. LET’S GOOO!!!
It is well that we should rest, my {0|liege|lord|lady}, and have faith in our commanding officer.
Whatever his plan is, I couldn’t care less. They won’t be allowed to come to fruition.
Looks like it’s up to us again, {0}. Once more into the thorns, I suppose…?
Pretty sure whatever’s goin’ on can be fixed with a good stompin’, too. Jus’ tell me where tae walk, {0}!
Don’ worry, Fronty. If’n ye got in trouble back there, I would’ve vouched for ya! Yar har har harrr!
When we get home, Frontiersman, why not let me give you a massage? You need to relax before we head out again…
I got your back, {0}.
Go home and rest? For once, I’m glad to be taking orders.
I’ve got a terrible feeling this is only going to get worse…
That <color=red>Vaskin Robbins</color> is a clever guy... How do we know he’s not expecting this?
We’ve made it this far, haven’t we? Let’s get home, get some rest and get back in the thick of it!
In any case, it looks like there’s only one thing left to do… Go home and get some rest!
Party Chime Ins
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[2]._stepCha: This dreadful brightness is an affront to my eyes. Truly this would be one of the times where Joaqim could be useful and pivot the station to face away from the sun.
It is a… Fine day. Really, what would improve it is a nice overcast to contrast the brutal light of the sun with shades of grey.
My umbrella’s service is most appreciated. My skin is far too delicate to receive the rays of sunlight completely unguarded.
It is a truly beautiful day, {0}, but I wonder if it would be improved if the sun decided to tone it down.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[3]._stepCha: Ah, today has some very lovely weather. It is a shame that I must enjoy it while I tolerate work at the same time.
Today’s weather carries with it a wonderfully dreadful ambience with it. Why, if I did not have work, I would be in my room with a lantern to read a book by.
Great care must be taken while I walk. A single misstep, or worse, a trip, and suddenly my ivory pleats become muddied.
Joaqim has been in a much more agreeable state. Quiet, only grunting in response to questions, I am overjoyed at his silence.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[4]._stepCha: This is the most agreeable weather for me. I only wish that the leaves did not find themselves attracted to my hair. You can hardly remove them in a ladylike fashion...
My choice of attire now has its time to shine. The wind will help me cut the most elegant figure possible.
This weather had put Joaqim in a most irritating mood. Should he continue making overtures to try and fly the station freely, I will have Keely cut his luxury rations.
Sometimes I lose my grip on my umbrella and it gets carried off by the wind. Quartermaster Keely seems most attentive, and has retrieved my umbrella for me each time.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[5]._stepCha: Haaahhhh… My work never ceases, even on days like this. How unfortunate it is to have to work when I would rather be in my room.
Weather like this makes me wish for an office… But then, I remember I would be sharing it with Joaqim, and that is enough to make the yearning disappear.
Storms like this were normal back home. I would watch the streets turn into avenues for boats to sail down, from the comfort of my suite.
A drizzle like this puts my clothing in danger of running. There is nothing a lady fears more than her precious chiffon turning into ecru, or even worse, cream!
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[6]._stepCha: Chicness does not mean I must be uncomfortable. All it took me was to double-layer my stockings and wear some thinner garments beneath my dress and I am as comfortable as I could be… Of course, my face is freezing, but we all must make sacrifices for style.
Care must be taken during this weather. Our work, mine especially, does not stop simply because of a blanket of snow
My umbrella’s service is to be commended today. Were it not for it, I would be covered in snow; a most unsightly sight for a lady such as myself.
The only thing keeping me warm at the moment are thoughts of me finishing my day, returning to my room, and reading my copy of ‘Romance on the Frontier’ next to a roaring fire.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[7]._stepCha: How are you faring in this heat {0}? Back home, weather like this would call for a tall, cool pitcher of sweet tea, and probably an awning to drink it under.
This heat is absolutely stifling. So much so that it is making me consider loosening my corset. Could you imagine such a scandalous sight?
I will admit, this heat is bothersome. I wind up needing to wipe myself down every so often so as to not stain my clothes.
Fortunately, I still remember my family’s recipe for a virgin mint. While I *would* love to add bourbon to it, simply having any refreshing beverage at all is more than adequate for me.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[9]._stepCha: The summers here are nothing like back home. In fact, I would say this is an almost pleasant heat.
I admit that I am equal parts relieved and frustrated due to my work slowing down. I finally have time for a break, but I must now wait on someone else before continuing my job...
Our ice consumption has gone up significantly now. It is a pain to balance the budget when dealing with changes like this…
Joaqim has been shedding everywhere with the summer heat. I am certain at this point we could make another, quieter version of him from the spare fur.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[10]._stepCh: You would not believe all the shipments I have to coordinate in order to keep our larders filled, not just for this season, but for the coming winter as well.

Truthfully the atmosphere has been more amicable, maybe it is because the warmer, spiced seasonal beverages are available. Giving people a choice between that, or the… ugh.. Beers and ales. Really, it’s no choice at all, who would choose a bitter ale over that?
I find Joaqim’s recent amiability due to the season distressing. Always upbeat and chipper, and he has the *gall* to wish *me* a good day.
The Fall releases have arrived, and I have procured several books to read at my leisure… Some day.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[11]._stepCh: It is exasperating to track down everything we have been shelving for the last year. Even though *my* documents are above reproach, apparently others are not as… Thorough.
Working with those who handle Provisions Day is a delight. If my staff were half as organized as they were, why, I might have more me time.
My winterized kit includes a much thicker dress, several heating pads, and a lantern for reading later at night.
The nights are longer, and more peaceful at this time of year. I find myself with the abundance of time I crave during the other seasons...
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[12]._stepCh: My favorite tea should be ready to be purchased by now. It is only sold during the springtime, and I have to bulk-order so it will last me the year.
This station *should* have more staff. It takes a small army of staff to manage the logistics, and yet, this responsibility falls squarely onto my shoulders.
While I do not detest spring, I wish the spring showers would stay around long enough for me to enjoy them.
One of my favorite authors just released their seasonal stories. I must contain myself. Even a single novela more might topple my stack of to-reads.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[17]._stepCh: I have heard that Yuuga is doing the rounds out here for Matchmaker Day. Proclivities aside, she is the prime example of a model worker.
Thanks in part to Yuuga’s enthusiasm for her job, she and I often peruse the same…. Fictive genres. I make sure to get at least a few recommendations from her before she returns to the capital...
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[19]._stepCh: I do not have much to say on the Solstice itself, save for that I have only participated in it when I was much younger.
I have accounted for the Solstice in my schedule. Which is to say, with so many asking for time off, I have temporarily halted some operations.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[20]._stepCh: I really appreciate working with Clara. Her yearly visits take a mountain of preparation, but she gets the provisions dolled out in almost no time.
Brume Roberts is a constant headache. He’s disruptive, emotional, and worst of all, unorganized. It’s like a second, more festive, Joaqim.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[21]._stepCh: It is nice to see Sebastian active in his old age. He is a friend of my family, you know. He fought alongside my family during the wars.
When he was alive, Sebastian was, as I understand it, an excellent captain. Whether that has carried on into his current condition, I have no idea.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[33]._stepCh: Detaining the Cat Sith has given us further access to the Savannah. I am certain that Captain Seiger has already begun his foray deeper in.
Transporting the Cat Sith from the Savannah to Cloverton was not an easy task. Even when pacified it still caused a large enough delay for it to impact our operation.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[34]._stepCh: I have no idea what happened in that Wizard’s lab, {0}, but barely an hour after you left apparently, there was a land-slide and the door was blocked off.
The Wizard’s lab has been fully sealed off thanks to that rockslide. All we can really do is keep the surrounding area blocked off so as to avoid any unfortunate accidents...
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[35]._stepCh: The forward base’s establishment has helped us greatly in establishing our foothold in the Savannah. Of course the person now in charge of getting supplies to that foothold is none other than me…
After the camp was secured, there have been no sightings of large homunculi like the Kelpie you fought. Suffice to say, there will be no breach in our defenses without us noticing.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[36]._stepCh: After reading the reports on the Poachers, I can only find myself baffled by how they managed to have such an expansive operation with their egregious incompetence.

Those that worked alongside the Poachers have been arrested. However, while Sieger has asked that those two remain detained in Cloverton for the time being; Their cohorts have already been shipped off to Peregrin.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[37]._stepCh: Joaqim has been practically vibrating now that these forests are within range, with untapped resources to use. I am finding it hard to relish the paperwork I will have to do in order to expand our operations.
At least with the Sniper dealt with, our scouts will feel able to venture further out. Perhaps this will allow us to set up better relations with the wolf clans and Holstaurs.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[38]._stepCh: First poachers, then snipers, now spectres…. Ugh, what’s next, dragons?
I’m sure Vaskin Robbins wasn’t expecting a raid on his factory, or you tussling with his body guard. I’m sure you’re well on the right track to bringing in that mediocre ice cream baron.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[39]._stepCh: With Vaskin Robbins finally out of the picture, we have been getting reports of erratic activity throughout the entire frontier. His underground empire spanned much further than initially believed. For once though, that is not my problem.

Joaqim has proposed that we celebrate your collar of Vaskin. I reminded him that we do not have the time, or budget for a large celebration…. So I convinced him we should have a much smaller one instead.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[42]._stepCh: Please excuse any overfamiliarity I may have today. I have found myself in a…. Good mood. The release of a particular monsterotica sequel I had been anticipating has arrived.
Were it within my power, I would have a library constructed to house my books. I am becoming tired of tripping over the novellas I have stacked in my room.
If I must suffer sleep deprivation in order to read, then so be it. I do not have enough time in the day to work, and indulge in my pleasures. I will *not* be robbed of my off time.
I plan on binge-reading tonight. A nice dark room, a single lantern, and a stack of my to-reads will be exactly the thing I need to ‘recharge’ myself… Even if it comes at the cost of tonight’s sleep.

My only ally in the constant battle against our ever-growing logistics is the tea rations that I have been able to procure. A strong pot of tea will get you through the whole week better than five weak ones.
Sometimes I muse that I should have chosen a different career path. I would make for a fine administrator almost anywhere… But then I remind myself it has to be me, someone else might do it wrong.

Much of my energy must go into being the cloud that rains on Joaqim’s day. Without someone to tell him no, he might go with every inane idea that pops into his head. Such as ‘Margherita Mondays’ or, ugh, ‘Casual Fridays’.

There is always *one* more thing to do, *one* more task to attend to. Even with any help I can get, I must always work late to ensure the station runs properly. If only Joaqim had a brain between his fluffy ears, I might be able to make use of him.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[43]._stepCh: Before I go on, was there anything you required?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[44]._rawCon: What would you like to ask Catarella
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[44]._option: I’ve been looking for the people who were with that caravan.
What do you think of everything I’ve been up to?
Would you be interested in a little… Liaison~?
ONLY AVAILABLE IF UPGRADES ARE DONE
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[46]._stepCh: Do you think I have the spare time to indulge in personal stories, {0}? I must appear, by all accounts, not busy!
Tell you what, once all this business with the poachers is over, I will indulge in your strange predilection for inane questions. Deal?
Good, now that that is out of the way; I have just enough time for other, more sensible questions.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[47]._stepCh: With my schedule suddenly *much* more agreeable, I believe I can indulge you. What do you wish to know?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[48]._rawCon: What would you like to know?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[48]._option: Why did you become a Station Mistress?
What is your day-to-day life like?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[49]._stepCh: Why did you become a Station Mistress?
Oh, following in the footsteps of your parents? That’s-
More like, if I want to be a true member of my family, I would need to have a record of service.
No. Well, it’s more complicated than that, but ‘family’ is usually enough to stop people from asking. I suppose for you I could elaborate…
My family has a proud military history. Back home, before it became a part of the Central Kingdom, it was expected that everyone serve in the military.
My family is… traditional. And, if I want a part of the family tontine-
Oh! In my family, our fortune is distributed via a tontine. Interest on our families fortune is paid out to living members who have been added to it.
Once someone dies, their own shares in the tontine are distributed evenly to other members, rather than to just their children.
And, if I wish to be a part of that, I will need a stint of military service.
So, here I am, another in a long line following traditions I had no say in creating. Oh well, at least the dividends are worthwhile.
That sounds rather intense. Sorry to hear that, Catarella.
Oh, don’t be that way, {0}! That’s just family after all! Besides, I like this line of work rather well. Else I wouldn’t be here, and I wouldn’t be as good at it as I am.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[50]._stepCh: What is your day-to-day life like?

The short answer is, exhausting. I wake up early, go to sleep late, and I often do all my work with little delegation. Often due to staffing issues, or being unable to trust that the work will be done satisfactorily.
Busy. I start my day off with a cup of tea. Depending on my night, it’s either a dark cup, or darker.
My morning work is mostly handling the work that piled up over night. Most of them are ship manifests and the like. Usually by the time I have handled that, it is late morning.

I have a brief-as-possible meeting with Joaqim, to coordinate what I need to do for the remaining day. *Most* of this is just more paperwork, so I will not explain the particulars, but this keeps me busy enough for it to go from lunch to dinner.
By that time, I coordinate with Quartermaster Keely on our supplies, and work out a ‘grocery list’, which will include any luxuries, personal orders from marines, and the like.

Come nightfall, the work dies down, and I handle expense reports, general accounting and duties for station staff for the next day. It is usually close to midnight when I finish work, bathe, and then sleep.
Just to start it all again the next day…
Well, if it’s any consolation Cat, I don’t think this place would be as effective without you.
That is very kind of you, {0}, thank you.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[52]._stepCh: Just stopping back by to resupply, and to see my favorite Station Mistress!
Truely? Even more favored to you than, say, Minerva?
Well, Minerva is my favorite Station Mistress in Cloverton, and you’re my favorite Station Mistress in Katarina!
As good a response as I was going to get, I suppose.
To my understanding, this has been your first outing into the Savannah, no? Tell me, how was it?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[53]._option: Almost like what I learned at the Academy.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[54]._stepCh: But not an all together bad one!
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[55]._stepCh: I’m a little relieved that my studying paid off actually!
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[56]._stepCh: I studied the wilds before, so my expectations weren’t all that high to begin with.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[57]._stepCh: However, I think there’s still much to learn from exploring, and I doubt I’ve seen everything the wilds has to offer.
Your optimism is truly sickening, but I am hopeful you find something fulfilling out there. For now, be well, Frontiersman.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[58]._stepCh: Your tales of daring have reached me within my offices, {0}.
They reminded me of some of my favorite books.
If you were a normal soldier, I would be giving you a pat on the head and readying your commendation.
I said, if you were a normal soldier.
As it stands, *you* should have the brains to know not to fight something like that without some sort of backup. And I...
You are a frontiersman, you should *not* be this stupid…
Do you have anything to say for yourself, {0}?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[60]._stepCh: After your rescue, Jurgen, Jaeger, and Jotun were able to successfully guide the miners back to safety.
The miners were given medical attention for their injuries and minor malnutrition, and the trio were given honors.
Joaqim wanted to throw them a *parade* at first. I was fortunate to talk him down to commendations and an increased reward.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[61]._stepCh: A homunculi… and a real nasty one at that. I am more surprised by such an infestation flying under our nose.
Homunculi are not a common threat in the Central Kingdom. But, here on the frontier, they can be quite the menace if not excised quickly.
You happened to encounter a *very* potent example of Homunculi, a Cat Sith. They can come to being when engulfing catfolk. I fear with all the catfolk here in the Savannah, there might even be more.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[62]._stepCh: Of course I am. You are supposed to be the best the Union can offer, and you went in with minimal reinforcements.
I wonder if you’re very much aware how…. Not smart it is… to do something like that on your own.
C’mon Cat, it’s no big deal! Everyone was safe, and I’m perfectly fine!
You are correct in that matter. There does not appear to be any *permanent* damage to you either.
But I question whether or not that brain in your head is functioning like it should.
Well, if it stops, I’ll let you know first.
Good, I would appreciate knowing if I am working with an imbecile beforehand. I do not want to be caught off-guard with another Joaqim if I can help it.
I think this has taken up enough of both our times, should you wish to talk more, you know where to find me.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[63]._stepCh: Does trouble follow you wherever you go, {0}? Or do you seek it out like Lamias hunting for warmth in a blizzard?
I am… Not sure I follow? The first one?
I am asking, {0}, because that Wizard’s lab you investigated is now covered under tonnes of rubble.
I… Didn’t know there had been a rockslide?
That is… Not what I was asking. We’re unable to send in a team to investigate the ruins. That, and Joaqim has been rather dodgy on if any reports have come in.
As it stands, I have almost no information on what happened there.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[64]._option: Has Giev shared anything about what happened?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[65]._stepCh: Even if we had the time and manpower needed to excavate the area, it is not like we would have permission to actually enter the lab.
Wizards like to keep their secrets close, and the red tape involved before any action could be taken means this will remain a mystery.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[66]._stepCh: I would laugh at that question coming from anyone else.
Giev has refused to return correspondence, regardless of how many we send to them. And, because Giev is a Wizard, there is no way we can actually order them to make a statement on the matter.
So, until Giev deigns to reveal their role in this, that avenue is closed.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[67]._stepCh: Nothing we can get at without an extensive construction crew working at it.
For the time being, the area is marked as dangerous. Our soldiers will be told to restrict their activities, so as to avoid that area.
That includes you, {0}. Should something happen because you’re fooling around in the rubble, you will have no one to blame but yourself for your idiocy.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[68]._stepCh: Leading the charge against a dug-in enemy now {0}? Were I more romantic, I would say you look {0|daring|dashing|dazzling}.
And what do you say, being less romantic?
Then I would say that idiocy and heroism are close bedfellows. And, you never know which you will wake up beside.
Like Sieger, or Joaqim. Ugh! Even heroism is an unpleasant thought, when I phrase it like that…
I suppose, however, you *did* achieve something notable, so I can not say you were idiotic.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[69]._option: Where did that thing come from anyway?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[70]._stepCh: Well, all reports from the lieutenant imply it was a Kelpie. Much like that Cat Sith, this time it was some unfortunate Centaur who became bound with a homunculi.
That makes sense. I think I’m starting to get the hang of these more troublesome homunculi. How do you know so much about them?

There was an extensive library at my family’s estate. Most higher ranking Union members will know a bit about them. Joaqim and other officers, for instance, learn about different threats as part of their officer training.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[71]._stepCh: About as secure as can be, given the circumstances.
It should be able to repel any minor homunculi attacks. However, at this juncture, I am more concerned with the pirate attacks that grounded our puffer network.
That shouldn’t be a problem now, right?
Luckily, deliveries of decoy puffer balloons have resumed. It should make travel deeper into the islands much safer again.
I have been working overtime to make sure of it.
Don’t be, {0}. If there is anything I take pleasure in, it is getting the logistics in order. The Union is truly blessed to have me.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[72]._stepCh: For you? Probably going down and putting yourself in even more danger.
For me, it means that I get to expand my duties without getting extra pay!
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[73]._stepCh: Tell me {0}, you have experience with the Gempas fruit. How does it taste? Is it a sweet fruit? Bitter? Sour?
Uh, well… It’s fairly sweet and juicy, and has a very potent taste. So it works well in juices, jams, cakes, and tarts.
That is excellent to know! So when I finally see your brain, I’ll know what to expect.
Come… Come again?
Gladly! You see, through your actions, I have come to the conclusion that instead of brains, you must have a Gempas between your ears!
So I plan on opening your head up to check for myself. Should there be a Gempas, I will make a nice tart out of it!
Now come here {0}~
W-woah, what brought this on!?
You, fighting the poachers of course! After the Cat Sith, the Kelpie, and now this. Someone *must* study you and find out how that brain works!
Besides Captain Sieger, you have got to be the *most* dunder-headed dolt I have ever met, and that is an accomplishment to be proud of.
Can we just talk about this!?
Sure we can after I… I…
I.. Whew… That was a lot… Oh, {0}, what are you doing here?
Do you… Not remember?
Apologies I… No, I do not.
A new shipment of energy supplements came in and it hit me… Harder than I expected. If something untoward happened, I am sorry.
Nono, it’s fine! Will you be alright?
I will be fine {0}! I think I will be lodging a formal complaint when I recover against our supplier.
Vaskin Robbins will have much to answer for, these were *not* the sort of things that should have been sold to consumers.
Yeah- you take care. Make sure to drink some extra water.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[74]._stepCh: {0}, I had heard you were rescued. I am… happy to see that you appear uninjured.
I would appreciate you not return my sincerity with sarcasm.
Sorry! What I meant was that… Well, I have had the impression you didn’t like me all that much.
I like you plenty {0}, you have many positive points, and desirable traits.
Desirable? Better to log that away for later…
Now now, {0}. While I will answer a few questions of yours, I will not completely surrender *all* my secrets to you.
… Alright, I suppose you have earned that dubious honor. But you only get *one* question.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[75]._rawCon: What do you want to ask Catarella?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[75]._option: Do you have any preference for food?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[76]._stepCh: If you must know, I am an avid reader. Surely a surprise, I know.

In particular, I am a fan of adventure-romances. Tragic heroes, star-flung lovers, and of course, love stories that involve a *lot* of horror elements. Monsterotica is a particularly guilty pleasure of mine.
My extensive collection is something I have to request bit by bit so as to not overload my room.
Wow, glad to find more bookworms out here! Anything you could recommend to me?
Hmm… I would need to get to know you better, but you really can not go wrong with a beginner’s story such as ‘The life and times of a widowed countess’ or ‘Short stories for the aching heart’.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[77]._stepCh: I love sweets most of all, given my need to keep my energy up. I have grown accustomed to cookies and teas to snack on.
However, the food I love most is Rouladan, a dish made with Bofroot. It was a common dish where I grew up, and even poorly made Rouladan is still very delicious to me.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[78]._stepCh: My formal education was at the Kingdom’s branch Academy in the Free States. Once I completed my education, I was given military instruction, and a broad spectrum of learning in order to be effective wherever the Union put me.
It just so happens that I am best suited to administration work~
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[79]._stepCh: Well, that was at least a little eye-opening.
Splendid. I believe we both have duties to attend to. So, we should probably go our separate ways.
I am glad you are safe. I do hope you know that.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[80]._stepCh: Haaahhhhh, there is so much paperwork to be done, I wonder if my work will ever be finished…
Well, with you at the helm Cat, I’m sure it will get done eventually!
As I understand it, I have you to thank for increasing my burdens once more.
What can I say Cat? I live to displease~!
Still, removing the threat that the sniper posed is commendable. Now our operation within the Forest has been able to proceed much easier.
It will not be too long before we have this poaching operation backed into a corner.
That is a relief to hear. Mind if I ask about how our operations are going in the Forest?
Hmmm, I think my schedule can be delayed long enough.
Has anyone found where the Sniper went?
Unfortunately, no. He appears to be a master tracker, especially if he knows how to hide his trail so well.
I was afraid of that. I was really hoping we could get a statement out of him…
You are doing well, {0}, I want you to know that. You have been instrumental in making the Frontier safer.
I have no doubt you’ll put a stop to all of this, and create a mountain of paperwork for everyone on the backlines to fill out~
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[81]._stepCh: Poachers, a Sniper, and now a Mirrorworld being… Oh my…
Vaskin sure has an eclectic bunch working under him.
From what I’ve heard, you’ve had your suspicions on him for some time.
Ever since I got to know Kiram.
With your proactive investigation, we are beginning to close in on Vaskin. The Mirrorworlder that you defeated was probably one of the last ‘cards’ he could play.
I suspect that soon, we will be able to mobilize fully against him.
It is, but you know what they say about a cornered animal.
So it may be prudent to ensure you take care of yourself, and prepare for whenever the final raid begins.
I will do my best, Cat.
While I’m here, do you mind if I ask a few questions?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[82]._option: How have you been keeping up with the work?
Have we uncovered more of Vaskin’s operation?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[83]._stepCh: I have been handling logistics for the Union forces within the Forest and Savannah. I’ve done my best to ensure that our supply lines remain unbroken.
It is a *lot* of work, and I have a feeling that Jubelle and other staff are equally overworked.
Is there anything I can do to help?
If I can request - Please capture Vaskin as soon as possible. Once he is gone, all this work disappears.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[84]._stepCh: Yes, thanks to your actions in the Forest, it seems that he’s been much less scrupulous with his actions.
You must have struck at the heart of his operation. His latest moves have been easier to track.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[85]._stepCh: Understood, and please, {0}, take care of yourself.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[86]._stepCh: Well, {0}, you did it. Vaskin is in custody, and I do not think he’s going to see outside of a cell for a *long* time, if ever.
Do you think he will actually get released one day?
While the Kingdom works hard to reform criminals, what Vaskin has done is completely reprehensible and vile.
Even if he did not act against the Kingdom and its interests, I do not believe he has earned any allies that would want to make the case for any sort of clemency.
Besides, his greatest assets, the pseudo-empire he built, is being dismantled as we speak. Soon, his assets will consist of whatever jailbird ensemble they provide. Apologies to any harpies.
That’s a lot of animosity you’re displaying. Not unfounded though.
He was the source of me doing so much work out here, he deserves more than a verbal lambasting!
However, this is not about him, it is about you. Congratulations are in order, Frontiersman. Without your assistance, Vaskin would have succeeded in his endeavors against the Kingdom.
I was really just doing my duty, anyone else would have done the same.
I wonder where it is you find that optimism, and if I could distill that into my morning coffee.
Regardless, I believe you must be *brimming* with questions, well, {0}, have at them.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[87]._rawCon: Let’s do this, one last time.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[87]._option: What will happen to Vaskin’s businesses?
Please tell me that Vaskin’s plot ended with him.
What will you do now, what happens to you?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[88]._stepCh: Well, if I were to guess, they will be dismantled and sold off. The estate sale would no doubt go a long way to helping everyone affected by his plots.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[89]._stepCh: Yes, it has. At least, as far as I can tell. While others worked with him, it sounds as though many were in it for wealth, not his philosophy.

CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[90]._stepCh: What? Are you looking to see me depart so soon?

CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[91]._stepCh: This won’t be the last time we speak, {0}, but for the time being, I think it’s best you rest and recover.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[92]._stepCh: Catarella holds her clipboard up, her eyes scanning over its contents.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[94]._stepCh: We still have missing people in the Savannah. I encourage you to pay attention to your surroundings, no doubt they are still within the initial area.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[95]._stepCh: You have my thanks for rescuing the workers from the Savannah. With them here now, my workload has finally reduced to ‘manageable’ instead of ‘manageable with exceptions’.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[97]._stepCh: {0}, normally I would not be so insistent on the matter, but I urge you to make finding the cookware for our beloved mess sergeant a priority.
Every serving of Union Survivor Slop does real damage to my psyche.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[98]._stepCh: With the Basile’s cookware returned, we have been able to move from Sailor’s Edition ‘Survivor’s Slop’ to actual food.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[100]._stepC: Our dear Quartermaster, Keely, remains missing. Last I recall, Joaqim had sent him out on some, undoubtedly inane, task. So maybe he has an idea where he went?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[101]._stepC: Thank you kindly for returning Quartermaster Keely to his post. Now if only others could be as dedicated to his task as he is. Present company excluded, obviously.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[103]._stepC: Katarina Station is still lacking a steady supply of building materials. Reticent as I am to suggest it, maybe Joaqim has an idea on what to do?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[104]._stepC: The statuary that Gelricht has inundated the station with has, in my opinion, vastly improved the aesthetics. Why, I might even take my break so I can enjoy them.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[106]._stepC: It would be very beneficial to have someone on retainer who can acquire monster cells for us. If you could find such an individual, I would appreciate it. Despite my enjoyment of monsterotica, a lady such as I is not about to get her hands, or dress, dirty.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[107]._stepC: Bang’s usefulness in procuring monster cells is only marginally balanced by the racket she makes. Fortunately, we have a supply of commercial grade earplugs to counter the noise.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[109]._stepC: There is still so much work to do, and *such* little time to do it. I cannot bring Katarina Station up to operating capacity on my own. If you can find the time, I would *greatly* appreciate your help, {0}.
Of course! I’ll help out as I can!
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[110]._stepC: It looks like the station is really coming together. Why, when I looked up from my desk, I didn’t have the overbearing sense of dread that comes from an insurmountable task.
Oh, but it *is*, {0|dear|sugar|hon}! The logistical issues of keeping the station running… Why, forget about upgrading it, we were barely able to keep afloat.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[112]._stepC: Pardon the pun. Though, now that I think about it, being able to outfit our station with newly fabricated engine parts would allow us to be much more proactive in the region… If only said parts weren’t on back order…
Well, that is a matter we’ll have to solve another time.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[113]._stepC: Regardless, I have documented your efforts to revitalize the station, {0}, and even if you do not get a reward for them from the Union…
You have my thanks in all this.
I am certain I do not know what you are referring to. Good day {0}.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[114]._stepC: {0}, you are.. Real, yes? Could it be I am having one of those…  nightmares where you’re *not* stressed?
I believe those are just called dreams, Cat.
Oh, I can not have a single moment to be dramatic can I?
I ah… I mean, you absolutely can! I thought it was a real question!
No matter, the moment has passed. I was mostly commenting on how the upgrades have finally been incorporated.
While my workload is still very formidable, it is nowhere near the Sea King it had been.
Why, I might even be as optimistic as to foresee a day off in my future!
I think I’d like to see that optimistic side of you!
But… I might decide to be a *little* more… accommodating after this, at least towards you. Thank you {0}.

Dreams and accommodations aside, this is a momentous occasion… One which I believe I must sour for myself with the sobering task of ensuring everyone is on the same page. Including a scatterbrained vulpe.
If you’ll accompany me, {0}. I believe that an important meeting with present staff is required.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[116]._stepC: So, I’ve been looking for the people who were with that caravan.
But I haven’t found all of them yet.
Hmm… I suppose I will not fault you for trying. Be sure to stay safe out there.
It would be even more exhausting for me if I needed to arrange for *another* search party.
Thanks for the concern Catarella, I’ll try my best!
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[117]._stepC: I’ve been looking for the people who were with that caravan.
And guess what, I have some good news!
Cat. You know, short for Catarella!
… I see… Now, you say you have good news?
I believe that I found the last member of the caravan.
You would be right. The last one came through just a short while ago.
I suppose that means congratulations are in order.
Your actions have brought back several personnel. An action such as that can not go unrewarded. So first and foremost, take this with my thanks.

Secondly, with all the workers returned, we can finish furnishing the lodge’s upper floor. Should you need to recover, or retreat to a private quarters while here on Katarina Station, you can have free room and board.
Now then, if there is no more business, I simply must get back to work. This entire debacle has left my time tables cattywampus. However, it would have been a lot worse without your help.
I’ll let you get back to it Cat, see ya around!
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[118]._stepC: Come with me, I have quite a nice spot for us.
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[119]._stepC: So, Catarella, with the station in better shape, are your evenings more… free~?
Thanks to you, I have found myself with some spare time on my hands.
I am glad to hear that, so-
Are you perhaps wondering if I would be interested in you joining me?
You are, by far, the most… amenable person I have met.
And one of the most attractive, if I must say~
I have a few books to enjoy, and some other…. More physically scintillating activities, if you are so inclined~
So, what do you say, {0}? Care to join me?
CatarellaBarksAndHub___nodes[121]._stepC: As you wish. Perhaps next time I have a moment…
LodgeScoutAndSailor___nodes[2]._stepChai: I’m tellin’ ye, t’was Union blade I saw. T’was a bit rusty, maybe, but Union make… Though it weren’t like some big battle in these parts left behind a pile o’ bodies n’ loot, was it?

Sold!? Could there be corruption in the ranks?

Ehh, I s’pose a hundred men movin’ across leagues o’ sky is bound to leave a trail…

Ughhh, I didn’t come in here ta *think* dammit!
Nice to see the two of you around! How’ve you been?
We got to thinking, and you’re pretty cool, and we were wondering….

Well I’m not one to shirk away from a good mystery~
Well I’m not one to shirk away from a good mystery~
Haha~! Well, let’s sneak away to a private place then~
No problem friend~ If you change your mind-
You know where to find us! Take care!
Ever since that encounter with the chest, we’ve been assigned more… Delicate work.
Apparently we got really good at helping Marines back into their rooms after they locked themselves out.
Well, glad to hear everything is going well for you two then! Take care!
LostWaterfallWoman___nodes[0]._stepChain: Oh it’s you. Thanks for the help clearing things out. Maybe when things calm down I’ll visit that waterfall again.

… I suppose I’ll check in with the quartermaster. I need to be sure my scouts are adequately supplied.
