MasonGelrichtKatarinaHubnBarks___nodes[2: O, glorious day~! I must use this time with the sun wisely, and finish my latest project.
My brethren are no doubt wrestling joyfully under this clear sky… A part of me longs to join them, but if I were to go traipsing off to every herd skirmish, I’d never get anything done!
Lo, the open sky!~ If only I could capture its immeasurable vastness in stone, so crude and finite… Alas!
We Centaurs venerate the open sky as we love the stable ground. With both, a Centaur’s spirit rides as free as the wind.
MasonGelrichtKatarinaHubnBarks___nodes[3: ‘Tis a bad day, for a ride. One must be sure-hooved to sally forth in the treacherous mud.
Although I dislike working in rain, it is good to see what a piece looks like soaked in water. It can change the quality of the surface wondrously!
This weather will no doubt delay my deliveries. Luckily, the rain also tempers my customers’ impatience!
My father would say that rain is a sign that the great sky is displeased… But I’ve always found such weather to feel quite tender, and even loving.
MasonGelrichtKatarinaHubnBarks___nodes[4: O, wondrous wind that blows through the heart, cleansing the soul, freshening the mind!~
This breeze is wonderfully invigorating! Luckily, my powers can keep the dust from blowing away…
A brisk wind can quicken ones’ pace, sharpen their mind, and invigorate their spirit. ‘Tis a great blessing, to my people!
Ahhh, this breeze is so sweet and gentle… I finally feel inspired to complete my latest project.
MasonGelrichtKatarinaHubnBarks___nodes[5: Behold, a wild storm is breaking across our humble station! But you brave Unioneers shall not falter…
The courage of the Unioneers in this storm is very inspiring! Their movements are so intentional, so practiced, so kinetic…!
I’m surprised to see that Union commerce thrives even in a storm. You humans are a tenacious lot!
O, thundering of hooves across the sky! O, mystic flash of striking stones!~ Great thunderstorms are often central to the myths of my people…
MasonGelrichtKatarinaHubnBarks___nodes[6: How rare it is to feel the fresh snow under my hooves! I must take this opportunity for a meditative stroll.
When a fresh snow falls, a temporary silence falls over the buzzing, teeming Savannah…
The cold season is a time for contemplation, meditation, and restraint. Snow on the ground can tame even the most restless Centaur hordes.
Our world is chilled to the bone… All is cold and quiet. It is a time for contemplating beauty and emptiness alike.
MasonGelrichtKatarinaHubnBarks___nodes[7: Even the most energetic Centaur warrior would be loath to ride in this abominable heat!
Take care of yourself today, my former muse!~ This terrible heat could ruin your perfect complexion…
An ice-cold, Doublehoof brew, chilled in the underground waters of the Savannah, is *perfect* on a hot day like this.
O’ brave Unioneers, *sweating* and *slogging* away under the PUNISHING sun…! Hm, I think I feel some inspiration coming on…
MasonGelrichtKatarinaHubnBarks___nodes[9: The warm Summer sun feels wonderful at rest, but one must be careful not to labor in the heat…
The Centaur brewmasters will soon collect the summer harvest for fermentation. I never wanted to take that path in life, but I must admit there is a certain artistry to it!
Behold yon brave Unioneers, toiling away under the hot sun! I should capture the essence of their determination in stone…
The time of the high sun is upon us!~ Woe to the creatures of the dark, and hail the sparkling of the sun!~
MasonGelrichtKatarinaHubnBarks___nodes[1: The cold season will soon arrive. I must prepare my shop accordingly. Perhaps with a hot kiln to warm my flanks!
The brewmasters are deep in their barrels, now that Fall is here. I wonder if my brethren have concocted any new ales…?
Fall brings wonderful colors to the Frontier! I must take advantage of the changing landscape, and capture its essence in stone…
Behold the changing of the seasons! So alike it is to the lives of men, whose circumstances change with every passing year…!
Winter is a slow season for building supplies. One would hope to have a ceiling over their head by first snow, after all!
During cold season, the Shettles grow their coats long, and the brewmasters empty the Great Barrels in the old halls. I miss that way of life sometimes…
A stolid Unioneer stands their watch on the walls of the citadel, wrapped in furs; the perfect Winter subject!
Our brewmasters work through the warm season to fill the Great Barrels in time for the cold. If you’re interested in Centaur ales, now is the time to make your inquiries!
Ahhh, a beautiful Spring day! I can feel the blackout curtains of ~inner turmoil~ flapping in the gusty breeze, parting to reveal the ~blinding light~ of inspiration!
This Spring weather has my flanks positively quivering with excitement… Alas! I must restrain myself, and remain at my station.
Ah~! I just know my people are cantering gayly in the fresh Spring meadows! Woe to those who must labor in such a joyful season…
The Spring sun is giving you such a ~virile sheen~, my former muse… Mm, perhaps you could pose for me again, for old times’ sake…?
MasonGelrichtKatarinaHubnBarks___nodes[3: I’ve heard tell of your past exploits, Frontiersman. To wade into battle against bloodthirsty pirates and mindless homunculi, to strike deep at the black heart of terror itself…!? Well, perhaps I’m being a *bit* dramatic…
Thinking back, there was a rumor amongst our herd of a human warrior defeating a fearsome, possessed Catfolk warrior… I’ll have to memorialize this victory in stone, and put it next to the others!
A strange being from the distant past, reborn into the present against his will… This poor reptilian warrior I’ve heard of must have been very lonely indeed.
This story I hear about an abandoned laboratory stirs the imagination. Hmmm, how might I engrave this dark and mysterious energy into stone…?
Woe indeed to those responsible for releasing those dark creatures upon my kind, and mercy to the warriors who suffer such violent possession…
I am relieved to hear you were able to help your people against the forces of darkness, Frontiersman… But it pains me to know my people are so vulnerable to their attacks.
You are a great warrior indeed,o' Frontiersman! Wretched though they are, I’m sure those Poachers were powerful fighters in their own right…
Such villains as those wretched Poachers must never be tolerated amongst Monster-kind. I am grateful that they have finally been brought to heel.
I’ve heard tell of this “Rook” fellow, a formidable marksman and vile Poacher… Once again, you have proven your valor and dignity are unrivaled,o' Frontiersman!~
Although I do not hail from these deep forests, I am grateful that this Rook fellow has been defeated. May his shadow never linger there again!
My people are very respectful of lost spirits, and one that is in the thrall of evil is no exception. May this Boethilde find the peace she requires to move on…
This warrior, Boethilde, would make a fascinating study… How would I depict her decapitated head? There are many possibilities…
My people can finally breathe a sigh of relief knowing the vile interloper, Vaskein, has been defeated. I must find some way to memorialize your penultimate victory!
Although he is a sinful man, this Vaskein fellow has a rather ~striking~ demeanor… Dare I carve his evil face in immortal stone…?
I’ve heard many a marine say this Vaskein was well respected in human lands. I’m grateful to know that this changed when his deeds were brought to light.
MasonGelrichtKatarinaHubnBarks___nodes[4: Greetings,o' Frontiersman!~ You look very striking today. Perhaps you could pose for me today, if you have the time?
-sigh-! There’s much to be done today, but the noise of the station is so distracting. I do find myself missing the quiet of the Savannah, at times…
The Stationmaster, Catarella, is quite a handsome and formidable creature. I’d ask if she would consent to a sitting, but I’m rather intimidated…
The Station’s cook, Basile, has been very eager to learn Centaur recipes from me. Fortunately for him, my people are known for quite easy and appetizing recipes.
Forgive my mindless chatter, {0}! Would you care to peruse the wares of this humble artist’s ~shoppe~?
Got a minute to chat, Gelricht?
Of course I can spare a moment for my former muse!
Are you saying I don’t inspire you anymore, Gelricht?
Of course that’s not the case, my dear {0}! It’s just that I’ve been *soul searching*, and what I saw within this corporeal shell was so…
No longer will I *leech* inspiration from others like a—uh—a *leech*...!
MasonGelrichtKatarinaHubnBarks___nodes[5: A leech…? I sense one day we will meet a man who takes exception to that particular insult…
I have my work, my customers, new friends and a regular schedule… People respect me and admire my work, even if it isn’t the *loftiest* of artistic achievement…
I’ve found a little life for myself here at the Station that I’m quite happy with, and it’s all thanks to you!
Sounds healthy! I’m really happy for you, Gelricht.
Thank you, {0}. Despite what I say, you are, and always will be, a font of inspiration for myself and others.
Gelricht’s eyes widen, and his powerful flanks quiver in anticipation.
O-oh!~ No matter the depths I fall, your light never fails to reach me!
I must have you, {0}... I mustn't wait any longer!
Take a tumble in the hay with a local artist?
And I am yours, {0}... But not here! Let us rendezvous at the nearby Inn.
Oh!!! Not only are you my heroic avenger, but my dark seducer…?!
Yeah, I was a double major.

Jam___nodes[18]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: If you’re the leader of the Brownie caravan, maybe you can tell me more about it? I only know a little about brownies.
Jam___nodes[18]._stepChain[1]._dialogPag: The…caravan? Oh, I suppose, if you’re curious, I could share a few details. And, there really is no one better than I for such things. You could of course ask our caravan’s elder, but I’m sure you would have a head of gray hairs before she was halfway through.
We’re a nomadic people, as you might know. What you might not know is that our caravan is one of many, sent out from our traveling city, Fi-Star.
Jam___nodes[18]._stepChain[2]._dialogPag: A traveling city? How on earth do you manage that?
Jam___nodes[18]._stepChain[3]._dialogPag: Well, Fi-Star is not so much a place, as it is a being. The Fi-Star is an enormous creature for whom we care for and in turn are protected by. Our little city rests upon its back, like dew on a rose petal.

Jam___nodes[18]._stepChain[7]._dialogPag: It’s a delightful little town, for the frontier. But we have to be careful. We’re a small people in a big bad world. We keep moving, keep making wonderful new friends, survive, and hopefully thrive.

KaleidaWithCaravan___nodes[2]._stepChain: You walk up to the booth, the eye-catching sign overhead only slightly less eye-catching than the woman behind the table. Your eyes meet her’s.
For a moment, your ears ring, as if adjusting to a change in altitude, and your sense of space is all… Wrong. The feeling passes, and you’re left staring into the brownie woman’s sanguine eyes.
Soo… See something you like, cutie~?
You blink, realizing that you HAD been standing in front of her and staring without a word.
While you’re very certain there is a lot to appreciate about the shorter woman, you shake your head, whatever that light was, it’s gone now. Time to be normal!
I was actually interested in this booth!
The woman’s finger slides from her hair and she presses both hands down on the table, raising her body up.
Welcome then, to Kaleida’s Mysterium! I am, of course, Kaleida, and THIS is where I reveal the futures of those willing, however good, or ill, they may be!
Oh? How much does it cost someone to have their future revealed?
The first fortune is always free!
Wait… That seems a little…. Odd. Don’t most people only get their fortunes read the one time?
Sometimes, yes! But, I am no charlatan, my predictions are ALWAYS accurate!
Because of this, many people become repeat customers… While some never wish to see me again for the same reason.

Kaleida smiles up at you and hops down from her booth.
Then come with me, and see your future!
Kaleida motions towards the caravan atop the large cloud skipper behind her booth.
The two of you clamber your way up to the doorstep of her house, and Kaleida welcomes you into her home with a gesture of her hand.
Show Kaleida interior

Inside, Kaleida’s furnishings seem set up primarily for the purposes of soothsaying, with only a few eclectic baubles and tchotchkes peaking out from the nearby cabinets and shelves. Kaleida moves aside a pile of books, as she makes her way to the opposite end of the room.
A moment later, curtains are drawn over the windows, as the light dims to the soft glow of the lanterns above.
Close curtains routine
Please, sit.
You take a seat at the table. Kaleida sits across from you, placing her green orb on a small plinth in the center of the table.
The orb casts a green pall over everything in its vicinity. With the mysterious atmosphere suitably set, Kaleida gives you a smile.

We, all of us, exist in a stormy ocean of fate. Each of us, sailors without a mast or sail to guide ourselves. We are left at the mercy of the currents and tides of chance, unable to see through the stormy waters, to see what is to come.
I, however, am one of the few gifted with the ability to see, to peer through the veil, to tell others what lies ahead.
Her eyes flick up to meet yours.

But, even I can not see through all veils, and can not fathom every current of the ocean. I can tell you what land lies ahead, but not always how you will be carried there, or what lies upon it. Do you understand this?
Y-yeah? No, wait, what’s an ocean?
Like an enormous lake, far beyond anything you can picture!
You nod, trying to internalize the idea.
Now, join your hands with mine, and open your mind to the future.
She stretches her hands out, waiting for you to take them.
You join hands, and she closes her eyes as you try to ‘open your mind’ whatever THAT means. Closing your eyes, you try to focus, maybe the key is to not think at all?
Moments pass, and you’re not certain you’re doing it right.
… Your nose begins to itch.
Am I doing it right-?
AH!
Kaleida pulls her hands back from yours, as if she’d just touched something hot.
Are you alright?
I… Yeah… Yes! I’m alright, how are YOU though? Do you feel anything strange?
You take a quick internal stock. There is a LOT of knowledge on farming in here, your shoulder twinges kind of a bit, and you could probably do with a meal, but…. That’s just ordinary.
No… Why though? What did my future say? Am I dying!?
Suddenly you become very concerned that your farming knowledge isn’t inclusive of illnesses, and that maybe your shoulder is twinging TOO much… And that hunger? Maybe a parasite!
What? Wait no! I’m so sorry, I didn’t mean like that! I should probably explain what’s happening first.
You see, when I looked into your future, seeing what lay a few seconds from now was easy enough. But, after you take a sip of tea, in a moment…
It was as if your own ship was surrounded by a storm so fierce, I could not glimpse the horizon. I couldn’t even see past the next tumultuous wave…
You’re not sure you follow. You’re trying to wrap your head around all this.
Now, how about that tea? Tea always helps.
Oh, yes please.
Open Curtains
Kaleida steps away from the table, and pulls the curtains open, letting light back into the room.
She steps away from the table, and walks over to a small kitchenette and retrieves a kettle.

When she returns, she holds the kettle in one hand, and a pair of saucers with tea cups precariously balanced in the other. Setting them down on the table, Kaleida pours the steaming tea into each cup, and passes one over to you.
Here, drink up. I think we can both do with something warm.
She blows on her cup and takes a sip, and you do the same. The taste isn’t very bitter, and you feel the warmth seeping into you.
Wait, what if I *didn’t* drink that tea just now?
Oh, not to worry, you did.
Kaleida curls a strand of hair around her index finger as she takes a slow breath.
Alright, I imagine you have many questions?

Liz___nodes[14]._rawContextHeader: Well, what would you like to know, sugar~?
Liz___nodes[14]._optionData[1].Tool_Comm: Only available after asking about how it's made
Liz___nodes[14]._optionData[2]._rawText: How do you get different flavors of starshine?
Liz___nodes[14]._optionData[3]._rawText: Can you tell me about the history of starshine?
Liz___nodes[14]._optionData[4]._rawText: I think I’m good for now.
Liz___nodes[15]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: I’m so glad you asked sugar!
Liz___nodes[15]._stepChain[1]._dialogPag: {1} takes a deep draft from her bottle.
Liz___nodes[15]._stepChain[3]._dialogPag: Ahhh~! First you have to understand the difference between beer and starshine, the process is ALMOST exactly the same, but starshine is distilled, whereas beer isn’t.

It all starts with the mash, those are really any grains that have been soaked in water and have been tricked into thinking it’s time to grow. The grains will start to produce sugars, and once they’re at that stage, I add in the yeast and extra sugar.
Then I let it ferment, this step is just a whole lot of waitin’ around, and occasionally stirring. After that, it’s time to distill!
Personally, I use the copper still that’s been passed down in my family for generations.
Liz___nodes[15]._stepChain[5]._dialogPag: {1} gestures to the copper apparatus nearby, steam hissing from the pressure valve, before she takes another log swig from her bottle.
Liz___nodes[15]._stepChain[7]._dialogPag: That’s a distiller?! I thought it was a boiler.
Liz___nodes[15]._stepChain[8]._dialogPag: Yep! It can produce up to 200 gallons of starshine when I make a full batch. But I like working in smaller batches.
Liz___nodes[15]._stepChain[11]._dialogPa: Distilling is the most important part, it’s what turns the fermented mash into the spirit we all know and love. It’s the most dangerous part of the process, because the still itself becomes too hot to touch, so I have to be very careful with the heat, otherwise it’ll ruin the batch.
When it’s ready, I’ll pull on the release, and the shine will pour out from the exit valve into whatever container I have on-hand.

The first percentage of starshine always comes out as toxic, if you drink it, you’re liable to go starblind. It’s best to throw that out, but you can also use it to strip paint down, or to put some shine on metal. The rest of it is safe to drink, save for the dregs, which is the last percentage of the shine.
The dregs are still useful though, they can be cycled back into the next batch for more impact, or you can use the dregs as fuel.
And that’s it! If you want to know more, well…
Liz___nodes[15]._stepChain[13]._dialogPa: She smirks and takes a slow swig from her bottle.
Liz___nodes[15]._stepChain[14]._dialogPa: You’d have to marry into the family sugar~
Liz___nodes[15]._stepChain[18]._dialogPa: I’m just kiddin’ sugar, these kinda things ARE trade secrets though. So I can’t tell just anyone about them!
So, anything else you’d like to know?
Liz___nodes[16]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: It’s a very unfortunate condition that comes around if you drink the toxic liquid that comes out of starshine. It was much more common before we figured out exactly how much of the first percentage we had to get rid of.
Liz___nodes[16]._stepChain[1]._dialogPag: {1} swirls the liquid in her bottle before taking another drink.
Liz___nodes[16]._stepChain[2]._dialogPag: When you go starblind, you’ll lose all your night vision, you won’t be able to see, even in dimly lit places.

It gets its name, partially from the starshine itself, but there is also a physical abnormality. After drinking the toxic part, your eyes will go completely black with white spots that just happen to look like the night sky.
The condition is much more rare now, but every now and then someone tries to make starshine on their own and suffers the consequences.
Liz___nodes[16]._stepChain[4]._dialogPag: So, what else would you like to know?
Liz___nodes[17]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: That’s an excellent question, sugar! You see, that comes down to the mash. The basic starshine is made with grains, but really anything that produces sugar will do.
So the other flavors come from changing up what I use as the mash. If I don’t want grains as the mash, I’ll cook up somethin’ else, and mash it until it’s a paste!
All I do then is toss it in the pot, and do the same as I always do when makin’ shine!
So, anything else you’d like to know?
Liz___nodes[18]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: I sure could sugar! The production of starshine has remained almost exclusive to Brownies ever since starshine had been conceived. It was thanks to my ancestors that the first brews were made.
However it was still dangerous. We didn’t... we didn't know… about…
Liz___nodes[18]._stepChain[1]._dialogPag: She opens her mouth wide, covering it as she yawns, taking a swig from her bottle.
Liz___nodes[18]._stepChain[3]._dialogPag: We didn’t know about how toxic it was originally, and the first sales came with starblindness running rampant among our customers.

So starshine was made illegal in the Kingdom, and while on the surface we obeyed their laws, underneath... Well, there was a lot of money to be made smuggling the stuff in. We could get ten times the price on the black market as opposed to doing normal trade.
For a while we operated in secret. Of course we could still make the stuff and distribute it amongst ourselves, but the money was in getting it to the taverns that included it in their secret menus.
This came with a certain amount of danger, and for a time, my family couldn’t associate with Fi-Star because of their operations.
Liz___nodes[18]._stepChain[5]._dialogPag: That’s a lot your family went through to keep their business going. Couldn’t they have just gone to selling wines or ales?
Liz___nodes[18]._stepChain[6]._dialogPag: It was long before my time, sugar. I can’t blame my kin for keeping food on the table anyway they could.
Liz___nodes[18]._stepChain[9]._dialogPag: I guess that’s true, sorry for assuming.
Liz___nodes[18]._stepChain[10]._dialogPa: {1} waves you off to show no harm done. Swirling the liquid in the bottle, she takes another deep pull of it, letting out a satisfied sigh.
Liz___nodes[18]._stepChain[11]._dialogPa: There were elements in my family though, that wanted us to go legitimate, which led to a feud. Eventually my great-great-great-somethingth grandmother figured out how much starshine to toss so it wouldn’t be toxic.
Liz___nodes[18]._stepChain[12]._dialogPa: Your... ancestor? ...figured out how to make starshine safe?
Liz___nodes[18]._stepChain[13]._dialogPa: Yep! Starshining runs deep through these veins, and not just because I’m drinking it!
Anyway, once we could prove the reliability in the process, we got the needed licenses, and became the only qualified sellers of starshine in the Kingdom!
So, anything else you’d like to know?
Liz___nodes[19]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: Sure thing, sugar. Anything else I can help ya with?
Liz___nodes[20]._stepChain[1]._dialogPag: I’m interested in a private tasting. If you catch my meaning.
Liz___nodes[20]._stepChain[3]._dialogPag: Well sugar, you’re clearly a connoisseur when it comes to starshine, so let’s go to the communal tent and I pull out some select stock~?
Liz___nodes[22]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: {1} playfully wiggles her hips, causing the rest of her to almost dance in front of your eyes. She sets off for the tent, and you’re not too far behind.
Liz___nodes[22]._stepChain[4].<Tool_Desc: Move camera to setup tent scene
Liz___nodes[23]._stepChain[2]._dialogPag: Well that’s no problem at all sugar, you know where to find me when you want to have a go~
Liz___nodes[24]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: No problem, sugar, you know where to find me!
BrownieCaravanMarineSleeping__line_6: r- Marine := Zzz What great big jugs… of starshine you’ve got… -SNORE-
BrownieCaravanMarineSleeping__line_7: r- Marine := Mnhh, go away… Can’t ya see- you’re drunk! -hic-
BrownieCaravanMarineSleeping__line_8: r- Marine := -SNORE- Mnnh… No Sieger, I wasn’t drink… Mnhh, you’re going to… punish me..? -SNRK-

BrownieCaravanMinerva__line_4: r- Minerva := My my, Madame Ocan has quite a way with words. Perhaps there really is merit to experiencing the oral tradition over novels after all.
BrownieCaravanMinerva__line_5: r- Minerva := This tradition of ‘Brownie tales’ they perform is quite charming. It is quite different from the tradition of sagas in the high sky.
BrownieCaravanMinerva__line_6: r- Minerva := I do wish these boisterous crowds nearby would settle down. Some of us are trying to enjoy less licentious forms of entertainment!
BrownieCaravanMinerva__line_7: r- Minerva := Ah, <PLAYERNAME>! You would not believe the breadth of novels and tomes Madame Ocan has in her company. While I commend her collection, I do wish she would keep them more organized…
BrownieCaravanMinerva__line_8: r- Minerva := Many of Madame Ocan’s fables have quite rewarding messages. Perhaps I should bring Tabbitha down here to listen to them?
Ocan___nodes[1]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: You walk up to a mature looking Brownie standing on a carpet. Her half-rimmed glasses balanced on the edge of her button nose, while she reads through a book braced on her hip. Surrounding her reposed figure are stacks of books, some as tall as a human while others are as short as a brownie.
Ocan___nodes[1]._stepChain[4]._dialogPag: She looks up from her book, placing it down on the floor.
Ocan___nodes[1]._stepChain[5]._dialogPag: Oh, dear me. Here I was, not taking notice of you. How rude of me, when so many events in your life have surely steered you along many currents. That is, until you’ve found yourself here, in front of me. You have my apologies.
Ocan___nodes[1]._stepChain[7]._dialogPag: Tell me, by what name do you go?
Ocan___nodes[1]._stepChain[10]._dialogPa: {0}, That is a good name. I think it suits you quite perfectly. It reminds me of how suitable Lazy Semtie’s name was, though I imagine that you are not at all lazy if you are a member of The Union.
Ocan___nodes[1]._stepChain[12]._dialogPa: You wonder what she’s talking about. Someone she knows perhaps?
Ocan___nodes[1]._stepChain[14]._dialogPa: What I was meaning to say, was that his name suited him rather well too. For you see, Lazy Semtie they say was an incredibly lazy Brownie.

He would spend all of the winter bundled up near the furnace, all of spring reclined about the home, all of his summers lazing out on the deck of his Cloudskipper home, and all of fall feasting at his kitchen table.

As a matter of fact, it was on a day such as this that he was lazing out on the deck, when his mother came to him and said, ‘Lazy Semtie, won’t you please go and fetch water for your poor mother, so that she may wash your clothes?’
Ocan___nodes[1]._stepChain[15]._dialogPa: You notice that her tone changes while she imitates a motherly tone. Is she…?
Ocan___nodes[2]._stepChain[2]._dialogPag: Ocan notices you from over the rim of her glasses, and looks up with a warm smile.
Ocan___nodes[2]._stepChain[3]._dialogPag: Ah, back again, dearie? Is there anything that I can do for you?
Ocan___nodes[3]._rawContextHeader: What can Ocan do for you?
Ocan___nodes[3]._optionData[0]._rawText: I was hoping you had a story or two…?
Ocan___nodes[3]._optionData[1]._rawText: Maybe you’d like to… make a story of our own?
Ocan___nodes[3]._optionData[2]._rawText: I think that’s all for now.
Ocan___nodes[5]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: I was hoping that you had a story or two that you might like to tell, or tell again if I’ve heard it!
Ocan___nodes[5]._stepChain[1]._dialogPag: Ocan smiles but shakes her head.
Ocan___nodes[5]._stepChain[2]._dialogPag: Hold on a moment, Dearie. You already heard one of my stories today. My jaw’s liable to get tired if I don’t rest it. Why don’t you ask for a story another time?
Ocan___nodes[5]._stepChain[3]._dialogPag: Okay, I understand. Another time then.
Ocan___nodes[6]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: I was hoping that you had a story or two that you might like to tell, or tell again if I’ve heard it!
Ocan___nodes[6]._stepChain[2]._dialogPag: Ocan smiles and beckons you closer.
Ocan___nodes[6]._stepChain[3]._dialogPag: For you, I think I have just the tale to tell. Come on over here and take a seat, dearie. Now, what would you like to hear?
Ocan___nodes[7]._rawContextHeader: What story would you like to hear?
Ocan___nodes[7]._optionData[3]._rawText: The Earnest Daughter and The Lousy Daughter
Ocan___nodes[8]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: On second thought, I don’t know if I’m ready for a story right now. Maybe another time!
Ocan___nodes[8]._stepChain[1]._dialogPag: Well that’s quite alright, Dearie! Is there anything else I can help you with?
Ocan___nodes[9]._stepChain[0]._dialogPag: Thank you so much for the storytime, Ocan. It was something!
Ocan___nodes[9]._stepChain[1]._dialogPag: Of course, dearie. I hope that you enjoyed it.
Ocan___nodes[11]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: I think that’s all for now.
Ocan___nodes[11]._stepChain[2]._dialogPa: Well, come back again soon, dearie. I’ll always have more for an inquisitive mind like yours.
Ocan___nodes[11]._stepChain[5]._dialogPa: Stay safe, dearie, and come back soon!
Ocan___nodes[12]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: It was great! It reminded me of when I was a little kid, reading books under the covers at night.
Ocan___nodes[12]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: Ohuhu! I’m glad you enjoyed it! Now, is there anything else that Ocan, the voice, can do for you?
Ocan___nodes[13]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: It was okay. It was really interesting to hear folklore I never heard when I was younger.
Ocan___nodes[13]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: I see. I understand what you mean. Next time you come, I will have plenty more stories to tell. You’ll love all of them, I’m sure. Is there anything else I can do for you, dearie?
Ocan___nodes[14]._rawContextHeader: Invite Ocan to let off some steam?
Ocan___nodes[15]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Maybe you’d like to… make a story of our own?
Ocan___nodes[15]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: Ocan seems to catch your meaning, and blushes.
Ocan___nodes[15]._stepChain[4]._dialogPa: My my, you seem so eager. How can I possibly say no.
Ocan___nodes[15]._stepChain[5]._dialogPa: She looks to everyone else nearby.
Ocan___nodes[15]._stepChain[7]._dialogPa: Excuse me just a moment everyone. {0} is a dear, and has offered to… assist me with an important body of literature. I’ll be back in just a moment.
Ocan___nodes[15]._stepChain[8]._dialogPa: She puts down her book on a nearby stack, before turning back to you.
Ocan___nodes[15]._stepChain[9]._dialogPa: Come along, Dearie. You can help me right over there.
Ocan___nodes[17]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Wait, just so I know, are you telling a story?
Ocan___nodes[17]._stepChain[2]._dialogPa: The brownie woman looks confused for a moment, and then laughs lightly.
Ocan___nodes[17]._stepChain[4]._dialogPa: Ohuhu! Dear me, I was beginning to prattle on. Are you not a fan of stories?
Ocan___nodes[17]._stepChain[6]._dialogPa: She looks at you inquisitively from over the rim of her glasses. Her expression suddenly serious.
Ocan___nodes[17]._stepChain[7]._dialogPa: Oh, I love stories! I actually read quite a bit, ever since I was a child.
Ocan___nodes[17]._stepChain[10]._dialogP: Oh! that’s good to hear then! You’ll have to remind me to tell more of that story. Perhaps when we’re not in the middle of introductions.
Ocan___nodes[18]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: The woman embellished her speech with practiced movements of her hands, and voices for each person. It becomes pretty clear that she’s telling a story of some kind.
Ocan___nodes[18]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Well, Lazy Semtie lifted his head, looked up at the green warts providing him shade and said, ‘Well, I would happily, mother, only I am very lazy,’ he said as he yawned.
Ocan___nodes[18]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: Well his mother kept pestering him, telling him ‘What would the rest of the caravan and kingdom think of me if they saw you walking around in dirty shirts and trousers?’ Eventually, Lazy Semtie walked down to the nearby spring with a jug in hand, and filled it with some water.
Ocan___nodes[18]._stepChain[6]._dialogPa: Well, as he returned home, he heard a voice from the jug saying, ‘Help, I am but a plankton, please let me out and I shall do you a good deed in return.’ And…
Ocan___nodes[18]._stepChain[7]._dialogPa: Ocan looks at you suddenly, stopping her tale.
Ocan___nodes[18]._stepChain[8]._dialogPa: Wait just a moment. I’m getting sidetracked aren’t I? And you, you were just going to sit there and listen to me prattle on?
Ocan___nodes[18]._stepChain[10]._dialogP: It sounded like a really interesting story, I wanted to know where it went. Sorry about that!
Ocan___nodes[18]._stepChain[13]._dialogP: Ohuhu! Not to worry dearie, there’s no harm in wanting to listen. It’s a fun little tale isn’t it? There isn’t much left of a moral to be learned, but it’s a good laugh.
Ocan___nodes[18]._stepChain[15]._dialogP: I’d love to hear the rest.
Ocan___nodes[18]._stepChain[17]._dialogP: Perhaps another time, when we’ve gotten introductions out of the way.
Ocan___nodes[19]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Now, I believe that you called yourself… Lazy, {0}ie?
Ocan___nodes[19]._stepChain[2]._dialogPa: N-not quite! My name is {0}, I’m a Frontiersman for The Union.
Ocan___nodes[19]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: Oh, then why did I bring up Lazy Semtie… Oh yes, the name suited you like his did, how forgetful of me!
Ocan___nodes[19]._stepChain[4]._dialogPa: Nothing to worry about. No harm done.
Ocan___nodes[19]._stepChain[5]._dialogPa: The elder brownie gives a slow, polite bow.
Ocan___nodes[19]._stepChain[6]._dialogPa: Well, you may call me Ocan, the voice. I am the elder of this caravan, and its storyteller by trade.
Ocan___nodes[19]._stepChain[7]._dialogPa: It’s an honor to meet you then, Ocan the Voice.
Ocan___nodes[19]._stepChain[9]._dialogPa: Ocan laughs and gestures towards you.
Ocan___nodes[19]._stepChain[10]._dialogP: There’s no need to be so formal, dearie. It’s just a fancy title all the old storytellers demand to be called..
Ocan___nodes[20]._optionData[0]._rawText: I wouldn’t call you an old woman, there’s nicer names
Ocan___nodes[20]._optionData[1]._rawText: I was taught to respect my elders.
Ocan___nodes[21]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: I wouldn’t call you an old woman, there’s nicer names for a woman like you.
Ocan___nodes[21]._stepChain[4]._dialogPa: Please, don’t make this old woman blush. You are a strapping young{0| man| woman|ster}, but we hardly know one another!
Ocan___nodes[22]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Well, even if that’s so, I was taught to respect my elders.
Ocan___nodes[22]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: Well, you have better manners than I’m used to, your parents must have raised you well!
Ocan___nodes[23]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Titles aside, you can see I’m quite the collector of books.
Ocan___nodes[23]._stepChain[2]._dialogPa: It’s quite the collection. What do you collect?
Ocan___nodes[23]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: A bit of this, and a dash of that. But stories are my greatest pleasure.
Ocan___nodes[23]._stepChain[5]._dialogPa: I can tell, you were telling that other one so well!
Ocan___nodes[23]._stepChain[7]._dialogPa: As you can see, I’m quite the storyteller. There’s a fountain of tales, aesops, folklore and more stored up in these books and this noggin. For a generous tip, I’ll gladly tell a few to you.
Ocan___nodes[23]._stepChain[8]._dialogPa: That would be fantastic. Thank you for the information!
Ocan___nodes[23]._stepChain[10]._dialogP: Ocan gives you a friendly wave and a warm smile.
Ocan___nodes[23]._stepChain[11]._dialogP: Of course, of course. Now, there’s plenty of things to see and buy today from our caravan, but you're always welcome to hear a story when you pay me a visit, you hear?
Ocan___nodes[23]._stepChain[12]._dialogP: You nod appreciatively as she turns her attention back to her book for a moment. Her fingers already finding the page she stopped on.
Ocan___nodes[24]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: So, you want to hear the tale of Lazy Semtie? It’s a rather old tale, but it’s still an interesting one.
Ocan___nodes[24]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Yeah! It’s that tale you started telling me. I really wanted to know where it went.
Ocan___nodes[24]._stepChain[2]._dialogPa: Well, let’s see, it’s been a while since I told it in full, but I think it starts like this.
Ocan___nodes[24]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: Ocan steadies herself as she slowly moves to narrate the story.
Ocan___nodes[24]._stepChain[6]._dialogPa: Once upon a time, there was a poor brownie woman, with hardly even a few coins to her name. She worked hard every day, for you see, her husband had died several years prior, and all she had in the world was an incredibly lazy son.

He was so lazy, that in the winter, he would stay beside the hearth, in the spring he would recline in the home, in the summer, he would laze out about the yard below their cloudskipper, and in the fall he wouldn’t budge an inch from their kitchen table.
In fact, he was so lazy that all the other brownies, and all the villages they traded with took to calling him, Lazy Semtie.
Ocan___nodes[24]._stepChain[7]._dialogPa: So, does he learn not to be so lazy?
Ocan___nodes[24]._stepChain[8]._dialogPa: Well… patience, dearie. Now where was I…

One day, while Lazy Semtie was resting under the shade of their cloudskipper’s warts. His mother came up to him and said, “Dear son, please take this jug and walk down to the spring nearby. I need you to fetch me a jug of water, so that I may wash your dirty shirts and trousers?”
Lazy Semtie simply lifted his head to the side said, “Oh, I would gladly go down to the spring for you mother, if only I were not so very lazy.”
Ocan___nodes[24]._stepChain[9]._dialogPa: Ocan puts two hands to the side of her head to pretend to sleep.
Ocan___nodes[24]._stepChain[11]._dialogP: Isn’t it? However, the poor woman continued to plead with her son, “Come my son, if you walk down to the spring, won’t you bring me back some water to wash with? What would the world say of me if they saw you walking around in filthy clothes?”
The woman continued to pester her son, until finally, Lazy Semtie grabbed a jug, and made his way down to the nearby spring, where he then filled it to the brim with water.

Lazy Semtie had just begun his walk back from the spring, when he heard a voice speaking up from inside of the jug. “Please, oh please,” said the voice. “I am but a small and tiny plankton who fell into the spring, won’t you please help me and put me back on the branches of the trees near the spring?”
Lazy Semtie yawned as he continued walking, “I would gladly place you back on the trees by the spring, tiny plankton, if it were not for the fact that I am very lazy.”
However, the small plankton continued to beg and plead with him, and eventually the boy  walked back down to the spring, and placed the small plankton high up on a nearby branch.

The plankton shook the water from its body and said, “Thank you very much. You have done me a kind deed, and so, there is ever anything that you require, simply say ‘Plankton, plankton, tiny plankton. Come and grant my inclination,’ and I shall grant whatever it is.”
Ocan___nodes[25]._rawContextHeader: Thoughts on a wish granting plankton.
Ocan___nodes[25]._optionData[0]._rawText: So, he just gets a wish granting plankton?
Ocan___nodes[25]._optionData[1]._rawText: I wish I had a wish granting plankton.
Ocan___nodes[26]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: I wish I had a wish granting plankton.
Ocan___nodes[26]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Well, when you get one, you can wish for it.
Ocan___nodes[26]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: Ohuhu! Sorry, I say that to all the younglings that say the same thing, they usually don’t realize so quick.
Ocan___nodes[27]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: So, he just gets a wish granting plankton?
Ocan___nodes[27]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: Mhmm. Now shhh, dearie, the story has barely started.
Ocan___nodes[28]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: With his good deed done, Lazy Semtie walked back home with the jug of water, and gave it to his mother to do her washing.

No sooner had he finished his task, however, did the boy place his hand on his belly and say, “Oh mother, I am very hungry.” His mother looked to him and said, “If you are hungry, then go somewhere where you might find some fruit, or berries.”
Well, that Lazy Semtie was just about to protest, when he remembered what the Plankton by the spring had told him, and said.

“Plankton, plankton, tiny plankton. come and grant my inclination. A table wide and laden heavy, filled with food for making merry.” No sooner had he said it, when the table in front of the two of them was full with food, and Lazy Semtie and his mother ate their fill until they could hardly stand back up.
Ocan___nodes[28]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Oh, so he makes his life better with his good deed. I think I see where this is going.
Ocan___nodes[28]._stepChain[2]._dialogPa: The old woman then went to work, washing clothes in the yard, while Lazy Semtie laid in a cool spot below their Cloudskipper.
Ocan___nodes[28]._stepChain[4]._dialogPa: Well, he is lazy, dearie. Anyway, suddenly, the local governor's daughter came walking by, and saw him lazing in the yard. “Ah, Lazy Semtie,” she said to him.

“You have time to be lazy, when other people are hard at work?” This made Lazy Semtie very cross, and so he said, “Plankton, plankton, tiny plankton, come and grant my inclination. Come make this girl quiet and hid, pregnant with a little kid.” You won’t believe this, but a miracle occured then, a wonder to all of us.
The governor's daughter became pregnant. Her belly began to swell, and nine months later, she had given birth to a beautiful baby boy.
Ocan___nodes[30]._stepChain[2]._dialogPa: You know, now that I’m remembering it, it does escalate quickly, doesn’t it?
Ocan___nodes[30]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: I definitely didn’t expect him to wish she was pregnant.
Ocan___nodes[30]._stepChain[4]._dialogPa: Well, it does work out alright in the end, just wait and see.
Ocan___nodes[31]._stepChain[2]._dialogPa: Ocan looks at you from over the rim of her glasses with a long, pained stare.
Ocan___nodes[32]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: The child was beautiful and bright eyed, but it was to no avail, for her father, the governor, had become inconsolable, and demanded to know who the father was. The girl could not tell him how she had been with child though, for she swore she had never once spent a night with a man.

The governor's advisors then proposed to him an idea, “If you wish to find out who the father is, then you should give the boy a bunch of gempas, and bring every man in the village to him. The boy will surely hand over the gempas to his father.”

Ocan___nodes[32]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: The governor thought this plan was sound, and sent out a message for all the men in the village and farms to come to his manor. First came the marines, and then the craftsmen, and then all the other men. One by one they came before the boy, but the boy did not give the gempas to any of them.

The governor was furious, for his advisor’s plan did not work, but his advisor’s insisted that it was not their plan that had failed, but that he had clearly failed to summon every lad from the village. So, an accounting took place, and sure enough, one man had not come at the behest of the governor.
And, do you know who that was? Lazy Semtie.

So, the governor sent his guards out to find him, but it took a day and a half for Lazy Semtie to go, for he had been asleep in the attic of his cloudskipper, and it was quite a task for the governor’s guards to wake him up. Ohuhu!

Well, when Lazy Semtie was brought to the governor’s manor, he was quickly pushed in through the doors to the main hall. No sooner had he stepped in, the child immediately walked over to him, and hand the gempas over to Lazy Semtie. Everything was then clear to everyone, Lazy Semtie was the father of the Governor's Daughter’s little boy.

Ocan___nodes[32]._stepChain[6]._dialogPa: Humans and brownies can have children together, dearie. Though it is uncommon. Even if that was the strange part, did you forget that a magic plankton was the one who did it?
Ocan___nodes[32]._stepChain[8]._dialogPa: The governor however, had been hoping that the child’s father would be a brave marine, or a handsome prince, and was not at all happy with this turn of events.

He shouted at his daughter, “You have embarrassed me, and so you will be banished!” So the governor had his daughter, his grandson, and Lazy Semtie sealed inside of a barrel, and thrown into the nearby river.

Ocan___nodes[32]._stepChain[13]._dialogP: The barrel flowed down the river, and inside of the barrel, the governor’s daughter sobbed and moaned at what had become of her.

However, she soon came to see that even though Lazy Semtie was an incredibly lazy man, was a good and honest one. She turned to Lazy Semtie, and said, “Lazy Semtie, please, this barrel is much too small for the three of us I wish to have a manor like the one I have grown accustomed to. Please do something to get us out of here.”

Lazy Semtie yawned and said, “Oh, I would gladly do something about it, but I am simply too lazy.” But she continued to beg and beseech him, and Lazy Semtie soon became tired of listening. And so, Lazy Semtie said, “Plankton, plankton, tiny Plankton, come and grant my inclination. A manor of splendor is what I wish rendered.”

You won’t believe this, but no sooner had he finished his wish, did the river toss the barrel out and onto the nearby bank. And Lazy Semtie, his son, and the governor's daughter found themselves in front of a wonderful and beautiful manor.
Ocan___nodes[32]._stepChain[15]._dialogP: “Oh, how wonderful,” said the governor’s daughter, “This must be our manor.” It was in fact, their manor, all thanks to the tiny plankton, and his magic. They made it their home, and lived happily ever after. The end.

Ocan___nodes[37]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: Soup from a Sword is a classic tale, one that I’ve told countless times. I think you’ll enjoy this quite a bit.
Ocan___nodes[37]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Ocan performs a short, sitting bow as she begins her story.
Ocan___nodes[37]._stepChain[4]._dialogPa: Once upon a time, on an island not far from here, there was a marine who was traveling home from the war. His journey had been long, and had made him very tired and very hungry. However, there was still a great deal of distance left before he would reach home, and the poor marine did not have any food to feed his hungry belly.
Eventually, his hunger grew so terrible, that the poor marine thought he could walk no further.
Ocan___nodes[37]._stepChain[5]._dialogPa: Oh wow, so he was a member of the union?
Ocan___nodes[37]._stepChain[7]._dialogPa: Sometimes he is marine, sometimes soldier, sometimes brownie, but that is not the important part of the tale, dearie.
Ocan___nodes[37]._stepChain[9]._dialogPa: Now, just when he had begun to give up hope, he came across a small village on a small island. It had many houses tucked into the crook of the island’s hillside, and it’s chimneys were already smoking with their own evening suppers.

The marine leapt for joy, for he was certain that someone would spare him a morsel to eat. He quickly walked up to the first house on the small dirt road, and knocked on their door. “Excuse me,” he said, “I am a marine who is returning home from the war, and I am very hungry, might you have something to spare?”

You would think they would help a poor marine, but, the door did not open. Worse still, no one had bothered to even come to the window to see who had knocked, so the straightened his hat, and walked to the next house on the road.
His attempt was in vain once more, however, as when he knocked upon the door to the next house, not single occupant bothered to come to the door for him.
“Please, I am a marine who is returning home from the war, and I am very hungry, might you have something to spare?” He asked, but even if they had heard him they had chosen not to come to the door.

The marine tried house after house on the road through the village, but the results were the same every time. Finally, the marine, even more tired, and even more hungry, began to walk out of the village.
Ocan___nodes[38]._optionData[1]._rawText: Maybe they didn’t have food to spare?
Ocan___nodes[39]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: That’s terrible! Did he really try every house and no one answered?
Ocan___nodes[39]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: Almost every house, for you see…
Ocan___nodes[40]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Maybe they didn’t have food to spare. This was during a war, wasn’t it?
Ocan___nodes[40]._stepChain[2]._dialogPa: That could perhaps be one explanation why.
Ocan___nodes[40]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: Maybe if he had something to trade?
Ocan___nodes[40]._stepChain[4]._dialogPa: Funny you should say that, for you see…
Ocan___nodes[41]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: When the marine came upon the last house on the road through the village, the marine marveled at it’s yard. Vegetables and fruit grew on stalks and vines all over the garden, and plenty of plankton fluttered about the yard.

“Surely the person who lives here may have a bit of food to spare to a poor marine,” he said to himself, and walked up to the door and knocked. “Excuse me,” he said, “I am a marine who is returning home from the war, and I am very hungry, might you have something to spare?”

The marine waited, and to his surprise and excitement, the door opened up, revealing a wizened old woman and her one eyed catgirl. The old woman barked at him, “Why have you come to bother an old woman? I do not care if you were a marine, or a tailor, or a king, I haven’t anything to spare to travelers."
"Not today, not tomorrow, not ever!” And with that, she slammed the door, and left the hungry marine standing outside in the twilight.
Ocan___nodes[41]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: What? She couldn’t even give him a mado to roast, or a speedweel?
Ocan___nodes[41]._stepChain[2]._dialogPa: Of course not, she is a wicked old lady, unlike myself. Now, you can imagine his disappointment.

The marine, dismayed, sat down for a long moment to rest. “Oh, what am I to do? I am very hungry, and I do not know if I will make it to the next village without a morsel to eat,” he said to himself. Just then, though, the marine got a clever idea, and stood up. Walking up to the door, he knocked again, and waited for the old woman.

“Who is there, can you not see you are bothering an old woman? Oh, it is you again, the marine asking for food,” the old woman said as she tapped her cane. “I am a marine, but I am not asking for food,” the marine told her as he reached for his sword.

“I was only hoping that you might let me borrow your cauldron, so that I can make some soup from my sword,” he said as he pulled his sword from his belt. The old woman looked at him strangely, “You can make soup from a sword?” The marine replied, “Oh yes, it’s quite a delicious soup too.”
Ocan___nodes[41]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: How in the world could you do that?
Ocan___nodes[41]._stepChain[5]._dialogPa: Well, much like yourself, the old woman was plenty confused and more than a little curious. She invited the marine into her home as she walked over to the cook fire and thought, “Soup from a sword, what a thought.” She pulled up one of her cauldrons, and placed it over the fire.

The Marine took his sword, and placed it inside the cauldron, and then drew water from a nearby well to fill the pot up. Soon, the water began to steam, and the woman looked over his shoulder while he stirred. “Is that all that it takes to make soup from a sword?” The woman asked. “Oh yes, that’s all,” the marine said in return.
Ocan___nodes[43]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: I’m no chef, but I don’t know how you could make soup from a sword. A boot maybe, but a sword?
Ocan___nodes[43]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: Things are not always as they first seem, dearie.
Ocan___nodes[44]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: What’s his trick? I know he’s not going to really make it from a sword, but he’s not just taking food either?
Ocan___nodes[44]._stepChain[1]._dialogPa: He may be a marine grunt, but he was a clever one, listen.
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: Then the marine stirred the soup, and soon tasted it with a wooden spoon and said, “But, it could use just a bit of salt to season it.” The woman looked over to the man and said, “I have a bit of salt in my cupboard!” She ran over to her cupboard, to get it muttering, “Soup from a sword, what a thought indeed!”

Well, she soon brought back the salt, and the marine added a few pinches of it to the water before stirring. “Is it done now?” The woman asked. “It will be, in just a little while,” The marine said as he stirred the pot. “But you know, soup from a sword isn’t really complete without a few mados,” the soldier said as he looked at the old woman.

The old woman snapped her fingers, and headed towards the cellar door, nearly tripping over her catgirl in the process. “I have a few mados in my cellar, those should do,” she said, and as she headed downstairs she pondered, “Soup from a sword, what a thought.”
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[2]._dialogPa: The old woman soon came back up with a few mados, and the two of them cut the mados up before putting them into the pot. “Is the soup almost ready?” the woman asked while the marine tasted it. He took another sip from the wooden spoon, “It’s just about done, but soup from a sword tastes best with a few chwons and bofroot.”

The woman lit up at that, “I have some chwons and bofroot growing in my garden right now.” She pointed to her catgirl and told her to fetch them from the garden, and she dashed off in a hurry quick as could be while the old woman palmed the head of her cane. “Soup from a sword, what a thought,” she said to herself.
Soon the catgirl returned with the chwons and bofroots laden in her arms, and the three of them set to work to cut them up and drop them into the soup.
Again, the marine tasted the soup, and again, the old woman asked if it was nearly ready. “It is almost done,” said the marine, “but it needs to thicken up a bit more before it is really ready.”
The old woman walked over to the cupboard, and took out a sack of hotton flour handing it over to the marine. “This should do the trick,” she said, “A bit of hotton flour will thicken it up nicely.”
The marine took the bag and measured out a bit of flour for the soup before stirring it in. “Soup from a sword, what a thought,” the old woman said as she sat down and waited for the soup to simmer.
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[4]._dialogPa: The three of them sat for a while, and after a time had passed, the marine took another sip from the cauldron. “Is it done? the old woman asked.

“Yes, the soup is now done, now we may eat,” he said as he brought the cauldron over to the table. The old woman fetched the bowls, and her catgirl fetched the spoons, and soon the three of them began to eat the delicious soup.
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[5]._dialogPa: Oh, they all ate it, that’s kind of nice.
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[6]._dialogPa: Well, it would be rude to borrow someone’s kitchen and not give them some of the food you cooked, Ohuhu! That sword soup was so good, that old woman ate down three bowls.

“This soup is delicious,” the old woman said as she finished her last bowl. “Yes, it is very filling,” the marine said, and the three of them ate and ate until at last the morning light came in through the windows.
The marine, standing up from the table with great difficulty, looked into the pot and saw that all that was left was his sword, and after washing it clean began to put it away.

“Wait,” the old woman cried as she stopped him. “I have never had soup from a sword before, and now I am afraid that I shall never have it again. Would you be willing to part with your sword, if you are indeed returning home from the war?”
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[7]._dialogPa: Does he give her the sword?

The marine thought long and hard, and told her that he couldn’t possibly part with it so easily, when it had helped him so much. “Please, I will give you two sacks of koronas,” the old woman begged, sending her catgirl off to fetch them. The marine considered her offer and said, “Alright, for two sacks of koronas, I will part with my sword for you.”
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[9]._dialogPa: Wow, how come the old lady let him have two sacks?
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[10]._dialogP: Now, once the trade was complete, the marine thanked the old woman, and headed back out to the road with his two bags of koronas.
The old woman saw him off, and as the marine rounded the bend he chuckled to himself and thought, “Soup from a sword, what a thought.” And that is where my tale ends.
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[11]._dialogP: Ocan finishes the tale, and gives a short, sitting bow.
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[12]._dialogP: Wow, he really tricked that old woman.
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[13]._dialogP: Ohuhu! Well, you can say she deserved it. It is a fun tale to tell, thank you for requesting it, dearie.
Ocan___nodes[45]._stepChain[14]._dialogP: That definitely lives up to the title of classic, thank you!
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[0]._dialogPa: Ocan sits back a bit, clearly getting comfortable.
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[3]._dialogPa: Ahh, this is a long one, but perhaps one of my favorites. It’s right up there with ‘Soup from a Sword,” on my list. Get yourself comfortable Dearie.

Now let us see… Once upon a time, in a kingdom far far away, there lived an elderly king who had three sons. His sons were all strong young men, but the king was now entering his twilight years, he had become so old that he could not even tour the countryside of his island anymore.
So, the king had a tall tower built in the middle of his castle grounds, so that he could be carried up to the top and look down upon his kingdom.
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[4]._dialogPa: Why didn’t he just hand the kingdom over to one of his sons?
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[5]._dialogPa: Well, perhaps he did not think any of them were ready for it yet, perhaps one of them shall be by the end of this tale, hm?
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[6]._dialogPa: Ocan chuckles, knowing something about the story you do not.
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[7]._dialogPa: However, on the day that it was completed, a beautiful singing fish poised itself on the pinnacle of his new tower. The fish’s son was magical, and when the king heard it’s melody he soon became a man half his age.

The king could not believe that such a magical fish existed, and asked for his sons to retrieve it for him. He begged, “Please my sons, fetch me that singing fish, that I may feel young tomorrow and every day thereafter.”
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[8]._dialogPa: Wow, if I heard one of those fish, I’d be back in school again…
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[9]._dialogPa: Well, the eldest son claimed that he would be the one to catch the fish. He climbed up the pinnacle of the tower, but when he reached for the fish, it swam away through the sky.

The eldest son watched as it swam into a forest on the western side of the island, and so when he returned to the throne room he said to his father, “Do not worry father, for I will go and retrieve the bird for you.” And so, the eldest son left the castle with his trusty pigmy wolf, and his riding carp.
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[10]._dialogP: Wait, they had pet pigmy wolves?
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[12]._dialogP: Depends on where the story is being told, sometimes they’re more like a companion or a friend.
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[14]._dialogP: When the eldest son reached the forest, he stopped and made camp for the night and began to eat his dinner. Just as he had begun eating, a catboy entered into the clearing. The eldest son feared that the catboy would try and catch the fish nesting in the forest and so he shouted to his pigmy wolf, “Go and chase that cat away!”

But the catboy suddenly pointed at the eldest son and his two animals and said, “Become as trees.” Before anymore could be said, the eldest son, his pigmy wolf, and his riding carp all turned to trees, fading into the forest.
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[15]._dialogP: Woah! How did a catboy do that?
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[16]._dialogP: Ocan taps you on the head.
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[17]._dialogP: Are you going to let me tell my story? We have barely begun and you are asking so many questions.
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[21]._dialogP: Ocan straightens her hair and finds her inner calm once more.
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[23]._dialogP: Now, you will find out before long, dearie.
When the eldest son did not return that night, the king grew old once more. However, when the sun began to rise, the singing fish returned to the pinnacle of his tower.

Again it’s song made the old king young again, and this time the second son tried to climb the tower to capture it, and again the singing fish swam off into the forest on the western side of the island.

The second son climbed down the tower, and when he returned to his father, he said, “Do not worry father, for I shall go and retrieve the bird for you, as my eldest brother has not.” And so, the second son left the castle with his trusty pigmy wolf and his riding carp.

When the second son reached the forest, he stopped and made camp just as his brother had the night before, and he began to eat his own dinner. Just as he had begun eating, a catboy entered into the clearing.
The second son was also worried that the catboy was there to catch the singing fish. He pointed at the catboy and shouted, “Go, my pigmy wolf, go and chase that catboy away.”
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[24]._dialogP: I think I know how this will go.
Ocan___nodes[46]._stepChain[25]._dialogP: When the second son reached the forest, he stopped and made camp just as his brother had the night before, and he began to eat his own dinner. Just as he had begun eating, a catboy entered into the clearing.
The second son was also worried that the catboy was there to catch the singing fish. He pointed at the catboy and shouted, “Go, my pigmy wolf, go and chase that catboy away.”

But before him or his pigmy wolf could do anything, the catboy pointed at the three of them and commanded, “Become as trees!” And just like his eldest brother before, the second brother, his pigmy wolf, and his riding carp all turned into trees.
The next morning had come, and the singing fish had returned to the pinnacle of the tower with neither the eldest brother or second brother to be seen.

The youngest son of the king, tried to capture the fish on the pinnacle, and again the fish swam off to the western side of the island, and so the youngest son promised his father, took his pigmy wolf and riding carp, went to the forest, and made camp for the night.

Suddenly, a catboy appeared at the edge of the clearing. The youngest son beckoned the catboy over, “Do not be afraid, catboy. There is no need to hiss. Come to the fireside and rest.” The catboy stayed at the edge of the clearing and said, “I would gladly rest by the fire, but I am worried that your wolf will chase after me.”

The youngest son pet his dog, “Then I shall send him home, so that you will have nothing to worry about.” Then, with two pats on his head and a gesture, the youngest son sent his pigmy back home to his castle.

When the wolf was gone, the catboy began to converse with the prince. He asked, “Tell me prince, do you think that the singing fish is here in this forest?” The prince could have lied about the fish, but he was an honest and good son. “I think that it is here in the forest, and I wish to retrieve it for my father,” the youngest son told the catboy.

Well, the two of them mounted the youngest son’s riding carp, and set forth on their journey to The Sky King’s kingdom.

She cleaned away the nearby weeds, and tidied the soil until the vine could sit comfortably against its trellis. The vine thanked the young girl, and so she went on her way once more.
Then, the young girl continued on her journey, until she passed by an old and crumbling oven. “Young girl, if you could please come here and help me?

Make me tidy and clean again, and I shall do you a good deed in return.” The young girl set to work, cleaning away the weeds, and stamping new clay, and before long the oven had been repaired and made tidy once more. The oven thanked her, and so the young girl went on her way.
