VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[22]._d: Has cost them their freedom. Their account is closed. Prison could be good for them, build their character, focus their minds. Smart criminals stay out of prison.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[23]._d: Sir, they may be able to piece together clues to your identity.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[24]._d: They know what I’ve allowed them to know, as they will know I’ve allowed them to live. Every breath they draw from now on they will do so thanking me for my mercy. By all accounts, I’ve given them a bargain for their services.
Enough about them. The Cloverton farmer, that’s what matters now. Wyrm, send a representative of your Sovereignty to bring {4|them|him|her} to me.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[25]._d: …I know an old deck scrapper around here who can get it done.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[26]._d: Good. I have faith you won’t fail in this.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[27]._d: If the korona is good, so are we.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[28]._d: How powerful the trust we have in coin. It would be dreadful then, to break such trust.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[30]._d: Bring me {4}, bring {4|them|him|her} to me in chains, bring {4|them|him|her} to me on {4|their|his|her} knees. Let me look into the eyes of the one brave enough to challenge me and see if their courage holds.
And then the account will be closed, one way or another.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[31]._d: You’ve got my word on it, and the word of the Pirate Sovereignty.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[36]._d: Be ready to clean up after Wyrm if he makes a mess.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[37]._d: It will cost the usual fee.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[38]._d: Of course, if it’s needed. Until then, pirates come so much cheaper, twelve to a penny. We’ll give them their shot.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[39]._d: There’s more to costs than coin, Mr. Robbins, believe me.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[40]._d: That’s something I always keep in mind.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[45]._d: It would be better to keep it simple.
VaskinIntro___nodes[0]._stepChain[46]._d: It is a paltry investment, to whet my curiosity. Besides, how boring life would be, if it was simple.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[0]._dialo: You’re looking up at the sky, deep tranquility passing overhead, dotted with fluffy clouds.
You feel at peace, is this what death feels li-
You feel pain flooding through you. You’re not dead, it seems.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[2]._dialo: Sitting up, you groan, you’re sore, but sore is good in this situation, sore means nothing is broken.
Looking around, you see a little shelter has been erected from the debris, and Kiram is sitting where a fire pit has been marked out… Well, as marked out as it could be really.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[5]._dialo: So, yer finally awake. Was worried ‘bout ya fer a moment there, blows to tha head an’ all that. Fortunately you’re thick-headed it seems.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[7]._dialo: Ugh… Isn’t this the second time you’ve welcomed me after a head injury? I’m picking up on a theme here.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[8]._dialo: Yarr harr! Well it ain’t intentional, just… Bad luck I s’pose.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[9]._dialo: Speaking of bad luck… You wouldn’t happen to know where we are, would you?
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[10]._dial: If I were ta hazard a guess, I think we’re somewhere between tha Kingdom an’ tha Sovereignty.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[12]._dial: Ah! Tha Pirate Sovereignty. ‘S where all tha pirates gather together, an’ where Rourke rules.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[14]._dial: Not as far as ya think! If yer Union buddies saw which direction we went, then I don’t think we’ll be here fer long.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[15]._dial: That’s good news… Wait, what does ‘for long’ mean to you?
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[18]._dial: A week!?! I have a farm to run!
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[21]._dial: Yes it is! You kidnapped me!
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[23]._dial: Kiram, at least, has the sensibility to look ashamed of himself.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[25]._dial: Okay… So we’re going to be stuck here for a bit, do we at least have food?
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[26]._dial: Aye! Fortunately we landed with a few larders full o’ stuff, an’ a pot just perfect fer makin’ Survivor Slop.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[27]._dial: Whew, even in your most desperate times you’ve tried to vary your meals up. The thought of eating Survivor Slop every day for a week or more makes your stomach feel heavy.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[28]._dial: Nothing to do but wait I suppose…
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[30]._dial: You take a seat near where the fire pit has been made. A moment of quiet, the first in a while that you’ve been conscious for, passes.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[31]._dial: Well, if ya wanna pass some time. I got an idea.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[33]._dial: I figure since we’re gonna wait, might as well get ta know each other better. So here’s the idea: You ask me a question, an’ I give ya an answer. Then I ask you a question in return.
We go back an’ forth fer a bit, an’ before ya know it tha day’ll be gone, an’ we can turn in!
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[34]._dial: You consider his proposal, then shrug.
Stranded___nodes[0]._stepChain[35]._dial: Sure, I don’t see why not! So I go first?
Stranded___nodes[1]._optionData[0]._rawT: Have you ever been marooned before?
Stranded___nodes[1]._optionData[1]._rawT: Why did you become a pirate in the first place?
Stranded___nodes[1]._optionData[2]._rawT: So who were you taking me to?
Stranded___nodes[1]._optionData[3]._rawT: What’ll you do now that you’re ‘retired’?
Stranded___nodes[1]._optionData[4].Tool_: Only shown when all previous options have been selected
Stranded___nodes[2]._stepChain[0]._dialo: Have you ever been marooned before?
Stranded___nodes[2]._stepChain[2]._dialo: Wait… Plenty of times? That doesn’t seem like it should be a common occurrence.
Stranded___nodes[2]._stepChain[3]._dialo: Oh it’s easy. Ya miss muster, take a bit too long gettin’ back to tha ship.
Sometimes ya get trapped an’ it takes tha crew a while ta get ya out. An’ other times… Well, it’s like this, ya take a ship out an’ hit a Spire like this, an’ have nothin’ ta do but pass tha time.
Stranded___nodes[2]._stepChain[5]._dialo: So far? It’s alright, but it’s not like tha time I got stranded on an island with all tha liquor I could drink, an’ all that shrubsticks I could eat, but not bad.
Give it a week though an’ ask me again.
Stranded___nodes[3]._stepChain[0]._dialo: Why did you become a pirate in the first place?
Stranded___nodes[3]._stepChain[1]._dialo: Believe it or not, I wasn’t always tha sterling example of morality ya see before ya.
Stranded___nodes[3]._stepChain[3]._dialo: Hard ta imagine, but in my youth I did anythin’ I could ta get by.
Stranded___nodes[3]._stepChain[4]._dialo: So how were you recruited in?
Stranded___nodes[3]._stepChain[5]._dialo: Started as a privateer actually. Fought durin’ tha Crystal Wars an’ made my livin’ durin’ that time.
I made a name fer meself, became a member of tha Corsairs, an’ eventually….
Stranded___nodes[3]._stepChain[8]._dialo: Kiram pauses for a moment, looking out over the vast blue sky.
Stranded___nodes[3]._stepChain[9]._dialo: Ah, ‘pologies, but I don’t feel much like speakin’ more on it.
Ye’ll have ta forgive an old man fer sufferin’ some old regrets in peace.
Stranded___nodes[3]._stepChain[11]._dial: You decide not to push the subject. He’s probably had to grapple with a lot the last few hours.
Stranded___nodes[4]._stepChain[0]._dialo: So who were you taking me to?
Stranded___nodes[4]._stepChain[1]._dialo: Well, I was s’posed ta take ya to tha big boss… But I was requested in secret to bring ya to Rourke first.
Stranded___nodes[4]._stepChain[2]._dialo: Wait… What? Why does this Captain Rourke want me?
Stranded___nodes[4]._stepChain[3]._dialo: One o’ tha first lessons ya learn as a pirate is not to ask too many questions. I imagine though it must’ve been important, they wanted ya alive after all.
Stranded___nodes[4]._stepChain[4]._dialo: Well that’s… Comforting. I guess your former crewmates’ plan was a terrible idea then.
Stranded___nodes[4]._stepChain[5]._dialo: Yarr Harr Harr! I was ready to keelhaul them, but Rourke would have done much worse!
Stranded___nodes[5]._stepChain[0]._dialo: What’ll you do now that you’re ‘retired’?
Stranded___nodes[5]._stepChain[1]._dialo: Well, given I don’t got much money squirreled away, I was gonna try my hand at bein’ a merchant again… But with no formal shop.
Stranded___nodes[5]._stepChain[3]._dialo: Well, I’m not rightly sure. Besides, it’s a bit of a pipe dream. I’ve made plenty of enemies out there, Yarr Harr!
Stranded___nodes[5]._stepChain[4]._dialo: Well, I hope you get the chance to do that someday.
Stranded___nodes[6]._optionData[0]._rawT: Talk about your time in the academy.
Stranded___nodes[6]._optionData[3]._rawT: Talk about getting involved with the poachers.
Stranded___nodes[7]._stepChain[0]._dialo: What sort of learnin’ d’ya got? Bein’ a farmer an’ Frontiersman requires some sort of education, aye?
Stranded___nodes[7]._stepChain[1]._dialo: It does! While I’ve had an otherwise normal education, I did attend the academy for my specialization in farming.

After the basics were covered, my grades allowed me to attend the academy on a scholarship. I had to keep up the hard work, a single slip would put my scholarship into jeopardy, two slips and I’d be out of school.
Stranded___nodes[7]._stepChain[2]._dialo: But yer here now, so ya passed aye?
Stranded___nodes[7]._stepChain[3]._dialo: Hah, aye! When I graduated, I was given the opportunity to choose my posting, and I picked out Cloverton. Being on a newly settled part of The Frontier, and having my own farm had a great appeal.
I’m still learning, however, my schooling helped, but I still have to run the farm with my own two hands.
Stranded___nodes[8]._stepChain[0]._dialo: I’m none too familiar with runnin’ a farm, mind tellin’ me about it?
Stranded___nodes[8]._stepChain[1]._dialo: The majority of it is just putting in the time and effort. If you work hard enough early, there’s less work to do later.
Keeping my monsters happy, making sure I use fertilizer that’s in-season, and accounting for my crops are all parts of the work that make up the whole.
Stranded___nodes[8]._stepChain[2]._dialo: Are there any difficulties ya have?
Stranded___nodes[8]._stepChain[3]._dialo: Oh, plenty. Monsters can’t plant new seeds after something is harvested. New seasons means spending days getting my farm back in working order.
That’s not even mentioning making sure everyone has enough food to eat. But it’s what I signed up for, so I can’t complain~.
Stranded___nodes[8]._stepChain[4]._dialo: Well, sounds like you run a tight ship, er- plot!
Stranded___nodes[9]._stepChain[0]._dialo: So, where’re ya from? Are ye a native ta these territories?
Stranded___nodes[9]._stepChain[1]._dialo: Oh, no, I moved to Cloverton relatively recently. Before then I lived in the Central Kingdom at the academy.
My original home is a communal house, but it was too far away to warrant me traveling back and forth.
Stranded___nodes[9]._stepChain[2]._dialo: A communal home? Are ye an-
Stranded___nodes[9]._stepChain[3]._dialo: Yeah, I don’t talk about it much, it was uh- different.
Stranded___nodes[9]._stepChain[4]._dialo: Oh! Naw it’s alright, I don’t wanna push tha subject if yer not comfortable with it.
Stranded___nodes[10]._stepChain[0]._dial: So, how’d a farmer get involved with all’o this?
Stranded___nodes[10]._stepChain[1]._dial: Strangely enough? Because a small island filled with angry cats crashed onto my farm.
Stranded___nodes[10]._stepChain[3]._dial: Oh! I never get to tell this story!

So, when I was starting on the farm, it was an absolute mess, but I was on my way to getting it fixed when, wouldn’t you know it, a small island carrying some *very* angry cat monsters crashed right into it.

I fought ‘em off, and was told that the island crashed because they were escaping something riling them up. Well, I figured I should do something about it, and I went to investigate. Eventually I discovered Sting and Prism’s operations.
I shut them down and figured that would be it… And then I was kidnapped.
Stranded___nodes[10]._stepChain[4]._dial: … Well I’ll be a Sea King’s concubine! I dunno what’s more amazin’. That ye were able to dismantle their operation so thoroughly, or that yer only involved because of a one-in-a-million chance that tha island they set loose landed on yer farm.
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[1]._dial: You barely notice how quickly time passes while you and Kiram talk.
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[3]._dial: It’s not until it’s too dark to see one another that you realize how late it’s gotten.
Kiram kindles the fire to keep the cold from setting in completely. He brings out some of the Survivor Slop to fill your stomachs.
The slop fills you up, but it was like a lead weight was sitting on your stomach.
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[5]._dial: If ya want, you can go an’ sleep first. I’ll keep tha fire goin’ so if anyone passes in tha dark, they’ll see us.
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[6]._dial: Are you certain? I can keep watch if you’re too tired.
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[7]._dial: Yarr harr harr! I’m certain. I’ve caused ya enough trouble fer one day, you bed down an-
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[10]._dia: From overhead, a single, bright light shines down on you and Kiram.
You shield your eyes, half-blinded, but able to barely make out the Union symbol painted on the side of the ship.
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[13]._dia: Frontiersman {0}! Finally! We’ve been searching for signs of you since your abduction! Glad to see you’re in one piece, haha!
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[14]._dia: Sieger! Scales, I’m so glad to see you! I thought we were going to be stranded here for weeks!
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[15]._dia: I said -A- week! At MOST! An’ see? Got rescued in jus’ a day!
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[17]._dia: Sieger turns, and pulls out a sheaf of paper.
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[18]._dia: Kiram, you’re under arrest for the abduction of {0}, Piracy, and a whole list of other charges we’ll work out back at Cloverton.
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[22]._dia: Several of the nearby soldiers approach Kiram. Kiram holds his hands out and together. Steel cuffs clamp around Kiram’s wrists.
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[23]._dia: Wait Sieger, he helped me escape!
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[24]._dia: He *also* was the one who kidnapped you. You might not have known {0}, but Kiram is a wanted man.
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[27]._dia: Swabbie, it’s fine. ‘sides, this ain’t tha place to argue.
I’m assumin’ the cell I’m gonna be sittin’ in has a good view, aye?
Stranded___nodes[11]._stepChain[29]._dia: That’s right Kiram, three square meals a day, and all the time in the world to think about your actions.
LostBattle___nodes[0]._stepChain[4]._dia: Kiram dives down, wrapping an arm around you. You can barely see as you look up, the Corsairs turn to look back, only for the ship to come to a crashing halt.
You fly forward, the Corsairs are flung from the deck, and the last thing you see is a wooden post flying at you before you black out.
WinBattle___nodes[0]._stepChain[2]._dial: Haaahhh.. Hhhaaahhhh… These guys weren’t so to-
WinBattle___nodes[0]._stepChain[3]._dial: You look up and see a spire of land coming towards you at an alarming speed.
WinBattle___nodes[0]._stepChain[6]._dial: You jump towards Kiram, pulling him down to the deck. You and Kiram are flung forward as the ship impacts, your face buried into his chest before you black out.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[1]: It has taken some time, but you have finally come to a consensus. Being kidnapped is VERY boring with no convenient ways to escape. You’ve tried to break free a few times, but with no success.
After hours of that, you’ve resigned yourself to laying on the somewhat clean floor, and farming in your head.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[4]: So if I plant something that takes ten days till harvest, but I forget to water it one day, would it be better to wait the extra days for it to finish growing, or should I just harvest it then? Well, one tenth of the price would be…
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[5]: Your musing is interrupted by several Corsairs tromping down the stairs.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[8]: Alright mateys, let’s do this afore tha Cap’n catches us.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[9]: Ah jus’ dun get why we’re s’ afraid o’ ‘im, ‘e’s jus-
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[10: For the love of… Stop TALKING like that!
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[11: Arr, come offit! Ya’s been ‘avin ah problem wif my talkin’ ‘er since-
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[13: Uhhh, fellas? Not that I want to get in the way of whatever is going on here, but I also don’t want to listen to you gripe at each other.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[14: Right! Right. Let’s get back to business *ahem*.
Now then! Tha Cap’n has given us orders not ta be botherin’ ya so we can transport ya safely… However.
It is the belief of tha crew that if ya *did* come ta harm, well, that might be the end of Cap’n Kiram, an’ we can FINALLY get someone with a backbone ta run the crew.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[15: That will be hard, since I’m in here, and you’re out there.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[16: Which is why we’ve got a set o’ keys here. We’re gonna make it look like you broke out, an’ left us with no alternative but ta put ye down.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[17: Well, I wouldn’t say it’s foolproof, but it’s a solid plan.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[18: The Corsair puffs his chest out, obviously the brains of the operation.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[0]._stepChain[19: Have you considered what would happen if you were caught though?
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[1]: Hah, we made sure tha Cap’n would be nice an’ distracted right now, he won’t be around till LONG after we’re done.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[2]: I wondered why my cabin was locked from the outside. So that was you swabbie?
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[6]: Save it swabbie, I don’t wanna hear whatever poor excuse you’ll use.
Yanno, I knew ya lot were ‘bout as dumb as ya could get, but I couldn’t IMAGINE that ye’d be dumb enough to break one of tha few rules that blasted Rourke keeps.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[7]: At the name Rourke, the other pirates freeze for just a moment
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[8]: If ye be flyin’ under the Cyan Corsair’s colors, ye could at least ‘ave a pirate’s dignity..
Now, all’o ya get outta ‘ere afore I throw ya to tha Sky Kings.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[9]: There’s grumbling, but then an assertion of ‘aye cap’n’ and the Corsairs depart.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[12: After the last Corsair leaves, Kiram turns to face you. In one of his hands is a sack, much like the one he’d served you from earlier.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[14: Huh? Oh. Aye, I figured ye’d be gettin’ hungry about now.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[15: Kiram passes you the sack. On opening it, you see-
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[16: More Survivor Slop and bread. All part of a balanced diet…
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[17: It is if yer a pirate. Three o’ these a day fer tha weeks, to months yer sailin’ the skies.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[18: From the accounts you’ve read, survivor slop was meant to be a last resort, not a staple! You can’t help but feel your stomach groan under the idea of such a heavy weight being added to it, but it’s either this or nothing…
You take out the food, with little ceremony you begin eating.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[19: Sho… Dis is jus’ mah observashon…
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[20: Please don’t talk wit’ yer mouth full o’ food.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[21: A pirate with table manners, that’s a new one. You swallow.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[22: Sorry… So, this is just my observation, but it seems like there is some tension between you and your crew.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[23: Pah! You could say that again!
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[24: But isn’t a pirate captain supposed to be like… Charismatic?
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[2]._rawContextHe: Is he a dashing rogue, or a drab drogue?
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[3]._stepChain[1]: Yarr harr! Well there ya go!
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[4]._stepChain[3]: Well, I suppose ya can’t please everyone! Yarr harr!

‘Sides, I could be tha most charismatic person in tha’ Kingdom, an’ these swabbies wouldn’t lissen ta me. I can threaten ‘em with Rourke’s name ta get them ta behave. Sometimes it sticks just long enough ta finish a job and get ta port.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[1]: His smile fades, and he takes a deep breath before sighing.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[2]: Well, these ain’t MY crew anyways, I did my best with them, but they ain’t… They ain’t REAL Cyan Corsairs.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[3]: I’m no artist, but I think I recognize cyan when I see it.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[4]: Ya got me there, they *are* cyan corsairs, but not *tha* Cyan Corsairs… Ya see, tha Corsairs have been flyin’ fer years, an’ ye can only be a pirate fer so long before bad luck, or age, catches up to ya.
Tha Corsairs haven’t been tha same since Rourke took tha title of Pirate Lord.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[5]: So this Rourke changed the Corsairs?
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[6]: Aye. Used ta be that hirin’ new crew members was tha duty of tha Cap’n an’ crew. Someone would bring on a potential crew mate, everyone would get a say, an’ if they passed muster an’ got enough people ta vouch for ‘em, they’d become one a ye.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[8]: Now though… Well now, if ya can’t fight, ya get left behind, an’ whatever gaps in tha ranks there are, are filled with whatever jetsam ain’t on a ship.
I’m proud ta say that it took years fer tha crew ta get replaced. We were tha best fer a long while… But every job doesn’t go perfectly, mistakes are made, crew gets left behind…
One day yer Cap’n yerself, ya look up, an’ realize all tha crew ya used ta know is gone, replaced by strangers…
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[9]: Sounds like things have changed a lot since you started.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[10: Aye, an’ the way it’s going, it’s all going ta go up in flames.
Used ta be that pirates acted with more discretion. We didn’t burn every town to tha ground, raid every little outpost fer everythin’ that ain’t nailed down.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[12: Now though, they want all that, an’ ta torch an’ pillage their way across tha Kingdom. They want ta haul back tha biggest treasures an’ show off ta other pirates. They do it fer tha gold an’ tha notoriety.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[13: I mean.. Isn’t that part and parcel of being a pirate?
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[15: Well… a little. But, if yer a good pirate, ya let yer legend speak fer itself. Ya don’t kill unless ya gotta, or ravage a place just fer tha thrill of it, an’ *whatever* ya do, ya *don’t* tangle with someone who has more ships than ya.

Rourke’s got tha power behind ‘em fer sure, but he *ain’t* tha Kingdom… One day, he’ll make a big enough mess ta wake the slumberin’ sea king that is The Kingdom Navy. An’ drag every buccaneer to the gallows with him.
Maybe that’d be fer tha better though…
Regardless, I can feel that I probably won’t be makin’ it to port.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[16: You think that the Corsairs might mutiny?
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[17: I’d put serious Krone down on them actin’ VERY soon… But they probably think my hand is off tha wheel, so they won’t expect me to act first. Which is why-
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[18: Kiram walks over to the nearby storage and pulls out a familiar looking spade.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[20: He tosses it to you, you catch it deftly. Finally, reunited with your best friend!
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[21: I think that should be enough ta break outta that cell… So when things start explodin’, you’ll find me gettin’ a lifeboat ready.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[23: Wait, if you do this won’t that make you an outlaw among pirates? Hmm, now there’s a strange thought.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[24: Aye, it would. But, that’s alright wit’ me. ya got me talkin’ and thinkin’ about a lot of things and well...
I’d promised tha old Cap’n ta keep tha Cyan Corsairs alive, an’ I thought I was, keepin’ this ‘ere ship together. But, this ship and this crew ain’t the Cyan Corsairs anymore.
Besides, tha way I figure it. As long as I’m still wearin’ tha colors, the Cyan Corsairs are still alive!
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[25: … Good luck then, I’ll be waiting for that explosion!
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[26: Kiram laughs, running up the stairs.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[5]._stepChain[30: An explosion rocks the ship, Kiram has made his move, and you follow suit. You bring your shovel down on the lock, slicing through it. The cell door swings open, and you step out.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[6]._stepChain[3]: Yarr… We drew short straws on the lifeboats. Now, we may be goin’ down with tha ship, but we’re gonna take ye with us Cap-.. No, Kiram! Yer no Cap’n of ours no more!
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[6]._stepChain[5]: Yarr harr harr! Took ya long enough ta be honest with me swabbie. I’ll return tha favor then.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[6]._stepChain[8]: If I had a Krone fer EVERY time one of ya disappointed me, I’d be tha richest man alive!
I’ve ‘ad more competent Cloud Skippers run tha ship!
An’ lastly, ya’ll ain’t worth my boot scrapin’s. Ya’ll would have NEVER lasted ten seconds against the *real* Cyan Corsairs!
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[6]._stepChain[10: Whew, that felt good to say ta ya’l.  Now, if I’m ta meet my end today, I’ll go down fightin’ like a REAL pirate!
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[6]._stepChain[11: You stand next to Kiram, drawing your shovel.
SecondMeetKiram___nodes[6]._stepChain[13: I don’t intend on going down at all, so let’s deal with them quickly and figure something out!
Pirate hit cell bars Screen Shake
You flinch as the hilt of a saber hits the bars of the cell.
I got tha short end o’ tha straw guardin’ ya, so if ya make even a PEEP I swear I’ll-
I would- Bring ‘em directly to ya! Yeah, that’s right! After all, yer tha CAP’N an’ all, an’ I wouldn’t DARE countermand yer orders, now would I?
Holy Hottons… This pirate is a really bad liar. He’s not even trying to hide his antagonism. Kiram’s eyes stare down the smaller pirate.
The larger man lets out a sigh and pinches the bridge of his nose.
Fine. Just… Get outta here afore I give ye twenty lashes.
The Corsair slides away from his captain, but you catch, on his way out, the Corsair giving Kiram a VERY rude gesture.
Pologies fer that. I’ve tried ta teach my crew some semblance of manners, but tha lessons never took.
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[1]._stepChain[11]: Ye’ll be under my care ‘till you get dropped off.
Under normal circumstances I’d thank you, but I’m guessing you’re the one that had me put behind these bars to begin with?
Yarr harr! Aye! Normally I wouldn’t bother with all this, but I’ve been told ta make sure ya stay put. So we used our best bars to keep you locked up.
I’d hate to see your worst bars then.
The larger man pulls up a chair and takes a seat in front of you, holding out a bag to you.
Taking the bag, you open it to find some assorted foodstuffs.
If ya get thirsty, we got water as well, fresh stuff pulled from clouds.
Fer food, I know it’s no shrubstick double down, but it’s what we got.
Things don’t look too bad until-
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[10]: Yarhar! Aye, Survivor’s Slop, made by hand jus’ this mornin’.
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[14]: Your eyes dart to the ends of his arms, relief washing over you as you see both his hands are intact.

The slop looks like the most foul concoction to ever make a mockery of cuisine… But your stomach grumbles, and the slop is better than nothing at all. There is, at least, a stool in your cell that you can pull up so you don’t have to eat while sitting on the floor.
The slop comes with a loaf of bread for sopping it up.
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[16]: I’ll just… Eat this in a little bit…
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[17]: Aye, take yer time, time’s about all ya got till we drop ya off.
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[18]: Which actually brings me to my first question. I doubt you’ll tell me much, but where am I?
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[19]: Well, ‘that’s easy! Yer aboard the Cyan Blade, an a’fore ya ask, it’s not ACTUALLY cyan, it’s just an old name.
As fer myself, I’m cap’n o’ tha Cyan Corsairs, as you’ve no doubt figured out. Your presence has been forcibly requested, an’ I was tha one who was sent out ta pick ya up!
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[20]: Okay… But, why am I needed, and by who?
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[21]: Ah! To tha core o’ tha matter.
You may or may not remember, but recently ya happened to defeat a couple  mercenaries.
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[23]: WELL! As it so happens, someone was VERY interested in them continuin’ their operations. So my guess is, that someone wanted me an’ my crew ta bring you in fer a little parley.
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[24]: A parley usually implies a peace deal of some kind. Somehow I have my doubts that’s the kind of meeting they have in mind.
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[25]: Nah, an’ honestly, it’s not one I really want ta take ya to either.
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[27]: Then why are you taking me?
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[2]._stepChain[28]: Well, afore I continue, mebbe you should start eatin’, ain’t like it’s gonna taste any better cold.
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[3]._rawContextHea: Partake of the lukewarm Survivor's Slop?
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[3].Tool_Label: Partake of the lukewarm Survivor's Slop?
As much as you’d rather not, Kiram is right, the slop won’t look more appetizing an hour from now. And, you don’t know the next time you might get a meal, if at all.

You break off a piece of bread, it’s still fluffy, just hitting that point where the bread is a little stale around the edges, but soft towards the center. You dip it into the almost-sludge, the bread sopping the slop up.
Moment of truth. You bring the bread to your mouth, the slop dripping back into the bowl as you open your mouth and take a bite.
It’s actually not bad. In fact, it’s kind of hearty, and reminds you a bit of the cafeteria back at the academy.
The rest of the bread disappears into the slop and then into your mouth. You don’t understand, it looks like something an engineer would drain from a machine, but the taste defies expectations.
Yarr harr harr! Good right? Comes from years of ‘avin ta make the stuff fer meself an’ tha crew.
I’ll take my chances with the cold slop, I wanna hear what you have to say.
Kiram leans back in his chair, crossing his arms over his chest in contemplation.
Aye, about yer kidnappin’. Like I said, I don’t really wanna be takin’ ya to the boss. Now if ye were cap’n of another ship, or military? Then it’d be different.
But takin’ ya in feels like punishin’ a farmer who happens ta be good in a scrap. Ya took out them poachers, an’ were I callin’ tha shots, I’d say fair game, an’ leave it at that…
But I ain’t callin’ tha shots. And, from what I’ve heard, yer a Frontiersman. So, I haven’t much room to decline.
sigh- you’ve got me wishing for the first time ever I wasn’t a Frontiersman.
Ye got me feelin’ tha same, boyo. It don’t sit well with me ta abduct someone like ya. So, after this, I intend on retirin’ an’ leavin’ this crew.
I know it don’t help much, but yer gonna be tha LAST person I abduct.
You’re right, that doesn’t help much… But since I’ll be the last person, and by all accounts I won’t be escaping, why not just… Tell me everything about who you work for?
Yarr harr! I know bait when I see it, an’ as fun as it would be to reveal everythin’ I know, that’ll just be askin’ fer trouble.
Now, I believe that’s enough talk fer tha time being. I’ll be back later… Oh! I’ll also be tellin’ tha crew not ta bother ya down here.
Appreciated… But, before you go, why are you being so accommodating?
Accommodatin’? That’s a strange way ta describe being held up in a cell.
Well, I’ve met a few piratey-types, and, while you’re not so different from them, they wouldn’t have bothered checking in on a captive. Let alone talking with them like this.
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[6]._stepChain[10]: He stops, and considers something for a moment, before running his hand through his hair.
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[6]._stepChain[11]: Have I lost the taste for it? Hmm…
FirstMeetKiram___nodes[6]._stepChain[12]: Kiram stands and departs, leaving you alone in your cell.
EvKidnapping___nodes[0]._stepChain[1]._d: It’s a fair day. The sun is shining, and the weather is calm. Puffy, white clouds hang in the distance, as your ship sails through calm wind currents.
EvKidnapping___nodes[0]._stepChain[4]._d: It’s a good day, isn’t it, Captain?
EvKidnapping___nodes[0]._stepChain[5]._d: ‘s alright. Though clear skies would be preferable to me. I’m not so fond of getting misted when we fly through em’. Reminds me of the time-
EvKidnapping___nodes[0]._stepChain[6]._d: As the captain begins to spin a yarn, a sailor on the deck suddenly yells out!
EvKidnapping___nodes[1]._stepChain[5]._d: He turns to the sailor, surprised.
EvKidnapping___nodes[2]._stepChain[1]._d: You turn to where the sailor points, seeing a dark silhouette descending from a nearby cloud bank. A large puffer carries a deck filled with swarthy looking brigands - Pirates!
EvKidnapping___nodes[2]._stepChain[6]._d: Boarding chains launch from its port and starboard cannons, embedding into your ship’s hull as it swings behind you.
EvKidnapping___nodes[2]._stepChain[7]._d: No! We’re carrying too much precious cargo!
EvKidnapping___nodes[2]._stepChain[11]._: Chaos erupts, you barely have time to react, as the burly brigands descend onto the deck of your ship, as a fight breaks out between both crews.
EvKidnapping___nodes[2]._stepChain[16]._: H-hey! Alright, time to teach these pirates a lesson everyon-
EvKidnapping___nodes[2]._stepChain[19]._: You catch it out of the corner of your eye too late. A heavy looking club descends from a pirate that’s already bested one of the marines.
EvKidnapping___nodes[3]._stepChain[4]._d: You feel your body hitting the deck with a heavy thud. You can’t tell if the wind was knocked out of you. Come to think of it, you can’t tell if you’re breathing…
EvKidnapping___nodes[3]._stepChain[10]._: You feel your body moving, but not on it’s own. It’s a slow, heavy drag across the floor, or is it the ceiling?
You feel another thud, but you can’t tell if it’s been seconds or hours since the last fleeting moment of consciousness…
RookBossStage Landing__line_8: You find a safe landing below, on a pile of rugs and leaves. Up above, the trap door closes with a grinding click.
RookBossStage Landing__line_10: <PLAYERNAME> := Ow… Least he put out something to land on.
RookBossStage Landing__line_19: if present Kreyton := Good thing ye landed on me, and not the other way around!
RookBossStage Landing__line_20: if present Stoic := I’ll be fine. I just need to… Nghh… walk it off…
RookBossStage Landing__line_21: if present Loving := Oh, <PLAYERNAME>. I think I’ll pull through.
RookBossStage Landing__line_22: if present Camellia := Normally I appreciate being swept off my feet… I’ll be fine in just a moment.
RookBossStage Landing__line_23: if present Brontide := I- I can’t feel my legs! Oh no, wait, those are your legs.
RookBossStage Landing__line_24: if present Goldra := Il me fait chier…
RookBossStage Landing__line_25: if present Melancholy := Of course we’d fall for that trap… At least he didn’t shoot us… yet…
RookBossStage Landing__line_26: if present Yonten := Inner peace… inner peace…. inner peace… is it over?
RookBossStage Landing__line_27: if present Cheerful := Hey, I think he meant to do that!
RookBossStage Landing__line_28: if present Garst := I have fared better… His honor is hurt more than my being.
RookBossStage Landing__line_29: if present Fio := Oohhh, ‘a course you realize this means war!
RookBossStage Landing__line_30: if present Jaero := Perhaps what he lacks in passion… he makes up for in efficiency…
RookBossStage Landing__line_31: if present Kaleida := I’m fine… that fall almost cracked my orb though! That jerk!
RookBossStage Landing__line_32: if present Kiram := Ooo! This old body’s banged up, but still skyworthy.
RookBossStage Landing__line_33: if present Mischievous := … Maybe it’s better we didn’t make a wager… ouch!
RookBossStage Landing__line_34: if present Grumpy := Now I’m gonna kill him, I’m really gonna kill him!
RookBossStage Landing__line_39: <PLAYERNAME> := Here again. Now to find Rook.
RookBossStage Landing__line_43: <PLAYERNAME> := Okay, nothing’s broken. Now to find Rook.
RookBossStage Defeat__line_4: Your shovel clatters to the ground, as you hunch over in pain. You don’t think you can keep fighting.
RookBossStage Defeat__line_5: Rook latches his gun on his belt holster.
RookBossStage Defeat__line_8: r- Rook := And so it goes… I must congratulate you on being a formidable quarry, but all good things must come to an end… Come along now, we’ll get you cleaned up and in shackles before supper.
RookBossStage Defeat__line_9: r- Rook := Hahh… That’s that, I suppose… I had hoped your bravado would equate to some fire in your belly. I suppose I shouldn’t have gotten my hopes up… Well, sweet dreams. You’ll need your rest before meeting my employer.
RookBossStage Defeat__line_10: r- Rook := Thank you, for a most invigorating hunt. It’s been so long since I’ve watched the defiance in someone’s eyes fade into confusion and resignation. I’m sure my employer would have enjoyed it as well, but we’ll keep this as our little secret, right? Right…
RookBossStage PreFight__line_9: Rook waits for you at the other side of this arena. His loyal dogfish sitting obediently next to him.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_14: Rook := What do you think of my training ground? Sporting enough accommodations?
RookBossStage PreFight__line_15: <PLAYERNAME> := More than anyone else tends to give. And, less cages everywhere. So do we just fight now?
RookBossStage PreFight__line_16: Rook := I’m more than prepared. Do you still need a moment?
RookBossStage PreFight__line_20: menu: Are you prepared to fight?
RookBossStage PreFight__line_21: "I’d rather talk this out, if that’s okay?":
RookBossStage PreFight__line_23: "Before we fight, who sent you after me?":
RookBossStage PreFight__line_29: <PLAYERNAME> := I’d rather talk this out, if that’s okay?
RookBossStage PreFight__line_31: Rook := Do you think if you plead with a few platitudes and saccharine words, I’ll cast aside my weapon? That’s quite the insult to my professionalism.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_32: <PLAYERNAME> := Kiram heard me out, so did Sascha and Gehman.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_34: Rook := You’re welcome to bring words to a duel, but I’ll not be changing my own tactics.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_41: <PLAYERNAME> := Before we fight, who sent you after me?
RookBossStage PreFight__line_42: Rook := You expect me to answer so easily?
RookBossStage PreFight__line_43: <PLAYERNAME> := Well, I came all the way out here, it seems the least you could do.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_44: Rook := You’ll have to bear the disappointment of working with a professional.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_45: Rook := This duel is already the small pleasure I indulge myself. Once we’re finished, and you’re captured, you’ll have your chance to meet him.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_46: <PLAYERNAME> := Tempting, but I think I’ll just defeat you instead.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_47: Rook := Yes, back to the matter at hand then.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_51: <PLAYERNAME> := You’ve been accommodating enough, but let’s get started.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_52: Rook := You seem a decent {fellow/gal/quarry}, a shame you have such a weakness.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_53: <PLAYERNAME> := Hasn’t let me down yet! You’re welcome to try though.
RookBossStage PreFight__line_58: Rook := So rare to hunt a quarry with such spirit. I’ll miss this bravado once you’re gone.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[2]: Rook stumbles back, a hand at his side. Surprisingly, he shrugs it off.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[4]: That was a good hit, but It’ll take a bit more than you’ve got to best me.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[5]: He rises to level his gun, only for his leg to buckle as he falls to a kneel.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[7]: Ough! Damn, perhaps you haven’t been found wanting…
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[10: I’m used to being underestimated at this point. Now, do you yield? Or am I going to have to pummel you a few more times?
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[12: Though I’m loath to admit it, I yield…
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[14: Good! Now, let’s get down to brass tacks.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[15: Rook pulls the cock of his gun back, pouring the measure of gunpowder onto the floor. Placing his gun down carefully, he drops a powder horn with it.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[17: Very well. First of all, congratulations are in order. You have been a formidable quarry.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[18: Thanks, I guess… but the real first point of order is your little comments about the people of Cloverton.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[19: Ah, yes, my implied threat in the letter…
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[21: You needn’t worry. You’ve already answered my challenge, and beaten me. I have no need to goad you into finding me again with such distasteful ultimatums.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[22: Huh… yeah, good! You’re done coming after me too?
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[24: Fret not, Robin Hood. It would be distasteful of me to simply hunt after you again and again until your luck ran out. Besides, my services are at a premium, even for the cream of the crop.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[25: Speaking of - Now that I’ve beaten you, why don’t we talk about your employer?
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[26: Careful now, the same professional courtesy I extend to you must also go to my employer. I think we both would rather I keep my word.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[27: Well, we’ll see how you feel after a few nights in jail. You’re under arrest!
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[28: Rook shakes his head, reaching down to pick up his faithful dogfish.
RookPostVictory___nodes[0]._stepChain[30: I don’t believe I ever agreed to such terms. However, since my challenge’s terms were rather one sided, I will leave you with this.
I enjoyed a stiff libation earlier, and have been far more loquacious than I usually am. Hopefully I haven’t had a slip of the tongue.
RookPostVictory___nodes[2]._stepChain[2]: Did any of you notice anything? I guess he called you all merry men earlier?
RookPostVictory___nodes[2]._stepChain[3]: Huh? No I was just wondering what a wet dogfish smells like… Is it like, normal or bad?
I’m afraid not. I wasn’t expecting an eccentric gunman to be so talkative…
Right? Not a damn clue what he means by it, though…
I beg your pardons, but the modern spoken word is already challenging enough for me.
He doesn’t sound very poetic… I’m not sure where he let slip anything.
Oh drat, I’m terrible with riddles…
Yarr, the few times I met him, he was speakin’ just like that. All posh and proper like and obtuse…
Hmmm. Wait, is this about folktales? I know I heard something somewhere… Think, Kreyton. Think!
I doubt he’s referencing any legends of the Yetis. Tales from another land, perhaps?
Hmmm…. Oh I know! He’s talking about something I don’t know!
Wait, he was giving hints earlier? Ohhh… any chance of a do over?
Speak your mind, instead of hiding behind wordplay and allegory.
Great, now the crazed gunman is speaking in riddles. Just tell us where your manifesto is!
Oh, Oh! This guy’s funnier than I thought! Sorry, {0}, you wouldn’t get it!
Ohh! I suppose we might have jumped to conclusions earlier. I think I know what he’s talking about!
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[0]: Hmm, you mentioned a Robin Hood, but it doesn’t ring a bell… Unless you mean Robin the Vulpe? That’s an old folktale!
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[2]: Right, of course it’s called that here…
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[4]: He stole from the wealthy to give to the less fortunate… (That definitely doesn’t rule out my prime suspect…)
Don’t think that makes up for your misdeeds!
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[5]: No? Well, I’ll extend some other courtesy, once I think of one.
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[7]: Rook reaches down, grabbing his powder horn. As he does, you notice that it’s been leaking powder into the small pile he started earlier.
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[10: Before you can act, Rook reaches down for his gun, bringing the cock and flint striker up to the small pile of gunpowder.
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[14: With a flash and plume of smoke, you lose sight of him. By the time it clears, he and his dogfish are nowhere to be found.
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[16: In the distance, you hear Rook’s voice call out.
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[17: Apologies, but I simply can’t allow you to capture me. Not only am I dreadfully disinclined towards incarceration –Ironic, I know– It would also be quite the stain on my professional record.
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[20: Now now, don’t be greedy. I’ll said I’ll extend some courtesy, just not that one. Ta-ta for now!
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[22: Wow, what a great guy… not.
RookPostVictory___nodes[3]._stepChain[24: You look around, seeing if there are any clues as to where he headed. While you don’t find any, you do happen upon a trinket near where he stood.
RookPostVictory___nodes[5]._stepChain[0]: While you look over it, your friends call out to you.
RookPostVictory___nodes[5]._stepChain[2]: Hey, {0}! I think I found a secret staircase!
{0}, dear! We really should be going. You’ll likely want to report back to The Union?
C’mon, {0}, let’s get going already! This trip was a bust! Look, the bastard left stairs over here…
My eyen doth spot from whence that vandal descended from his dwelling!
I’ve had enough of this wild plankton chase. I have paperwork to file, and no doubt you have an eyewitness account to deliver?
While the din of battle has ceased, there is still much for us to do. Ready, {0}?
Oh, how did my readings go all wrong here? I just don’t understand it!
We best be goin’, {0}. No sense waitin’ around here for him to come back. He’s too smart fer that!
Aw pish! All this work, and nothin’ ta show for it but some trinket! I say we go and get somethin’ ta eat.
We should talk over this hunter’s riddles… But I’m afraid we’ll only uncover more illusions.
It was a good shot! We’ll figure this all out, don’t you worry!
Well, at least he left a staircase over here. I found it after banging my foot on it…
What is that you picked up?
Damn damn! Well that all went tits up in a ditch…
I wonder if he left anything else behind. Think we can go through his cupboards before the rest of the union gets here?
You fought so valiantly. It truly was inspiring, {0}!
RookPostVictory___nodes[5]._stepChain[3]: You pocket the trinket for now.
RookPostVictory___nodes[5]._stepChain[5]: Alright, time to get going. I should probably report back to Katarina Station…
RookPostVictory___nodes[6]._stepChain[0]: You close your fingers over it in your palm before taking a look around.
RookPostVictory___nodes[6]._stepChain[2]: Hmm, maybe I shouldn’t have come alone. How am I going to get out of this?
RookPostVictory___nodes[6]._stepChain[5]: After a few moments looking around, you manage to find a secret staircase leading back up towards Rook’s lodge.
Camellia Scene Door__line_7: <PLAYERNAME>	:= Whoa my, that's one pretty piece of architecture. Is that some nob's house?
Camellia Scene Door__line_8: Jubelle ears twitch as her head tilts.
Camellia Scene Door__line_10: Jubelle			:= Nob? Unsure of what you mean by that, but this here is The Monarch Inn.
Camellia Scene Door__line_11: <PLAYERNAME>	:= Huh, alright. Sounds shady and dangerous, honestly.
Camellia Scene Door__line_12: Jubelle			:= I don't know where you get that idea just from the name. This is the oldest building in Cloverton. It's been here longer than anyone here can remember. It also acts as a tavern.
Rekk Market Day__line_4: Rekk := Ah! Excellent, a customer comes to Rekk. No doubt you’ve thrown something away and want it back.
Rekk Market Day__line_9: "Wait, did you go through my garbage?":
Rekk Market Day__line_23: Rekk puffs out his chest, he looks proud of himself.
Rekk Market Day__line_24: Rekk := I go through everyone’s garbage. I’m a veritable veteran in the war on waste!
Rekk Market Day__line_30: Rekk := I specialize in the sale of things discarded by others.
Rekk Market Day__line_34: Rekk := I prefer to call it junk; because everyone wants my junk.
Rekk Market Day__line_36: <PLAYERNAME> := That can’t be a good business model.
Rekk Market Day__line_38: Rekk := Actually it is, I sell only the finest repurposed goods. Indeed, if you throw away something, you may find that I, or one of my associates, have actually improved it before selling it! Regardless, I sell junk, and I guarantee that you will be interested in the junk in my trunk at least once.
Outpost Scene__line_5: Slowly climbing the stairs, you reach the top floor. The sound is even louder in here than it was from outside as you glimpse Union soldiers going about their daily routines.
Outpost Scene__line_6: Their communal table is laden with food, paper... Weapons. It's only when your vision is blocked by rolling hills of muscle that you notice someone with draconic features looking down at you.
Outpost Scene__line_7: Brontide		:= Woah, careful there, civilian. This here is the Union Outpost. Please, state your business.
Outpost Scene__line_8: <PLAYERNAME>	:= Oh! Union Farmer <PLAYERNAME>, for your service- AT your service!
Outpost Scene__line_9: Brontide		:= The new Union Farmer! I wasn't expecting you to drop by so soon. Captain Brontide, or Brontide for short.
Outpost Scene__line_10: Brontide        := I hope we'll be able to collaborate in the future. Now, with formalities out of the way, what can I do for you, Cadet?
Outpost Scene__line_11: <PLAYERNAME>	:= Well, I thought I'd introduce myself and see what the Outpost had to offer, it looks like a really impressive building.
Outpost Scene__line_12: Brontide		:= You don't know the half of it. Seriously, this is only half the building, the rest is embedded deep into the island.
Outpost Scene__line_13: <PLAYERNAME>	:= Wow! What's all the way down there?
Outpost Scene__line_14: Brontide		:= Well the training grounds are just down the staircase on the opposite side of this room.
Outpost Scene__line_15: Brontide        := For now though, feel free to take a look around, I'll join you to make sure everyone is on their best behavior.
Outpost Scene__line_16: <PLAYERNAME>	:= That would be great, let's about face!
Outpost Scene__line_17: Brontide		:= That means turn around. Haha, I like the spirit though!

SummerEventHub__line_69: <PLAYERNAME> := Oh, hello, can I help you?
SummerEventHub__line_71: Lucien := Why indeed you can! I am Lucien, a traveller from far afield, I come to Cloverton on Sabbatical and seek the aid of an experienced farmhand.

SummerEventHub__line_83: Beth := One invoking the nature of harvest and care for the land. We have many skills and can manage some agricultural feats on our own but we aren’t specialists like you darlin’.
SummerEventHub__line_89: "No, this sounds way too freaky for me":
SummerEventHub__line_93: Lucien := I see... I understand your decision, we will be here all week, but please understand, the influence of the book means that we cannot remain after the mid-week of summer has ended.
SummerEventHub__line_94: Beth := These take time to grow, so you must approach us soon if you change your mind.
SummerEventHub__line_99: Lucien := Take these seeds, grow them and then when they have reached fruition they will open a portal into my tome. From there gather your mightiest comrades, overcome the beasts within and you shall gain a great prize.
SummerEventHub__line_109: Lucien := A powerful tincture that can make any crop reach harvest in but a moment.
SummerEventHub__line_111: Beth := A kind of super fertilizer if you will. That’s an oversimplification but it’s to the point really.
SummerEventHub__line_113: Lucien := hope to see you soon <PLAYERNAME>.
SummerEventHub__line_119: Lucien := You must be more careful, we do not have time for mistakes, I shall grant you one more.

SummerEventHub__line_146: Lucien := Heh, you’ll excuse me I hope. I like to make light of things to keep the dark from creeping in.
SummerEventHub__line_148: <PLAYERNAME> := It’s cool, but seriously why is everything so weedy?
SummerEventHub__line_150: Beth := The power of the Emeraldicon has reached its zenith. In time we hope to drain it off so that we may settle somewhere.
SummerEventHub__line_151: Lucien := But as it is the tome poses a dire threat to each world it in habits.
SummerEventHub__line_153: <PLAYERNAME> := Well that isn’t ominous at all, you guys can keep it in control right?
SummerEventHub__line_155: Lucien := Indeed, we’ll simply leave before it reaches critical mass.
SummerEventHub__line_156: Beth := The plants will recede and things will return to normal.
SummerEventHub__line_158: <PLAYERNAME> := And I will have some cool fertilizer.
SummerEventHub__line_164: Lucien := Excellent, as you can see the page contains the story of one of the Emeraldicon’s protectors and the fate they could not escape.
SummerEventHub__line_165: Beth := Should you so choose you can enter the tome and do battle.
SummerEventHub__line_166: Lucien := Do you wish to face the godseed in mortal combat?
SummerEventHub__line_175: Lucien := Then let the contest begin!
SummerEventHub__line_182: Beth := No shame in being safe Darlin’.
SummerEventHub__line_183: Lucien := Indeed, return to me should you wish to face the godseed in combat.
SummerEventHub__line_188: Lucien := They are pages from the Emeraldicon.
SummerEventHub__line_189: Beth := Dear I do believe we’ll have to be more descriptive.
SummerEventHub__line_192: Lucien := I am a Keeper of The Godseed and caretaker of the Emeraldicon.
SummerEventHub__line_196: Beth := It is something of a problem.
SummerEventHub__line_198: Lucien := It is an artifact of terrible power, a creation so incredible its legacy has brought ruin across a thousand thousand worlds.
SummerEventHub__line_199: Beth := We keep its power in check.
SummerEventHub__line_200: Lucien := At one point I feared the burden would crush me beneath its dire responsibility and that I would go the way of my predecessors.
SummerEventHub__line_204: Beth := Indeedy! Together we carry the weight of the tome and the imprisoned Godseed.
SummerEventHub__line_208: Lucien := They are keys, access to the slumbering god of the bloom inside and its protectors. Defeat them in mortal combat and harness its power.
SummerEventHub__line_209: Beth := You’ll get an extract that can make any seed reach fruition in a moment, and you should be able to grow them and fight the guardians so long as we are here.

SummerEventHub__line_223: Lucien := We’d need to deal with this tome issue however, at least one page would have to be grown before we could find some time to share our bed.

Yonten Intro Before__line_5: Jubelle			:= Morning Yonten. I have a Frontiersman here with me. This is <PLAYERNAME>. <PLAYERNAME>, this is Yonten.
Yonten Intro Before__line_6: Yonten			:= Greetings! I'm Yonten the shopkeeper.
Yonten Intro Before__line_9: This is a real-life yeti! The yeti are so rare they're almost legend.
Yonten Intro Before__line_10: Granted, those legends are mostly about derelict ships with suspicious claw marks and blizzards engulfing towns in the middle of July.
Yonten Intro Before__line_11: You feel a chill running down your spine. It turns out that the air around Yonten is pleasantly cool.
Yonten Intro Before__line_14: <PLAYERNAME>    := Yes! No. No, you didn't. I was just surprised is all.
Yonten Intro Before__line_15: He scratches the back of his neck.
Yonten Intro Before__line_16: Yonten			:= Ah, yes. It happens all the time. I am an unusually tall fellow.
Yonten Intro Before__line_17: And a yeti. But you can't tell him that.
Yonten Intro Before__line_18: Yonten			:= If you ever need me, I'll be behind the counter from sunrise to sunset, six days a week, for all of your salt and biscuit needs.
Yonten Intro Before__line_19: <PLAYERNAME>    := Sound great! I'll be sure to visit often.
Yonten Intro Before__line_20: Yonten			:= I'll be here. Anything else you want to know?
Yonten Intro Before__line_27: "How long do you plan to stay in Cloverton?":
Yonten Intro Before__line_33: Yonten	:=	I used to be a sheriff before. Now, I administrate some of the Union's local dealings. That doesn't mean I won't take my shopkeeping duty seriously.
Yonten Intro Before__line_37: Yonten := I don't plan to be a shopkeeper forever, though. One day - I'll leave. Maybe I'll go further into the Frontier, maybe back home. Maybe...
Yonten Intro Before__line_38: His face changes for a second. It seems like an unpleasant memory flashed in his mind.
Yonten Intro Before__line_39: Yonten := Sorry. I'm getting melancholic. Too much time under the hot sun. Anyway, come visit whenever you need something. Chances are, we'll have you covered.
Yonten Intro Before__line_43: Yonten := Pleasure meeting you. Hope to see you soon.
Vodan Market Day__line_4: You find yourself looking at a venerable harpy matron. She spends a few brief moments cooing at some nearby eggs and stroking them before turning to face you.
Vodan Market Day__line_6: // pick one of these at random as a greeting.
Vodan Market Day__line_8: r- Vodan := Ah, what a fine, strong looking guilder! Have you come here in search of new blood for your line?
Vodan Market Day__line_9: r- Vodan := Oh, now what is this pretty thing in front of me?
Vodan Market Day__line_10: r- Vodan := Ah, what a handsome human that has come to see me!
Vodan Market Day__line_11: r- Vodan := You look like you have good, strong hands...
Vodan Market Day__line_12: r- Vodan := You look like you know a thing or two about making eggs~
Vodan Market Day__line_25: "Sure let me see what you got.":
Vodan Market Day__line_34: Vodan := Children, or perhaps more accurately, eggs. I can offer you unappraised eggs if you feel like being surprised by your new child’s uniqueness, ones from specific lines, and appraised eggs if you’re feeling finicky about your offspring’s traits.
Vodan Market Day__line_36: Vodan := Can I offer you a new source of joy in your life?
Vodan Market Day__line_50: Vodan presses a taloned hand to her beak and chuckles.
Vodan Market Day__line_52: Vodan := I am a lady with a great love for the world and all the people in it. Eggs and children in particular. It is my intent to provide a loving nest and warm feathers for every child I can. Sadly I cannot fit every egg I have in my nest, not that I haven’t tried!
Vodan Market Day__line_54: Vodan := But I can always vett clients and ensure that any unloved or unfortunate eggs can find their way to a loving home.
Vodan Market Day__line_58: Vodan := Ah, I always did like humans, ones with your build in particular. No fur, no scales, no feathers, smooth and innocent, like a fresh laid egg in need of a warm body to shelter them.
Vodan Market Day__line_60: <PLAYERNAME> := Sounds like you’ve made an egg or two in your time.
Vodan Market Day__line_62: Vodan := I do know a thing about the birds and the bees yes~ You should ask Bella some time, but alas, I do have a business to run sweetie.
Vodan Market Day__line_63: Vodan := Now can I interest you in my services?
Jaero Scene__line_5: The moment the door opens, you hear the sound of a chime rhythmically tinkling against itself just above your head, and the sound of a babbling brook echoing throughout the building.
Jaero Scene__line_6: It's clear why. The walls of the lower floor are a series of hydroponic tiers, cascading from upper layers to lower ones. Each one is a host to beautiful succulents, flowers, mosses, ferns, and reeds.
Jaero Scene__line_8: Jaero			:= A beautiful bounty of biodiverse plants patiently persisting through practiced prudence?
Jaero Scene__line_9: <PLAYERNAME>    := Yeah, that! Woah, what are you?
Jaero Scene__line_10: In your rapture you didn't notice that one of the most colorful flora in the room was actually a person, with a delicately muted complexion, and a mane of vibrant yellow petals.
Jaero Scene__line_11: Jaero			:= Ah, but where are my manners, let me just say it is a very good honor to meet you, and you may call me Jaero. Jaero the Mandrake.
Jaero Scene__line_12: <PLAYERNAME>    := Oh, a Mandrake! I'm sorry about that, I haven't yet had the honor of meeting your kind.
Jaero Scene__line_13: Jaero			:= Well, then I will consider myself lucky as a lark to be the first. So tell me, what brings you to my humble house of horticulture?
Jaero Scene__line_14: <PLAYERNAME>    := I'm <PLAYERNAME>, the new Union Farmer assigned to this town. I thought I'd introduce myself to everyone!
Jaero Scene__line_15: Jaero			:= Then you'll likely see me often, and be one of my most commonplace clients. For you see, I sell seeds and both plants in bloom, and ungerminated.
Jaero Scene__line_16: <PLAYERNAME>    := Oh! Then I'll definitely be by soon, my farm will need plenty of seeds! Especially some, special, sprouting... specimens?
Jaero Scene__line_17: Jaero			:= Or perhaps some venerable varieties of vegetables?
Jaero Scene__line_19: Jaero			:= Of course. Perhaps I'll even have some tea prepared for you next time. Until then, have a delightful day!

ToKatarinaStation__line_3: Captain := Ah, <PLAYERNAME>! Ye be back already? Hopin' ye could catch a ride over ta that Dungeon Island, I expect?

ProvisionDay__line_45: Clara := I'm sorry, but it's the end of the season. I must be going...
ProvisionDay__line_55: Clara := Yes, you look strong and reasonably inclined towards helping your fellow man (and monster.)
ProvisionDay__line_67: Clara := Ah, the Frontiersman, heard the call of the people yet?
ProvisionDay__line_77: Clara := Ah, I see. I’ll be here if you change your mind.
ProvisionDay__line_81: Clara := Excellent, do you know why we celebrate Provisions Day Frontiersman?
ProvisionDay__line_90: Clara := When winter comes very few crops can grow, as such people who have failed to farm properly will starve unless their neighbors are very kind.
ProvisionDay__line_92: <PLAYERNAME> := I mean, that can’t be much of a problem any more can it?
ProvisionDay__line_94: Clara := Bad nobles can also hoard food and create famines to drive up food prices.
ProvisionDay__line_96: <PLAYERNAME> := Good thing the Union puts a stop to that and keeps a surplus to feed the needy.
ProvisionDay__line_98: Clara := Indeed, so in memoriam of winters past, and in the interests of making the world a better place, the Central Kingdom distributes food and simple goods to the frontier.
ProvisionDay__line_100: Clara := Which leads me to the main point.
ProvisionDay__line_104: Clara := A group of pirates are trying to steal Provisions Day supplies. This will have a terrible effect on downtrodden people who have need of those goods.
ProvisionDay__line_108: Clara := We’ll be going to Big Crest first.
ProvisionDay__line_110: <PLAYERNAME> := Hey I’ve heard of that one, it’s a town meant for large monster folk.
ProvisionDay__line_112: Clara := Quite, their needs are often overlooked as they tend to be somewhat specialized. Thankfully Big Crest is proving to be an excellent way to address the problems that face the large folk.
ProvisionDay__line_114: <PLAYERNAME> := I do like it when people get what they need.
ProvisionDay__line_116: Clara := Come on, there’s a lot of food and plus sized items at stake here.
ProvisionDay__line_126: // return player to cloverton, now with table full of food.
ProvisionDay__line_135: Clara := And it looks like the locals have started the feast!
ProvisionDay__line_137: <PLAYERNAME> := I bet Thrund is behind this. He's talented enough to keep that table full for the rest of the season.
ProvisionDay__line_139: Clara := It is quite impressive. Yet before you tuck in, I'm afraid we still have work to do.
ProvisionDay__line_140: Clara := If you need to do something before our next trip, then now is your chance.
ProvisionDay__line_162: Clara := We'll be going to Peregrine’s Gate next.
ProvisionDay__line_164: <PLAYERNAME> := Oh hey, I’ve been there before! It’s one of the most prosperous and successful trading cities in the world!
ProvisionDay__line_166: Clara := Indeed, yet there's also a great many impoverished people there, as well as overcrowding. The Union is trying to alleviate these issues with provisions, but also by offering to help individuals move to settling sites with more opportunities.
ProvisionDay__line_168: <PLAYERNAME> := It’s a good thing there are ways for the less fortunate to get help when they need it!
ProvisionDay__line_170: Clara := Indeed. Now let’s make sure they’re not left out in the cold tonight!
ProvisionDay__line_178: // Return player to cloverton, presents have been added to the tree.
ProvisionDay__line_189: Clara := That they do. Some merchants gave us those in gratitude for our valiant efforts. Don't get too distracted though, there's still one more journey to make.
ProvisionDay__line_190: Clara := Here's your chance to run any errands before we make our last trip.
ProvisionDay__line_216: Clara := We’ll be going to Silentville.
ProvisionDay__line_217: <PLAYERNAME> := The town where they train witches?
ProvisionDay__line_218: Clara := Indeed, they’ve been given some much needed amenities and a handful of obscure occult supplies.
ProvisionDay__line_219: <PLAYERNAME> := Always wanted to learn how to do magic as a child but then I learned that magic drains a human's life force.
ProvisionDay__line_220: Clara:= True, the sacrifice that human magi make for knowledge is profound.
ProvisionDay__line_221: <PLAYERNAME> := All the more reason to make sure they get the support they deserve, and that no one ruins their Provision Day!
ProvisionDay__line_232: Brume := Ah, looky here, lackeys of the central kingdoms!
ProvisionDay__line_233: Clara := Brume! What are you doing here? Still stealing provisions to distribute as you see fit?
ProvisionDay__line_234: Brume := Why not!? The Central Kingdoms produce more than they could ever possibly consume!
ProvisionDay__line_235: Clara := These goods are already slated for citizens in need!
ProvisionDay__line_236: Brume := The people beyond the kingdoms' islands need those goods more than the Ivory Tower’s pets!
ProvisionDay__line_237: Clara := Please stop him <PLAYERNAME>! I’ll keep the rest of his goons at bay.
ProvisionDay__line_249: Brume := I can’t believe I was defeated by a farmer and an ambulant doll.
ProvisionDay__line_252: Clara := The prison transport is full... and I know Brume isn't the mastermind behind the other thefts.
ProvisionDay__line_253: Brume := Heh, no... I was just trying to redistribute some of these provisions to those the kingdoms ignore.
ProvisionDay__line_254: <PLAYERNAME> := Wait so you’re not behind the attacks on the Provision ships?
ProvisionDay__line_255: Brume := Nope! Why would you think that?
ProvisionDay__line_256: <PLAYERNAME> := You were taking provisions and fighting soldiers and stuff!
ProvisionDay__line_257: Brume := Well, admittedly I did knock out a guard... or five... but just so I could snag a surplus shipment... or seven. But I’m certainly not the one knocking over entire ships to steal everything in them.
ProvisionDay__line_258: Clara := No, sadly the provision ships make for excellent targets. There's no shortage of ruffians seeking to plunder them every year.
ProvisionDay__line_259: Brume := It’s a holiday for all, criminal and citizen alike!
ProvisionDay__line_260: <PLAYERNAME> := So wait... What? You were just trying to take these provisions to help out other people?
ProvisionDay__line_261: Clara := Brume actually used to have my job. However he eventually started diverting shipments to people outside of Union territories: food, medicine, the odd bag of stuffed animals.
ProvisionDay__line_263: Clara := Maybe, but so do the ones the goods are meant for. It's not our call to decide who gets what!
ProvisionDay__line_264: You decide you should interrupt before this escalates.
ProvisionDay__line_265: <PLAYERNAME> := Okay... To fend off an argument you two probably have every year, let’s cut to what we’re going to do with Brume.
ProvisionDay__line_267: Clara := If you promise to trouble us no further... then I’ll let you go Brume, you and your men. You are still helping people... citizen and rogue alike.
ProvisionDay__line_268: Brume := I always do Clara, you know that. And fine... I’ll stop stealing from the Union. It'll be more trouble, but we can focus on already stolen goods for the rest of this season.
