<PLAYERNAME> := Thanks, I’ll keep that in mind. So long, Brontide!
Brontide gives a salute with a friendly flourish at the end.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_4: You approach Minerva, who is peering at her ledger from only a few inches away.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_6: She startles for a moment before she looks you over.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_7: Minerva := Oh, <PLAYERNAME>. You will have to excuse me. I did not see you approaching.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_9: Minerva := Yes, well, please avoid sneaking up on me again, dear.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_10: Minerva straightens herself up before continuing, her hand moving up and adjusting her narrow pair of glasses.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_11: Minerva := Now then, hasty greetings aside, I was hoping that I might run into you today, <PLAYERNAME>.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_13: Minerva := What is this I hear about you getting into a violent altercation with some feral Catfolk, hmm? Were you never taught as a child to avoid such violence?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_14: <PLAYERNAME> := Huh? But it happened so fast, I couldn't-
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_15: Minerva now reaches over and grabs your ear, tugging you closer to her. Her avian features towering over you.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_17: Minerva := And, what's this new business that I have been hearing about?! You, planning on gallivanting off to a Dungeon Isle and... and... Cavorting around with ne'er-do-wells?!
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_26: Minerva lets go of your ear, but it keeps stinging.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_27: Minerva := Oh, well that is a relief to hear, dear. I should have known better than to take those marine's chit-chat into consideration.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_28: <PLAYERNAME> := Oh, well Sieger said I could go. But, I don't think I really want to. Fighting those Catfolk was already an ordeal.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_29: Minerva := Well, that is wise of you, dear. More people your age should take after you. It is far better to settle down, and avoid such frivolous and dangerous pursuits.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_31: Minerva := To avoid them as much as possible. The inherent risk is acceptable for the good work that we do. And, the good work that you do, farming and cultivating.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_32: <PLAYERNAME> := Yeah, that is the main reason I was sent here, I guess.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_33: Minerva := That is a relief to hear, dear.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_34: <PLAYERNAME> := Did you really worry about me that much?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_35: Minerva := Somewhat. Someone has to look after the younger members of the Union, such as yourself and Tabbitha.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_36: <PLAYERNAME> := Hehe, well thanks! Was there anything else you wanted to speak to me about?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_37: Minerva := No, that was about the sum of it. Good day to you, <PLAYERNAME>. I have used up enough of your time already, so run along now.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_43: Minerva tugs on your ear harder, like a school teacher disciplining a bad student.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_45: Minerva := 'What's wrong with that?' Oh dear, I can not believe my ears. You and Tabbitha are going to put me into an early grave.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_46: Minerva := There are plenty reasons not to go, each more terrible in outcome than the last. You could be scratched, shot, impaled, imploded, burned, bludgeoned, or worse!
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_47: <PLAYERNAME> := Okay I get it, please let go!
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_48: Minerva finally lets go. Your ear stings, from her grip her and chiding equally.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_49: <PLAYERNAME> := (Damn that smarts) You must be really worried.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_50: Minerva := I heard that. Mind your manners, <PLAYERNAME>. And yes, worried. I feel I must: someone has to look after such daredevil youths, like yourself and Tabbitha.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_51: <PLAYERNAME> := Well, be that as it may; It's not like the Union didn't train me for this kind of work. A Frontiersman has to be a jack of all trades when it comes to the Frontier.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_52: Minerva := Even so, it is a dangerous enterprise you are walking into, young <PLAYERNAME>.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_56: Minerva := I still strongly stress that you would be better served settling down, and learning to culture an appreciation for the quiet moments found on the Frontier.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_57: Minerva's gaze narrows. Even if her demeanor has lightened, her attitude towards you visiting the Dungeon Island is made very clear.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_58: <PLAYERNAME> := I will, don't worry about that! It's not everyday that you get a chance to explore something like a Dungeon Island, though. I've only ever read bits and pieces about them.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_59: Minerva := I see. Well! I can not stop you, dear. So, I will be forced to simply wish you good health, and to return safe and sound AND in one piece.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_60: <PLAYERNAME> := I promise to do my best. When I get back I'll tell you all about it.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_61: Minerva := Please do not. Now run along, I have much more work to attend to, dear, as I am sure you do as well. Take care for now, and may the Ether lead you back safely.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_62: Minerva nods her head, bidding you farewell as she turns back to her duties, the feathers on the back of her head ruffled.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Minerva__line_67: Tab := Before you go I made you this! An adventuring picnic basket of goodies! When you're feeling tired or hurt eat some of these and they'll help you out!
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_8: <PLAYERNAME>    := Forgemaker save me! What is that?!
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_11: Jubelle			:= That would be her name. It's just Kreyton.
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_12: The island rumbles as a giant figure approaches.
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_14: You answer with a whimper. There is no other sound you can muster for this towering ogre woman.
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_15: Jubelle			:= Hello, Ms. Kreyton. This is a new resident to Cloverton. They're a farmer like yourself.
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_16: The ogress stops, looking at you intently - and then smiles from ear to ear. Sincere happiness flashes in her huge, honest face.
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_17: Kreyton			:= A landowner! Like me! It is the most amazing thing! I am Kreyton! Kreyton, the ogress, landowner of this cheery farm!
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_18: You gulp. She's extremely loud, and extremely large, but she has a friendly, pleasant voice.
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_19: You give the farm a good look around and are taken back.
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_21: <PLAYERNAME>    := Honestly, it's amazing. The plants are all perfectly cared for, and the soil isn't tired in the slightest. Are you the only one working here?
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_22: Kreyton			:= Aye. It's my heart, this farm. It feeds me! And anyone hungry. So I keep it healthy. If yer ever hungry, come! I also sell things. Jerkey. Fresh crackers. Good for a journey!
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_23: Jubelle			:= Kreyton has a gentle touch when it comes to crops. She certainly knows her stuff.
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_24: You calm down. Cloverton is full of surprises and you're beginning to learn looks are deceiving. Kreyton is a big-hearted woman that knows how to give the land some good ol' tender loving care.
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_25: <PLAYERNAME>    := I'll be sure to visit from time to time. Just remember, I'm a farmer too! I hope we can have some friendly competition!
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_26: <PLAYERNAME>    := If I ever need anything from you, I'll be sure to let you know!
Kreyton Before farm Scene__line_29: Jubelle			:= Come along then, there's still much to do.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_0: //-When Player enters The Monarch Inn for the first after the Farm-
Camellia Scene After farm__line_3: A woman appears next to you out of nowhere.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_6: The woman standing before you looks very fancy. Fancy in many, many places. All the right places.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_7: Camellia		:= Well hello there, <PLAYERNAME>. Admiring the scenery?
Camellia Scene After farm__line_9: <PLAYERNAME>    := Ah! Yes. Cloverton is rather breathtaking. I'm sorry. How did you know my name?
Camellia Scene After farm__line_10: Camellia		:= I know a great many things. I also have many expectations of you, my dear.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_11: She presses a finger to her lips as she gives you a coy smirk.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_12: <PLAYERNAME>    := Expectations! Sure, 100%!  What sort of expectations?
Camellia Scene After farm__line_13: Camellia		:= Eager and straight to business! Wonderful! I want a local supplier to stock my bar and kitchen. I plan to have a lot of coin invested in your farm, <PLAYERNAME>.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_14: Camellia		:= Not immediately, of course. Eventually I'd like to serve Cloverton Pleepo Gratin with a glass of Cloverton Cheronne Blau. I would love to take pride in something locally grown and locally made.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_16: <PLAYERNAME>    := You will! I may be young, but I know my way around a farm patch! Cloverton can count on me!
Camellia Scene After farm__line_17: You give a quick flex of your arms and pose for Lady Camellia.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_18: Lady Camellia responds with a thin smile, immediately hidden behind her fan.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_19: <PLAYERNAME>    := May I ask who you are?
Camellia Scene After farm__line_20: Camellia		:= But of course, my dear! I am Lady Camellia. Owner of this fine establishment. I also help run things around Cloverton.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_21: <PLAYERNAME>    := Looks like I'll be seeing you often then! I'll do my best!
Camellia Scene After farm__line_23: Camellia		:= On to it, then. It was a pleasure to meet you in person, <PLAYERNAME>. Enjoy the rest of the day.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_25: Lady Camellia leaves and reclines on her lounge chair.
Camellia Scene After farm__line_26: You let out a sigh. You hope you weren't too awkward.
SiegerTalkAfterDungeon__line_3: It takes several hours of travel, and a strong enough cage to reassure the Captain, but Cloverton finally comes into view as you round a bank of clouds.
SiegerTalkAfterDungeon__line_4: By the time you set your boots down on the dock, you’re explaining to the town guards the situation. Word is sent to Sieger and, before long, the creature you subdued is moved to the bottom level of the Union Outpost.
SiegerTalkAfterDungeon__line_7: //[At this point fade into the scene in the town square]
SiegerTalkAfterDungeon__line_8: Sieger := Citizens of Cloverton, fellow marines of the Union, no doubt you have felt unsafe as of late. However, with the accomplishments of our newest member of the Union, you may wake up to a safer dawn.
SiegerTalkAfterDungeon__line_9: A few people gawk at the creature you fought as it rests in the nearby metal cage.
SiegerTalkAfterDungeon__line_10: Sieger := So, let’s hear it for Frontiersman <PLAYERNAME>! Who, has not only landed on their feet, but hit the ground running in their duties!
SiegerTalkAfterDungeon__line_12: //[If the design changes to using the dialogue during the cutscene, here would be a good place.]
SiegerTalkAfterDungeon__line_14: //The crowd gives you an applause. Mixtures of cheer and claps from all but the most curmudgeonly. As they cheer, Sieger leans in a bit closer to speak to you.
SiegerTalkAfterDungeon__line_15: Sieger := Stand proud today, <PLAYERNAME>. You may not have caught those poachers, but you saved a comrade's life, and likely more. Now, why don’t you take it easy for the rest of the day?
SiegerTalkAfterDungeon__line_16: Sieger pats you on the back and gestures for you to excuse yourself. It’s been quite the day.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_5: Jubelle := Ah, I had a feeling I might see you, Sailor!
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_6: Jubelle stands at attention, her tail flicking jauntily as she watches you approach.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_9: Jubelle := Well, yes! Sieger told me about-
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_11: <PLAYERNAME> := About how I got into a brawl with some Catfolk that crash landed on my farm? No biggie!
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_12: Jubelle := Cats?! He told me to prepare you for an expedition in search of some *Poachers* in the *Savannah*!
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_15: Jubelle’s flicking tail falls still, and her brow furrows with the kind of worry only an overworked bureaucrat can muster.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_16: Jubelle := When I heard about a newfish getting into a scuffle, I didn’t think it was *you*, of all people. Are you alright?
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_18: "Don’t worry, I took care of it!":
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_20: "I took my lumps, to say the least...":
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_25: <PLAYERNAME> := Don’t sweat it, Jubelle! I took care of it before the Marines even showed up.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_26: Jubelle lets out a sigh of relief.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_28: Jubelle := Well, it’s good to know you can handle yourself in a skirmish, especially if you plan on tracking down those Poachers in the Savannah.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_30: <PLAYERNAME> := Don’t worry about me, Jubelle! I’ve got a whole Ivory Tower seminar on survival under my belt.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_31: Jubelle := Well, now I am worried.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_32: Jubelle brings a hand to her snout as a chuckle escapes her lips.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_33: Jubelle := Sorry, Sailor, but I’ll feel much more at ease once you have some *real* experience under that belt of yours…
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_38: <PLAYERNAME> := I took my lumps, to say the least…
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_39: Jubelle’s anxiety visibly spikes as her hackles raise on the back of her head.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_41: Jubelle := You need to be careful, <PLAYERNAME>! Without the proper inoculations, you could come down with cat scratch fever from their dirty claws.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_42: Jubelle := Are you *sure* you’re ready for an expedition into the Savannah? Poachers are very dangerous people, you know… Far more dangerous than a couple of Cats.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_44: <PLAYERNAME> := I have to admit, it was my first time getting jumped first thing in the morning. Next time, I’ll definitely be ready!
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_45: Jubelle := Well, alright. Just remember that coming back safe and sound is the first priority of any mission, so don’t hesitate to return home and rest if you’re injured in the field.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_46: Jubelle takes a deep breath, and smooths out her raised hackles with a well-practiced swish of her hand.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_51: Jubelle := Where are my manners, talking your ears off like that. We should discuss the expedition Sieger has planned…
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_52: Jubelle straightens herself out and holds up her trusty clipboard.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_58: Jubelle := Welcome back, Sailor! Anything you need?
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_59: <PLAYERNAME> := I need a refresher on this expedition to the Savannah…
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_60: Jubelle’s tail swishes happily as she raises her trusty clipboard.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_61: Jubelle := I’d be happy to brief you again, <PLAYERNAME>! What did you want to know?
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_66: "What can you tell me about the Poachers?":
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_70: "How do I get to the Savannah from here?":
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_76: <PLAYERNAME> := What can you tell me about the Poachers, Jubelle?
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_77: Jubelle’s ears fold back as she considers the question.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_78: Jubelle := Poachers are often very dangerous and difficult to apprehend. If they’re any good, they’ll be relying more on subterfuge to meet their goals rather than force: Bear Traps, Lures, Decoys and the like.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_80: <PLAYERNAME> := They definitely don’t teach you about *that* at Ivory Tower!
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_81: Jubelle := Part of your objective on this expedition will be to disable as many of these traps as you can. The Poachers have laid many of them throughout the Savannah, so I advise that you proceed with caution.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_84: Jubelle := The way they’re acting with such impunity, I just know there’s a healthy flow of dark money involved…
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_88: <PLAYERNAME> := Are you coming with me on the mission? Your logistical insights would be useful in the field.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_90: Jubelle’s tail swishes excitedly, but she suddenly becomes sheepish…
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_91: Jubelle := Sorry, Sailor, but I have to decline… My post is in Cloverton, and there’s much to accomplish here.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_93: Jubelle := That doesn’t mean you should head out alone, however. A few of the locals are quite capable, and would be happy to help hunt down the Poachers.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_96: Jubelle := Well, there are a few shopkeeps with combat experience: Yonten was once a Union man himself, and Jaero is a student of the Falling Leaves school of martial arts.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_97: Jubelle := There’s also Kreyton the Ogre. As a farmer, she has a personal stake in the town’s safety, and her immense strength would surely come in handy.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_98: Jubelle := Finally, it seems Lieutenant Commander Brontide has taken a liking to you recently… He’s personally volunteered to serve under your command.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_100: <PLAYERNAME> := If nothing else, I do give a solid first impression! Thanks for the tips, Jubelle.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_102: Jubelle := Just doing my duty, Sailor.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_106: <PLAYERNAME> := So, how do I get to the Savannah from Cloverton?
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_108: Jubelle := Following any preparation you wish to do, simply head here to the town docks and talk to the Union Captain. He’ll provide transportation to *Katarina Station*, our closest outpost.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_109: <PLAYERNAME> := Huh? I thought Cloverton was the main Union outpost in the area.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_111: Jubelle := It’s no surprise you haven’t heard of it, sadly. Katarina Station may have been a prominent Mobile Base in her day, but that was before the Crystal Wars…
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_113: <PLAYERNAME> := Whoah, a Mobile Base! Never thought I’d see one of those.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_115: Jubelle := Although the Union still controls the station, I’m afraid it’s become little more than a landing pad for wildcat miners as of late.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_116: <PLAYERNAME> := Sounds like Katarina Station could use a Frontiersman’s touch!
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_118: Jubelle := Stationmaster Catarella has been desperate for help rehabilitating the station ever since her assignment. You should seek her out when you arrive.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_124: <PLAYERNAME> := I better get a move on if I want to catch those Poachers!
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_126: Jubelle := Your enthusiasm is infectious, <PLAYERNAME>! I admit I was anxious at first, but you have a way of putting the heart at ease.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_128: Jubelle := Remember, while you’re in the field you should disable any traps that you find. Making the local monster habitats safe again will discourage them from wandering into Cloverton anymore.
Act_1_Part_1_Jubelle__line_130: <PLAYERNAME> := Whelp, it’s finally time to put that diploma to good use!
Player_Monologue__line_3: <PLAYERNAME> := I will never get used to this. The Wizards are always inventing new ways to blow our minds. This is normal for them!
Player_Monologue__line_4: <PLAYERNAME> := I mean, look at this! Where are we!?
Player_Monologue__line_6: You look about you at the void around you.
Player_Monologue__line_8: <PLAYERNAME> := Whatever this Giev fellow wants, it must be important. We seem to be traveling really far in the space of an instant.
Player_Monologue__line_10: You stare out at the vast world in front of you. From here it looks like you are surrounded by sky both above and below.
Player_Monologue__line_12: <PLAYERNAME> := Wizards are absolutely amazing. If not for them, our incubators and many other technologies wouldn't exist, but this... This is amazing!
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_6: The moment the door opens, you hear the sound of a chime rhythmically tinkling against itself just above your head, and the sound of a babbling brook echoing throughout the building.
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_7: It's clear why. The walls of the lower floor are a series of hydroponic tiers, cascading from upper layers to lower ones. Each one is a host to beautiful succulents, flowers, mosses, ferns, and reeds.
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_9: Jaero			:= A beautiful bounty of biodiverse plants patiently persisting through practiced prudence?
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_10: <PLAYERNAME>    := Yeah, that! Woah, what are you?
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_11: In your rapture you didn't notice that one of the most colorful flora in the room was actually a person, with a delicately muted complexion, and a mane of vibrant yellow petals.
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_12: Jaero			:= Ah, but where are my manners, let me just say it is a very good honor to meet you, and you may call me Jaero. Jaero the Mandrake.
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_13: <PLAYERNAME>    := Oh, a Mandrake! I'm sorry about that, I haven't yet had the honor of meeting your kind.
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_14: Jaero			:= Well, then I will consider myself lucky as a lark to be the first. So tell me, what brings you to my humble house of horticulture?
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_15: <PLAYERNAME>    := I'm <PLAYERNAME>, the new Union Farmer assigned to this town. I thought I'd introduce myself to everyone!
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_16: Jaero			:= Then you'll likely see me often, and be one of my most commonplace clients. For you see, I sell seeds and both plants in bloom, and ungerminated.
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_17: <PLAYERNAME>    := Oh! Then I'll definitely be by soon, my farm will need plenty of seeds! Especially some, special, sprouting... specimens?
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_18: Jaero			:= Or perhaps some venerable varieties of vegetables?
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_20: Jaero			:= Of course. Perhaps I'll even have some tea prepared for you next time. Until then, have a delightful day!
Jaero Scene With Jubelle__line_21: Jubelle         := Nice to see everyone hitting it off so well. C'mon there's more to see.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_4: <PLAYERNAME> := Hey Yonten. You're just who I wanted to see!
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_5: Yonten := I thought you might show up, and I have an idea what it's about. A few marines were in here earlier.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_6: <PLAYERNAME> := Oh yeah? Did they mention the scuffle I got into with those Catfolk?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_7: Yonten := That, and that Sieger was apparently giving you a request to help deal with some issue.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_8: <PLAYERNAME> := Yep! He thinks the reason the Catfolk were so agitated was due to a group of poachers that have been sighted in the region. He was hoping I could investigate the nearby Dungeon Island.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_9: Yonten's hair stands up on end as you fill in the details for him.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_10: Yonten := Poachers, huh? That's grim business then.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_12: Yonten := You'll have your work cut out for you then. With the poachers, and with that Dungeon Island.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_17: <PLAYERNAME> := Hey Yonten! I was wondering if we could talk more about those poachers.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_18: Yonten := We can if you need to. What did you want to know?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_26: "You seem upset, like this is personal.":
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_37: <PLAYERNAME> := Yeah. I mean, they're obviously bad. I was hoping you'd have insight on them and the Catfolk.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_38: Yonten := I don't think there's too much to add. People like that are lower than plankton.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_42: "What could bring someone to do that?":
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_46: <PLAYERNAME> := You seem to really hate them.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_47: Yonten := Anyone with a conscience would, wouldn't they?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_48: Yonten's calm, contemplative demeanor seems vacant. Instead, he seems on edge, chewing the inside of his cheek.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_49: <PLAYERNAME> := I mean, you seem especially angry. Have poachers shown up around Cloverton since you started manning the store here?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_50: Yonten := No, but they show up in a lot of places along the Frontier. They ply their bloodtrade till they can't, or are forced to stop.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_51: Yonten seems even more tense, his fists clenched.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_55: <PLAYERNAME> := What could bring someone to do that? I mean greed is one thing, but that?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_56: Yonten := Does it matter why? People like that should just be crushed, and destroyed.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_57: Yonten's face turns into a sneer, his teeth and his fangs visible in an aggravated and disgusted display.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_58: <PLAYERNAME> := I certainly don't like them either. Sieger wants them brought back alive if possible, though.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_59: Yonten := So he can put them in the stocks and they'll repent and change? It never works that way.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_60: <PLAYERNAME> := You seem like you know something about that. Have you ever run into poachers before?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_62: You get the feeling Yonten isn't laying all his cards out on the table...
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_66: Yonten takes a deep breath before leaning on the counter.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_67: Yonten := Either way, people like that are bad news, and should be stopped before they hurt anyone else.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_72: <PLAYERNAME> := You seemed like you were speaking from firsthand experience when we started talking about poachers earlier. Why was that?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_74: Yonten's gaze seems distant before his senses return to him.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_75: Yonten := Oh, poachers are terrible people. Of course I don't like them.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_76: <PLAYERNAME> := Well anyone, and everyone, has already said something to that effect. Your anger seems personal though. You used to be a Union Marine, right?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_78: <PLAYERNAME> := Did you ever receive orders to fight poachers?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_81: Yonten := I didn't get orders, I gave them. I used to be a Marine Captain.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_83: Yonten's arms cross as he seems to chew over his next words. The air grows a bit chilly as he moves to speak.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_84: Yonten := Yeah. Back before I was reassigned, I served as the protector of a village on the Frontier. Sort of like a Sheriff. When you do that kind of work you see what poachers can do firsthand.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_85: <PLAYERNAME> := Oh... Were they the worst you dealt with?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_86: Yonten := No, but they were bad all the same.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_87: <PLAYERNAME> := I think I see why you don't like them then.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_88: Yonten := I appreciate it, <PLAYERNAME>, but you won't truly understand what they're like till you see it yourself.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_90: Yonten raises his head and sighs.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_91: Yonten := Anyway, that was all years ago. What matters now is the poaching in the present.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_92: <PLAYERNAME> := Yeah, you're right! Thanks, Yonten, you have a lot of insight I could learn from.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_93: Yonten looks over a nearby case of products, but his mind seems elsewhere.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_98: <PLAYERNAME> := So, want to come with me? I'm sure with someone like you, we could put a stop to the poachers hanging around these parts.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_99: A moment of silence becomes an uncomfortably long pause.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_101: Yonten := Yeah, I think I will come along with you, if you're willing. Poachers are one of the few kinds of people that I won't pull my punches against in a fight.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_102: Yonten punches a fist into the palm of his other hand and a few snowflakes leap out from the space between them, drifting down onto the counter.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_103: Yonten := I don't need to do much to prepare, and my momo can watch the shop any day that we're out.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_105: Yonten := So, whenever you're ready, just talk to Jubelle. I believe she handles all of the logistics in Cloverton.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_110: <PLAYERNAME> := Well, you've given me lots to think about. Want to talk about something else?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_111: Yonten looks back over to you.
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_112: Yonten := Oh, yes. It's grim business, and without a happy ending for now. Hopefully not for much longer though. Was there anything else you needed?
Act_1_Part_1_Dialogue_Yonten__line_113: <PLAYERNAME> := No, I think that was everything. See you later!
Giev_Genodriver_task_received_V2__line_4: Giev is working fervently in his lab as you come in.
Giev_Genodriver_task_received_V2__line_5: <PLAYERNAME> := Hey Giev, I'm back. You wouldn't believe what's been happening around here.
Giev_Genodriver_task_received_V2__line_6: Giev := Would it happen to involve a magitek artifact, or gathering more islands to your lump of dirt?
Giev_Genodriver_task_received_V2__line_7: <PLAYERNAME> := No, but there were poachers and-
Giev_Genodriver_task_received_V2__line_8: Giev looks back down and begins working fervently with his papers and contraptions.
Giev_Genodriver_task_received_V2__line_9: Giev := That's fine and all, but if you're done gallivanting through the bushes and furballs, I have a job for you.
Giev_Genodriver_task_received_V2__line_1: Giev := As I recall, you Frontiersmen are qualified to go into the dungeon. Have you explored very far into it?
Giev_Genodriver_task_received_V2__line_2: <PLAYERNAME> := I've had some experience with it, you could say.
Giev := Very good then. I assume you haven't seen a Wizard's Lab out there recently during your dungeoneering?
<PLAYERNAME> := Uh... No, you're the only one I've had the pleasure of meeting.
Giev := Ah well, it doesn't surprise me, he is often secretive in his endeavors.
Giev := I need you to find a colleague of mine, a rather auspicious fellow and leading expert in antiquities.
<PLAYERNAME> := Him being the only wizard other than you around here?
Giev := Yes, indeed. As one of my brethren, he is quite recognizable. His name is Tro. Be careful around him, though. He is far less composed than I.

Giev := You are quite useful to me, and I would hate to lose an asset such as yourself. The fact that he chose to go into that place to perform his research himself,  rather than use a capable tool such as you, baffles me.
Giev := He never was much of a fighter, you see, always stuck to his books.
Giev_Genodriver_task_received_V2__line_3: You look around the room at all of Giev's books and contraptions and wonder how dedicated this Tro fellow could be for Giev to speak of him in such a way.

Giev := But it is not my place to judge. His research is outstanding, and our joint efforts have led to a few breakthroughs in my research. As it stands, he hasn't gotten back to me on our last agreement, and I would like to know his status.

Giev := Find his lab in the Dungeon Isle and give him this letter. It contains a reminder of some items and documents I requested from him in our last correspondence. No doubt he is lost in his research and has simply neglected to get back to me.
Giev := Do try not to lose it, or die on your way back. I would hate to have to find someone else to do it. Everyone seems to get discouraged when they hear of someone's death on my tasks.
<PLAYERNAME> := Wait, has anyone died doing this?
Giev := That is irrelevant. I will ensure you are properly compensated in some way for your efforts once the job is complete.
Giev := Your prompt assistance on the matter is appreciated. You may go now.
Giev_Genodriver_task_received_V2__line_4: Giev looks up from his notes at you for a moment.
Giev := As I am sure you have already noticed, even on the most peaceful days there is always something that isn't alright.
Giev := I am pursuing many projects at the moment, and need to maintain the utmost focus to ensure none of them return invalid results.
Giev := The amount of effort I have put into these projects is not dissimilar to the amount you put in to gain your title and the farm you now oversee.
Giev := I am sure you can see the reason I am so curt.
Giev := Now, for that task I have for you.
ProvisionDayGroup3Birma__line_6: Birma := All right, watch your hands with that crank hun, you don't wanna burn your hands. The juices and oil are extra hot!
ProvisionDayGroup3Birma__line_7: Clodagh := I can do this! HHHHHHHNGH
ProvisionDayGroup3Birma__line_9: Clodagh strains while trying to turn the crank above the barbeque pit, which does not budge.
ProvisionDayGroup3Birma__line_11: Clodagh := Oughhh... I've worked so hard to finally be able to handle the spitroast this year, but I'm still too soft!
ProvisionDayGroup3Birma__line_12: Birma := Aw hun, you don't have to prove anything. Your softness is perfect just like you are.
ProvisionDayGroup3Birma__line_15: Birma wraps her hand around Clodagh's waist and gives her a smooch.
ProvisionDayGroup3Birma__line_21: Kellam := DEAR GODS WOMAN, THE MEAT IS BURNING!!!
YuugaEventStart__line_2: // NOTE: this event is only triggered when entering from a scene change.
YuugaEventStart__line_3: //       This guarantees there's only two entrances (currently) so i've placed a 'greeter' guard on both. See RoyalMatchmaker NPC Gathering.prefab
YuugaEventStart__line_9: As soon as you set foot in Cloverton, you're immediately greeted by a guard.
YuugaEventStart__line_11: UnionSoldier := Greetings <PLAYERNAME>! If you wouldn't mind, please proceed directly to the town square so that you can receive your Matchmaker assignment from Miss Yuuga.
YuugaEventStart__line_21: // show the crowd gathered around Yuuga for a moment
YuugaEventStart__line_22: Doing as you're told, you quickly find yourself before Yuuga and her floating pot of scented oils.
YuugaEventStart__line_28: Yuuga := And a fine hello to you <PLAYERNAME>! I hope you haven't made any plans just yet, because it's MATCHMAKING time of the year!
YuugaEventStart__line_29: Yuuga := Now, being a Kingdom dictated event, I unfortunately am mandated to give you the whole history and guidelines of this event. Regardless of whether this is your first time or not.
YuugaEventStart__line_30: Yuuga := Let's try to get through this together, shall we?
YuugaEventStart__line_35: "Zone out until the rules are read":
YuugaEventStart__line_42: Yuuga := I, as always, am your splendiferous hostess, Yuuga.
YuugaEventStart__line_43: Yuuga := Years ago, the Kingdom started growing concerned about the growing piracy on the frontier. To combat it, they decided that they could strengthen those outlying communities by "encouraging" bonds among the residents.
YuugaEventStart__line_44: Yuuga := Their brilliant plan - sorry, mandated reading - was to randomly pair up the residents, and force them to associate in what they affectionately termed "dates". The Kingdom generously offered to pay for these dates, but in exchange, anyone that refused would spend a day in jail.
YuugaEventStart__line_45: Yuuga := Which is what brings me here today! I, having a penchant for pushing people together, was offered the title of Royal Matchmaker. Its my job to oversee this event, work a bit of my magic~, and help bind this lovely little town tightly~ together!
YuugaEventStart__line_46: Yuuga := Plus, I take a perverse joy in getting to send the odd curmudgeon, someone who's too petty to enjoy a little bit of government mandated fun, to jail!
YuugaEventStart__line_52: Yuuga := The rules - laws really - for this fun little get together are quite simple. Every citizen will be assigned a random partner. You and that partner are to spend a few hours together, in some manner of activity.
YuugaEventStart__line_53: Yuuga := The ONLY thing being mandated during that activity is that the two of you stay in each others presence. The normal rules of society still apply, so make sure to respect each other! There's no need to get all hot and bothered about any other expectations~ for your "date".
YuugaEventStart__line_55: Yuuga gives you a saucy wink.
YuugaEventStart__line_57: Yuuga := That said~, don't be afraid to put yourself out there! The Kingdom wants everyone in this town to bond~, so try not to pass up any golden opportunities!
YuugaEventStart__line_58: Yuuga := Now, these dates are determined randomly. In the event that you have irreconcilable issues with your partner, then I'm afraid that there is a penalty.
YuugaEventStart__line_59: Yuuga := If you refuse to spend time with your assigned partner, then you will be dragged off to jail for a full day. There, you will spend your Kingdom mandated social time associating with any other spoilsports.
YuugaEventStart__line_60: Yuuga := As a generous offer to those with... picky tastes... I have been allowed to restrict the pool of candidates to suit some preferences.
YuugaEventStart__line_61: Yuuga := Though I personally feel that you'll miss out on some quality time with good friends if you choose to do so.
YuugaEventStart__line_62: Yuuga := And that's pretty much it!

Kreyton Recruit__line_8: <PLAYERNAME> := Wait, you already know about that?
Kreyton Recruit__line_10: Kreyton := 'Course I do! I’m big, strong, and a good back up when Seiger needs help!

Nirya Quest Completed___nodes[0]._stepCh: While the forest was beautiful, I’m grateful to return home and tell everyone about it.
Know-it-all Noble Pre-Quest Completion__: NobleHandler := I told them to stay on the path! I had been telling them ‘stay on the path, if you wander off, you’ll get lost’! Now look at what's happened. If they’d only listened to me, they wouldn’t be Scales knows where!
BoethildeGateClosed___nodes[0]._stepChai: Before you floats a field of debris, levitating in air by unknown means.
I wonder how the debris gets like this… Well, there’s no path forward, so I’ll have to figure something else out.
Rico Post-Battle___nodes[0]._stepChain[1: The wolves turn tail, running with their tails between their legs!
Rico Post-Battle___nodes[0]._stepChain[3: There, that’ll teach you to pick on people!
Rico Post-Battle___nodes[0]._stepChain[4: Ah, mio {0|salvatoru|salvatore|salvatrice}! Thank you! Thank you!
Rico Post-Battle___nodes[0]._stepChain[5: The fox man gives you a nod, as he dusts off his coat.
Rico Post-Battle___nodes[0]._stepChain[6: No problem, it’s what I do!
Rico Post-Battle___nodes[0]._stepChain[7: Now that they’re not nipping at my heels, I can finally figure out just where I am!
Rico Post-Battle___nodes[0]._stepChain[8: You wouldn’t happen to be part of Kalsang’s tour, would you?
Rico Post-Battle___nodes[0]._stepChain[1: Well, you’re nearly back at the camp. They’re waiting for everyone that got lost just over that way.
You direct him back down the path.
Ah, grazie! I shall not waste time in heading back to civilization!
He moves past you, heading down the path to the camp.
Jack Quest Completed___nodes[0]._stepCha: I say, you did a cracking good job! And just in time, I really need to get some of these supplies back to my ice box!
Nirya Missing Nobles___nodes[0]._stepCha: Thank you again, but if I may ask something of you, we need the rest of our compatriots to return before we can leave.
BonesingerWolfSexHub___nodes[2]._stepCha: You’re the Frontiersman, yes? The one responsible for… cleaning up our breeding den?
I am! I couldn’t just let those Homonculi run amock, yanno?
I, and the rest of the pack here, appreciate your assistance. Not everyone is… prepared to fight <color=red>the fomoire</color>.
Is there anything you require in exchange? I have time, now that our members are… preoccupied.
BonesingerWolfSexHub___nodes[3]._optionD: You’re a Bone Singer, right? What does that entail?
Well, if you have the time, I wouldn’t mind learning about your breeding grounds. (Sex)
Nothing right now, but it’s a pleasure! (Leave)
BonesingerWolfSexHub___nodes[4]._stepCha: Nothing right now, but it’s a pleasure!
I assure you, the pleasure was mine.
BonesingerWolfSexHub___nodes[5]._stepCha: You’re a Bone Singer, right? What does that entail?
My role is medicinal in nature. Well… Sort of.
Yes! My talents lie in unlocking the morphological changes in our blood. Changes such as elevating members of our pack into Alpha Wolves are something only Bone Singers, like myself, can facilitate.
I thought Alphas were naturally occurring?
They are, but we sometimes have need for more than the number of Alphas on hand.
When that time comes, it is through the Bone Singers’ connections to <color=blue>The Choir of Fangs</color>, that we may elevate the ferocity of our fellow wolves to that of Alphas.
Wait, back that up. Aren’t you going to explain what <color=blue>The Choir of Fangs</color> is?
It’s… the primordial voices of wolfkind… Do humans not have that?
N-nah, that’s why we write everything down in books!
I was gonna say the same thing… Good to know!
BonesingerWolfSexHub___nodes[6]._stepCha: If you have the time, I wouldn’t mind learning about your breeding grounds.
Hmm… You’re not normally my type, but I’m not against showing you the ways of our breeding dens. Come, let us mate!
BonesingerWolfSexHub___nodes[7]._stepCha: Tell me, what need do you have? I may have some spare time for the savior of our breeding den.
ForestF1CampGroupOf5___nodes[2]._stepCha: So what do you think. Here?
I don’t know about here. Not very defendable is it?
The cuss do you mean not very defendable? It’s plenty defendable! We can build a wall, put a cannon right here. We got good choke points in each direction, good elevation.
If we build a wall, sure. Do we have the supplies to build a wall? I don’t know if the captain will approve the supplies.
WE’RE IN A BLOODY FOREST! PICK UP AN AXE AND MAKE THE SUPPLIES!
There’s no need to yell. We’ve talked about you yelling.
This is the third site we’ve scouted! Every time there’s something not good enough! Tough luck! This is it! I’m not looking at another site!
Yeah I’m kind of done looking too. I don’t need to get shot at again today. That sharpshooter nearly took my head off.
Calm down, he missed.
Barely. And I heard he bullseyed a bunch of scouts who got too close to his area the other day. Wiped them out. No survivors.
Who told you that?
One of the scouts.
Of the party that got wiped out?
Yeah!
The one with no survivors?
…yeah? Um, what's your point?
Blistering barnacles, this damn outfit.
You guys talking about that crazy sniper taking potshots at anyone gets too close to his base?
No we’re talking about your mother.
I heard he winged the captain, didn’t hear anything about him wiping out any scout team.
See, it’s nothing.
Man, I can’t wait to get back to Peregrine Gate. I really want to head to the Milky Matron.
Oh yeah! I really want to spend some time with Clara. I bet she’s needing a milking right now.
You know holstaurs need milking every day right? I’m sure she’s getting it taken care of just fine.
Oh don’t say that!
Can we get off Cow Milkers and focus on building the camp?
Sure, you won’t be able to get me off them come shore leave though!
That reminds me, Mom asked me to say hi to you guys in her last letter.
Oh man I miss your mother.
She makes the best gempas tarts!
Shucks guys, Mom’ll be really happy to hear that!
Huh, your mom? She’s great, but is your sister still single?
I…I hate all of you; every one of you, in a special, different way.
ForestF1CampGroupOf5___nodes[3]._stepCha: Look, it’s that fronty that went off towards the crazed gunman. Hey, did he get you?
I mean, {0|they're|he’s|she’s} right in front of us…
I know, that means we can ask ‘em!
Uh, not last time I checked…
Damn, is that why we saw all those marines heading out a while ago? I was hoping that we stumbled onto another sex cave!
You’re really going to make us look bad in front of the Frontiersman?
You guys talking about that Fronty that we took bets on coming back with no survivors?
Cicerone Repeatable Quest Finished___nod: I sure owe you one! You ever want to cash it in, I'll be over at Katarina Station!
Holy Bell Interaction___nodes[0]._stepCh: The heavy hunk of metal rests in front of you, it’s thickness and size almost daring you to attempt to lift it. The power emanating from it leads you to believe it must be the Holstaur’s bell fragment.
<speed=slow>T-this is bigger than I thought it would be…</speed> There’s no way I can carry this back by hand…
Looking over it, its surface is covered in sweeping curves.Not only that, but the proximity to the bell feels as though it stirs odd feelings in you. You shake your head to dispel them.
Holy Bell Interaction___nodes[2]._stepCh: Feelin’ a touch flush, but I be fine.
Whew! I could go for an ice tea right now…
Yeah, I’m feeling a little horny. Wait, did I say that out loud?
My my, what an interesting curio these Holstaur have.
I fear if I stay here too long, I might bloom. I must concentrate on my mantras.
Ooh, it’s got me girls a little ready to go, but I’ll be fine.
I doth feel my loins protesting, but I shall persevere.
Cor… Can’t imagine how strong the bell must be when it’s complete.
… What troublesome curios we’re always made to collect.
Wow, you know {0} you’re lookin’ extra cute today!
Hey, hey {0}. Wanna play rock paper scissors? Winner tops!
I’m fine, don’t ask me stupid questions!
Oh, I just feel so at one with everyone here. Maybe we could sit down and hug for a bit before we go?
Well, I feel a little less stressed right now, but I don’t really want to talk about what else I’m feeling…
Holy Bell Interaction___nodes[3]._stepCh: Might be a bad idea to even try and bring this back on my own. I better call in for backup.

It takes some time to send for help, but eventually a union scout squad shows up with a puffer in tow. Despite some blushing and overly-friendly glances at each other, they hook up the fragment and fly it off towards the Holstaur Village.
I hope it gets there in one piece…
Oswalt Quest Completed___nodes[0]._stepC: Well, everyone seems to be accounted for. I was just about to finish stretching and go get them myself, hah!
Oswalt Missing Nobles___nodes[0]._stepCh: Ah, there you are! Let me know if you need any help finding the rest of those stragglers. Once I finish stretching, that is!
Cicerone Quest Completion___nodes[1]._st: Well there {1|they|she|he} {1|are|is|is}! Thank you so much for finding my lost clients! Worse than sheep for wandering off.
And I tell ya if I lost one more of ‘em I was thinking about packing it in and heading back to High Climb!
Nothing to it, always happy to help.
Like I said, I owe you one! I’ll be heading over to Katarina Station after this if you ever want to cash in that favor!
Cicerone Quest Completion___nodes[2]._st: Well there {0|they|he|she} {0|are|is|is}! Thank you so much for finding my lost clients! Worse than sheep for wandering off. And I tell ya if I lost one more of ‘em I was thinking about packing it in and heading back to High Climb!
Nothing to it, always happy to help.
Like I said, I owe you one!
You really need to be more careful, Kalsang.
Yeah, all right! Absolutely! Won’t happen again!
Yeah, you’re always serious you ol’ sourpuss! I owe you one. Maybe two if you could…not mention this to the elders if you’re ever back home?
Kalsang claps her hands together in a pleading gesture.
You’re the best Yonten! Catch you later buddy!
Hey that Kalsang sure seems nice! You never told me you had friends back home. Not that you talk about your home.
There’s…nothing to talk about. Can we move on?
Pre-Quest Completion___nodes[0]._stepCha: It’s so sad, Mistress Kalsang can be a little too forgetful. I hope the tour group she was leading can find their way back here.
Flynt Rescue___nodes[0]._stepChain[2]._d: You approach the avian avian- feline? He looks over the waterfall as you clear your throat
Flynt Rescue___nodes[0]._stepChain[5]._d: The man wheels around, fight or flight kicking in before seeing you standing there in your overalls.
Flynt Rescue___nodes[0]._stepChain[6]._d: Ey! Don’t go sneakin’ up on people like that! You didn’t scratch any of my merchandise, did you?
Flynt Rescue___nodes[0]._stepChain[9]._d: Alright, well whaddya want? Flynt’s a busy man. I’m trying to get a look around.
Flynt Rescue___nodes[0]._stepChain[11]._: You have a feeling what he’s looking for…
Flynt Rescue___nodes[0]._stepChain[12]._: The camp’s back that way. Kalsang is waiting there with the rest of the-
Flynt Rescue___nodes[0]._stepChain[15]._: Anyways, I’m outta here… Next time you see me, maybe buy something instead of giving me your two cents!
Flynt Rescue___nodes[0]._stepChain[17]._: Well, I feel sorry for any wolf that gets in his way.
WolvesPortalDialogue___nodes[2]._stepCha: You hear the chanting of assembled wolves as you approach the back of the cave. Their song a mixture of growls and rhythmic chants. It seems to be having an effect on the portal in front of them.
Clumsily, you inch closer, knocking an errant stone into another with a sharp <size=35><shake=strong>-klak!-</shake></size>
The wolves’ heads snap to look back at you.
Oh uhm… L-lovely weather we’re having? Name’s {0}.
WolvesPortalDialogue___nodes[4]._stepCha: You stand victorious! The wolves nurse their scrapes and bruises as they stand definitely between you and the portal.
You may be strong, human, but we do not yield, not while we must still undertake our duties!
WolvesPortalDialogue___nodes[5]._stepCha: You grab your side, wincing. These wolves fought you with a desperate ferocity.
You are beaten, frail human. Return to your pack and leave us to our concerns.
WolvesPortalDialogue___nodes[6]._stepCha: Wait! I’m not looking to fight you… more than I already have! Let’s talk, please!
WolvesPortalDialogue___nodes[7]._stepCha: You emerge through the portal, only to be greeted by a series of rhythmic growls and chants falling short. The wolves eye you cautiously, seeming to decide just what a human coming through their portal is a portent of…
Uh… hi? Nice to meet you, I’m {0}!
Human… For what reason have you come from Cole Turem?
Oh! Your people allowed me to use this portal, so that I could find my way down to the dark wood…
WolvesPortalDialogue___nodes[8]._stepCha: The leader of their pack seems to judge your words carefully…
Very well, then tell me of what business you have down here in our dark wood.
I was hoping to ask you that actually… But I suppose I can go first-
Their leader interrupts you, holding a hand out.
We shall speak first, as we will not be second in anything!
We are in the middle of closing this portal to Cole Turem. Our grounds have been taken by foul creatures like pine tar. If left unchecked, they could herald forth a <color=red>Cu Sith</color>.
We plan to stay behind. We will fight for our clan’s boughs. Only if we succeed, may we reopen the portal again, but only from this side. To open it from the other side risks too much on the unknown.
These creatures you seek to fight, they sound like <color=red>homunculi</color>. I’ve encountered them before.
Then you know of such creatures. We call them the fomoire.
WolvesPortalDialogue___nodes[10]._stepCh: Well, you’re in luck! I already cleared out your breedings grounds after seeing the mess those homunculi were making!
The wolf looks gobsmacked. A few wolves seem to look from their pack leader to the portal.

<speed=slowest>T-that’s…</speed> It is a good thing you showed up when you did then. If we were to have closed our portal, it would be many moons before we would be able to conduct the ritual to create one again…
You have our thanks, human. We shall return to Cole Turem, and let our kin know that our breeding grounds may once again be visited. We shall name our children in your honor.
Before you can protest, the wolves pounce through the portal…
Well, let no good deed go unpunished…
WolvesPortalDialogue___nodes[11]._stepCh: I happen to have extensive experience fighting them, and I want to help.
I can head back down that way, and deal with your infestation. That way you won’t need to close the portal!
You would be willing to lay down your life for our people?
Er… hopefully not! I think I can deal with them!
We shall wait here, until we hear the death throes of you, or the fomoire… Should it be the latter, we shall be glad to have our breeding grounds back!
Should it be the former, your sacrifice will not be forgotten! Our howls will join yours as you leave the world of the living!
The other wolves nod in agreement. You find it a little hard to consider this the honor they think it is. Either way, It seems you’ve got some more homunculi to deal with!

I would welcome you with a spot of tea, perhaps some biscuits. However, I can see you are {0|an|a|a} {0|individual|man|woman} of action. I’ll pay you the professional courtesy of not wasting your time.

FindingCrumblingKeyPieceDialogue___nodes: You find a strange stone piece. Looking it over, it clearly has some sort of esoteric design on the front of it.
Hmm, maybe it’s some kind of talisman, or something? I should hold onto this for now.
You find another stone piece of the talisman you found earlier. Pulling out the piece you already have, you connect them together with a dull -thunk!-
Alright, that’s one piece down… however many more to go!
You find another piece of the stone bauble you found earlier. You slot it into the center of the assembled stone, turning it so it slides into place.
It turns into place, the action brings the other pieces in closer, locking them together around the central piece.
Looking at the whole thing, it’s absolutely some sort of device, meant to plug in to something… But you’ll probably have to look around to figure out where it goes.
You find a stone piece. It has some sort of esoteric design, and seems to be made for other pieces to fit into it.
This design, I wonder if this is part of a key to that gate I saw?
You stow it away for now, until more pieces can be found.
You find another piece of the stone key. Pulling out the piece you already have, you connect them together with a dull ‘thunk’.
It’s still incomplete, but the shape is coming together, you just need a few more.
You find another piece of the stone key. You slot it into the assembled stones, and turn it to lock it in place.
It locks into place, the other stone pieces locking together around the central slab. Looking at the whole thing, it’s finally complete.
Alright! Maybe I can use this to open up that gate now.
As you approach, the avian man takes stock of you, a slight look of confusion clear on his face.
I am not as well acquainted with the agrarian trade, but I hardly think the forest is a prime location for farming…
I’m not just a farmer, I’m a Frontiersman! The name’s {0}.
Ah, it is a pleasure to make your acquaintance. You may call me Irelius.
I say, you haven’t happened to see a Yeti woman, have you? About yea high?
Was that her name? Well, I would very much wish to know her whereabouts. You see, I took in a rather breathtaking vista, when I noticed they had moved on without so much as a “Should you wish to know”... Really.
Well, she’s back at the camp, waiting for everyone to show back up. You’re a little bit aways, but if you head back down thataways, you’ll find the camp.
Well, finally someone with clear and concise directions. Thank you, commoner. You’re a credit to your class.

Scholar_MisplacedPlanktonQuest___nodes[2: Woah! And here I thought that only sailors swore like that! What’s got you so riled up?
Oh! I’m *so* sorry! I didn’t see you there.
Normally I wouldn’t be partial to utilizing that spectrum of my vocabulary, but I am rather miffed!
Some ne'er-do-wells have completely wrecked the local plankton nests, driving them all away! I’m doing the best I can to restore the nests, but it will be an age and a half to get them livable again.
Especially since I can’t venture out myself to get the necessary supplies.
Well, anything I can do to help with the restoration?
Oh I couldn’t possibly ask…. But, if you happen to find material from their nests, I’d be very grateful.

Of course, glad I had it on hand!

Wow, I didn’t expect this! I will need to quickly think up a reward for your services.

ilaHolstaurVillageIntroduction___nodes[0: You had some ideas about what a Holstaur village might look like, some ancient ruins converted into a cozy market town probably wasn’t one of them.
Hmmm, massive horns, giant bell… Looks pretty official. Better check it out!
Greetings, Frontiersman… Welcome to the humble village of <color=blue>Hoovren</color>.
You can’t help but be impressed by the appearance of this proud-looking Holstaur, who curtsies so gracefully that the bell hung between her horns remains perfectly silent.
ilaHolstaurVillageIntroduction___nodes[2: Remembering what your formal education taught you, you lift your hands to your ears to resemble horns and curtsy back with a heart-felt “Moo.”
<wiggle=strong>Mrrrrhhh!</wiggle> My name is {0}, Frontiersman First-Class. It’s a pleasure to meet you, and to serve the herd.
To your chagrin, the Holstaur lets out a demure giggle when you rise back up.
<wiggle>Ho, ho, ho!</wiggle>~ My, it’s been quite some time since I’ve seen a human give a proper tipping.
Don’t fret, Frontiersman. You performed admirably, but such formalities are fast going out of fashion.
You and I might be the only ones in this village who take the old ways seriously, each for our own reasons…
ilaHolstaurVillageIntroduction___nodes[3: Right back at you! The name’s {0}, Frontiersman First-Class!
The Holstaur replies to your friendly greeting with an ironic smile.
Well, well! I see you Unioneers are as direct as ever.
Ah-I apologize if I overstepped my bounds, Ma’am!
It is already forgiven, Unioneer. Your people have long considered my children equals… Sadly, not all have shown them such kindness.
For your open and friendly Human nature, I am supremely grateful.
Whew! Sometimes all you need is a dash of Union equanimity to get you out of an awkward situation.
ilaHolstaurVillageIntroduction___nodes[4: I am <color=blue>Ila</color>, the Heifer-Priestessess of this humble village. It pleases me greatly to see a Unioneer in these pastures, and since you’ve made it this far, I’m sure you’ve ascertained the reason for my relief…
Lemme guess: Starts with <color=red>P-</color> and is a little <color=red>-Irate</color>?
Indeed. In the past, Pirates have left this village alone. Our guardian Bulls and Mistresses are not to be trifled with.
As of late, however, there have been brazen thefts. Some have even disappeared.
I cannot abide their intrusions any longer, and I would appreciate the Union’s help in these serious matters.
I’ve had my attendant, <color=blue>Beatrice</color>, speaking with everyone to see if they know where the pirates’ base may be.
Hiii! Wow, a Frontiersman. I’ll try and help you any way that I can? I’ll just try and answer any questions you might have
Come speak to me when you’re finished speaking with Priestess Ila~
The priestess seems to wait a moment, before interjecting.
All the while, I have done what I can to maintain the spiritual fortitude of my people, during this tumultuous time.
ilaHolstaurVillageIntroduction___nodes[6: Actually, I managed to uncover the Pirates’ base of operations.
Indeed? And, what did you uncover?
Um, would you believe me if I said ‘Evil Ice Cream Milking Factory?’
The Vaskin Ice creamery has offered many jobs to members of our village… Many of them came home after working for some time, so we had not thought the ones who hadn’t returned were…
No, the fault is mine. Many members of my herd believed it to be a good opportunity, so I did not think it prudent to see for myself. I will be sure to forbid any from returning.
You see a flicker of guilt cross her pious face.
You couldn’t know pirates were occupying it.
<size=20>(Or what Vaskin is up to. Even I’m not fully sure of that…)</size>
I’m making sure your missing children will soon be home safe and sound, Ma’am. Don’t worry about a thing!
It seems I must place my trust in the Union. Thank you. Their return will be well-received by my children, indeed.
Should you need any help, please, speak with my attendant, Beatrice. She spoke with several representatives of the Vaskin Ice Creamery company when they first began building.
Additionally, if I could ask you for yet more help, with the spiritual matters I mentioned earlier?
ilaHolstaurVillageIntroduction___nodes[7: I’m here to serve, Ma’am! Just let me know what the Union can do for you.
There are many villages in this world nestled among the ruins of the past. Ours is only one among many. You may have even seen such ruins on your adventures…
But Hoovren has become an important place of pilgrimage. Our Bulls and Herd Mistresses watch carefully over two sacred shrines that have long stood here.
Recently, during a time of rest and rumination, brigands broke into the shrine and damaged the bell, making off with a significant <color=orange>fragment</color>…
With the bell broken, the villagers are distracted, fearful, demoralized… They cannot muster the will to fight back against the Pirates’ aggression.
Sadly, you will find many of them unwilling even to trade, let alone speak with a stranger until it is made whole again.
Don’t worry, Ma’am, I’ll find that <color=orange>fragment!</color> Keep your people calm, and leave the fetching to me.
I would forever be indebted to you, brave {0}. If you have any questions, feel free to ask me, or my attendant here, with typical, human candor.

ilaHolstaurVillageOutro___nodes[2]._step: With the Shrine of the Sacred Bell restored, my people’s courage is restored with it. We can turn our attention to more than our immediate security.
Such as the pirates I encountered in the Vaskin Ice Creamery.
The priestess frowns at the mention of the factory.
Yes, and members of our herd who have not yet returned from that factory. My concern now grows to worry…
Something is definitely going on, and I’m still trying to get to the bottom of it. You have my word, Priestess!

ilaHolstaurVillageOutro___nodes[3]._step: With the Shrine of the Sacred Bell restored, and the pirate threats curtailed, my people can finally return to the narrow cow-path of peace…
I thank you, {0} for all you and The Union have done for us thus far.
Why don’t you take a walk around the village, and graze on the spoils of your victory?
Don’t mind if I do! Time to check out some of that Holstaur hospitality I’ve been missing out on.
ilaHolstaurVillageOutro___nodes[4]._step: With our strength of will restored, the time has come to return my children to the herd… By force, if necessary.
I’ve received word from our patrols. That the pirates seem to come and go from boughs near the <color=blue>Vaskin Ice Creamery</color>.

It is difficult now to ignore that several members of our herd who have gone to work there have yet to return. Had I such suspicions earlier, I would have forbid any members of the herd from working there…
You have done much already, but if I could request your assistance…
Don’t worry about a thing, Priestess! The Union of Frontiersmen is on the job!
The priestess lets out a sigh of relief.
I know the whole village feels much safer in the capable hands of the brave <color=orange>Bellmaker</color>.
I’ll do what I can, now I just need to figure out how to… make an unannounced inspection…

I believe we may be able to aid you on this. When you have time, please speak to my attendant, Beatrice. She spoke with representatives of the creamery when they first built it, and may be able to help you find a way in.
Hiii! I’ll try my best to help you however I can?

BeatriceHUB___nodes[2]._stepChain[3]._di: Oh, hiii, Union-kin! Was there something you needed?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[2]._stepChain[4]._di: Hi, Beatrice! I wanted to talk to you about a few things.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[2]._stepChain[5]._di: Well, alright, but I don’t know what those things are yet~!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[2]._stepChain[6]._di: Well, I haven’t… let’s just get to those questions!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[3]._rawContextHeader: What would you like to talk about?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[3]._optionData[0]._r: “I wanted to talk about the kidnapped Holstaur?”
BeatriceHUB___nodes[3]._optionData[1]._r: “Can I ask you a personal question?”
BeatriceHUB___nodes[4]._stepChain[0]._di: I wanted to talk about the kidnapped Holstaur?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[4]._stepChain[1]._di: Beatrice perks up, with a jiggle and jingle.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[4]._stepChain[2]._di: Oh that’s perfect! Priestess Ila put me in charge of handling all of that~!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[4]._stepChain[4]._di: Er, yeah… So, how are things going?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[4]._stepChain[5]._di: Hmm, well, why don’t you tell me what you Union-kin know?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[4]._stepChain[6]._di: Uhh… S-shouldn’t you be telling me what you know?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[4]._stepChain[7]._di: Well of course. But, if I don’t know what you already knew, how will I tell you something you haven’t already been told?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[5]._stepChain[1]._di: Can I ask you a personal question?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[5]._stepChain[2]._di: Hmm? I don’t see why not~
BeatriceHUB___nodes[6]._rawContextHeader: What would you like to ask the attendant?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[6]._optionData[1]._r: “What made you want to become a priestess?”
BeatriceHUB___nodes[6]._optionData[2]._r: “So what was that earlier about baptising? (Flirt)”
BeatriceHUB___nodes[6]._optionData[2].To: “I think I’d like to talk about something else”
BeatriceHUB___nodes[6]._optionData[3]._r: “I think I’d like to talk about something else”
BeatriceHUB___nodes[7]._stepChain[0]._di: What’s it like working for Priestess Ila?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[7]._stepChain[1]._di: Oh, it’s a wonderful position. I prepare a lot of implements for rituals, and perform some of the lesser rites and services for our shrine. All the while, Priestess Ila prepares me to take on her duties as well.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[7]._stepChain[2]._di: Really? What will she do, once you’re able to do all of them?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[7]._stepChain[3]._di: The matron thinks, putting a finger up to her bottom lip.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[7]._stepChain[4]._di: Well, I guess she would move to one of the sister shrines in her rotation. So, we’d probably see her in another thirty years? That’s how long she was gone last time…
BeatriceHUB___nodes[7]._stepChain[5]._di: T-thirty years? Wait… how old is she?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[7]._stepChain[6]._di: Come to think of it, I’m not sure… But I heard she trained my great aunt the time before that…
BeatriceHUB___nodes[7]._stepChain[9]._di: Don’t be silly~ She’d have to be older than that, if she was the Heifer-Priestess then too!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[7]._stepChain[12]._d: It’s impolite to ask a priestess her age, even if indirectly~
BeatriceHUB___nodes[7]._stepChain[13]._d: Whoopsie! I shouldn’t have answered that!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[7]._stepChain[17]._d: In any case, she’s very experienced at training priestesses, so I’ve learned a lot under her!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[8]._stepChain[0]._di: What made you want to become a priestess?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[8]._stepChain[2]._di: The Matron seems to take a moment to remember…
BeatriceHUB___nodes[8]._stepChain[3]._di: Ah, That’s right! I’m a Matron!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[8]._stepChain[4]._di: How did she forget that part…?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[8]._stepChain[5]._di: As a Matron, I already worked hard to nurture the members of our village, so attending to their spiritual development seemed natural~ After all, I spent quite a few years rearing young Holstaurs from many mothers here. A few are even my own~
BeatriceHUB___nodes[8]._stepChain[6]._di: Those child-bearing hips are not just for show, it seems!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[8]._stepChain[7]._di: O-oh? So priestesses can… you know?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[8]._stepChain[8]._di: You’re not sure how to gesture for <speed=slow>“making a Holstaur egg,”</speed> but settle for making two horn signs with your hands, and mashing them together…
BeatriceHUB___nodes[8]._stepChain[9]._di: Hehe, goodness~ Of course they can, but only those who have not had children can become a Heifer-Priestess. Though I’ll still be able to become a Holy Cow-Priestess.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[8]._stepChain[10]._d: The Matron beams with pride at that statement, leaving you to assume that is still a prestigious position.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[9]._stepChain[0]._di: So what was that about baptising?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[9]._stepChain[1]._di: The matron attendant lights up at the question.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[9]._stepChain[2]._di: Oh, are you interested in becoming a member of our herd?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[9]._stepChain[4]._di: First, you would declare yourself as faithful to the sacred cowherd. This process may take as long as a few months. Afterwards, you may enter into a cow-venant with our spiritual creator, and may be baptized in the sacred milk of a priestess!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[9]._stepChain[6]._di: O-oh, that’s a bit more than I expected… <size=25> I thought it’d be a sex thing…</size>
BeatriceHUB___nodes[9]._stepChain[7]._di: The attendant laughs lightly, but smiles to ease your embarrassment.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[9]._stepChain[8]._di: Oh my, you flatter me~ I’m sure an eager, young {0|Union-Kin|Union Bull|Union Heifer} like yourself will many in our herd are more than willing to roll around in the hay without need for rituals~
BeatriceHUB___nodes[10]._stepChain[0]._d: I think I’d like to talk about something else.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[10]._stepChain[1]._d: Of course~! What else do you need, Union-kin?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[11]._stepChain[0]._d: I should get going. Still have lots to do out here!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[11]._stepChain[1]._d: Beatrice nods, giving you a warm, comforting smile.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[0]._d: So far I only know what The Union does: <color=red>pirates</color>, <size=25>and a few other persons of interest,</size> are causing mayhem in this Great Forest.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[1]._d: Oh, I know that too~! I also know a few more things as well!
We’ve had a number of kidnappings…
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[3]._d: I actually helped a Grazer Holstaur who was almost kidnapped.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[4]._d: Priestess Ila tasked me with finding something in common with all of these kidnappings, but they seem to be grabbing anyone and everyone who goes out alone. Grazer, Matron, Herdmistress, even a few of our bulls, so it can’t just be for milk…
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[7]._d: No… It’s got a lot of Holstaurs very tense and nervous. I don’t blame them either…
Perhaps if you roughed up a few pirates, you might be able to figure something out?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[8]._d: Maybe… but they’re pretty cagey about details. They seem more afraid of their bosses than of me.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[9]._d: You hesitate before asking the next part.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[13]._: Uh… yeah, anything weird about it?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[14]._: Not really… I mean, a few Holstaurs haven’t made the journey back, but that’s usually because they have a lot of milk to relieve, or they’re probably waiting out all this pirate business… Why? Is there something wrong?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[15]._: Er, nothing I can confirm yet. I’m just… chasing a lead. Speaking of, do you know how I could get into the creamery?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[16]._: Well, I don’t know too much about Union-kin laws, but… <color=blue>You could try knocking</color>? Or maybe see if there’s <color=blue>another way in…?</color>
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[17]._: It’s not the best suggestion, but it’s not like you have a better idea…
BeatriceHUB___nodes[13]._stepChain[18]._: Thanks for the help, I’ll see what The Union can do to help!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[0]._d: Since those Holstaurs still haven’t returned from the creamery, I’ve decided to see about getting inside to investigate.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[1]._d: Priestess Ila is starting to doubt their generosity… We really thought it would be a benefit to our village…
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[2]._d: She looks a bit dismayed, before shaking her head.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[3]._d: Thank you for looking into it!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[4]._d: Of course, but I have to get in first.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[5]._d: The Matron attendant thinks for a moment, putting a slender finger to her bottom lip.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[6]._d: Well, no one here would complain if you <color=blue>kept beating up those pirates.</color> If they’re connected to the factory, then I’ll bet <color=blue>they’ll have a clue on them.</color>
As for the factory… -sigh- If only you were a Holstaur, <size=25><color=blue>or at least looked like one…</color></size> Maybe then they’d let you in…
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[7]._d: I am rather human looking, true.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[8]._d: Then again, you remember your genealogy class from The Ivory Tower. Humans and Holstaurs have a lot of intermixing throughout history.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[9]._d: Maybe it wouldn’t be that hard to <color=blue>dress up like a Holstaur…</color> You’d just need to find someone who could help with that…
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[10]._: Well, if you can’t get in through the front door. Maybe there’s <color=blue>another way in?</color>
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[11]._: I could look into it, no way a factory like that doesn’t have plenty of runoff pipes or what have you.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[12]._: The Matron nods, knowingly not knowing.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[14]._stepChain[13]._: Well, we wish you best of luck with your investigation. If it leads to members of our herd returning, then we will be in your debt!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[15]._stepChain[0]._d: I actually managed to get into the factory as part of my investigation.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[15]._stepChain[1]._d: Oh, did you figure out what’s going on?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[15]._stepChain[2]._d: Yes, it’s not exactly great. The pirates and the factory are related.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[15]._stepChain[3]._d: Ila’s attendant frowns as she crosses her arms, her bells’ jingling cut short.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[15]._stepChain[4]._d: I see… That’s not good then, is it?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[15]._stepChain[6]._d: Well, no. if I’m being honest. But! As a representative of The Union, I’m doing everything in my power to make it right. I want you and your village to know that.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[15]._stepChain[8]._d: I’ll be sure to let everyone know then that they should stay away from it. We’ll also be handling any recruiters from the creamery, should they show up again. Not that I think they would.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[15]._stepChain[10]._: Thank you, Union-kin! We eagerly await the return of our sisters and brothers!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[0]._d: Beatrice! I have some good news finally!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[1]._d: She claps her hands, giving a small, jingling jump.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[2]._d: Oh goodness! First, many of our captured herd have returned, and now we have more good news?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[4]._d: Er, sorry, that is the good news!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[5]._d: Ohhh! Well, that is plenty of good news on its own!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[7]._d: You and The Union have the respect and admiration of our village, Union-kin! You also have the thanks of not just Priestess Ila, but me as well~ It’s so wonderful to see my daughter again!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[9]._d: Your daughter was one of the Holstaurs kidnapped?
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[10]._: It had me sick with worry! That’s why Priestess Ila placed me in charge of finding them.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[12]._: Thank you so much, {0} of the Union. Were it not for you, perhaps I never would have seen my mother again.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[14]._: Oh, well, it’s the least I could do!
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[15]._: I couldn’t thank you enough if I tried, but please, make yourself comfortable and partake in the comforts of our village.
BeatriceHUB___nodes[16]._stepChain[17]._: Looks like this is an even happier ending than you could have predicted!
105B - Exhausted Marine - Post-Bell Frag: Hahh… hahh…I’m so full of milk…
PiratePoacherBullyingGrazerDialogueBubbl: Watch it, fellas. Those horns are REALLY sharp.
You kick me again, and we’ll see how it goes!
Hey! Watch those hooves! Almost kicked me!
Oi! We don’t need ta fight!

-Wah!- Those pirates were so mean, shooting me in the cheek! All I did was hit one of them with another one of them!
