ScholarBlueprintsQuestForestF2___nodes[2: <size=20>Yeesh! Surprising to hear a Scholar be so foulmouthed…</size>
ScholarBlueprintsQuestForestF2___nodes[4: Actually, recent experience suggests it may not be that surprising.
ScholarBlueprintsQuestForestF2___nodes[5: I heard that, whoever you are… And I’m not a “Scholar,” unlike that LAYABOUT from “Plankton Sciences,” who couldn’t even be bothered to leave basecamp!
More specifically, I’m an Adjunct Professor and Researcher of the Ivory Tower School of Reproductive Sciences!
It’s a pleasure to meet you, mister Adjunct Professor! I’m—
A Frontiersman, yes, that’s obvious. And you showed up not a minute too soon!
ScholarBlueprintsQuestForestF2___nodes[6: You see, I came to this desolate wood in search of a very valuable piece of Magitech… Valuable not only for its historical significance, but its potential re-introduction into modern usage.
It could prove very useful to you, in your work!
ScholarBlueprintsQuestForestF2___nodes[7: Come for the history, stay for the modern!
ScholarBlueprintsQuestForestF2___nodes[8: So, what’s the trouble? Is it guarded by Pirates? Surrounded by acid? Really really high up?! Inside of a-!?
No, no, nothing so trite as any of that. This is far, far worse! My way to the artifact is blocked…
Huh! For a second there, I could almost imagine a world where it wasn’t in my job description to deal with this exact scenario.
So you’ll help me!? Thank the Scales and Sky I won’t have to say “scarousal” during peer review…
Head to the Wizard ruins in the southwest, past the gempas trees. You’ll find the artifact on top of a functional Magitech pylon on the east side of the ruins.
ScholarBlueprintsQuestForestF2___nodes[1: <size=20>Wait, wizard pylon… Giant woman… </size>
Frontiersman! You have it here, with you?!
My, those Union-issue overalls really have deep pockets, don’t they?
Early Turn In Intro
No sweat, Prof. I’ll be back before you know it!
R-Really? Well, I wouldn’t blame you i-if you took your time! I-I mean, provided you came back with a thorough report on your experiences!
StartQuest
[DATASTEP] Quest Start Dialogue
What kinda modern usage are we talking about, here?
As you hopefully know, the standard Incubator provided by the Union is perfectly capable of hatching a wide variety of familiar monster species.
Unfortunately, the current design is too rigid to prevent more uncommon species from being adapted to the machine…
Um, these “non-standard species” wouldn’t be of the <color=green>crazy, toothy and ANGRY</color> variety, would they?
What? No, don’t be absurd. If such a species were to be discovered, I would be the first to know!
What I’m talking about are highly <color=orange>unorthodox species</color> and ancient species.

Such species would likely be born from Wizard cloning technology. Which begs the question of why the Wizards that helped us create the <color=blue>Incubator</color> didn’t provide such functionality to begin with…
But now I’m spoiling the contents of my thesis paper, heh heh!
Modern Usage
Sorry, I can’t help you right now. I’m just passing through.
Ohhh, Barnacles… What am I going to do now?
Well, this offer will be outstanding for some time, should you come by this way again.
Bye
You’re back, Frontiersman! Are you here to help me with my work?
NoQuestReturnIntro
Oh, Frontiersman! Did you recover the artifact? M-more importantly, d-did you encounter any…
<shake>DANGEROUSLY LARGE BOVINE WOMEN!?</shake>
Um, sorry! I haven’t found the artifact yet, but there’s plenty of Holstaurs around and—
What good is that to me!? Get back out there, pronto!!!
Maybe you could take some paper with you? To take notes on what you see? For my r-research, of course! Very important research!
Return Not Completed
Frontiersman! Scales and Sky, I see you have the artifact with you! My, those Union-issue overalls really have deep pockets, don’t they?
Quest Turn In Intro
Wait here a moment. I’m very confident in my research, and should only need to make a few adjustments to my notes.
Faster than you can respond, the Researcher eagerly pulls the recovered artifact out of your hands, and he disappears into a nearby tent…
Quest Completed, Sounds Of Clattering, Breaking, Smashing, Tearing
Whew, what a rush! As promised, I can provide you with blueprints for improving the <color=blue>Incubator</color> on your farm.

As a reward for your hard work <size=20>and sparing me from the lustful denigrations of a large, muscular, motherly woman  <wiggle>forcing </wiggle> me to suckle from her…</size> I am more than happy to part with these schematics!
You take the blueprints from his surprisingly sweaty hands…
That's an oddly specific concern, but your safety is my professional obligation, mister Adjunct Professor!
…… Right. Well, if you have any questions about how the new Incubator works-the <color=blue>NEO Incubator</color>, if you will-please, feel free to ask!
[DATASTEP] Quest Complete
Heya, Prof! How bout a crash course on the <color=blue>Neo Incubator</color>?
Happy to oblige, Frontiersman. Let’s see if I can break it down in a way you’ll understand.
The standard Incubator only allows you to raise the most recognizable monster species we know of today: Catfolk, Wolves, Holstaurs, Harpies and the like.
The <color=blue>Neo Incubator</color> will allow you to raise any <color=orange>unorthodox species</color> or ancient species.
Basically, if you ever come across an egg that is rejected by the standard Incubator, you should try putting it in the <color=blue>Neo Incubator</color>.
Sounds good! Thanks for the quick lesson.
NeoIncubator Info
Post Boethilde - Soaking Herdmistress__l: r- HerdmistressHandler := Now that the Creamery has been shut down, I’ve been much busier helpin’ our Dairypals get milked.
r- HerdmistressHandler := With our Dairypals back, I’ve noticed that many have become more buoyant thanks to their enlarged breasts and packages~
Fascinating… So their natural food source here would be… Oh… Hm… That’s a problem then….
The scholar seems lost in thought, hardly even noticing that you’re watching him…
You’re that Scholar from before, right? The one who needed help with the plankton nests?
The scholar takes your hand, shaking it vigorously!
That I am! A pleasure to see you again! It might delight you to know that the plankton have since relocated and are happy in their new nest!
That’s great to hear! But, I have to ask, What are you doing out here? More research?
Somewhat. Since we last spoke, I have been taking notes of the local food that is available to native plankton populations.
As luck would have it, I have discovered that the plankton here feast on <color=blue>a particular fungi</color>.
Let me guess, the Fungi isn’t easy to retrieve?
Precisely, and I am nowhere near as brave as my colleague! The madman dashed out to research magitech before I could even arrange for accommodations here.
-Sigh- With all the news of <color=red>pirates</color> and such, I haven’t been brave enough to leave this village. That and well, another thing-
You can’t tell from under his hood but you swear his eyes glance over towards a nearby holstaur grazer…
Ahem… Well, I should stay here. Until it’s safe to leave!
Do you help him out again?
I could get it for you!
I could get it for you!
You would? I wouldn’t say no to it, but I also wouldn’t want to impose.
It’s no imposition at all! Besides, I do a lot of walking anyway, so I’ll probably run across it if I know what to look for.
Then I won’t turn down your help!
Any idea where I should start?
Hmm.. I did see a <color=blue>four-way fork</color> in the branches while I was surveying the area. I think the fungi is somewhere along the <color=blue>southern branch</color>.
Wait… The southern branch over that way?
Oh, yes! If you like, I think I have a map somewhere in my bag…
You reach into your rucksack, pulling out the fungi.
By any chance, is this the fungi?
The scholar nods, giving you a short wave.
Likewise! I’ll be here for a while yet, if you want to talk!
I’ll keep my eyes peeled if I’m headed that way!
Oh, they are alright, still studying the local flora for Plankton food sources.
I remember you said something about the food… Where was it again?
Ah! If you find yourself at an <color=blue>intersection of four branches</color>, the <color=blue>southern branch</color> seems the most likely place to find it!
PlanktonScholarFungiForestF2___nodes[10]: Oh, they are alright, still studying the local flora for Plankton food sources.
I remember you talking about that…
Very much so! <color=blue>Rare fungi</color>, <color=red>dangerous wilds</color>, alluring… educational opportunities here…
I fear it may be quite some time before I could muster the courage to go out there and look for it. By then it may even be out of season!
PlanktonScholarFungiForestF2___nodes[11]: I think I found the Fungus you were looking for!
You produce the Fungus you collected.
PlanktonScholarFungiForestF2___nodes[12]: Oh! This is it! Yes, thank you!
This will be a great help, and will allow me to further the studies into plankton habitats!
I can’t thank you enough, though maybe… here!
The Scholar produces a plankton from their robes. It undulates excitedly as the sunlight hits it.
This is a Doofler plankton, it’s native to this Forest, but I believe you’ll be able to take good care of it!
I’ll do my best! Thank you!
You’re welcome, now then, I must study….
You hardly get the chance to leave, before you can hear him muttering to himself as he looks over the fungi.
ilaPreQuestCompletionHUB___nodes[0]._ste: Welcome back to our gentle pasture, {0}. I hope you’ll stay a while, and graze with us.
ilaPreQuestCompletionHUB___nodes[1]._opt: Time to get a moove on (Leave)
ilaPreQuestCompletionHUB___nodes[2]._ste: Got any tips? Anything you can tell me about the theft of the bell fragment would be helpful.
The thieves were certainly unusual. They flew a true <color=blue>wooden galleon</color>, rather than a Ray or a Puffer.
They steered their ship <color=blue>north</color>, and made fast for the treetops. Curiously, nobody has spotted them leaving the Forest as of yet.
I suggest you begin your search by making your way up as high as you can. One of our guardian bulls will get you up to speed on the dangers you may face there.

You will know you’ve reached the treetops when you find a small copse of <color=orange>white Coltus</color>. It is a beautiful pasture, and my children graze there on special occasions. Please act accordingly.
ilaPreQuestCompletionHUB___nodes[3]._ste: Actually, I’d like to know a little more about this <color=blue>bell</color>. Does it have any special properties?
The bell is a beloved, ancient relic of my people. It has been kept in several villages and strongholds throughout their history, and can be heard for many miles around when rung.
When the bell is struck, feelings of love, and sometimes lust, are felt by whatever willing ears can hear it.
It is a very powerful artifact. Even a small fragment of the bell, without the protection of a sacred cowherd, can cause amorous stirrings in those close by.
Gotcha. I’ll keep an eye open for any <wiggle>“amorous stirrings”</wiggle> going on. Could be a sign!
Sounds familiar, though Kochamuran bells usually encourage you to empty your mind, and not your… Well…
I hope they don’t ring it on the hour. Can you imagine how pent up you’d be at noon and midnight?
Happily, the bell only seems to stoke the feelings of the willing. Still, I imagine it could stir up business in certain contexts…
Fascinating! I would love to get my hands on such an interesting piece of history… Perhaps you could help me with my research, {0}?~
Wot, a cowbell that makes ya horny? And we’re gonna fix it? Honestly, can’t think of a more Union-y way to spend my time.
It shall be a merry day indeed when this bell is set right, but I would ask not to stand nigh when it ringeth…
… If you have any sense, {0}, you’ll leave this task to someone who *doesn’t* walk around with butter-planktons in their stomach all day…
Keep an eye on me, Sunshine. If I begin to get giddy and bloom, it could be a sign we're getting close.
Oy, maybe we should ask ‘ow big this thing is? Ah feck it, I’m shore I can ‘andle it.
The ship was never heard from again? That be terrible news, unless ye be a fan a ghost ships!
Oh, {0}, that bell would be PERFECT for the farm!~ Do you think they’ll let us borrow it next!?
Fixing the horny bell, huh? Just another day in the life in the company of a Frontiersman, I guess.

AfterBossPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[8: Nah, I’m sure she’ll be back after you chased her off… Though I should probably report back at Katarina shouldn’t I? Can’t stay out here being cuddled by catfolk forever.
AfterBossPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[1: Well, the path behind me is completely open, so you should be able to reach the station without much more trouble!
Appreciate it! I’ll see you around!
StalkerGuardedPeasants___nodes[1]._stepC: Purrrr, O~h yes. That’s the stuff girls~ I haven’t had my fur brushed like this in forever~.
It’s been hours, your furrrrr looks *great*, let us take a break *please*.
Hmmm.. Hmmm… Nyo~ I think my fur could use *one* more hour of brushing~.
The women, clearly more than done with this task groan in unison. Something must be done!
Excuse me, but uhm… Do you two need… Help?
Yes, *please*! We’ve been trapped here for hours and she won’t let us stop!
Hiisssss! What’s it to you, gak? My furrrrr needs to be brushed, and these two lovelies said they’d do it as long as I kept them safe.
And now it looks like you’re working them much too hard. I think it’s best if you let them leave.
Well well, if you want them to leave so badly, you’ll have to fight me for them!
StalkerGuardedPeasants___nodes[2]._stepC: The stalker runs off, hissing and cursing you as she leaves small tufts of fur behind…
Whew… Thanks there, really appreciate it…
Yeah, if I had to keep brushing her tail, I think my arms might have started to fall off.
Well I’m happy to help, though she’ll probably be back.
We’ll be long gone by then I think.
Oh! Were you two a part of a caravan that got attacked?
We are… How did you know?
I’ve been looking for people separated from the caravan to send them back to Katarina. I’ve actually cleared a path back, if you two follow my trail, you’ll be able to make it back in no time!
Really? Whooo…. You’re… I dunno what I can say, thank you!
All in a day’s work! Think you’ll be okay?
We’ll be fine, you, however, take care of yourself out here, mmkay?
And again, thank you for coming to our rescue!
GenericTrapDisarm___nodes[0]._stepChain[: Another one of these traps. Must have missed this one earlier.
You find another one of the traps that Sascha and Gehman left behind.
These are only going to be more dangerous now that they’re not checking on them regularly.
You take a few moments to undo the works of Gehman’s trapmaking… Once it’s finally disarmed, you toss aside the broken pieces.
Hopefully the scouts will clean the rest of this up. Don’t know if I could carry all of this back to Katarina right now…
PoacherTrapsCompleted___nodes[0]._stepCh: You pause a moment, and take stock of all the traps you’ve disarmed…
You know what? I think that’s the last of them!
Not too bad, if I don’t say so myself. I should report back to Jubelle and let her know!
JumpPadsEntrancePeasant___nodes[0]._step: Y-yes! Oh thank you for stopping. I have… Well…
She gestures down to her foot, where her ankle is clearly very swollen.
Oh, twisted that pretty bad, huh? I know a bit of field medicine, want me to try?
Kneeling down, you pull out a small kit, and open it up, pulling out a splint and bandages to dress her ankle.
I was with a caravan when the catfolk ambushed us. I was able to make it through the gate before it closed, and jumped…
Sprained my ankle though. I was afraid I’d be out here a while without help.
Don’t worry. I’ll get you fixed up and on your way. The path back should be safe enough now.
You finish the wrap and check over the work.
I’m no doctor, but this should hold, just take your time and step carefully.
You help the woman up and she tests her footing, she winces, but otherwise seems fine.
I will, I can navigate back on my own, just stay safe out here.
She heads off, a slight limp, but she should get back fine.
CatEggPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[3]._: H-hey! Hey you! Yeah, in the overalls!
CatEggPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[5]._: Yes! You! Get out of here! I got a good thing going on and I don’t need the competition.
CatEggPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[7]._: What in the… What are you talking about?
CatEggPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[8]._: Look, these catfolk took me in, apparently they needed a ‘Denwife’ whatever that means, and I’m the perfect candidate.
All I need to do is look after the eggs, and every few days I have a *train* of catfolk women that ride me. This is a *great* deal!
You wouldn’t… Happen to have been a part of a caravan that was meant to go to Katarina could you? Catarella has been looking for you.
The man thinks for a moment.
Ugh… What do I do? Yeah I like being out here, but I also want to sleep in a normal bed, and not have to learn how to groom with my tongue…
In my shoes, how would you solve this?
Well, you could always come back out here on your days off? Sounds like they’d be glad to see you.
You know what, that’s a convincing argument. I’m going to go back to Catarella, report in, and then sneak off back here in my free time~
… Well, I can’t stop him, hope that works out.
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[0]._stepChain[3]._: You see a carving before you, the bas-relief is about as tall as you, with a very prominent face carved into the gate.
The relief’s mouth hangs open, with an old inscription below in a language you don’t recognize.
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[0]._stepChain[5]._: Luckily, off to the side, you see a slightly less old inscription scratched into the door. The craftsmanship is unmistakably Catfolk.
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[0]._stepChain[6]._: Hmm. It says… ‘Greet with the round treat that is sweet to eat, if passage beyond is what thou dost seek’.
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[0]._stepChain[7]._: The translation is followed up with some crude comments by other catfolk, some who apparently wished they had just put the answer down.
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[0]._stepChain[9]._: Not a single ‘nya’ or ‘meow’? Are you certain this is catfolk?
I’m sure you’ll figure it out. You’re so smart {0}!
Ooooo~! I wonder what the solution could be.. Oh! Maybe we gotta stick something in the mouth!
Oh what joy, another puzzle… Can I sit this one out {0}?
… Why would they put something like that on a door? Why use this door at all?
Oo, what a charming lil’ puzzle {0}!
Ah, what twisting vines puzzles like this weave. It would be so much easier to just go around…
I have heard *more* complicated riddles before I suppose.
Right, right… Well, there’s plenty of food growin’ around ‘ere, so it probably needs somethin’ local aye?
Hitherto now, I hadst worried the newe fashion of tail rhyme would be forgotten in this strange age. What comfort to have seen it endured.
Something sweet? Well there are plenty of things like that darling… So maybe anything will do?
It appears this catfolk was a bit of a scholar and poet. The internal rhyme is a little rough, but still… a surprise.
So wait… It’s asking for food?... Well I could go for a bite right now too!
So it seems like it needs something circular to eat? Maybe there’s something around that fits in the mouth? Or maybe I have something on me…
Shows up if holding a Speedweel crop
Shows up if holding stone Speedweel
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[2]._stepChain[0]._: After inserting the Speedweel into the mouth, it slowly closes, crushing it between those stone lips. The gate parts, letting you through.
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[2]._stepChain[2]._: Good thing I took my hand out of there! Yeesh.
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[2]._stepChain[6].<: Door Opens, player loses speedweel crop
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[3]._stepChain[0]._: Hefting the stone speedweel into the carving’s mouth, the lips come down and wrap around it.
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[3]._stepChain[2]._: The gate slides open, allowing you passage.
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[3]._stepChain[4]._: Oh! How fun! The solution was an actual stone speedweel!
Know what we should do on our way through~? Take the speedweel and hide it again so someone *else* has to solve the riddle next time!
… Now I want a speedweel too…
There are still primitive gates like this that are functional?
Ah, so it was one of *those* sort of puzzles. I am surprised to find something like this so far from The High Climb.
… I cannae believe… What sorta mechanism have they *got* in there that makes tha door work like that? Wish I could take my time and disassemble it.
How delightful~ Whoever created this must have really loved speedweels to go through the trouble!
I suppose I’ll never kno’ what that tasted like. Ah well…
What a… charmingly artless solution… Alright, allons-y.
Dang, looks like it’s really stuck in there now, I doubt even a herd of Centaurs can pull it out.
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[3]._stepChain[5]._: … Well! I guess we’re free to move through now!
CatDoorPuzzle___nodes[3]._stepChain[7].<: Open door and remove stone weel from inventory
TrapWarningPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain: A woman runs towards you, disheveled and out of breath. She stops and bends over, hands on knees, panting heavily.
W-wait… Don’t… Whew… Don’t go any further. Traps!
Glad I didn’t step any further…
The woman steadies herself, standing up.
Sorry for the sudden yell, but I’ve been trekking back to the station for a while now. So I’ve gotten pretty good at avoiding all these hazards.
And you’ve been the first person I’ve seen that wasn’t a pirate, or catfolk.
What happened? Why were you out here?
I was helping move a merchant’s goods through this part of the Savannah, nothing unusual, but then something really riled the catfolk up.
We get attacked, because of *course* we do, and I’ve been running for… Hours? Days? I don’t remember really.
Well, I can offer you some good news.
You hook a thumb over your back.
Katarina Station is behind me, and there shouldn’t anything in your path stopping you from getting back.
Really? Oh you must’ve been sent straight from the Firmament!
Nothing so fancy, just a Frontiersman!
Before I go though, look out for traps. They’re everywhere down here, so watch the ground carefully.
I’ll keep my eyes open for them, just get back to Katarina safely, alright?
Will do! Hope to see you there!
F1EntranceScout___nodes[0]._stepChain[3]: Here to get yer hands dirty, Frontiersman? Word of advice: Keep yer eyes peeled down to the fruit.
These islands are ancient and rich. Full of buried treasure and *secret caves*, hidden behind overgrowth and *waterfalls.*
And if you find a flowery *fairy circle* off the beaten path, consider takin’ yer shovel to it. Y’never know what you’ll find when you break ground!
BobruyskF1___nodes[0]._stepChain[7]._dia: You look up as a rotund, wealthy looking man walks up to you, his hands clasped in front of him.
BobruyskF1___nodes[0]._stepChain[8]._dia: I was beginning to think The Union was delaying, and dragging its heels about my requests.
Now, if you had been here earlier, we could have prevented this kerfuffle. Tell me, are the rest of them coming soon?
BobruyskF1___nodes[0]._stepChain[9]._dia: The- who? Wait, wait… who are “the rest of them”?
BobruyskF1___nodes[0]._stepChain[10]._di: … You’re not with the platoon I requested? Then they really are dilly dallying, and all over a few embellishments from my workers!
BobruyskF1___nodes[0]._stepChain[12]._di: The man ignores you, his brows furrowed as he thinks. Even so, he never seems to break eye contact, and finally points directly to you.
BobruyskF1___nodes[0]._stepChain[13]._di: Well, if The Union is going to take too long, I think I may have a lucrative proposition for a {0|pal|fella|gal} just like you!
I need someone to go and see about a mine I own on this here cluster of islands. I need them cleaned out, and I’ll readily reward you or anyone willing to do it.
So, how’s a deal like that sound to you… What’s your name again?
BobruyskF1___nodes[2]._stepChain[0]._com: … Wot? I en’t Union, don’ lookit me!
BobruyskF1___nodes[2]._stepChain[1]._com: I’ll just add, I was a Union Sheriff.
My farm is part o’ a Union homestead program, does that count?
… I’m just a fortune teller, but I can vouch for them!
And Me, *Union Admiral!* Keh heh heh!
BobruyskF1___nodes[3]._stepChain[0]._dia: The man doesn’t even flinch at the emphasis on “Union”
BobruyskF1___nodes[3]._stepChain[2]._dia: Bobruysk Medvedov, of the Medvedov Family! But you can call me Bobruysk. So, do we have a deal?
BobruyskF1___nodes[3]._stepChain[3]._dia: This is a lot to take in all at once, it might be good to ask some questions first.
BobruyskF1___nodes[4]._optionData[0]._ra: Wait, so you were with the caravan?
BobruyskF1___nodes[4]._optionData[2]._ra: You said it needed to be cleaned. Of what exactly?
BobruyskF1___nodes[4]._optionData[3]._ra: What sort of reward are we talking about here?
BobruyskF1___nodes[4]._optionData[4]._ra: I’ll see what I can do?
BobruyskF1___nodes[5]._stepChain[0]._dia: Wait, so you were with the caravan?
BobruyskF1___nodes[5]._stepChain[2]._dia: I financed a fair portion of it, 51 percent to be precise! Quite a few folks were itching to venture deeper into these islands. A few scholars, a mercenary, you know the kinds.
As long as they weren’t taking an interest in my business concerns, I figured the more the merrier… and the safer.
BobruyskF1___nodes[5]._stepChain[4]._dia: Nope, I sure was wrong on that one. As soon as those cats sprung their trap, just about everyone ran off. I stayed behind and did my best to defend the caravan.
Luckily I managed to hide myself and that young woman over there underneath some supplies, until those cats got bored and wandered off.
BobruyskF1___nodes[5]._stepChain[5]._dia: It’s good to know everyone seemed to have gotten away without getting scratched up too bad.
BobruyskF1___nodes[6]._stepChain[0]._dia: So where exactly is this mine? I just got here so-
BobruyskF1___nodes[6]._stepChain[1]._dia: *My* mine can be found a few islands down from this one.
BobruyskF1___nodes[6]._stepChain[2]._dia: He motions over to the lift just downhill from your vantage point.
BobruyskF1___nodes[6]._stepChain[3]._dia: It’s a bit of a trek to get there, but if you follow these trails, then you’ll reach it before long.
BobruyskF1___nodes[8]._stepChain[2]._com: Is this a bad time to tell you I’m bad with real life directions?
We can get there, but that’s going to be a long walk…
There will be plenty of catfolk between here and there…
BobruyskF1___nodes[8]._stepChain[3]._com: It shouldn’t be too bad a jog, I can carry anyone who gets tired!
BobruyskF1___nodes[10]._stepChain[0]._di: You said your mine needed to be cleaned out. Of what exactly?
BobruyskF1___nodes[10]._stepChain[1]._di: Some kind of Catfolk, from what my workers can manage to report. Half of my workers fled the scene at such a sprint, you’d think they never saw a whisker before in their life!
BobruyskF1___nodes[10]._stepChain[2]._di: Bobruysk takes a moment to check his pocket watch, wind it, and safely stow it away once more.
BobruyskF1___nodes[10]._stepChain[3]._di: Their fearful embellishments in their accounts have made for fine folktales for the patrons back at the lodge house. So I decided to go and investigate myself.
Especially since the good captain and station mistress seem reluctant to spare any platoons…
BobruyskF1___nodes[10]._stepChain[4]._di: You decide to let that slight stand, he’s clearly frustrated…
BobruyskF1___nodes[11]._rawContextHeader: Perhaps you should ask about the accounts?
BobruyskF1___nodes[11]._optionData[1]._r: Well, I’m sure it’s nothing that I can’t handle!
BobruyskF1___nodes[12]._stepChain[0]._di: Well, what exactly did they say?
BobruyskF1___nodes[12]._stepChain[2]._di: Yeah, even if it’s over the top, it might be helpful to have an idea.
BobruyskF1___nodes[12]._stepChain[3]._di: I would not put much stock in their accounts. Most of them mention some monstrous Cat-like creature.
BobruyskF1___nodes[12]._stepChain[4]._di: That doesn’t sound bad to you?
BobruyskF1___nodes[12]._stepChain[5]._di: Bobruysk lets out a dry chuckle. Waving his hand to dismiss the notion.
BobruyskF1___nodes[12]._stepChain[6]._di: Hardly a thing to trifle about. I’ve worked long in this business, and I’m sure the “monster” they describe is merely a Denmother.
BobruyskF1___nodes[12]._stepChain[7]._di: A Catfolk Matriarch? That would make the most sense.
BobruyskF1___nodes[12]._stepChain[8]._di: Precisely. Troublesome, yes, but not something to flee from. I’m sure you’ll convince her to scamper off from our worksite.
BobruyskF1___nodes[14]._stepChain[2]._co: Oh, it’s gonna be a scrap, but we’ll knock some sense into her!
BobruyskF1___nodes[14]._stepChain[3]._co: Oh, that’s easy, I thought we’d have to fight her.
I mean, maybe we could convince her, maybe she just needs a hug and a smooch~
I’ve dealt with some territorial disputes like this before, it shouldn't be too bad.
A shortie like her will be pure pimps ta drive off.
BobruyskF1___nodes[15]._stepChain[0]._di: I’m sure it’s manageable, as long as she isn’t too ornery.
BobruyskF1___nodes[15]._stepChain[1]._di: He seems pleased with your agreement, and prims his spruce moustache.
BobruyskF1___nodes[15]._stepChain[2]._di: That’s what I like to hear. You should be at ease now, yes?
BobruyskF1___nodes[16]._stepChain[0]._di: Well, I’m sure it’s nothing that I can’t handle!
BobruyskF1___nodes[16]._stepChain[1]._di: That’s exactly the sort of initiative I like to see. And it is the sort of character that I pay properly.
While you’re at it, once you’ve dealt with dreadful hold up, you can send the rest of the workers back to Katarina.
At least they didn’t turn tail, perhaps they can teach the rest of them a lesson in mental discipline.
BobruyskF1___nodes[16]._stepChain[2]._di: I’ll certainly make sure they’re alright. Hopefully she hasn’t used any of them as scratching posts!
BobruyskF1___nodes[17]._stepChain[0]._di: So, what sort of reward are we talking about here?
BobruyskF1___nodes[17]._stepChain[1]._di: He waves his hand as though dashing your concerns away.
BobruyskF1___nodes[17]._stepChain[2]._di: You need not worry. I am an important man with a great many concerns across The Frontier and Kingdom. Your payment will be substantial, and well worth the cost of getting my mining operation back on track.
I have substantial lines of credit all throughout this region, so you can rest assured you’ll have a bevy of bullions to count once you return.
BobruyskF1___nodes[17]._stepChain[3]._di: He seems rather confident he can reward you handsomely. If not, You’re sure Catarella or Joaqim could enforce *something*.
BobruyskF1___nodes[17]._stepChain[4]._di: Alright, I’ll take your word for it.
BobruyskF1___nodes[18]._stepChain[0]._di: I’ll see what I can do then. It sounds like I’ll be heading towards your mine while investigating this Savannah anyway.
BobruyskF1___nodes[18]._stepChain[1]._di: Bobruysk nods, moving to shake your hand.
BobruyskF1___nodes[18]._stepChain[2]._di: Excellent! I look forward to seeing you again then. Once my mine has been restored back to order.
BobruyskF1___nodes[18]._stepChain[3]._di: He shakes your hand excessively, like he was getting the last drop of molasses out of a jar.
BobruyskF1___nodes[18]._stepChain[4]._di: Now, if you’re going to head down, I suppose I will begin the long trek back to Katarina Station on foot. I wouldn’t mind sitting in my private quarters with a strong cup of tea, and a refreshing bubble bath!
BobruyskF1___nodes[18]._stepChain[5]._di: I’ve already cleared the way, so it shouldn't be too dangerous of a walk back.
BobruyskF1___nodes[18]._stepChain[6]._di: Then, as soon as I collect a few things, I’ll be off. So should you, it’s time we all stopped dilly dallying!
BobruyskF1___nodes[18]._stepChain[7]._di: He takes a look at his watch, and begins to collect his things as he gives you a short nod.
BobruyskF1___nodes[18]._stepChain[10]._d: Well, since I’m headed that way anyway, perhaps I’ll take care of this problem too!
BobruyskF1___nodes[20]._stepChain[2]._co: Long as he’s paying big, I guess we can go down there.
I’ve never scrapped with a denmother before, might be a bit of fun!
Let’s rescue those miners, and bring some light into those dark tunnels!
Nyeh heh! Maybe we can tell him it was three denmothers, and get a bigger reward.
I’m concerned, a man that wealthy might be less likely to part with his coin than a beggar.
F1EntranceMarine___nodes[2]._stepChain[0: Are you stocked up for the journey ahead, Frontiersman? The Savannah is dangerous, and only gets worse the deeper you go.
F1EntranceMarine___nodes[2]._stepChain[1: The Station’s supplies are limited, so I’m going to have to make due with what I’ve got!
F1EntranceMarine___nodes[2]._stepChain[2: Well, I can’t stop you. Hopefully, the *Quartermaster* and *Cook* will be back in working order soon.
One more thing, Frontiersman: There’s not much we can do if you’re beaten in a fight, so you’d better be prepared for the next life!
F1EntranceMarine___nodes[2]._stepChain[3: Don’t worry, I can take care of myself.
F1EntranceMarine___nodes[3]._stepChain[0: Are you stocked up for the journey ahead, Frontiersman? The Savannah is dangerous, and only gets worse the deeper you go.
F1EntranceMarine___nodes[3]._stepChain[1: I’m all stocked up and ready to go!
F1EntranceMarine___nodes[3]._stepChain[2: Aye, it’s been a relief to have the Quartermaster and Cook back in business.
We still have a personnel issue, though. There’s not much we can do if you’re beaten in a fight, so you’d better be prepared for the next life!
F1EntranceMarine___nodes[3]._stepChain[3: Don’t worry, I can take care of myself.
F1EntranceCaptain___nodes[0]._stepChain[: Welcome, Frontiersman, to our humble basecamp.
My men are well-acquainted with the area, so I advise you speak with them to get an idea of what you’re in for out in the Savannah.
When you’re ready to go, use the blue painted jumpstone down there to reach the nearest island.
Whoof, these jumps have been taking a lot out of me…
You’re in luck then, I have just the thing to help with that!
PreBossPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[12]: I’D APPRECIATE THAT MY VOICE IS STARTING TO HURT!
PreBossPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[15]: Sorry but… I didn’t see you standing there.
PreBossPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[16]: It’s fine, I’ve been a bit jumpy since the caravan attack.
PreBossPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[17]: Oh! Then it’s good I’ve found you. I’ve cleared a path all the way back to Katarina, it should be almost a straight shot there.
PreBossPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[18]: Blessings on you. Now you be careful!
PreBossPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[20]: No, I mean it. Just behind me is the strongest Catfolk I’ve ever seen.
She’s got ahold of another one of the Caravan members.
I’ve been waiting here so that anyone from the Union that comes by can get a warning before the fight.
PreBossPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[21]: I appreciate the advice then! And don’t worry, I’ll get your caravan partner back!
PreBossPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[22]: Thanks. And here, some food for your troubles. It will help you recover your strength before you take on that overgrown furball. Now, I’m out of here!
DoorScout___nodes[2]._stepChain[0]._dial: Ah, trying to open the door are ye? I’ve seen many try, but none succeed!
DoorScout___nodes[2]._stepChain[3]._dial: … No… You’re just the first person I’ve seen actually interested in the door, and I figured I’d try out being cryptic.
DoorScout___nodes[2]._stepChain[6]._dial: Oh absolutely. But still, no one has been able to get past this door either.
DoorScout___nodes[2]._stepChain[8]._dial: I can see that it’s locked, but I can’t tell by what mechanism.
DoorScout___nodes[2]._stepChain[9]._dial: I’ve prodded it a bit, there’s a hole in the center that I think some sort of crystal goes into.
DoorScout___nodes[2]._stepChain[10]._dia: Do you have any ideas on where to start looking for it?
DoorScout___nodes[2]._stepChain[11]._dia: None, but with any luck, it’ll be somewhere nearby. I’m going to stay here though and see if I can find a way around.
DoorScout___nodes[2]._stepChain[12]._dia: Sounds like a plan to me, see you around!
DoorScout___nodes[3]._stepChain[0]._dial: Ahoy, found any way of openin’ this door yet?
DoorScout___nodes[3]._stepChain[2]._dial: Well, I haven’t figured out a way around either, besides *building* something, but that takes more know-how than I got.
DoorScout___nodes[4]._stepChain[1]._dial: Yeah, I found this weird crystal and it opened the door up.
DoorScout___nodes[4]._stepChain[2]._dial: In~teresting… I’ll be sure to relay that when I return to Katarina Station.
DoorScout___nodes[4]._stepChain[4]._dial: Not yet, I think I’ll stay here and rest a bit, good luck ahead!
PylonPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[3]._d: C’mon… C’mon… Just… A little… More….!
PylonPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[4]._d: The man’s hands are wrapped around an exposed piece of metal on the pillar, his arms bulging. He’s trying to rip off the exposed plating.
PylonPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[12]._: Oh.. Hoh… Holy Heated Hottons… Nearly scared me to death there… Don’t be… Sneaking up on people like that…
PylonPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[15]._: Sorry about that, you shouldn’t let your guard down though, it’s pretty dangerous out here.
I mean, there are catfolk out here sneakier than I am.
PylonPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[16]._: Wait where… Oh… Oh no… I’m…
PylonPeasant___nodes[0]._stepChain[18]._: Very, yeah. I was part of a caravan taking supplies down to a mine. Everything was going alright until these catfolks descended on us and we scattered.

You seem like an experienced adventurer, Zynn. Know anything interesting about this area?
I’ve heard tell of a special cave in the area, sacred to the Sunnyfur Catfolk pride.
Few claim to have laid eyes on it, but Travelers have reported strange noises in the vicinity of the waterfall: Booming echoes, unusual rumblings… Even loud moans.
Many Monster species tend to congregate in secluded caves to… Well… “Relax.”

To be welcomed in such a special place can be very rewarding for pleasure-seekers. If one really is hidden in this area, I’d be keen to discover it.
What’s ‘the Great Root’? I’m not sure I’ve ever heard of it.
It is the spiritual practice of those whose souls are evergreen—Mandrakes, Dryads, Satyrs and the like— although other Monster and Human practitioners are not unknown.

Shamans and Druids of the Root teach that the universe is a long sequence of events, symbolized by the Great Root itself—as though the whole universe were a living road, stretching on forever in every direction…
My old mentor was a staunch believer in the oneness of the Great Root’s universal path… But I believe we choose our own paths in life, even if it means living with a certain degree of anxiety.
I am personally not a follower. But meditations on the Great Root are undeniably calming for those whose paths might seem dark and uncertain.
Interesting! I’ll have to order some books on the topic from Ivory Tower.
ZynnF2Dialogue___nodes[10]._stepChain[1]: It’s been a pleasure, Zynn, but I should keep moving.
ZynnF2Dialogue___nodes[10]._stepChain[2]: Keep your eyes peeled, {0}. The world's greatest bounty truly lies in the nature that surrounds us!
ZolandAnnalizzaDialogue___nodes[1]._step: *Sniff sniff* Hmmngh… Hotton? Mm yes, noting hints of nuttyness and even a spice to it… But…. *Sniiiiiff* It’s tasty, but not more than that.
Excuse me?
This hunched-over person’s head turns, giant, floppy ears twitching. His sightless eyes gaze in your general direction.
Haah? Huh?… *Sniff sniff* Human-thing? What are you doing here? it’s not safe to be traipsing around.
I could ask you the same question, this is hardly the place to be standing and sniffing.
I’m not standing! I'm hunting!
Hunting… for what exactly?
Herbs and stuff! Many ingredients for making the potions, I need to do *lots* of sniffing to find the good ingredients.
Isn’t it sort of dangerous to be sniffing out here alone?
I’m not alone! Marines are around, plus, mom is good at looking out.
Ma! A strange human is speaking while I’m sniffing!
Oh! Hello Human! A pleasure to meet you!
A pleasure, I’m {0} by the way!
Annaliza, and this is my son, Zoland.
Zoland seems fairly preoccupied with shoving his face into a thicket of weeds and taking a large whiff.
So you two are potion makers?
Yes! My son and I run a potion business, but we are nomadic.
Our little shop is mobile! Right now we are hunting for new ingredients to replace our depleted stocks.
Well don’t let me stand in your way then, though before I go, can I ask a few questions?
Of course! I’m happy to talk!
ZolandAnnalizzaDialogue___nodes[2]._opti: Do you mind telling me a bit of the potion-making process?
Does your son always sniff things so intently?
ZolandAnnalizzaDialogue___nodes[3]._step: Very! When someone comes to us with a pain, whether it is joint, back, or head, we make a potion specifically for them.
Our potions are not cure-alls, they are made specifically for our clients, and we make sure they know that they should *not* be sharing them.
ZolandAnnalizzaDialogue___nodes[4]._step: Do you mind telling me a bit of the potion-making process?

Usually it starts with a consultation, I deeply inhale the client’s scent, and keep that in mind as I try to brew up a potion. Your scent can tell me a lot about what ingredients will work and what won’t!

Of course, then we begin to brew up a potion. It can depend a lot on what ingredients we have on hand. Some are better for circulation, or smell sweet, and others are good for illness, or else they might be spiny.
And some smell pretty blue, and others smell red!
And then you heat them until just right! That part can be difficult, as the altitude and water quality can affect the result. I’d say “Just eyeball it but…”
Annaliza waves her hand in front of her pale eyes.
We do it all by scent and sound! That’s about it!
That is… quite a process you have.
The thing you should take away from this is you need a lot of experience!
ZolandAnnalizzaDialogue___nodes[5]._step: Does your son always sniff things so intently?
Yes! I must sniff things, *all* the things. Scents are like a library, each book is a liiiiittle different.
The more sniffs I smell, the more books go into the library. The library gets bigger meaning I can know what needs to be used with what to make potions really well.
It’s very important to our trade, and he has a very good nose. He’s been able to tell when a potion is ready for its next steps by the smallest adjustment of smell.
I am very proud of his ability, honing your sense of smell is necessary for Batfolk, but even more so with those that learn our trade. I couldn’t ask for a more talented and smart apprentice!
ZolandAnnalizzaDialogue___nodes[6]._step: We will be here for a while longer, and I won’t say no to your company if you stop by again!
Sure! If I have the time!
ZolandAnnalizzaDialogue___nodes[7]._step: *Sniff sniff* Ma! I smell a familiar face! Smells like that farmer!
Oh! It’s nice to see you again {0}!
Annaliza, Zoland! You’re still searching for ingredients?
Yes yes! This island has plenty of potential ingredients, so we’re taking our time here!
Well, while I have you, mind if I ask a few questions?
Not at all! What would you like to ask?
SlainPiratesInteractionDialogue___nodes[: A grisly end to a brutal way of life… I pray they learn their lessons after their rebirth.
Damn, this is brutal! What are these spikes made of—Coral? *That’s* not a good sign…
Their deaths were clearly violent… But what could have left these spikes? I need more information to cross-reference my readings…
Lookit the size o’ them spikes! Only one thing coulda left these behind, and I’m prayin’ we never run into it.
These spikes… Could they be the work of a powerful Centaur Mason?
Reminds me o' the tales back home… Great beasties overgrown with coral, forever searchin' fer a meal big enough tae slack their hunger! Guess no one ever made them a proper bit o’ scran!
How awful! You won’t let anything like this happen to us, right {0}?~
A bad way to go, even for Pirates…
Great, just what we need: Mysterious death coral spikes to spice up our day.
Look out {0}, I think I see something huge coming from above!!! … What, too soon?~
I can only hope we don’t meet such a nasty end…
At least it looks like they died… Quickly? Um, it’s always good to look on the bright side!
BigBiteInteractionDialogue___nodes[0]._s: Huh! That landslide looks suspiciously like bite marks.
BigBiteInteractionDialogue___nodes[2]._s: Creatures of this size are very rare in the High Climb, but everyone’s heard stories of massive Sea Kings prowling the open sky…
Only one thing coulda taken a bite *that* big, and we don’t wanna be within fifty leagues of it!
I’m starting to notice that when my readings imply something big is coming, they sometimes mean it quite literally…
That’s quite a pair o’ chompers! Dunno if the bunny buster’d even leave a scratch on a beast that size…
My master used to tell me tales of great beasts that roam the Skies… Here’s hoping we don’t end up proving him right, eh?
Och, the poor lad must've been pure starvin'! I feel 'is pain...
Oh, how frightening! But I know you could protect us if it came back! Right {0}? …... Right?
… This beast has a big mouth.
Mounting proof of a giant, deadly creature flying around the area. Great.
Hmmm, reminds me of the bite I took out of your birthday cake…
How would one even fight such a creature? We wouldn’t stand a chance…
Wow, the big guy must’ve been *super* hungry!
PostFirstHomunculusFightDialogue___nodes: You take a moment to wipe the sweat off your brow and observe your work. The pirates were incapacitated, but those sludge-like creatures…
On closer examination, those sludge-like creatures-
Are these… homunculi?
You recognize them from the classes on obscure beings, back at the academy. Creatures that seemed to be composed of only black goop.
These ones are rapidly dissolving, their bodies bubbling away until only inky black dregs remain.
Intro
PartyPresent
NoParty
PartyCheck
We’ll have to move quickly, and stamp them out before them and their ilk starts to spread…
Eugh, homunculi… least it’s just the little ones. I hear they can get BIG.
Oh goodness! I’ve heard a few ghastly tales about them told around the communal areas of the Fi-Star!
Ugh, call em what you want. To me they’re a bunch of disgustin’, walkin’ mud puddles!
Oh, homunculi. I didn’t expect to see *these* out this way. Why do you think it was consorting with pirates?
Mucky thing. Could almost ruin me appetite.
I’m glad no one got hurt during that fight {0}... Well.. That none of *our* friends got hurt.
I think we’re done here.
That’s what you get for messing with us!
Nyehehe, they never saw it coming!
I just hope that’s the last of them…
Hey {0}, we totally beat them without breaking a sweat!
The only question is, why were these things were fighting *with* the pirates… Hmmm… Alright, I can’t solve a mystery with so little information, so we should carry on.
PartyChimeIn
These things aren’t known for fighting alongside non-homunculi. Something strange is going on here.
Ending
SailInteractionDialogue___nodes[0]._step: You find a torn sail that likely belonged to the pirates. With a sail this big all by itself, you don’t imagine that the ship it came from was able to land in one piece without it.
Which leads to the question, where is the *rest* of the ship?
SailInteractionDialogue___nodes[3]._step: This close to Cloverton? That would be *very* bad for us.
You should make sure Sieger knows about it then. He’ll want those windmills hoora tuned!
SailInteractionDialogue___nodes[4]._step: Guess I’ll let Sieger know about this once we head back… oh and Goldra, this is kinda like her *thing*.
I’d say that there are a few beasties I know of big enough to take a bite of a ship. I wouldn’t want any of *those* around here though.
You’d be a tube not ta think something’s off about all this. If I’m right, let’s hope the thing that did this is long gone by now.
I pulled The Maelstrom for this place. I doubt it was a literal storm that knocked the ship out of the sky, but something *like* a storm.
I genuinely hope that whoever crashed, pirates or otherwise, were able to make it out alright.
I don’t imagine anyone that made it through the crash would have stuck around for long. And neither should we {0}.
Well! With all this spare material, maybe the Catfolk here will use it for their dens!
Those brutes must have come up here to recover it. But you looked so dashing putting a stop to them, {0}!
Ohh, something bad happened here... maybe we should head back for now, and you could come back tomorrow?
Oooo, think we could make some trip traps with this spare rigging~?
Hm… this must have come from a sizable ship. There may be more pirates than we thought…
If you ask me, whatever did this should have been more thorough!
Yeah, whatever happened here has clearly passed on, so we should too.
SailInteractionDialogue___nodes[5]._step: No use standing around however, there is plenty more to do here.
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[0]._stepCh: Before you is a rambunctious Hairball, heavily armed with pots and pans. She happily smashes her ill-gotten cookware together in a thunderous one-cat drum show.
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[2]._stepCh: You don’t need a fortune teller to figure out where those pots and pans came from…
And here I was thinking I was the only one who liked banging things together for fun!
Hmm. I *could* be wrong, but I think we’ve found our kitchen thief~
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[4]._rawCon: Sorry, what!? YOU’LL HAVE TO SPEAK UP!!!
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[4]._option: Use your superior size to stop this madness.
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[5]._stepCh: Yeah, nevermind. I really can’t deal with this right now.
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[7]._rawCon: Can you please do something about this!?
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[7]._option: I am a warrior of peace!
I cannot permit this to continue.
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[8]._stepCh: You reach out and grab one of the Hairball’s pans mid-swing. With your perfectly average-sized human strength, it’s no problem to lift the pan with the hapless Hairball dangling off the handle.
The Hairball blinks and stares at you in confusion, as though noticing you for the first time.
Whuh? Who da gak are you? Whatcha want, nekky?
You drop the pan, and the Hairball falls back on the ground with a soft thud.
That’s better! Listen, about those pots and pans-
The Hairball raises the pots and pans with an eager glint in her eye.
No, no-no bang! Please no more bang!
Look Hairball, this cookware doesn’t belong to you. I need to take it back to Katarina Station so the Cook can do his job properly!
Do his “job”?! Pffft! Nekky no watch their stuff an’ now they miss it. Typical nekky!
I ain’t thievin’, but I’m gakin’ bored an’ I like *BANG!*
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[9]._stepCh: You flinch, fully expecting the cacophony to continue.
Instead, the Hairball suddenly stops their banging and looks as though noticing you for the first time.
Whuh? Who da gak are you? Whatchu want, nekky?
Whew, finally! So, about those pots and pans-
The Hairball raises the pots and pans expectantly.
No, no-no bang! Please no more bang!
Look Hairball, this cookware doesn’t belong to you. I need to take it back to Katarina Station so the Cook can do his job properly!
“Owner”?! Pffft! Nekky no watch their stuff an’ now they miss it. Typical nekky!
I ain’t thievin’, but I’m gakin’ bored an’ I like *BANG!*
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[10]._rawCo: Only so many ways to distract a cat…
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[11]._stepC: You pull a loose thread off your shirt, which quickly gets the Hairball’s full attention.
Her giant yellow eyes carefully follow the movement of the string as you start flicking it playfully over her head.
She jumps and flops clumsily on the ground, batting and swiping at the string with shocking ineptitude.
After a few minutes of this vaguely amusing display, the Hairball collapses on the ground with her round belly heaving from the heroic effort.
Whew, zat was nice workout! Good workout, nekky!
Ok, you can have bang now. Enjoy bang-bang!
Thanks, I will! But what about you?
Pfft, I still bored. I gonna do the Other Thing now.
The… “Other Thing”? Did you mean “something else”?
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[12]._stepC: You reach down and hold out your hand, letting the Hairball sniff you curiously.
After some carefully snuffling, she approves of your scent and rubs her face and head on your outstretched fingers.
You give the Hairball a few minutes of completely unearned affection. She even rolls onto her back and makes you rub her belly, the criminal!
Ahhh, zats th’ stuff. You nekky good for something after all!
Ok, let’s deal: You get bang-bang, I get scratchy whenever I want! Capiche?!
Ok, deal! But how will we find each other for scratchy?
Hehe! Silly stupid nekky, I mark you for life!
BangCookwareRetrieval___nodes[14]._stepC: *Please* tell me that’s the last we’ll see of that furball!
What a clamor for such a wee lass! I’m downright impressed.
Meh! I’ve heard louder. Wot? Why are you so quiet suddenly?
This “Bang” creature’s motives are shrouded in mystery… I can’t detect their aura!
Stripes n’ Scales, I’m glad that’s over.
I dunno, she seems cool to me!
So *that’s* how to get your attention. Didn’t anyone ever warn you about positive reinforcement, {0}?~
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[2]._stepChain[0: You see a stooped, shaggy figure with a sturdy pickaxe searching the rocky ground intently.
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[2]._stepChain[2: Hey there! Are you looking for something?
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[2]._stepChain[3: Huh? A Fronty…? What’re you doin’ out here?
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[2]._stepChain[5: Well, I’m a Frontiersman—{0}, Frontiersman First Class to be exact! This is the part of the Frontier I was posted to. What about you?
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[2]._stepChain[6: The miner looks distracted as their eyes dart quickly around the ground, but avoid meeting your gaze.
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[2]._stepChain[1: I-I mean, I am *a* digger… W-well, I’m really more of a prospector, really… Wait—What was the question?
Ugh, sorry… Don’t got much room fer words in a head full o’ rocks. Normally I let someone else do the talkin’ for me, like Edeline…
If it’s alright with you, let’s just call each other “Fronty” and “Digger” and consider the introductions settled.
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[3]._stepChain[0: … Huh? Oh, hey Fronty. Keepin’ busy?
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[4]._optionData[: Lemme get out of your hair.
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[5]._stepChain[1: So, what’re you looking for out here?
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[5]._stepChain[2: See these coral spikes? Yer probably familiar with the material, bein’ all educated and such, but most folks don’t get just how peculiar it is.
I mean, a rock that grows…? Could be useful, ‘specially out here in the great vasty nothin’.
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[5]._stepChain[4: Come to think of it, there was a working group back at Ivory Tower that studied the potential of coral for growing habitations!
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[5]._stepChain[5: Habitation, sure. But if you stretch yer imagination, I ain’t sure there’s nothin’ you *couldn’t* grow outta coral.
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[5]._stepChain[7: You could grow a coral statue… Coral walls for yer garden…
Coral table… Coral chairs… Coral… Butcher block…?
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[5]._stepChain[9: Coral planter boxes… Coral fountain… Coral stepping stones…
Coral garden furniture… Coral garden gnome!
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[7]._stepChain[2: Coral prayer beads? I’m always losing mine…
Coral… Armor? Would that work? … Nah.
Coral charms are *quite* popular with tourists…
Coral, coral, coral, blah blah blah! Aw, now it sounds weird when I say it…
Growin’ coral? Pah, forget it—Leave it tae good ole wood n’ stone!
Some coral jewelry might look… Charming?
Coral cocktail garnishes might be a novel idea.
Coral exfoliating paste might be rather chic…
A necklace of coral would protect thine self from illness and enfeeblement.
… Wuh? Sorry, as soon as a renewable resource was mentioned, I started cooking up a profiteering scheme as a reflex.
Hmph. I don’t like coral. It’s coarse and rough and irritating, and it gets everywhere.
Wow, this woman is a genius! So many great ideas…
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[8]._stepChain[0: … And you could always do coral modern art.
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[8]._stepChain[1: Wouldn’t a coral statue turn out like modern art, anyway?
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[8]._stepChain[2: Eh… I was thinkin’ more like somethin’ you’d hang on the wall.
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[9]._optionData[: I think I get the picture.
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[10]._stepChain[: I think I get the picture, Digger.
Yeah, exactly—Like a hangin’ picture frame!
… Wait, what were we talkin’ about?
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[11]._stepChain[: What about coral loofahs? Bet you could make tons of those.
What, like a pumice stone? Huh, I guess that’d work. Let’s see…
You and Digger continue listing countless objects that could be produced by growing coral—a few even verge on sounding remotely useful.
After an unknown amount of time passes, Digger stops listing objects out loud and just stands there quietly for a moment, blinking distractedly.
……… And that’s just about it, I think.
That was very enlightening. Thank you.
DiggerF2Dialogue___nodes[12]._stepChain[: Lemme get outta your hair, Digger.
Huh? Oh, sure. See ya round, Fronty.
PoacherTrap2___nodes[0]._stepChain[2]._d: I’d be amused if this were some type of joke, but I’m fairly certain this is a serious attempt at a trap…. Is this working?
PoacherTrap2___nodes[0]._stepChain[3]._d: Standing… Or rather, floating before you is a juicy turnog clearly suspended by a wire inside of a poorly painted archway.
Blues and whites practically slapped onto a wooden board to give the appearance of a sky. It is… Of remarkably poor quality.
PoacherTrap2___nodes[0]._stepChain[4]._d: This will just wind up hurting someone if I leave it out like this, so now…
PoacherTrap2___nodes[0]._stepChain[5]._d: The process is quick, your shovel comes out and cuts the string holding the turnog up. The mechanism whirrs to life and…
PoacherTrap2___nodes[0]._stepChain[10]._: The cage slams shut with a heavy clang. Swinging your shovel again, the shovel’s blade cuts through the metal and thoroughly dismantles the device.
PoacherTrap2___nodes[0]._stepChain[12]._: You survey the damage, there’s really not much that could be salvaged from this mess.
At least, until you manage to spot the reverse side of the painted background.
PoacherTrap2___nodes[0]._stepChain[13]._: There’s a bunch of measurements taken…
PoacherTrap2___nodes[0]._stepChain[14]._: Looking over them, they’re very sound and precise, but-
PoacherTrap2___nodes[0]._stepChain[15]._: There’s a lot of attention to detail for someone with such sloppy handwriting… Clumsy perfectionism?
Pirate Note__line_3: <PLAYERNAME> := There is a note saying "I've stashed the ley acumen and some of the migration patterns of the beast in the nearby waterfall, the cave should be empty sans a construct I’ve modified to protect it. Using some accurate pathing I should be able to maneuver past the currents and enter the cave-"
(Interaction) PreQuest - Denmother___nod: Listen, Fronty… I know this looks bad, but I want it t’be known that me mates an’ I had no clue things would get this sour.
We were told this’d be a quiet gig! We didn’t wanna turn everything into goo…
Surrrre ya didn’t. And I bet you were about to give up your life of crime, and sign yourselves up for community service too!
Back off, fishbrains! I spent the whole battle watchin’ yer back, just to get lip from ye after… So ungrateful!
Just as the two humans put their hands on their swords, the Denmother suddenly looms over them…
… And easily drags them away, separating them like bickering children.
Um, I’ll come back when things are a little less tense around here…
(Pre-Battle) Denmother, Pirate and Marin: A desperate scene unfolds before you as a pirate, marine and catfolk den-mother struggle for survival against a gaggle of gargling homunculi. What chaos!
Whoah, this looks *really* bad!
Init
Is Present
Not Present
Is Party Present?
Hmm, let’s see… A pirate, a marine and a den-mother walk into a bar…
They’ve put aside their differences to help one another… Let’s help them quickly, {0}!
Evil goo in front of you, bandits at your back? Tough spot for a soldier!
When the cards are down, the need for survival creates strange bedfellows indeed!
Hah, wot a brawl! Let’s get in there quick, {0}!
Right then, let’s get these lads and the beasties separated!
Ohhh, they’re fighting together, sweating together, bleeding together…! I wonder if they’ll kiss~
Practical. It’d be foolish to cling to rules and laws in a situation like this.
Let’s get this over with!
Hmmm, clever pirate! Methinks they’ll let him off easy for this~
-groan-... Those pirates better be grateful for this!
C’mon, {0}, let’s go help them!
Is Present
Time to get our hands dirty!
Start_Battle
Start Battle
(Interaction) Ghost Worker___nodes[0]._s: Um, excuse me… Sir? Do you need help moving on? Are you… In pain?
Oh, uh… Sorry, I didn’t mean to offend you!
S’alright. I might be a Ghost, but my skin’s not THAT thin.
… Ya know, this is going a lot differently than I thought it would.
Yeah, tell me about it. One second I’m minding my own business, then I’m fallin’ balls-first into a big pool of acid.
Now I feel tired all the time, my skin looks worse than ever, my clothes are a mess, and worst of all? I’ve got “brain fog”!
It’s probably for the best, anyway. After all, what if I had a *bad* life? A broken marriage, an incurable disease, a bunch of debt, a criminal record…
Or maybe even… A RevEeEerse MoRtGaGe!?~
Burnin’ Bofroots!!! That’s such a poor financial decision!!
Well, is there anything I can do to help you… “Move on”?
Believe it or not, I actually feel alright. I mean, I barely feel anything at all, really… Just a vague sense that I got what was comin’ to me.
I’ll probably stick around for a while and try not to worry about it. After that, who knows? Maybe I’ll just disappear, eventually.
Huh! I guess that doesn’t sound so bad. Good luck with that, then!
(Interaction) Ghost Worker___nodes[3]._s: There you have it. Was it worth it, Kaleida?
Well, I think I learned what a “reverse mortgage” is, soooo… No. It really, really wasn’t.
That’s what you get when you play around with things from beyond this mortal coil: Cursed knowledge.
(Interaction) Ghost Worker___nodes[4]._s: As clever in death as he was in life, I’m sure. No doubt he’s just covering up the ugly truth with humor.
Hmmmm, I wonder if he can still feel… Certain things. You know what I mean, {0}?~
Hah! It seems that dead humans are even more amusing than living humans… Oh, um… That definitely sounded more ominous than I intended.
This man is avoiding the truth regarding his attachments. When a spirit can't move on, there’s always a reason—whether we like it or not.
Ohhh, I *really* didn’t need to look death in the face today…
Clever as he is, this fellow is clearly used to avoiding serious conversation. Perhaps avoidant behavior in life has kept him here in death…?
Oof, how horrible… I was just thinkin’ what it’d be like to never have snacks again!
Damn, ya hate t’see it… I just wish there was somethin’ we could do, y’know?
Is it normal to laugh when you’re scared? I mean, not that I was *really* scared… Heh?
Oh! Well, um… That was neat! I guess he’s fine, then?
Very amusing. Can we go now? It’s friggin’ freezing in here, and it smells like coins!
Do you think his body is still around here somewhere? Maybe he left something interesting behind…
At the very least, we can have Jubelle or Catarella write up a report and have someone try and wrap up his loose ends. We may need to anyway, with the state of the mines…
(Interaction) Ghost Worker___nodes[6]._s: What’s up, bub? See somethin’ you like?
Who “are” me? Well that’s part of my problem: I don’t really remember!
Nor do I hold any remembrance of who or what I once was. Be then be we the same manner of being, thou and I?
I don’t think so, bub. I might be a bit of a dim bulb at this point, but I can tell when something’s living and when it’s dead…
And you, my friend, are *not* dead. Not completely anyway.
Hold on—What do you mean, he’s “not completely” dead?
Like I said—Got a bit of a dim bulb up here. I’m kinda runnin’ on nothin’ but vibes, lately.
But when I look at your big blue friend here, even I can tell that only a *part* of him is dead. It’s like there’s two separate things, and *one* of them is pretty much alive.
Um, would you excuse us please?
Are you okay, Garst? That was probably a bit weird to hear.
… Y-yae, {0}, I am well. Though it be dread atimes, the more knowledge I gain of myself, the better.
I thank thee for thy concern, but I am ready and able to serve thee, to the fullest extent of my power.
(Interaction) After Explosions___nodes[0: You did it, {0}! And it looks like the battle is starting to clear up, too!
I can’t take credit, Keely. It was your plan!
Ohoho, this is *definitely* going in the logbook…
Wrecked Acid Refinement___nodes[0]._step: Whew, that’s one big alembic. If I remember rightly, that model is used for acid refining.
I wonder what they were making here… Could be that they were trying to make the acid into something that could sell elsewhere.
Or they could have been refining it into a form they could use to purify certain metals… Hmm…
Whatever the purpose, it’s likely to go unused for some time, if ever again.
Dad was real proud when they started selling these. you wouldn’t believe how much acid you can find on the frontier- or wait, maybe you can now…
Looks like a Stromboli Industries model 2A. Versatile, but prone to breakin’ down over time. Good fer short-term cleanup projects.
I’m definitely not touchin’ that. Knowin’ me I’m liable to break it just lookin’ at it funny…
Seems rather industrial and cold. And, it’s making my stem itch something fierce.
We should bring this back to the farm. It would greatly impact our ability to refine goods.
Wow, do you think we could bring it back to the farm and use it as a communal gempas wine decanter?
The only thing we might save from these mines is the equipment…

Ripper Boss Intro___nodes[1]._stepChain[: ZoomIn
There, at the end of the cavern, past the scattered jetsam and flotsam, you spot a remaining Union Marine, wounded.
Ughh…
He looks badly hurt, and discombobulated, his attackers still nearby, and closing in on the last remaining member of their prey.
Holy barnacles! What is *that*!?
Show Boss

The largest of the homunculi looks viscous and sharp, it stands at the back of the cavern, seeming to be holding its ground… guarding a massive, pulsing growth which takes up the entire back wall of the cavern.
Show HEART
And what is THAT?!
The huge mass of black ichor hangs from the ceiling, its texture like firm gelatin as it throbs and undulates. A deep red glow slowly peeks through the thinner membranes of its surface.
Whatever *that* is, it’s no good. I can tell you that much.
Looks like a bit o’ haggis got forgotten at the bottom of an icebox fer too long!
Woah, that is Ug-gly!
Oh dear…
Woah, it looks like something you’d find in an outhouse!
It’s something that’s got my hackles on edge. Should we be here, {0}?
More importantly, it looks like all those homunculi are guarding it. I say we pan it!
I feel as though ancient memories are warning me about it.
It clearly wasn’t here before. So I say we smash it and all of these vandals!
I’ve only heard scary tales of these from Ocan, when I was a wee brownie… This is Serious, {0}!
The dark seed of homunculi has taken root in this island. We should excise it.
That *thing* looks like a homunculi nest… I’ve *never* seen one this big before though!
The more you look at it, the more a strange, bestial hiss escapes from the big creature’s maw. The marine, seeming to come to his senses, realizes that he’s surrounded.
H-Help! Oh scales preserve me! This thing ripped through my squad!
Boss Roar
MEERGROOOOWWWWW!!!
You slap your shovel down on a nearby rock, bringing its attention up to you.
Hey you fleabag! Pick on someone who can fight back!
HISSSSSSSSSSS!!!
Aw barnacles, I think I fit that description. Alright, let’s rumble!
Ripper Boss Intro___nodes[3]._stepChain[: Right behind you, {0}. Or, in front of you, that’d probably be better!
Unless the current of fate is shifted, he is doomed. Help him, Wavemaker!
This big meanie is gonna get what’s coming to them!
{0}, we have to save that marine!
To arms, comrades, let our blows sing doom to the enemy!
Strike fast and hard. its aura is one of pain and wrath!
If it wants to pick on someone it’s own size, it sure picked the wrong lass to tangle with!
Hey, goo-for-brains, that’s a face only a mother could love, too bad you probably never had one!
Alright, ya munter! Let’s take a few spins on my Bunny Buster, and see if ya feel lucky?
You picked the wrong bunch to mess with, you sludge chuggers!
Maybe we should go and get reinforcements? No? Okay, let’s go…
Ripper Boss Intro___nodes[5]._stepChain[: You slap your shovel down on a nearby rock, bringing the Ripper's attention up to you, again.
Hey you fleabag! Pick on someone who can fight back!
Lost Peasant - Post Cleanup___nodes[0]._: Hey! Nice to see you again!
Nice to see you too! Still lost in here?
Nah, I found a way out, apparently I have you to thank for making the surface a bit safer.
Buuuut, I think I’m gonna stay here for a while more, I really like this place.
Well, don’t let me stand in your way then!
Blue Pre-Cleanup___nodes[0]._stepChain[3: That’s right! The name’s {0}, Frontiersman First Class.
Blue Pre-Cleanup___nodes[0]._stepChain[4: Oh my gosh, a First Classman!!! You can call me Blue, {0|sir|sir|ma’am}! Frontiersman Third Class, at your service!
Panzer, Volamont and myself were sent here from Dogwood to evaluate the mines for rehabilitation. Obviously, things didn’t go *quite* as planned…
But now that a First Classman is on the scene, things are gonna turn right around—I just know it!
Blue Pre-Cleanup___nodes[2]._stepChain[1: I’m gonna do my best, Blue! Do you know anything about what happened here?
Blue Pre-Cleanup___nodes[2]._stepChain[3: Boy, do I!!! I was right there when the Marine Ray crashed and the fighting broke out.
Panzer and I hustled into the mines for safety, and we managed to stay hidden for a while. I was so worried about Volamont, who got separated from us in the chaos…
Imagine my surprise when a couple’a Pirates came down and said they’d lead us to safety! Would you believe it?
Blue Pre-Cleanup___nodes[2]._stepChain[5: W-well, I guess I’m kinda trusting like that… I just knew they weren’t gonna hurt us. And now here we are!
Blue Pre-Cleanup___nodes[3]._stepChain[1: Have you spoken with my partner, Panzer? I think he saw something *weird* happen down there… He hasn’t quite been himself since we escaped…
Volamont Post-Cleanup___nodes[0]._stepCh: Hey there! I don’t think we’ve met—I’m {0}, Frontiersman First Class!
Well well, a First Classman! The name’s Volamont, but you can call me Monty.
Say, you don’t happen to have a ball on ya…? I’m freakin’ bored out here.
A ball? You mean… For playing fetch?
Of course, for playin’ fetch! What else—Juggling?
I may not look it, {0}, but I’m the star chaser of our hometown league back on the mainland.
Lying to a First Classmen on the job, Monty? I oughta write you up!
What lie? I meant *rising* star, obviously! The rising was implied!
Don’t listen to him, Monty. I believe in you!~
Blue blows her partner a big kiss, making Volamont blush and scratch shyly behind his ears.
Volamont Post-Cleanup___nodes[2]._stepCh: So, what’s the story with you three? You seem like a tight crew!
Noticed that, did ya? Panzer, Blue and I have been tight ever since we first met back at Ivory Tower.
I mean, *really* tight. Tighter than tight. Tight, tight, tight, like tighter than a Hairball’s—
Well, the profs did too! They put us on the Facilitated Breeding track together, and we’ve been a team ever since.
So *that’s* why you’re all Third Classmen… They never finished upper class certification for the Breeding specialists.
We Breeders don’t get no respect from bureaucrats, I tell ya. They think it’s all bumpin’ and breath mints, but we’re some of the most highly educated relationship psychologists in the Kingdom!
I don’t mind, though. Titles don’t matter much to me, so long as I get to stick with Blue and Pan!
Volamont Post-Cleanup___nodes[3]._stepCh: What, was it somethin’ I said?
Pirates vs Homunculi___nodes[0]._stepCha: Crabs! These homunculi are getting rowdy!
Let the bloomin’ Catfolk tangle with ‘em! We’ll just head-
Pirates vs Homunculi___nodes[2]._stepCha: Lads, you’re not *possibly* thinkin’ of makin’ a mess an’ not stickin’ around to clean it, are ya?
You’re not going anywhere till you pick up all of your toys!
You ruffians think you can do all of this? You’re not going anywhere!
Oh~hoh! Looks like they’re thinkin’ about makin’ a run for it! Think they can make it past us~?
You seem a bit unbalanced. Consider us a well needed karmatic adjustment.
Halt, villains! Throw down your arms!
Oh hey new friends!... Wait, no pirates aren’t friends… Oh hey new enemies!
Not a chance, pirates. You’re not getting away.
You’re really gonna turn and run? I really don’t want to chase you, so please just fight us…
Wowee fellas, you’re gonna run? Well then you better go catch it! Er… wait, that didn’t work…
Oohhh, sorry but… No… I’m afraid you’re not going anywhere… Sorry….
I suppose we couldn’t convince you to lay down your arms and surrender… No? Ugh, let’s hurry this up then…
I don’t think this will take much time. We’ll get you nice and tied up for the Union to haul you in.
Pirates vs Homunculi___nodes[3]._stepCha: Sorry to say, but you’re not getting past me.
Pirates vs Homunculi___nodes[4]._stepCha: Ah shuck me sideways! C’mon, if we want to leave, we’re gonna have to fight our way out!
Jurgen___nodes[0]._stepChain[2]._dialogP: Making your way farther up the platform, you find Jurgen working on an injured Miner’s mangled leg.
Jurgen___nodes[0]._stepChain[3]._dialogP: Nnngh… Can’t ya just magick me back to normal or somethin’?
Jurgen___nodes[0]._stepChain[4]._dialogP: “Or something”? Ugh, what do you think this is, a walk-in clinic?
Jurgen___nodes[0]._stepChain[13]._dialog: Jurgen quickly stuffs a rag into the Miner’s mouth, allowing him to bite down through the pain as the rabbit-boy forcefully sets his broken leg.
Jurgen___nodes[0]._stepChain[14]._dialog: Also, I’m NOT a healer. I’m a smith.
Jurgen___nodes[2]._stepChain[2]._comment: Out on the frontier, one must be prepared to master many arts—Especially medicine.
Whoof, I remember my first broken leg. Tickled somethin’ fierce!
Very noble, but I think I’ll leave this kind of dirty work to the experts, if you don’t mind~
Hooo, now *that’s* a good settin’! Had to do quite a few o’ these meself, back in the bad ole’ days…
Ahhh, human medicine is so interesting! Now, if it were *me*, I’d just cut the stem below the joint, and two new legs would grow out! … Juuust kidding~
Oof! Felt a twinge in me leg just hearin’ that!
Sometimes, a bit of pain is necessary to heal. That looked like a lot of pain…
Oh, to be a rugged explorer on the frontier, getting by with nothing but wits and grit!~ … Oh. That’s already us, isn’t it…
Ugh, these conditions are terrible! We better do somethin’ about this quickly, {0}.
Ohhh, the looks on those miners’ faces when they heard that *pop*~
Jurgen___nodes[3]._stepChain[1]._dialogP: Seeing you approach, Jurgen flashes a handsome smile and casually wipes some blood and grime from their forehead.
Jurgen___nodes[3]._stepChain[2]._dialogP: If it isn’t {0}, Frontiersman First Class! Just the person I wanted to see~
Jurgen___nodes[3]._stepChain[3]._dialogP: I’m, uh, glad to see you too, Jurgen! Looks like you guys have a pretty solid operation going, here.
Jurgen___nodes[3]._stepChain[4]._dialogP: Yep, we got ourselves a pretty good spot. In theory, we’d have no problem holding here until the battle subsided…
Jurgen___nodes[3]._stepChain[7]._dialogP: This is no ordinary battlefield, {0}. Although these homunculi are weak and slow, from this vantage I’ve seen that they’re produced from some kind of nest inside the mines.
If we don’t take care of that nest soon, we’ll all be overrun—Pirates, Marines and Catfolk alike!
Jurgen___nodes[3]._stepChain[8]._dialogP: No sweat! First things first, I’m gonna blow the Quartermaster’s charges (which I’m definitely qualified to do) so everyone can escape.
Once the retreat begins, I’ll head into the mines and take care of that nest!
Jurgen___nodes[3]._stepChain[9]._dialogP: You’ve got to be careful, {0}. I’ve seen the creature that guards the nest with my own eyes. Even the three of *us* wouldn’t be able to take it on…
Jurgen___nodes[3]._stepChain[11]._dialog: Well, you’re a smith—Not a fighter!
Jurgen___nodes[3]._stepChain[12]._dialog: Jurgen’s faded smile brightens with a short burst of laughter.
Jurgen___nodes[3]._stepChain[13]._dialog: You got that right! I still wanna scout the area for raw materials once the trouble passes, so you better not fail~
Jurgen___nodes[3]._stepChain[16]._dialog: Alright! All that’s left to do now is blow those charges.
Let’s see if this mystery creature can handle the best Frontiersman the Union has to offer!
Volamont Pre-Cleanup___nodes[0]._stepCha: The Scales must be in my favor… I can’t believe I made it out of there alive!
Tell me about it. And how bout those pirates, huh?
Aye, we were very lucky to have their help…
Now that we’re all friends, do you think they’ll let us in on some trade secrets?
You know, like how many of them have real peglegs n’ hooks, and which ones are just for show?
… Did… Did I actually die? Is this some kind of test?
Savannah 4 Crusher__line_3: Looks like the Centaurs created some kind of crushing device with their geomancy. If you’re careful you could probably use this to crush, pulverise, and or smash something.
Savannah 4 Crusher__line_19: <PLAYERNAME> := Hmm, I’m starting to think these centaurs are smarter than most give them credit for.
Savannah 4 Crusher__line_21: You chuck the unusually tough crop into the makeshift device and the massive rock formation slams down onto the toughened Boozant, a loud crack fills the air and the shell flies out, oozing a thick, viscous ichor that burns the skin when touched.
Savannah 4 Crusher__line_25: <PLAYERNAME> := Wow! This stuff is potent, guess I’ll have to dilute it.

Savannah 4 Excavation Cave Waterhole Bee: // 	After the water turns to beer:
Doctor1 := We went through all of this for BEER?

Heather := Fascinating, so the liquid gold referred to in the texts described beer! Considering this area is chock full of boozant and half the centaurs are all inebriated that makes a lot more sense.
Heather := I'm sure Merchant Companies will pay top korona for high grade centaur alcohol.
Doctor2 := Really now... well then, start bottling this stuff up, chop chop, we’ve got a shipment to make!
