Savannah 8 Kaga's Cave Greeter__line_3: As you enter the cave, you’re met by a bouncing ball of fur. You’ve seen shettles before, they populate much of the underground and are often companions of Centaurs. However, this is the first one you’ve seen that is so... exuberant.
Savannah 8 Kaga's Cave Greeter__line_12: Shettle := Hello! It is splendiferous to be of your acquaintance! I am Greeter, and you have entered the Cave of Murals, watched over by Kaga, historian of the tribes. If you need help, please come to me, but for an oral rendition of our history, please speak with Kaga.
Savannah 8 Kaga's Cave Greeter__line_15: Shettle := Oh! Nice to see you again farmer, did you have a question?
Savannah 8 Kaga's Cave Greeter__line_21: "You're a shettle? But you’re so... talkative":
Savannah 8 Kaga's Cave Greeter__line_25: "Thank you, I’ll let you know if I need something":
Savannah 8 Kaga's Cave Greeter__line_29: Greeter shuffles around for a moment, his flowing mane of hair covering every inch of his visible body, the only identifying features being the two piercing eyes that look up from the darkness concealing more fine features.
Savannah 8 Kaga's Cave Greeter__line_30: Shettle := Oh, we are very talkative! There is just not much to say, usually we are told 'go here, do this, stand guard, stop peeping on the baths', however as I am told to greet people here, I have done my best to be as amicable as possible! I hope that answers your statement!
Savannah 8 Kaga's Cave Greeter__line_34: Shettle := Oh! Well, there’s not much to say that you haven't already seen, we roam around in large packs, mostly traveling with our larger cousins.
Savannah 8 Kaga's Cave Greeter__line_35: Greeter seems to beam with pride at this.
Savannah 8 Kaga's Cave Greeter__line_36: Shettle := And we usually relax by whatever lakes they stop at. We're a very kind, and easygoing people, the only ones to ever rile us out of our naps are the big ones… Huh… Maybe that's why we travel with them? Does that help you?
Savannah 8 Kaga's Cave Greeter__line_40: Shettle := Well alright then! Just stop by and see me if you have a question!
Dungeon Lab Log 8__line_7: TroHandle := Personal log, entry… One-hundred and ten… By the Novas… I was ensconced in my study today, trying to make sense of the data presented to me. I’ve stopped experiments temporarily, nothing is coming of them, and it’s pointless to cause the Servants to suffer.
Dungeon Lab Log 8__line_8: TroHandle := I felt them again… Makaris, standing over my shoulder. This time, instead of correcting me, they offered advice. I needed to go for a walk, to clear my head, and… Well, they were right. I left my lab for Sun knows how long, and got some fresh air.
Dungeon Lab Log 8__line_9: TroHandle := As I wandered the Savannah, I took note of the monsters, they were territorial, but backed off when they saw me properly, I could only imagine what stories their forebears have passed down to them about Wizards.
Dungeon Lab Log 8__line_10: TroHandle := I was examining some particularly interesting microplankton when my eyes fell on a cave, and I felt a stirring in me. I felt more like myself, the me that I had been when I had started out this venture, and pursuing the feeling, I went inside.
Dungeon Lab Log 8__line_11: TroHandle := What I found inside was.. Intriguing. Wounded, and completely oblivious to the world, was a creature I had not seen before. The molecules in the air were going wild, whatever this thing was, it was strong enough to warp the air around it, and yet… It was hurt, trying to recuperate through sleep.
Dungeon Lab Log 8__line_12: TroHandle := I wrapped it in some cloth and carried it back, depositing it in one of the medical tanks I have on hand. I can study this thing while it recovers, discover what powers it has… It will be a useful distraction to keep my mind off of my other failures. End log.
Dungeon Lab Log 11__line_9: TroHandle := -Servants have reached fin- …gestation period an- …I have found a way of synth- …Atlas’s DNA! Ma- -lled it the ‘Atlas Strain’ how clever! It’s much - configurable.
Dungeon Lab Log 11__line_10: TroHandle := -ted a device to use At- Strain, I ca- it the Genodrive! The eff- - -orary, but the raw power is wor- - sacrifice!
Dungeon Lab Log 11__line_11: TroHandle := It can within- …inject- …and transform the wearer into a Saurian! - -nly that, but there is potential for MORE config-ations!
Dungeon Lab Log 11__line_12: TroHandle := I have - Atlas Strain into three different… -ules. Currently the on- one I have on-hand is the Saurian configuration, but I- on utilizing the others later. I will store the b-prints in here for -er use!
Dungeon Lab Log 11__line_14: The Vox Cube descends into a garbled mess, and ejects a small tube. Inserting the tube into your own Vox Cube, it churns for a moment.
Dungeon Lab Log 11__line_21: GievHandle := Fascinating… The data is vastly incomplete, but given time… Alright <PLAYERNAME>, hold on to this, bring it back to me when you’re finished here.
Dungeon Lab Log 3__line_7: TroHandle := P-personal log, entry… I don’t know, look. I heard them today. Makaris. They… They’re dead! I watched them get unmade in the heat of battle, using a blasted Mirror World Equalizer! There’s no way to recover from that, it’s the same sort of technology we used to ensure that the Tyrants’ egos were WIPED from this world…
Dungeon Lab Log 3__line_8: TroHandle := And yet, I felt them… As I was studying my notes I felt their presence behind me, hovering as they would, and then… They offered advice. It was a simple grammatical error they corrected, but that was so like them to nitpick when I was taken by my work.
Dungeon Lab Log 3__line_9: TroHandle := This place is bringing up old memories. Things I’d rather have left forgotten, I feel. Maybe it’s because I’m surrounded by relics of the Tyrants that I am feeling more vivid recollections of those terrible days during The Liberation.
Dungeon Lab Log 3__line_10: TroHandle := The work we did then, it was inspired and cruel, to wipe out an entire race, down to its footprints on the world… At the time we deemed it necessary, that there was no other way, as long as the Servants existed, their masters, The Tyrants, would return. Now though… hearing Makaris… Maybe there *was* another way?
Dungeon Lab Log 3__line_11: TroHandle := If their research allowed them even an inkling of ego to exist beyond death, and even to contact me… What if.. What if I could undo that wrong while also keeping the world from suffering the Tyrant’s return?.. A thought for later, I’m going to rest my mind for now, log end.
Dungeon Lab Log 10__line_7: TroHandle := Scientific log, entry thirty two. The pace being set now is absolutely exospheric! I’ve had multiple breakthroughs in these bottlenecks thanks to the specimen Atlas.
Dungeon Lab Log 10__line_8: TroHandle := I’ve had to keep the poor thing drugged, but… Surely it would understand why, and when I bring it back, it might even appreciate its blood being used for this!
Dungeon Lab Log 10__line_9: TroHandle := With further experimentation on my end, and Makaris’s assistance, I… We have figured out the trick to Atlas’s DNA. It isn’t so magical as to perfectly replicate DNA from other beings, but it’s VERY good at substituting.
Dungeon Lab Log 10__line_10: TroHandle := Atlas’s DNA will start to imitate whatever it’s applied to, and seek to make coherent strands from the information given!
Dungeon Lab Log 10__line_11: TroHandle := For my purposes, this means that this, combined with Giev’s sequencing, has sped up efforts substantially! Currently there are more gestating, and while I wait on those, I’ve begun a little side-project to *really* test out what Atlas’s DNA can do. End Log!
Dungeon Lab Log 4__line_7: TroHandle := Experimental log, entry one. Subject: Specimen Zero. Outcome: failure. Log begins.
Dungeon Lab Log 4__line_8: TroHandle := This was a tremendous failure, but what’s that saying about pancakes? The first one is always a failure? Maybe the same could be said here. Fortunately this was not an issue of technology, my machines are working as expected. Instead the issue stems from the genetic source itself.
Dungeon Lab Log 4__line_9: TroHandle := Reverse-engineering a genetic code from a compromised source was never going to be simple, but maybe I had let it go to my head that things were going so well that the first try would be more… Promising.
Dungeon Lab Log 4__line_10: TroHandle := Regardless, Specimen Zero, as I had dubbed them, was little more than a blob of meat and organs packed together. I’d have even called it spare parts were any of it usable. I ended it swiftly and dissected it for confirmation of my hypothesis.
Dungeon Lab Log 4__line_11: TroHandle := I may need assistance in this matter for a solution. Giev is amicable, as long as I indulge in their fetish for fine tableware, I’ll seek out their assistance once I am done sterilizing. Log ends.
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_7: TroHandle := Agh! Nebulas and Novas!… Experimental log, entry two. Subject: Void-headed… Specimen One. Outcome: Failure. Log begins.
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_8: TroHandle := And I was full of such hope too! Ugh, I just want to curl up and expire…. Alright, get a hold of yourself Tro… So! Silver-lining in all this? Specimen One came out as I remembered the Servants to be. It was a completely intact replica of the original. Save for its mind.
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_9: TroHandle := Maybe I had tried too fast.. Given that we destroyed almost every trace of the Servants, it’s no surprise that I am now discovering I have no idea about the process between their gestation, and adulthood. Maybe this ferocity that it displays is due to a hormonal imbalance of rapid gestation?
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_10: TroHandle := All I have of them is this fossil I stole from The Forbidden Terrace… Well, no use in getting stuck in this, I’ll try again later, refine the process a bit. The Ivory Tower wasn’t built in a day, I should know… Log end.
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_11: Tro's recording continues after a few moments.
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_12: TroHandle := Addendum: Remember to re-do this log and remove mention of The Forbidden Terrace, I do not need the other Wizards to discover this and start asking questions. Log end… Again.
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_19: <PLAYERNAME> := What is the Forbidden Terrace, and why would this fossil be there?
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_20: GievHandle := Currently? It’s a place full of brigands and blackguards, outside The Kingdom’s reach… However it was otherwise the home of The Servants… The Saurians.
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_21: GievHandle := During The Liberation, we had to forcibly purge that area of the Servants, they were the power base of their Masters. With them gone, the Tyrants were substantially weaker.
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_25: <PLAYERNAME> := An entire species gone, just to... to get at their masters!?
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_28: GievHandle := Are you looking for a moralization, or how we actually did it? To the latter, I will not share such secrets with you.
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_29: GievHandle := For the former… The Liberation had spanned over many years. The Servants were ever slavishly-loyal to their masters, to the extent that, when a Tyrant died, the Servants drowned the Forbidden Terrace in blood to bring them back.
Dungeon Lab Log 6__line_30: GievHandle := This is not to paint our actions in a lighter shade, what we did was unforgivable. But the world we live in now would not be possible without that action, remember that.
Dungeon Lab Log 2__line_7: TroHandle := Personal log, entryyyyy… Twelve! It’s somewhat… Crass to say, especially so if I were in  polite company, but I miss The Liberation sometimes.
Dungeon Lab Log 2__line_8: TroHandle := Yes it was a time of great upheaval, but I also feel it was when many of us flourished. For me especially. I remember those days where dozens of us would throw ideas back and forth on countering the Tyrants and their machinations.
Dungeon Lab Log 2__line_9: TroHandle := A dozen egos crammed into a hall and given a problem, spending days debating a hundred solutions, testing, re-testing, it was… Magnificent.
Dungeon Lab Log 2__line_10: TroHandle := What stands out to me now is Makaris’s hypothesis. Makaris was like a singularity of genius through which esoteric ideas flowed.
Dungeon Lab Log 2__line_11: TroHandle := Makaris posited research into the Mirror World, a dimension that souls entered where they died. Their requests for resources to research further into it were denied. We had deemed the Mirror World too dangerous for further study … Well, save for Kuttler.
Dungeon Lab Log 2__line_12: TroHandle := They, Makaris, invariably suffered the same fate as any who had made forays into investigating the Mirror World. One day they went too far with their research and was… Unmade. Was it pride that led them to disobey us, or were they driven by something else?
Dungeon Lab Log 2__line_13: TroHandle := Even now, I wonder if I am feeling what they felt. What I am doing would be considered wrong by my peers, but… If I don’t do this, no one will… End log.
Dungeon Lab Log 1__line_3: You pick up a Vox Cube, turning it over in your hand and pressing down on an indent in the side. The Vox Cube lights up, and begins to play.
Dungeon Lab Log 1__line_8: TroHandle := Personal log, entry one. I have finished clearing out these old ruins and made them ready to begin my research. The proximity to Giev, the old fuddy-duddy, is less than ideal, but his gripes about ‘safety’ at least comes from a place of genuine concern.
Dungeon Lab Log 1__line_9: TroHandle := It has been… Long enough since the Liberation from Tyrants that the old flames of hatred have cooled enough that I can begin correcting the mistakes we made in a time of crisis.
Dungeon Lab Log 1__line_10: TroHandle := I’ve begun some promising preliminary testing, and at the very least this place is far enough away that it is ‘safe’ from others, and… Well, if I mess up? Giev has always been reliable enough to clean up my messes. Log end.
Dungeon Lab Log 1__line_17: <PLAYERNAME> := What mistakes did you make during this… Liberation?
Dungeon Lab Log 1__line_18: GievHandle := Too many. The Liberation was our greatest fight, it cost us our ability to leave this plane, but in exchange, the greatest extant threat to this world, and possibly ALL worlds was removed.
Dungeon Lab Log 1__line_19: <PLAYERNAME> := So… This isn’t even your home?
Dungeon Lab Log 1__line_20: GievHandle := No, I’ve made the best of it, but… The Firmament will never be my true home.
Echo Stone Reminder__line_6: GievHandle := <PLAYERNAME>, you need to leave. Use the Echo Stone from your backpack, now.
EchoStoneFirstUse__line_2: Echo Stone in hand, you do as you are told; You feel the hairs on the back of your neck rise, bumps form across your skin and in one brief moment, 'there' becomes 'here'.
Atlas Encounter__line_0: //Subject: Player interacts with Atlas’ containment capsule, and can chose to free him or not.
Atlas Encounter__line_1: //To be played: Player approaches/interacts with Atlas’ Capsule.
Atlas Encounter__line_4: //Scripting: Repeatable conversation. Plays cutscene where Atlas Escapes.
Atlas Encounter__line_10: The capsule of fluid lightly bubbles around a strange, humanoid creature. Your gaze snaps down to the flickering display which activates as you lean closer.
Atlas Encounter__line_15: The symbols on the screen force your eyes to strain, and through beads of tears, you can somehow glean meaning from the arcane runes...
Atlas Encounter__line_17: It seems as though much of the text is beyond your ability to comprehend.
Atlas Encounter__line_27: Your hand hovers over the display, before you touch the runes you interpret as "yes".
Atlas Encounter__line_32: The fluid in the capsule begins to drain away.
Atlas Encounter__line_35: The sound of the creature’s body crashing against the ground betrays a density you were not expecting.
Atlas Encounter__line_42: The creature stirs, pulling itself up as though it were weightless. As you get a clear look at it, it’s featureless face seems to peer through you.
Atlas Encounter__line_46: The creature’s limbs extend into razor sharp talons, as it turns away from you and begins ascending into the darkness.
Atlas Encounter__line_51: A moment later... it’s gone, the puddle of medical fluids the only hint it was ever there.
Atlas Encounter__line_57: You retract your hand from the display.
Atlas Encounter__line_59: <PLAYERNAME> := I *really* shouldn’t be releasing something, given everything that’s happened in this lab...
Dungeon Lab Log 5__line_7: TroHandle := Personal log, entry eighty-five. Oh Giev, I would kiss you if the thought of our bodies touching didn’t repel me so utterly. Your work in genetic sequencing is finer than a world-class pianist’s fingers on the ivories.
Dungeon Lab Log 5__line_8: TroHandle := Without waxing too poetic, Giev’s work has given me a breakthrough in this bottleneck I was suffering. The residual DNA from the fossil has been tidied up considerably, and for the low cost of a tea set.
Dungeon Lab Log 5__line_22: TroHandle := I’ve set the next specimen up for gestation, and am awaiting the results as I speak. I don’t want to sound *too* hopeful, lest I be made a fool of by happenstance… But this next one should have *much* more promising results than Specimen Zero.
Dungeon Lab Log 5__line_23: There’s the distinct sound of a siren sounding in the background.
Dungeon Lab Log 5__line_24: TroHandle := Ah, there’s the noise I’ve been waiting for! Log end.
Garst In Lab Void__line_3: You look at the swirling purple mass of clouds spewing debris and various oddities back and forth, an ominous sight. Most would consider it foolish to venture this close to the maelstrom.
Garst In Lab Void__line_4: But then, to your surprise, you see a man lying beneath it; half conscious on the floor. Something is off about the color of his skin and clothing, but he doesn't seem deceased... Either way it is a frontiersman's duty to recover those lost in the wilds.
Garst In Lab Void__line_5: Perhaps more importantly, you see that the echo stone has rolled over towards the man and settled next to one of his thighs.
Garst In Lab Void__line_9: <PLAYERNAME> := I guess this guy is lucky I dropped the stone.
Garst In Lab Void__line_12: You temporarily stash the echo stone in your inventory, so you can get a firm grip on the man and move him to a better position from which you can teleport.
Garst In Lab Void__line_19: <PLAYERNAME> := Giev? I'm going to be teleporting back with a guest.
Garst In Lab Void__line_25: GievHandle := What was that? I need to adjust my signal... something you've just done is disrupting my link...
Garst In Lab Void__line_26: GievHandle := Hmm... Just what is that? Yes, hmmm, such a strange variable? ...Bah, nevermind this now.
Garst In Lab Void__line_27: GievHandle := <PLAYERNAME>, go ahead and bring whatever that is with you and we'll deal with it here.
Dungeon Lab Log 9__line_7: TroHandle := Personal log, entry One-hundred and eleven! Breakthrough! While I was observing my newest patient, by the by I’ve taken to calling it Atlas, I made an amazing discovery!
Dungeon Lab Log 9__line_8: TroHandle := I took a small sample of its blood to see if it would need any particular nutrients, and while studying it, I witnessed a mutation!
Dungeon Lab Log 9__line_9: TroHandle := I don’t know what brought about the change, but through a more thorough examination, I saw how adaptive it was. My obvious first thought was to use it on the fossil, but that could have had some disastrous effects.
Dungeon Lab Log 9__line_10: TroHandle := I tried a few more experiments with it, and decided that I could take this small risk, with great success! There are a few errors with this new strand of DNA, but nothing I can’t repair with some ingenuity.
Dungeon Lab Log 9__line_11: TroHandle := Makaris is even here with me now. They’re phantasmal, perhaps composed of some form of psycho kinetic energy? It’s clear that their experiments were a success too!
Dungeon Lab Log 9__line_12: TroHandle := With both of us working together, we can undo the mistakes! Repair genocide! Maybe.. Maybe even the Tyrants can be brought back this way. Not as they were, but… If I can repair the Servants, maybe I can do the same to them? Oh the possibilities are endless! End log!
Dungeon Lab Log 9__line_19: GievHandle := Bring them BACK!? What is that fool thinking? It cost us *everything* to kill The Tyrants and Tro… Novas…
Dungeon Lab Log 9__line_20: <PLAYERNAME> := Is... Are they *that* bad though Giev? To justify killing their entire species?
Dungeon Lab Log 9__line_22: GievHandle := The world you live in now may have its problems, but you’re not breaking your bodies into shapes more pleasing to The Tyrants, or working until your last breath in forced gratitude to their whims.
Dungeon Lab Log 9__line_23: GievHandle := If the others knew of this, Tro would be killed… I might even kill Tro myself if I get my hands on them!
Dungeon Lab Log 9__line_24: GievHandle := Enough standing here, let’s continue onward.
Tro's Corpse__line_10: You approach Tro's corpse again, apprehensive of the creature lurking in the shadows of the lab.
Tro's Corpse__line_14: You approach a body slumped against the far wall. It’s been heavily beaten, with clear breaks hidden beneath dark, voluminous robes.
Tro's Corpse__line_20: The stylized fashion of a Wizard, not to mention their height, is unmistakable. From the running carnage you’ve passed by, this must have been where the fight had reached its apex, ultimately ending when the Wizard was dead.
Tro's Corpse__line_28: GievHandle := Blast… Careful <PLAYERNAME>, it takes a great strength to kill a Wizard. Leaving is advisable.
Tro's Corpse__line_31: However, you see another Vox Cube, illuminated by a warm, green light.
Tro's Corpse__line_33: <PLAYERNAME> := We’re not done yet, there’s one last Vox.
Tro's Corpse__line_35: You crouch down and slide the Vox Cylinder from its cradle in the cube. It’s splattered with blood, which you do your best to clean off before inserting it in your Vox Cube.
Tro's Corpse__line_40: TroHandle := -Cough cough-... I am a fool Giev… You’re probably standing here right now… Oh what am I -cough- You sent someone to investigate, always so ensorcelled by your hobbies old friend…
Tro's Corpse__line_41: TroHandle := They had a glint of intelligence to them, this I swear. They understood their surroundings, they comprehended!
Tro's Corpse__line_45: TroHandle := I thought it safe… Or maybe… *Cough* I wanted it to be safe… I released one, and attempted to communicate. I did not consider… Did not…
Tro's Corpse__line_46: TroHandle := The Tyrants Giev… Their work was always subtle when it needed to be… *Cough*, we always expected the sword to our front.. But never the knife to our backs.
Tro's Corpse__line_47: TroHandle := The specimen spun quickly, and knocked its vat over, it cascaded *perfectly* into the others, I couldn’t stop it in time, and had to fight as the other vats were released.
Tro's Corpse__line_48: TroHandle := I’ve relived some of my worst days in the span of a few hours. Maybe this is what I deserve for my hand in the war… I always regretted The Servants’ extinction, and my carelessness has incarnated their revenge against us.
Tro's Corpse__line_52: TroHandle := It… It took… *Cough cough!* Everything I had to put them down… But still it wasn’t enough.
Tro's Corpse__line_53: TroHandle := I know you came to find your dinnerware set, but I am afraid that it was a lie, much like the small cakes I also promised… A ruse, to further my work.. Please, collect my research, preserve it.
Tro's Corpse__line_55: There is a momentary ‘pause’ as the Wizard coughs wetly into the Vox Cube.
Tro's Corpse__line_57: TroHandle := We made a mistake in rendering them extinct… But I still believe we can reverse that error… Someone else, with a more sober mind than I should be able to do something… To see where I failed and not make the same mistakes…
Tro's Corpse__line_58: TroHandle := … I think… I think I can hear Makaris again… Strange.. He sounds different than when we….
Tro's Corpse__line_62: His voice trails off, and for the rest of the recording there is only silence until the cylinder reaches its natural end.
Tro's Corpse__line_76: Massive claws scrape against the floors, followed by a deep roar that vibrates your bones.
Tro's Corpse__line_100: You have enough time to dodge out of the way as the massive Lizardman crashes against a supporting pillar.
Tro's Corpse__line_101: The platform you are standing on cracks, and shakes. You have a moment to process what will come next when the floor gives way completely, tossing you and your party into the darkness below.
Tro's Corpse__line_120: You shake your head, that fall was devastating. You have no idea how far-
Tro's Corpse__line_122: You hear that teeth-chattering roar again, and cover your ears. It looks like all the fall did was make the Lizardman angrier.
Tro's Corpse__line_127: There’s… A strange sound coming from nearby, your eyes glancing over the ground where you had fallen.
Tro's Corpse__line_130: There’s a shimmer in the dirt, and you see a strange device half-buried in the ground, near where Tro’s corpse fell.
Tro's Corpse__line_138: Suddenly, you’re back at the Academy, remembering some of your earlier days training. You remember the voice of your instructor, cool, officious, in control. Their orders were followed to the letter by everyone, including you. This voice… Somehow it came in that same cadence, same tone, everything about it was the same.
Tro's Corpse__line_139: Before you realized what you were doing, your body had moved into action, grabbing the device, and almost instinctively knowing that it slides on your wrist, like a bracer. The device whirrs and clamps down onto you, and you feel a surge of -something- coursing through your body.
Tro's Corpse__line_141: You felt great! Better than great! You felt strong, capable! Seeing this Lizardman, you feel that its roars aren’t out of confidence… But fear.
Tro's Corpse__line_142: You understand how frightened it must be… But its lashing out indiscriminately has already killed one person, and if it were able to escape, you can only imagine the damage it would do. You take a firm grip of your shovel and slam the ax of it on the ground, like a challenging gong.
Tro's Corpse__line_144: The Lizardman turns to you with… Surprise? On its face. It lets out a roar, raising its massive claws to attack!
Tro's Corpse__line_148: //Stage direction: End scene, begin combat with the Lizardman.
Dungeon Lab Log 7__line_7: TroHandle := Experimental log, entry twenty-nine. Subject: … Just… So many Specimens… Numbers fourteen through thirty six… Outcome: Failures. Log begins.
Dungeon Lab Log 7__line_8: TroHandle := What am I doing wrong? Several iterations later and they are still feral. The *only* good thing is that one of the subjects showed some intelligence today. While I was otherwise distracted, one of the specimens escaped!
Dungeon Lab Log 7__line_9: TroHandle := I was going over some preliminary data, and heard glass shattering. I ran over and saw one of the specimens half-formed and crawling across the floor towards me! It was… heart wrenching to have to kill it.
Dungeon Lab Log 7__line_10: TroHandle := The first specimen to exist outside of its containment, even if half-formed, and there I was, forced to put it down. It’s frustrating for me, a step forward, and then a step to the side, one solution and a dozen problems.
Dungeon Lab Log 7__line_11: TroHandle := It seems, however, that this may be the limit of the fossil. With the limited genetic coding I can pull from it being clearly insufficient. They have the bodies, the strength and ferocity, but just… Not the mind…. -Sigh-...
Dungeon Lab Log 7__line_12: TroHandle := I’m going to clean up, salvage what I can, and get some rest, maybe a fresh start will bring with it some ideas. End log.
Lizardman Corpse__line_8: Laying before you is the remains of some large creature, reminiscent of an amalgam of species.
Lizardman Corpse__line_10: <PLAYERNAME> := Naga… Dragon… But it’s much more squat and bipedal? What in the…
Lizardman Corpse__line_12: if present 0.6 1.0 3 Kaleida := I’m sensing much pain from this thing, it did not have a long life… But still…
Lizardman Corpse__line_13: if present 0.6 1.0 3 Goldra := Wizard experimentations… Were it up to me, there would be more regulations on what they’re allowed to do in their labs.
Lizardman Corpse__line_14: if present 0.6 1.0 3 Brontide := Big thing like this… I wonder what it’d be like to hunt… Well, too bad it’s dead.
Lizardman Corpse__line_15: if present 0.6 1.0 3 Kiram := I heard somethin’ about experiments like this bein’ carried out… Never thought they were real.
Lizardman Corpse__line_16: if present 0.6 1.0 3 Yonten := A profane thing if I ever saw it, there’s something *wrong* with this creature.
Lizardman Corpse__line_17: if present 0.6 1.0 3 Jaero := We should ignore it <PLAYERNAME> it’s a dead thing, and I do not feel comfortable around it.
Lizardman Corpse__line_18: if present 0.6 1.0 3 Camellia := What a delightful monster the Wizard has created. If only they put their energy towards something more productive.
Lizardman Corpse__line_19: if present 0.6 1.0 3 Fio := Don’t like the look of this <PLAYERNAME>, we need to be alert.
Lizardman Corpse__line_21: <PLAYERNAME> := Well, whatever it is, it’s not been long dead.
Lizardman Corpse__line_23: <PLAYERNAME> := It’s best to pay attention here, who knows what could be lurking in the shadows.
PostLizardmanFight__line_4: character alias GievHandle Giev (Nearby Echo Stone)
PostLizardmanFight__line_7: Giev's voice, presumably via the echo stone, emanates as if from much further away.
PostLizardmanFight__line_9: GievHandle := I suppose I must apologize, <PLAYERNAME>, you are in far more danger than I intended to place you in.
PostLizardmanFight__line_10: GievHandle := At the risk of stating the obvious, the Maelstrom is an extraordinarily unstable place.
PostLizardmanFight__line_11: GievHandle := Which is why you are leaving. Stand by for teleportation. Please remove the echo stone from your bag and grasp it firmly; this will make triangulating your position easier.
PostLizardmanFight__line_15: You pull the echo stone from your pack and... realise you must've dropped it during the fall.
PostLizardmanFight__line_17: <PLAYERNAME> := Wait, ahh barnacles! Better find that if I don't wanna figure out how to farm in the Maelstrom.
Dungeon Lab Log 12__line_7: TroHandle := Personal log, entry one-hundred and fifty-three. At Makaris’s request I purged the latest batch of specimens being gestated. He’d said something to the extent that they wouldn’t obey orders, they were probably rushed anyway.
Dungeon Lab Log 12__line_8: TroHandle := This pause between batches, as the next one gestates, has given me time to work on the Genodrive a little bit more, as well as continue tending to Atlas. The Genodrive is as complete as I can make it without testing.
Dungeon Lab Log 12__line_9: TroHandle := It has also come to my realization that the Genodrive is too resource-intensive to mass-produce… But there *is* appeal in having several specialists. Obvious military use aside, imagine the data we could gather from having someone, even if in body, perfectly replicate a species and its powers!
Dungeon Lab Log 12__line_10: TroHandle := I think I’m almost ready to start presenting my research to my peers. I’ll start with Giev, winning them over will-
Dungeon Lab Log 12__line_12: There’s the distinct sound of a siren that cuts through what Tro was about to say.
Dungeon Lab Log 12__line_14: TroHandle := Ah! This batch is finished gestating. I don’t want to get my hopes up, but I think these will be the closest I’ve come yet to bringing The Servants back to life! Log end!
Dungeon Lab Log 12__line_21: <PLAYERNAME> := So… Given what we’ve seen, do you think he was successful, or failed?
Dungeon Lab Log 12__line_22: GievHandle := Both… They failed because they believed that the Servants were anything but the creation of their Masters… And succeeded because they brought them back.
Intercom - Object 7___nodes[0]._stepChai: You reach out and push a button on the speaker device. Cheery music begins to play as a deep and round voice emerges from the speaker.

You slap the button on what appears to be the final station on this level of the facility. That familiar crackle starts as you hear the recorded sound of milk cans, heavy ice boxes, and the rumble of heavy machinery.
Now, you're just about to venture down to the production floor of the facility, or as we like to call it, the "dance floor". It's the very heart of our humble operation here on The Frontier.
Many rival ice cream companies would pay a fortune to see what you're about to see! In fact-
Vaskin seems to drone on about proprietary secrets as you get distracted.
Wasn’t this meant to be a public tour though? This isn’t like the secret 32 spices for Kingdom Fricasseed Shrubsticks, is it?
{0}, stop getting immersed in the tour… Right!

Which is why we have spared no expense in securing a state of the art security system! One that will repel any corporate spies, as well as keep all faculty and guests safe and sound while they're within these walls.
Additionally, should any emergency occur, these tour stations will broadcast up to date information, and our highly trained staff will escort you to the nearest exit or safe area.
So, please, follow your tour guide down to the floor below with the peace of mind that, like our precious ice cream, you're in good hands!
I doubt he meant the pirates when he said "state of the art security…"
Intercom - Object 7___nodes[2]._stepChai: Anyone know what kind of security large corporations use?
Nah, but feel free to lead the way!
I am not well versed in the art of corporate espionage…
You mean breaking in wasn’t the easy part…?
Whatever it is, we'll give 'em a proper scrap!
Hmm, my vote is on an army of shark men with lasers mounted to their heads!
If these pirates are considered the backup, then I dinnae think it'll be a problem!
Doth he not keep a small cadre of housecarls? How would he persevere though any siege without them?
Usually Thrund and Morgan take care of any drunkards… Apologies, hon.
Hmm…. I started reading the cards while he was prattling on… I pulled The Fortress, The Caduceus but reversed, and The Mirror… I’m not exactly sure what that means… So be on your guard!
Oooh! What if there’s a laser grid hallway we have to dodge. Or maybe shinobi guards? Hmm… No they wouldn’t get along with the pirates…
I wish I knew, but we’ll best it either way!
Whatever it is, we won’t drop our guard.
The man has likely sent pirates and an assassin after you. Do you think he’s reasonable? Donne ma langue au chat…
Arr! I just hope it isn’t a top shelf mercenary, or his bodyguard…
Oh ho  Perhaps a series of guards, trip wires and air ducts! Maybe this calls for a dashing break in! I can see you now, the picture of a roguish {0|thief|gentleman thief|femme fatale spy}!
Whatever it is, you’re not dangling by a zipline over a bunch of lasers!
Intercom - Object 7___nodes[3]._stepChai: Well, there’s only one way to find out!
You leave the kiosk with a new sense of caution about your surroundings.
Wata pre-Agatha Pick Up__line_3: <PLAYERNAME> := Woah! I’ve never seen something like this before…
Wata pre-Agatha Pick Up__line_5: := And growing in the Mirror World of all places! You must be rare…
Wata pre-Agatha Pick Up__line_7: <PLAYERNAME> := I’ll tuck you away for now and see if someone could use you.
Boethilde Mechanic Post Combat Dialog__l: As your final blow lands, the deadly apparition evaporates into a wispy green fog that slowly disperses…
<PLAYERNAME> := So much for active listening. Looks like talking’s out of the question, this time.
<PLAYERNAME> := Based on what I know about spirits, that might not be the last we’ll see of her. I need to stay on my toes out here…
Wata post-Agatha Pick Up__line_3: <PLAYERNAME> := Oh! This must be the <color=blue>Wata</blue> that Agatha wanted.
Wata post-Agatha Pick Up__line_5: <PLAYERNAME> := I wonder why it grew here of all places…
Wata post-Agatha Pick Up__line_7: <PLAYERNAME> := Oh well! I’m not the expert on these, into the bag ya go little fella!
Boethilde Mechanic Second Appearance Dia: Your blood runs cold in your veins…
:= Think, , THINK! She’s obviously a spirit of some kind… Are these illusions? Is she splitting herself into multiple, autonomous forms?
<PLAYERNAME> := According to my <wiggle>Thanotology 201</wiggle> course, there are some class seven spirits that can create weaker, class four, focused, repeating apparitions…
<PLAYERNAME> := That means the original spirit must be around here, somewhere. If I want to find the source, I should investigate these Mirror World pockets carefully.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[0]._stepChain[2]._di: You take a look at this strange desk surrounded by towering shelves of tomes, and can’t help but wonder what you might find here.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[0]._stepChain[5]._di: Is this… Vaskin’s desk? It’s pretty conspicuous, either way.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[2]._stepChain[0]._di: You turn to the desk, and get your hands on one of the cold, iron padlocks. The cold suddenly intensifies.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[2]._stepChain[2]._di: You take your shovel, and strike the lock. You mar the desk with a wicked looking dent, but at least now you can look through it.
You press your advantage and search the desk. There are some strange documents inside, including a few very old diagrams that look like they belong in a museum.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[2]._stepChain[3]._di: Hmmm. These diagrams seem to show <color=blue>monster essences</color> being used in some kind of experiment. I’m not sure, but these strange figures look like...
It’s not perfect, but this might connect the poachers, the pirates’ smuggling Homonculi, and Vaskin’s activities here at the Creamery!
Along with testimony from local escapees, I might be able to build a case here!
Vaskin Desk___nodes[2]._stepChain[4]._di: You stow away the evidence for safe keeping, turning your attention back to the rest of the factory.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[3]._stepChain[0]._di: With Boethilde taken care of, I can take a proper look at all of this.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[3]._stepChain[1]._di: The bookshelves are unprotected, but the desk drawers are kept shut by cold, iron padlocks with no visible keyhole.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[4]._rawContextHeader: Search the desk, or the bookshelves?
Vaskin Desk___nodes[5]._stepChain[0]._di: You get on your toes and start scanning the enormous volumes of books… The ones you can reach, anyway.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[5]._stepChain[1]._di: Hmmm… These are organized like log books, but they’re completely empty… Are they written with some kind of special ink?
Vaskin Desk___nodes[5]._stepChain[2]._di: As you flip through the pages of one of the logs, it suddenly becomes painfully cold in your hands.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[5]._stepChain[5]._di: You can’t stop yourself from dropping the book. When it hits the floor, it vanishes in a wild burst of cold, green flames.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[5]._stepChain[7]._di: I’m guessing these are Boethilde’s doing…
Vaskin Desk___nodes[6]._stepChain[0]._di: You turn to the desk, and get your hands on one of the cold, iron padlocks. The cold suddenly intensifies.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[6]._stepChain[2]._di: You release the padlock as it disintegrates into black ash.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[6]._stepChain[4]._di: Is this because Boethilde is gone?
Vaskin Desk___nodes[6]._stepChain[5]._di: With the lock gone, you press your advantage and search the desk. There are some strange documents inside, including a few very old diagrams that look like they belong in a museum.
Vaskin Desk___nodes[6]._stepChain[6]._di: Hmmm. These diagrams seem to show <color=blue>monster essences</color> being used in some kind of experiment. I’m not sure, but these strange figures look like.
It’s not perfect, but this might connect the poachers, the pirates’ smuggling Homonculi, and Vaskin’s activities here at the Creamery!
Along with testimony from local escapees, I might be able to build a case here. I should find <color=blue>Sieger</color> as soon as possible, and share everything I’ve found!
Boethidle Pre-Combat Dialog___nodes[0]._: The pursuing apparition hesitates, their eyes flashing with deadly intent.
Can’t we talk about this? I-I assure you that I’m a very productive negotiator! <shake=weak>I’m proficient in spectral sign language!</shake>
You must be stopped. This is my task. It will be done.
O-oh, well, I understand, this sounds really important to you, and-
Hypothermal Cell Sequence___nodes[1]._st: The cell fizzles and swirls with energy, its housing connected to the advanced magitech internals of the machine.
This thing is really advanced! But if what I learned in Magitech 101 is correct, then this cell is powering the device. Disconnecting it will probably power down the factory!
You grab a large locking bracket, unlocking the cell from the housing. Before you can remove the cell, a warning light comes on.
A speaker crackles to life, along with a musical tone.
Hold on now. The Robbinator 9001 has not been properly shut down. Disengaging the Hypothermal Cell from the device may lead to damage to the facility.
You mutter to yourself as you disengage the lower locking bracket.
Yeah, that’s not exactly my problem…
Hypothermal Cell Sequence___nodes[2]._st: Warning, incorrect disengagement of the Hypothermal Cell from the device will result in termination.
The Hypothermal Cell rests loosely in its housing, ready for you to remove.
Hypothermal Cell Sequence___nodes[4]._st: You reach out and grasp the Hypothermal Cell. It’s painfully cold to the touch.
Tch! Like I care about getting fired from this place!
You wrench the cell back, pulling it free from the machine as The Robbinator 9001 shuts down with a slow, droning whirr…
You’re… not a recording, are you?
And you’re far from a welcome guest in my factory.
Hypothermal Cell Sequence___nodes[6]._st: Only a fool hides behind smoke and mirrors, show yourself!
Oh, wow that was a real trick! I was not expecting that!
Yeah yeah, why don’t you come out and fight already?
Great, now he’s going to monologue…
This is hardly a hospitable establishment…
Oh good, you’re here. Now we can break your toys and your face on the same day!
Okay… breathe…. breathe… Oh, we’re deep in it now…
Hey, jolly cream giant, why don’t you come down here so we can beat you up!
I’m not sure who could possibly be welcome in a place like this.
So I’m guessin’ there won’t be free samples at the end of this tour?
Ah, there be the big man himself!
Hypothermal Cell Sequence___nodes[7]._st: Listen, why don’t you just turn yourself in and make this easy for everyone?
Make things easy? The only thing in life that makes things easy is power. Something which I am in ample supply of, and you, my little variable, have very little of within these walls.
Well, just one look at this place shows that we have more than enough evidence to put you away!

Evidence of what, exactly? A few poor hiring choices? Some workplace violations? Oh, of course I am more than willing to comply and show that these were some mistakes my corporation intends to correct immediately!
Y-you! That’s not what I’m talking about!
Oh? Well, then I’m not sure what you’re going on about. Now, if you’ll excuse me, I have important concerns to attend to. Hopefully my staff can see you’re properly looked after.
The speaker goes silent, as you stow away the Hypothermal Cell.
That’s not going to happen! I know you’ve done much worse than even this, and I’m going to find the smoking gun that puts you behind bars!
Hypothermal Cell Sequence___nodes[8]._st: Maybe I don’t remove this just yet… I should look into what else I can investigate around the factory first…
BoethildeScenarioPostFight___nodes[1]._s: <speed=slow><size=44><wiggle><color=black>AS YOU PULL AWAY FROM YOUR LAST STRIKE, THE FURIOUS SPIRIT DOES NOT FALTER BUT SIMPLY STOPS.</speed></size></wiggle></color>

<speed=slow><size=40><wiggle><color=black>‘SHOULD I BE DESTROYED,’</speed></size></wiggle></color> <color=#888888>the spirit growls, as bitter as dying embers,</color> <speed=slow><size=40><wiggle><color=black>‘I WILL BE UNABLE TO OBEY THE MASTER THAT HOLDS MY WILL TO HIS. I MUST FLEE.’</speed></size></wiggle></color>

<color=#888888>With one trembling hand, she seems to reach out for some shred of comfort that isn’t there.</color> <speed=slow><size=44><wiggle><color=black>‘COWARD…! FAILURE…!’</speed></size></wiggle></color>
<speed=slow><size=44><wiggle><color=black>I… LOST IT…! I’M SORRY… HE TOOK IT. TOOK IT AWAY…</speed></size></wiggle></color>
BoethildeScenarioPostFight___nodes[2]._s: ‘You don’t have to do this! You don’t have to obey him! It’s over now!’
<speed=slow><size=44><wiggle><color=black>... BUT YOUR PLEA FALLS ON EARS AS DEAF AS STONE…</speed></size></wiggle></color>
As Boethilde’s straggling voice fades away into the void, you feel yourself drift away in the opposite direction…
… But you aren’t alone. There is warmth, here. Security.
Soft fabric rustles pleasantly around you. Soft hands touch your shoulders with familiar tenderness.
BoethildeScenarioPostFight___nodes[3]._s: I got something for you, Boe. Here - For our portrait!
As she holds the black dolly out in front of you, your arms compulsively wrap around it with an excited shiver.
Oh, he’s so <color=black>cute!</color> I love him so much!
As you hear yourself speak, you feel anxious and shameful. What would father say? You’re hardly a baby, anymore.
… But… I can’t. I’m too old for such things. You should know better!
… Well, couldn’t you hold onto him for a while? I’m afraid I might lose him, since I’m getting more forgetful these days.
Empowered by this sense of responsibility, your arms tighten around the doll, and you finally relax.
Just don’t lose it, ok? Keep him close, now!
With a little gasp, you feel yourself falling downward at a rapid pace. Just as you begin to panic, you feel fabric sheets in your balled-up fists.
BoethildeScenarioPostFight___nodes[4]._s: … Looks like the worst of it is, anyway. I can’t sense her, anymore… <wiggle>-Whew!-</wiggle>
BoethildeScenarioPostFight___nodes[6]._s: Whew! She was one tough old maid… I’m almost sorry we had to put her to rest!
Unfinished business is a VERY dangerous thing.
Hah, ya see the way she run off?! Yer nothin’ but a bitta green fog, ya hear?!
… Might one such as I ever passe on to the next life, after all…?
Great, that’s done with. Now, maybe we could leave before the next-highest ranking noblewoman’s nightmares start manifesting?
When we get out of here, {0}, we should talk about what you’ve seen… Visions of the past can warp the untrained mind, let alone your OWN past.
She’s quite a comely lass, if ye can get past th’ butcher’s knife as big as yer ‘ead. Think we’ll be seein’ ‘er again any time soon?
That was a right tough gran! Do us a favor and rest in peace now, would ye?
Thank Kochamura, it’s over… You should try and meditate to clear your mind when we get home, {0}.
We beat her? The huge ghost lady!? Oh, great! Let’s get the FlimFlan out of here!
I have a feeling Boethilde isn’t going to find peace any time soon…
Great Gempas, is it finally over? Once we get home, I’m gonna sleep like the dead.
If she’s outa the job, maybe we could ask her to clean up the farmhouse? Assumin’ the outfit wasn’t purely metaphorical, heh heh!~
O-oh… We beat her? I really wish we could have resolved things peacefully… It’s so unfair.
BoethildeScenarioPostFight___nodes[7]._s: Now that Boethilde is hopefully gone, Vaskin’s factory should be easier to navigate. Let’s see what he’s been hiding!
BoethildeScenarioPostFight___nodes[8]._s: As you start to walk away from the bed, you sense there’s something off about the weight of your inventory…
When you check inside, you’re greeted by the beady yellow eyes and stitched smile of a <color=black>strange doll</color>.
How the Hottons did YOU get in here!? Do you belong to Boethilde???

The doll stares back at you with a peculiar grin. Although you don’t recognize the species, you have an inexplicable feeling that it was in fact real, and far older than anyone now living could tell you…

Y-you know, I should try returning you to Boethilde, one day… Definitely not today, though.
<size=24>Or tomorrow, or the day after that…</size>
Third Portrait - Return___nodes[0]._step: This is the third portrait you’ve found that depicts an important memory from Boethtilde’s life.
You shudder at the sight of the elaborate floral shroud, fleshy roses drained of color as the body they adorn.
The single mourner sits with her arms crossed tightly across her chest, so still that you wonder if she’s even breathing anymore.
Mirror Transporter First Interaction___n: You stare at what you can only call a rectangular shaped hole in reality, the edges pulsing and reverberating with fiery green energies. Unfortunately, it feels like the hole is also staring back at you.
That… definitely wasn't there before…
Before you can examine it any further, you feel the edges of your vision grow dark as you’re pulled towards the portal…
<wiggle=strong>W-Wooooahhhhhh!</wiggle>
MovePlayerToMirrorWorld
Mirror Portal Interaction
For a brief moment, it feels as though your entire body wants to invert on itself. Your muscles and bones feel as though they're threatening to push past one another to accomplish it.
In a flash, the sensation passes, and your senses are overwhelmed by the sudden reintroduction of gravity…
You land with an unceremonious <shake=weak>-thud-</shake>! As you stand up, you find yourself in a strange and foreign landscape…
Well… I'm definitely not in the Central Kingdom anymore…
At Mirror World
ArePresent
NotPresent
Are Garst & Kiram Present?
Arr… Sound off! Who's not dead?
To which fate doth undeath belong?
Er… Sorry, Garst! Didn't mean nothin' by it. Yer with us!
I am glad to be included. Though, that being said…
Garst & Kiram Chime Ins
IsPresent
NotPresent
IsPartyPresent?
I feel as though I may be less affected by what hath transpired than you.
I’ve had hangovers worse than whatever that was…
I appreciate the concern! I’m fine, {0}!
Whatever ordeal that was, it found me wanting.
Owie! That hurt! Kinda felt funny though!
Man, I am really glad I don’t have house keys to fall on… Instead I fell on your shovel…
I've fared better, but goodness it's chilly here!
Achh! This place gives me the right willies…
Uh… anyone know why it looks like we fell into a Spectre Hallowed Haunt Store?
{0}... Only you would see a doorway to a parallel dimension and think "Oh, let's have a quick look, that's different!" Merde…
When you see a mysterious portal to some parallel dimension you stay away from it
Mmm… To find ourselves somewhere unknown, for breaking into a place without permission… To be expected, I suppose.
… I don't like the look of this place one bit. I've got a very, very bad feeling about this…
Well… At least I know nightmares are real. The uncertainty was making it hard to sleep at night…
Where are we exactly…?
Whew, looks like we're all here.
Party Chime Ins
That being said, where exactly is here?
It’s cold… a near chiralic event… feelings of dread…
This is the Mirror World… Specifically the Anterior Mirror Wilds, but still, the Mirror World…
Long hours perusing the restricted parapsychology wing of the Ivory Tower's Central Library has gifted you with a precise, and sadly more terrifying clarity on your predicament…
Okay, time to tread carefully. There must be a reason why this portal showed up in Vaskin's factory…
Closure
FirsOpentBoethildeGate___nodes[1]._stepC: Camera Pan
You stare at the loosely floating jetsam of whatever pathway once rested here.
The energy crackling around them reminds you of Boethilde… You’re starting to think she’s the demolitionist behind these blocked areas.
I sure wish these were all obeying the law of gravity right now…
Wait… maybe they could be…
You reach into your rucksack, pulling out the doll that you purloined from Boethilde’s mirror domain. Holding it aloft, you clear your throat.
Uh… Boethilde! I command you… to return any places you’ve broken to their original state, should I come across them!
A moment passes, and you begin to wonder if you’ve done something wrong, when suddenly the broken fragments of reality begin to reknit themselves back together!
Gate Animation
Ugh… It feels wrong to command someone like that… I’m not doing that again.
Race you to the other side!
What once was undone, now whole again.
Thank goodness it’s repaired. I was afraid you were going to toss me with a rope around my waist…
The cycle of destruction and rebirth is complete.
Are ye sure this area is sturdy? Maybe we should give it a minute to set…
Knowing our luck, it’ll open up right as I’m passing by...
Should be easier to get around now. Just be sure to send her a thank you card! Might need a few stamps to deliver it to the Mirror Wilds…
I wouldn’t be concerned, undoing the mess she made is the least she could do.
I hardly think you’re using that in the same way Vaskin did.
We should be sure to thank her, should we cross paths again.
Our perseverance is rewarded. Now we can head further into the woods!
Damn, here I was hopin’ you might encourage her to do some cleanin’ around the farm… It’s a joke, that was a joke… Ah, prolly in poor taste.
Arr… Don’t let it weigh on you, {0|mate|lad|lass}. She suggested killin’ ya ‘afore they decided I’d kidnap ya.
Quit your grumbling, you had her repair what she broke!
I wouldn’t worry, {0}. You’re only using a cursed artifact to bend an otherworldly being’s will to your own!
Let’s move…
You put the doll away, and test ground with your boot.
Seems firm enough. Alright, let’s get a move on!
Second Portrait - Interaction___nodes[0]: A second portrait of Boethilde stands before you. You can feel the blackened frame of twisted vines humming with negative energy.
In spite of the danger of your situation, you can’t help but feel a kind of sympathy for this spirit, whose power seems locked in obedience to hateful forces.
You take a deep breath, and answer the quiet, yearning call beyond the surface of the portrait with only a brief hesitation.
FadeWorldMusic
You feel a sinister presence behind you.
For a man of his size, you’d think his breathing wouldn’t be so quiet. You’re not sure he even breathes at all.
The time has come, my little ladies…
You can feel the metal of a goblet in your hands. The liquid inside must be freezing to make the cup so cold to the touch.
Delita is nearby, but you can’t see her. You wish you could, but you also wish she wasn’t there. You’re both sick, now - but HER medicine is different. You don’t know how, but you can feel it.
Why would it be different? Surely father can see you are both equal to the task? That you are both just as fit and capable as each other?
No. You already know the truth: Your father has chosen Delita. She was favored. Her medicine is different. Yours isn’t medicine at all, is it?

‘It’s probably <color=black>poison</color>,’ you think to yourself. With the both of you sick, it stands to reason that father would take the opportunity to dispose of the weakest. In fact you’re sure of it.
BellSound&PortraitSwitch
My little ladies, the pain will soon be over. Drink your <color=black>medicine</color>, now. Your father will be very pleased.
Yes, father will be very pleased with himself. Everything is going exactly as he planned. That’s why he didn’t even bother to be here: He knows Delita will survive and you will die.

You’re so furious with yourself, the frozen goblet begins to feel warm in your hands. ‘Fine, then,’ you think to yourself. ‘Better to be a good girl, and do as father says. It’s better to be put out of my misery.’
You shut your eyes as you raise the goblet to your lips, and pray for the Scales to give you what you deserve…
ResumeWorldMusic
The vision ends, but remains frozen in the painting that looms before you in the present.
It’s easy to see why she’d try to keep these memories hidden… Turns out the Mirror World isn’t the best place to hide your spiritual baggage.
Whatever’s going on, I better keep it moving if I want to get through this.
Start
Second Portrait - Return___nodes[0]._ste: This is the second portrait you’ve found that depicts an important memory from Boethtilde’s life.
Two young sisters stand back to back, poised to drink from mysterious goblets. Standing over them in a position of control is a dark figure with large, menacing hands as bloated as a corpse.
Although their fates are bound within the framing of the portrait, you can feel that the two sisters are now completely alienated from one another.
As you picture Boethilde drinking from her cup, you feel a hateful, blackened seed deep within her being watered by that bitter draught…
Boethilde Mechanic First Appearance Dial: You freeze in your tracks as a haunting noise echoes across the lifeless crags of the Mirror World.
<PLAYERNAME> := Uh oh… Looks like Vaskin’s “security system” just kicked in. I better move fast!
BoethildeScenarioFinalPortrait___nodes[3: This is it. This has to be the final memory. I can feel it!
<speed=slow>… Huh… I don’t feel anything… </speed> What’s going on? Is there something different about this one?
B-Boethilde, wait! I-I think I almost understand what’s happened to you. Maybe we can-
You can’t understand. Not this. Never THIS.
BoethildeScenarioFinalPortrait___nodes[4: Your Father was unfair to you.
BoethildeScenarioFinalPortrait___nodes[5: What about your <color=blue>sister</color>? Don’t you think there’s something strange about what happened to you two?
<speed=slow>… Delita.</speed> That was her name, right? I’ve seen your memories. She… They did something to her, too. Just like they did to you.
No! They knew it wouldn’t cure you... They didn’t care what happened, either way! I’ve seen it! I can feel it!
You want to stop, but you feel yourself carried away on a wave of negative energy. It’s as though the emotions trapped within Boethilde’s memories have infected you.
Why? Does it bother you, knowing what Delita would think of you? Of what you’re doing, right now?!
You obey an evil man! And I’m going to put a stop to this!
BoethildeScenarioFinalPortrait___nodes[6: Boethilde, stop. What happened wasn’t your fault!
Think about it. Whose responsibility was it to… To care for you? To try and make you feel better?
W-Was that his name? Was that who hurt you, and Delita?
Boethilde… Laughs. The nigh-indescribable sound is like a dozen broken bells clamoring out of tune, and makes your skin crawl.
Saved you? Saved you from what? From… Your father?
Boethilde’s body appears to clench, and you perceive a rush of foul wind passing discordantly through a jagged crevice - the unnatural gasp of a furious spirit.
BoethildeScenarioFinalPortrait___nodes[7: H-hold on Boethilde. I know you want to stop me, but I have something I want to say first!
None of this is your fault, Boethilde. You… You were just a kid. You know what that means, don’t you?
It wasn’t your burden to succeed. It wasn’t your responsibility. It couldn’t have been!
Boethilde… Laughs. The nigh-indescribable sound is like a dozen broken bells clamoring out of tune, and makes your skin crawl.
<shake=strong><color=black>Ha-ha-ha-haaah…</color></shake> You don’t understand. You believe children are blameless. Of course you do.
No, Boethilde, you and your sister were innocent! There’s only one person who could’ve prevented all of this… Your own father!
Boethilde’s body appears to clench, and you perceive a rush of foul wind through a jagged crevice - the unnatural gasp of a furious spirit.
BoethildeScenarioFinalPortrait___nodes[8: You know what? I’ve had enough of this!
Good! Throw up your dukes, Boe, cus I’m not scared of you anymore!
BoethildeScenarioFinalPortrait___nodes[9: Your head swims as you suddenly feel swallowed in blackness - pulled, drained down into another, smaller world no bigger than a child's bedroom…
<speed=slow><size=44><wiggle><color=black>... AND WHEN YOU AWAKEN IN THAT DARK AND SILENT ROOM, YOU SEE YOUR DOOM AWAITING YOU…</speed></size></wiggle></color>
<speed=slow><size=44><wiggle><color=black>… A PITCH-BLACK SHADOW IN THE CORNER BY THE BED, BURNING WITH VOICELESS HATRED…</speed></size></wiggle></color>
<speed=slow><size=44><wiggle><color=black>IT WAS A TRAP ALL ALONG, YOU REALIZE… </speed></size></wiggle></color>
<speed=slow><size=44><wiggle><color=black>A SIMPLE TRAP, MADE PERFECTLY FOR YOU. YOU, WHO ONLY KNOW HOW TO</wiggle></speed> <shake><speed=slowest>MOVE FORWARD.</speed></size></shake></color>
<speed=slow><size=44><wiggle><color=black>YOUR CONFIDENCE IS BROKEN. YOUR SENSE OF SAFETY HAS ALL BUT VANISHED.</speed></size></wiggle></color>
<speed=slow><size=44><wiggle><color=black>ALL YOU FEEL IS ICY SWEAT ON YOUR KNITTED BROW, AS YOU REALIZE:</wiggle></size> <size=48><shake=strong>THIS IS IT.</speed></size></shake></color>
Another portrait of Boethilde looms over you. The hostility in her face now seems desperate somehow, as though straining to prevent you from stepping into her malevolent shadow…
Hopefully, this is the last one… For both our sake’s.
WorldMusicFadeOut

As you slip away into the narrow passage of distant memory, you immediately detect a heavy musk of flowers. The smell is so overwhelming, the delicate passages in your nose and face are immediately inflamed.
<color=black>‘They overdid it,’</color> you think through the pain.
BellSound&PortraitSwitch
She failed.
Delita failed. You succeeded.
You weren’t there when it happened. You had quarreled again. Gone to bed without so much as a sidelong glance at each other. You meant every word of what you said. Even now, you won’t apologize.

<color=black>‘Father pushes you harder because you’re the favorite,’</color> you had said. She had looked at you like a frightened animal. You weren’t used to seeing her calm and delicate face twisted so hideously by emotion.
It disgusted you to see the depth of her failure, in that moment. She was the weak one, after all - not <color=black>you</color>. You stayed strong. You stayed obedient.
You even drank your medicine, knowing it would kill you. Even now, standing over your sister’s coffin with your heart beating slowly in your chest, you believe the medicine killed you and not her.
No, she didn’t die from the <color=black>medicine</color>. She died from <shake=weak>sheer <color=red>weakness</color>.</shake>
<shake><speed=slow>And <color=black>YOU</color> will continue on, in perfect obedience. Especially in <color=black>death</color>, you will never fail to obey.</color></speed></shake>
<speed=slowest>… </speed> The pain in your skull is excruciating.
‘They overdid it,’ you think again. ‘Damn these flowers.’
Delita failed. <speed=slow>You succeeded. <shake>You will live on.</speed></shake> <shake=strong><speed=slowest><color=black>YOU WILL OBEY.</color></speed></shake>
ResumeWorldMusic
The vision ends, but remains frozen in the painting that looms before you in the present.
<shake=weak>-sniff-... Owwww…</shake> Is that my sinuses…?
You take a moment to recover, as you fight back what feels like an ocean of tears that aren’t your own.
The more of these memories I find, the more certain I am they hide the key to stopping her. I have to find the last one!
Start
The imposing portrait of Vaskin’s security chief looms over you, her eyes frozen solid in a hateful glare. Or is it something else? Resentment? Judgment? Sorrow? Jealousy?
Beneath the radiant chill, you feel a distant yearning pull you forward, deeper into the painting…
I have a feeling I’m going to regret this… <size=25>Not that it’s ever stopped me before.</size>

As you make your decision, you feel yourself - Not your body, but your immaterial consciousness - pulled into the close, pitch-darkness of an unlit hallway, as though falling into a long and terrible dream.
FadeWorldMusicOut
Stop…
Ow! I said STOP!
Hehe!
Slightly Windy Day Ambient
Your enjoyment of the warm, fragrant breeze blowing through the trees is broken by the pelting of acorns. Then, a familiar voice reaches your ears: Your sister.
You know, Boe, if you would just pay attention to your tutor, we wouldn’t have to do this at all.
If I’d known these “lessons” would involve getting pelted with acorns, I would have been a model student. Besides, it’s far too warm to pay attention.
Acorns and hot weather seem like a fine substitute for father’s usual punishments, don’t you agree?
Father’s punishments are fair. I’m not a good student.
…… Well, I don’t trust the new tutor. There’s something strange about him<speed=slow>…</speed> <shake>-cough-!</shake>
He’s a friend of the royal family, AND a doctor. As the eldest, I thought you would’ve learned to trust father’s taste in friends by now.
… Del! Is that blood?
<shake=weak><speed=slow>No, I-I’ve just…</speed></shake> Smeared my lip stain. Let’s continue, okay? Do try to pay attention, now!
You continue the lesson with Delita, your bickering smothered momentarily in the familiar warmth of their childhood home. After a while, you decide to head back to the house for refreshments.
As you pass the darkened dining room on the balls of your feet, you stumble upon a strange scene…
